book.title: Birthday Girl
book.author: Penelope Douglas
book.description: JORDANHe took me in when I had nowhere else to go. He doesn’t use me, hurt me, or forget about me. He listens to me, protects me, and sees me. I can feel his eyes on me over the breakfast table, and my heart pumps so hard when I hear him pull in the driveway after work.I have to stop this. It can’t happen.My sister once told me there are no good men, and if you find one, he’s probably unavailable. Only Pike Lawson isn’t the unavailable one.I am.PIKEI took her in, because I thought I was helping. As the days go by, though, it’s becoming anything but easy. I have to stop my mind from drifting to her and stop holding my breath every time I bump into her in the house. I can’t touch her, and I shouldn’t want to.But we’re not free to give into this. She’s nineteen, and I’m thirty-eight.And her boyfriend’s father.Unfortunately, they both just moved into my house.

reviews.text: 4/5 ★’s⁀➷god, she’s so beautiful it hurts. my lungs ache for air as desire rips through me.i ache for this man. yk…yeah.°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･･:*.ೃ࿔⋆❀°𝓼𝓹𝓸𝓲𝓵𝓮𝓻 𝓯𝓻𝓮𝓮 𝓻𝓮𝓿𝓲𝓮𝔀dear cole lawson,i want your daddy too🧎🏻‍♀️xoxo💋-your new step motherplease forgive me for taking over a month to write this review😭😭i don’t know what’s gotten into me…unfortunately not pike lawson, ugh.ANYWAYS, this review is gonna be a short one😙°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･･:*.ೃ࿔⋆❀°❥ 𝓂𝓎 𝓉𝒽𝑜𝓊𝑔𝒽𝓉𝓈:i was not expecting to enjoy this as much as i did. taboo romance books are a guilty pleasure of mine…so this book is definitely not for everyone and that’s okay! this was so good tho. i loved how i could feel the chemistry between the two mc’s right away. the TENSION? the sexual tension guys i could not handle it. the build up was so good and usually i hate third act conflicts because i feel that they’re unnecessary, but it was necessary in this book and written really well! can i just say that it blows my mind that this book was written by the same person who wrote punk 57 and credence?okay i actually enjoyed credence,please don’t come for me in the comments🫣but punk 57 was AWFUL. so if you’re looking for a swoon worthy romance written by penelope…this is the one for you bestie. only if you don’t mind the plot.❥ 𝓅𝓁𝑜𝓉:the fmc basically said i’m old enough to date you or your daddy. i respect it🙏🏼his son fucks you over so you fuck his dad, slay queen. she’s 19 and he’s 38…just to warn those who aren’t fans of age gaps.also, just a little psa:i don’t romanticize relationships like these in real life, but i’m never one to judge. at the end of the day, if they’re of age…it’s their choice. i feel it unnecessary that i even have to say this, but i’ve had a lot of people attack me for reading and enjoying certain books🥺so let’s just keep this a judgment free and safe space!<3❥ 𝓌𝓇𝒾𝓉𝒾𝓃𝑔:i think this was the best written book i’ve read by penelope douglas. the smut? bye i was BLUSHINGGG."oh, fuck, fuck..." she cries. "pike...""i know, baby."you feel so good.LIKEEEEE🦋🦋🦋when a man tells you that you feel good when he’s inside you…i stg it’s one of the sexiest things a man could say. argue with a wall bestie.but yeah, the writing kept me hooked and i thoroughly enjoyed it! the chapters were long at times but it pretty much went unnoticed because i was fully invested in what i was reading😌°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･･:*.ೃ࿔⋆❀°❥ 𝓅𝒾𝓀𝑒 𝓁𝒶𝓌𝓈𝑜𝓃:⁀➷❝time passes you by like a bullet and fear gives you the excuses you're craving to not do the things you know you should. don't doubt yourself, don't second-guess, don't let fear hold you back, and don't base your decisions on how happy it will make others. just go for it, okay?❞favorite dilf🥵when i tell y’all i fell in love with this man on PAGE 14…yeah. he was such a perfect man🥹well, most fictional men are. the amount of butterflies he gave me throughout this entire book. his pov’s were EVERYTHING.the only time i’ll ever care about a man’s thoughts or what a man to say😍⁀➷❝you gave people a chance, and they abused it. that's their fault, not yours.❞he tips my chin up so my eyes meet his.❝don't think it has anything to do with who you are. and don't let anyone make you afraid of yourself. you're incredible.❞this is so hot to me. idc. it’s the little things this man did that made me need to press my legs together…just saying. and he was protective? we love that🧎🏻‍♀️❥ 𝒿𝑜𝓇𝒹𝒶𝓃 𝒽𝒶𝒹𝓁𝑒𝓎:⁀➷it's always the same wish. every candle. every time. i want a life i never want to take a vacation from. that's my goal.i love and adore jordan sm🥺after everything she’s been through, she deserves the kind of life daddy pike can give her🤭and i’m over here cheering her on. but move over babe, and pls share.⁀➷❝nowadays, we don't hold a woman responsible for a man's behavior.❞jordan has this “i don’t take shit from any man” attitude and that’s so me. i loved this about her so much hehe.°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･･:*.ೃ࿔⋆❀°❥ 𝒻𝒶𝓋𝑜𝓇𝒾𝓉𝑒 𝓆𝓊𝑜𝓉𝑒𝓈:➛my body warms, remembering that. the sight of her walking naked to the kitchen one night, and how i wouldn’t let her get a midnight snack until i got mine. she was so beautiful.PLEASE😫imagine being pike’s midnight snack…➛he makes me laugh, and when i’m with him, i’m home.➛he took it upon himself to put his hands on her. he’ll never even set his fucking eyes on her again if i have anything to say about it.mmm😋➛❝did he touch you?❞a jealous man will bring me down to my knees in an instant🥵°❀⋆.ೃ࿔*:･･:*.ೃ࿔⋆❀°❥02/28/24𝓅𝓇𝑒 𝓇𝑒𝓋𝒾𝑒𝓌:excuse me while i just go serve myself on a silver platter to my baby daddy, pike lawson🫦rtc hopefully soon!! i have another one to write before this for daisy haites: the great undoing. i’m in such a bad review slump🥲❥ 02/18/24let’s just call this: me, spiraling.i’m spiraling.so, penelope, here i am. happy birthday to me🤸🏻‍♀️it’s not my birthday.i heard pike lawson is daddy…literally!😀

I've been putting off this review because quite a few of my friends on here love this! Apologies to those of you who did love this, please don't be offended, different tastes and all that.....I didn't really like this one that much...not necessarily because of the age gap; my parents have almost 9 years between them, but because of the maturity of the characters, and their suitability for each other. I just didn't find it a believable relationship.I didn't like Jordan. She was meant to be portrayed as this mature and somewhat perfect girl, but she actually did act her age of 19, and sometimes younger. I found her whiny and annoying, with her tantrums and strops! It was meant to be written that she was mature because of the things she had been through, but it didn't really work. She was an immature girl, who had been through some stuff.I didn't like the power imbalance here. Jordan was staying rent free in Pike's house. It made me feel uncomfortable, and a bit like he took advantage of her.Mainly though, I hated the way that they interacted with each other. Pike frequently spoke to Jordan as if she was his teenaged daughter! I got whiplash from going between scenes where he would first lust after her, to then reprimanding her! So creepy.I guess the sex scenes were hot, except for the loofah scene! I mean I don't know what loofah's you use in the states, but here they are really rough and that shit would chafe! Ouch! Oh and the desk corner scene?!! WTAF?!I kind of thought it would have more of a love triangle, but Cole was written as a 2D douche, and rarely spent time with Jordan in the book, so it didn't really work like that. I would have personally liked to see Jordan and Cole have some interaction on page after it all came out.As much, as Jordan annoyed me, she had so far had a shit life, and had been treated like dirt by all of the men in her life, including Pike a few times. I personally think that girl should spend some time on her education, making friends, and to well grow up a little bit, like most 19 year olds.Final thoughts....this book was too long, it dragged, I didn't connect with either of the characters, and I probably wont remember this book in a few days. I was bored reading this and only finished it because of the many people who loved it.I'M SORRY! PLEASE DON'T UNFRIEND ME!!!!

5 stars!Birthday Girlby Penelope Douglas blew me away! Once I read the synopsis for this book, I knew it would be one that I would drop everything to read. And I did. I stayed up till 4 am reading this book the night it landed on my kindle. I wanted to slow down and enjoy the story, but I had to know what would happen. I could not put it down. This book was straight up addicting and I loved every minute!Jordan is a strong and independent woman. She may be only nineteen, but she’s a hard worker and wise beyond her years. Her boyfriend Cole... not so much. He gets them in a bit of a tough spot and they end up moving in with Cole’s father, Pike. Oh Pike… I freaking loved Pike. Everything about him. Pike is 38. He’s a great man and wants to help out his son. The sparks between Pike and Jordan fly, even though neither one wants that. Gah. It’s insane. You just need to read it for yourself to see how this one turns out…“You’ve always watched me, haven’t you? Always wanted to have your fun with me.” I falter, knowing what she says is true. I’ve wanted her since the first time I saw her. “It’s okay,” she whispers. “I always knew, and I always liked it. Keep watching me, Mr. Lawson.”I love forbidden romance and taboo love stories. I love when there is a great slow burn and the sexual tension is so freaking high your siting on pins and needles just waiting and waiting for that one moment… I love angst and when two people have undeniable chemistry, even when it’s obvious they shouldn’t. When there is push and pull and you are completely on the edge of your seat. Seriously, there is nothing better. This book had all this and more in spades.I’ve read a lot of Penelope Douglas books, and I have to say,Birthday Girlis my favorite of hers to date. I gobbled it up, not able to stop reading for a second. It was exhilarating, made me giddy, and was a perfect mix of everything I love in a book. This book is a must read!

"You ever going to tell me what you wish for?""It's bad luck to tell...But I will say, I always wish for the same thing, and everyday it comes true."holy mother of sweet sweet taboo. this was so damn excellent.reading the synopsis(kinky and fucked up)and seeing the author(devour every book they write)i knew i had to have this. and in true chandler-reading-p-doug fashion, i finished this in a day.the story follows jordan and her relationship with her bf's dad, pike. it sounds totally ridiculous & i was really thinking that this story would go the way of"falling for my boyfriend's dad"and be ridiculous smut but it actually... wasn't? jordan has had a hard life with virtually no family or friends. all she really wants is a life of her own and someone to be happy with. and pike wants the same thing.for me, douglas's books work because they focus on themes and stick to them.this book was so refreshingly coherent because you knew that both of the characters wanted the same thing out of life and that the story was going to reach those characters' shared goals.and also, their characters are just so relatable, and human, to me. jordan was sweet but not naive, strong but not a posturing bad ass, and she had REAL dreams. i loved that.and you're probably wondering"but chandler, is there hot ass elicit sex?"and of course the answer is a resounding: fuck yes.so if you're into stories with taboo, great plot, and bumping uglies you've got to add this to ur TBR.********************edit 4/17: a buddy read withjess***********************oh P. Doug, you kinky ass bih. I've never been more EXCITED.

➳A forbidden fruit, a romance filled with angst, and the struggle of battling your inner thoughts of the forbidden and desires, making your craving enhance more and more for Pike and Jordan...➳There are truly noother novels that can utterly compare to anything that is byPenelope Douglas. To me, sometimes I find it hard to believe that once I finish a book by Pen I think to myself"let's google, 'books similar to Penelope Douglas'"but nothing ever seems to come up because every search I make, is never appealing to my eye. Penelope is definitely one of a kind, one that never sees toamaze me with her writing and mental state behind her stories. Every word that she expresses is one that touches home-base to my mind because of how exceptional her fictional"worlds" are.Birthday Girlis yet again another of her novels that has brought a new meaning to "forbidden," and hasindulged in the aspect of age gap romance novels as well, the one thing that made me crave Pike in such a filthy, dirty,and un-moral way.➳When it comes down to theromance between Pike and Jordan, there is nothing more appealing then two individuals knowing internally, and battling their inner thoughts about the wrong thing being right, and the right thing being unfulfilling.Obstacles are in the way of them expressing their candid thoughts, but yet again, that is appeal for me since the angst in this novel is one that I was actually able to tolerate and absolutely adored.However, once the tables turn and they stop fighting each other, there is no turning back to craving each other more and more;"We're victims of circumstance. At least I can feel confident that I would've liked him no matter what. If he were any other guy who came into my bar, sat down, and talked to me, I would've wanted him..."➳Everything there is to know aboutPike is just drool worthy delicious. I have read many romance novels where the male character is older, but nothing utterly compares to Pike and the struggles he has gone through.The emotional aspect of it all is one that has captivated my heart in every essence. No matter what it is, Jordan is also a strong heroine who has proven that anything ispossible when working hard for it and having a mindset of determination. As a girly-girl with the love of pink and an admiration for outside landscaping decorations, Pike is an individual with an old soul but a young body.Nothing about her screams "innocent, the girl next door" kind of appeal. She has hooked everyone's mind with her maturity and way of standing up for herself. That's what attracts Pike in every essence, wanting to claim her, put her in her place and making her his;"I knew the moment his eyes were on me tonight when he walked in and I was at the juke box. I could see him out of the corner of my eye when I walked to the bar, too, and I know that look. Possessive."➳Overall, everything about thisnovel is just meant to be read and thoroughly enjoy. If you are an individual with an age gap issue, then skip this book. However, if you are interested I recommend hopingon this bad boy and riding it like there is no tomorrow. One thing is for sure that has me excited for more books by Pen, her surprise reveal ofMotelis coming our way, and Danni (the female main character) will definitely have me on the verge of mystery behind her story.Thank you so much to Penelope for sending me a copy of her novel, one in which I am looking forward to re-reading in the future because this girl finds her forbidden fruit something to absolutely cherish.➳ARC kindly provided by Penelope Douglas, in exchange for an honest review...NOW LIVE ON AMAZONhttps://www.amazon.com/dp/B07C7N5CBS/➳Follow Me On:Blog♕Instagram♕Facebook♕Twitter

*me bypassing all the stars and settling on 2 again*After continuously hearing my friends gush and preach about the excellence of this book, I've decided to reread and remember why the hell I gave it 2 stars🤡Upon finishing it I literally have nothing to say except I don't know💀 there was nothing wrong with it, I simply don't like it.QUICK SUMMARY THOUGH:Jordan is a 19 year old broke student with a deadbeat boyfriend named Cole.One night Cole fails to pick her up from work so she goes to see an 80s movie alone, where she meets Pike - the super hot dilf.Obviously they connect because Pike is an ancient 80s baby (38) and Jordan is that one mature, different girl because she's obsessed with...you guessed it...the 80s.She's an old soul🤪with young eyes👁️👄👁️and a vintage heart😫In all seriousness, she did have to grow up young due to some unfortunate life experiences. I just don't understand why all the heroines in age gap romances have to be obsessed with vintage shit. Especially since they act relatively their own age.... Does liking your grandma's sweaters instantly make you seem more mature or am I missing something?Either way, turns outPike is Cole's father.Bummer............or not😏 depends on how you look at it.Pike offers his son & Jordan his home because Cole, the dumb fucker that he is, trashed their place and got them kicked out.During that time the unlikely duo forms a bond by acting like a 50s married couple. This basically entails Jordan acting like a middle aged housewife: cooking, cleaning and taking care of the man.....The father being the man, because Cole is god knows where 99% of the time.Pike of course takes the role of a lusty, chauvinistic, jealous provider. I personally had no problem with that because it was the single interesting aspect of his character.Long story short they play house, fall in love and live happily ever after in a suburban neighbourhood with a pool.THE ENDThe only good thing about Birthday Girl is the 19 year age gap. Oh and the fact that she fucked her boyfriend's father. That's all.Since my input was useless I might as well give out a friendly dose of advice:If you try really REALLY hard.....you might just not fall asleep while reading this book😀One of the few times I wasn't yawning was during the sex scenes and when I read this quote:“She seems nervous around you.”I chuckle. He could say the same for me if he were looking close enough.“Well, I can be intimidating,” I joke.“Yeah, she looked like she wanted to straddle your hard, intimidating cock right there in the bar.” My eyes pop open completely, and I glare over at him. “Are you kidding me? What the fuck?”While Pike is being a guilt-ridden drama queen in denial, his best friend gave no fucks😂 I appreciated that.PS: my opinion from 2019 still stands. 50% of this is made up of random little details that still confuse me on their relevance._____________________________________________1ST READ: 09.20192 starsThis was simply way too long for so little plot.

I devoured this book in one night.. it’s that ADDICTING. the way my dislike for age gaps FLEW out the window when I met Pike Lawson… 🥵🌶

Rating and feelings still stand. This was even better than I remembered it to be and it’s now become one of my all-time favourite romances.I think Penelope Douglas wrote and told the shit out of something that could’ve been really awkward and uncomfortable, but she made it fuckin’ work.These two made sense like no other.For some bizarre reason, I haven’t picked up any Penelope Douglas book since I first read this 2 years ago. I’m worried that no other book of hers will live up to this.Regardless, I need to fix that little situation.———————————————Original review:There’s something about forbidden romances/taboo topics that tests and pushes and makes you questions your original stances on it, whatever they may have been, and from whatever preconceived notions they may have originated from.Birthday Girlwas that novel for me. I thought I would be uncomfortable reading it, because really, who would want to get themselves into a predicament where they develop feelings for their boyfriends father? It’s not even something you think about. (I hope…)I love the way the author dealt with this. It’s a sensitive issue and I felt that it was approached and told in a respectful way.I fell in love with the main characters. The development of their stories and their characters made sense to their story. I loved the dialogue, their individuality, the tension, the romance, the sex (my goodness!).I wasn’t expecting to fall in love with it and I love that I did.———————And the romance adventure continueswith my babe!❤️

★*⁠.⁠✧ “𝒜 𝓈𝒽𝒾𝓅 𝒶𝓉 𝒽𝒶𝓇𝒷𝑜𝓇 𝒾𝓈 𝓈𝒶𝒻𝑒, 𝒷𝓊𝓉 𝓉𝒽𝒶𝓉’𝓈 𝓃𝑜𝓉 𝓌𝒽𝒶𝓉 𝓈𝒽𝒾𝓅𝓈 𝒶𝓇𝑒 𝒻𝑜𝓇.” ✧.*★Completely devoured this in one sitting. So addictive, couldn't flip through the pages fast enough!˗ˏˋ ★ ˎˊ˗ 5 𝓢𝓽𝓪𝓻𝓼 ˗ˏˋ ★ ˎˊ˗We follow Jordan and Pike in this standalone forbidden romance. Jordan and Pike first meet on a chance encounter and are immediately drawn together, but since Jordan has a boyfriend, nothing happens between them.But when Jordan and her boyfriend Cole have to move out of their apartment and in with his dad, she comes face to face with Pike. Pike never expected to be so drawn to his son’s girlfriend, but the more time they spend together the more he realizes why none of his relationships have ever worked out. But what happens when they find their attraction and connection more than they can continue to fight?ೃ⁀➷I loved these characters so much. They might have been 19 years apart and forbidden due to how they met and their circumstances, yet they couldn’t have been more perfect for one another.Pike and Jordan were made for each other. They connected emotionally as well as physically, and I couldn’t get enough of them. They shared common interests and you could just feel how genuine everything was between them.I loved the slow burn and chemistry between them, and I was on the edge of my seat waiting for things to explode between them.I was lost in their world and never wanted it to end. I really cannot even begin to describe how much I loved this book and Pike and Jordan. Pike was sexy and sweet, thoughtful and caring. Jordan was genuine and sweet, and I loved how she fit with Pike. They were everything the other needed.ೃ⁀➷Overall, this might be my favorite book Penelope Douglas has written. It was forbidden which has always been my jam, but it was so much more than that. It was believable and just felt right. It wasn’t forbidden just to be forbidden, but the story was good and the characters felt like they were supposed to wind up where they did because that was their story.Nothing about it was forcedand honestly I found myself lost in their story so much that their ages didn’t even register to me and neither did their circumstances. This truly felt like a situation where these two had always been meant to wind up where they did and I was just happy to be along for the ride with them.ೃ⁀➷I can’t recommend this one enough.As soon as I finished, I missed the these two immediately and jumped online and ordered the paperback so that I could add it to my favorites shelf. This will definitely be one I could read again in the future, and I am really looking forward to getting to know Jordan’s friend Dani better in her book Motel.

I read the summary. I knew the premise. And I was ready to rock and roll. If you’re going to sell something to me as taboo, you better deliver.Birthday Girl, sadly, did not deliver. Going in, I expected a full-on romantic triangle featuring a guy and girl in their late teens and the guy’s much older (obviously) father. I anticipated the fact that our main character is interested in both father and son and the struggle this causes to be our primary source of drama. That’s not really what this book is about.Our “triangle” is made up of:-Jordan: a supposedly wise-for-her-years 19 year old who is actually written with the maturity of a tenth grader. Has a passion for landscape design that has literally nothing to do with this book, but has a bizarre amount of page time.- Cole: Jordan's boyfriend and supposedly lifelong best friend. Zero personality or complexity of character. Spends most of the booknotin the book.- Pike: Cole's dad. Tattooed construction worker who has a crappy relationship with the son he had in high school, thanks to a beleaguered relationship with his "psycho ex."I put triangle in quotes because it actually isn't much of a triangle. You’d expect the "taboo" aspect of the novel to be that Jordan falls for her boyfriend's dad. But Cole and Jordan are on the outs from the beginning and Cole is never developed beyond a one-dimensional, clichéd douchebag. Then Jordan is delivered an easy out on a silver platter.She walks in on him in the middle of having sex with another woman! In the pool of the house where she's staying!So not only is Cole a complete LOSER, he's an idiot as well!Once Cole is handily out of the way, the relationship is able to proceed with no further detriment. Is one single character in either Pike's or Jordan's life hesitant about their relationship? Nope. Pike’s friends find it HILARIOUS and spend the majority of the novel cheering him on. Jordan’s sister (who constantly pressures Jordan to give up her dreams and become a stripper in theleast sisterly relationship I’ve ever read) also pressures Jordan to jump into bed with Pike as soon as they meet. Even while she's still with Cole. So there are no barriers to their relationship to be found. Doesn’t sound all that taboo to me.All right, so the book isn't all that scandalous.At least there's a strong, compelling romance between Jordan and Pike, right?Um no. Once they live together, Pike, despite constantly fantasizing about her sexually, treats Jordan like a child. He second guesses her every move. He reprimands her in front of his friends. He tells her who she can hang out with, what she can wear, and how she should spend her time. Like…a parent. So it isn’t a forbidden romance situation that’s sexily taboo. It’s instead the mindset of the character, flipping back and forth between lusty dude to parent that just makes things cringey.But the couple must have heaps of tension and chemistry and the sexy times are sizzling, right!?Wrong again. This book ishellalong considering there is literally ZERO plot and it takes ages for this element of the book to kick in. By the time the characters sleep together, these scenes are uninspired and prosaic. They could be cut and pasted from any $1.99 kindle romance with a mostly naked dude on the cover. There is no emotion and no spark between Jordan and Pike. After waiting 200 pages, it is all overwhelmingly disappointing.People are obsessed with Penelope Douglas, which has me confused. This was my first novel of hers and at this rate, it will be my last.1 star

5 STARSYou make my heart pound so hard it hurts, Jordan. You, your mouth, and who you are, it all makes me want to touch you. It makes me not want to stop this.”When I saw that Penelope Douglas was coming out with a surprise release, I didn't even blink before adding the book. When I read the blurb, I think a small part of me cringed, while a bigger part of it was screaming GIMME!! See, I love a forbidden romance. But there's something about the whole father thing that just gives me the blehs too. It takes an immensely talented author to pull off a story like this without making it torrid, pervy and cringe worthy. Luckily for me, Penelope Douglas is that rare unicorn of authors that can not only pull it off, but make you fall in love with it too.And that’s what I am. Her boyfriend’s father. Nothing more. But deep in my heart, the small ember growing bigger and bigger every day knows that’s a lie.Jordan is a heroine that's impossible not to like. Mature beyond her years due to the way she grew up, this is not your typical 19 year old. With a mother that left years ago and a deadbeat trailer trash father, it's been her and her older sister fending for themselves. With an adorable adoration of all things 80s, Jordan was easy to connect with. While her sister has no problem supporting herself and young child by being a stripper, Jordan wants more. Moving in with her boyfriend's father rent free will allow her to save up enough to put herself on her feet and to get that more that she craves. But it also brings a whole lot more complications in the form of an interaction to her boyfriend's young father that seems to only intensify over time.I think what really made me connect with Jordan is how well Douglas handled this character's development. There's glimpses into a teenage girl; enough to make you remember her age. But at the same time, she's not sheltered and naive. This is a woman that knows what she wants. She's not shy and afraid to go after it. She's solid. She's mature beyond her years. But yet she's still a young woman.I simply adored the way the romance unraveled and developed between her and Pike. There was something so real and raw about it. It wasn't torrid, even though it was forbidden. You understand their attraction. You believe in it. You yearn for it. The tantalizingly slow build makes it even better.I know everything we’re doing is wrong, and I don’t know how I’m going to explain this to anyone, but right here—right now—I don’t want to be anywhere else. I wish I could die as happy as I am right now.Pike is a man that was forced to grow up fast when he became a father at 19. Giving up on his dreams to support his new son was not something he ever gave a second thought about. This is clearly a man that wants a relationship with his son but due to circumstances, it's a struggle. I don't want to give too much away about the dynamics, but trust me when I tell you that you will understand. You'll even root for him.The romance and age difference is handled with tact and done incredibly well. The situation with the boyfriend is as well. There was simply nothing about this book that I didn't love. This was clearly a book the author wanted to write and was meant to, and it came across vividly clear in the writing. I fell so hard for this story and these characters.Birthday Girl is a story of forbidden romance, yes. But it's so much more than that, too. It's a story of strength and forgiveness. Of making mistakes and learning how to write them. It was raw, and sexy, and unique. I loved every single page of it and cannot recommend it enough.ARC courtesy of author in exchange for an honest reviewFind me on:

MY REVIEWcan also be found on my blog:KITTY KATS CRAZY ABOUT BOOKSI'm not even going to write up my review how I normally would, just go in blind, enjoy, love like I did, this surpassed all my expectations, it easily went from a 5 star to jumping onto my favourite shelf of 2018. And that alone should tell you how much I loved this book. Get ready to swoon because swoon you will!! Pike crash landed into my heart the very first chapter and he's still there even after I inhaled that last page. A bittersweet feeling that my time in this book has ended. I wholeheartedly recommend this book!!Is it so bad that I didn't even read the synopsis or really take note of the cover, the name of the author was enough to want me chase this book, this book had everything that I love in a book, the angst, the feels, god this author is known to throw us under the bus with her feels and this book rolled over me as it took off, I freaking loved this book. I'm not going to lie this has been done before, older guy, younger female but I don't think I've read the sons girlfriend before so I was a wee bit worried that some sleaze would make it's way under my skin but how far wrong was I..This book was perfection!!

reread (2020): 5 stars— still hate Cole more than i hate Joffrey and Ramsay combined.— karma is literally the queen here since Jordan dated Cole's dad. best accidental revenge ever.* * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * *original rating (2018):5 STARSThis book was apage-turnerfor me! I literally could not stop reading because I was so hooked, even from the beginning. Whenever Penelope Douglas comes out with a new novel, I’m always so excited to read it because she writes such intriguing stories that hassteamy as hellsex scenes. Honestly though, like, her steamy scenes are so freaking great. And this book does not disappoint at all.— The heroine,Jordan(19), loves anything about the 80s. She’s always had an old soul because she is wise beyond her years and she had to grow up pretty early because of her lifestyle when she was young. She’s smart, understanding, and super mature. And I like her but to be honest, she’s not my personal favorite. I’m pretty biased though because I love sarcastic and witty heroines that makes me laugh out loud. Just because that is my personal preference, does not mean that Jordan is a horrible heroine. She’s actually pretty great and I admire her resilience, her perseverance, and her apparent independence.— The hero,Pike(38), is the father of her "boyfriend", Cole, at the time... damn. The drama though... AWKWARD. Even though he’s quite old (19 years older than her), he is still so hot and has a young soul, but also very mature and responsible. He is kind of perfect, actually. He has the perfect house, the perfect job, and so on. I really like Pike because he’s such a nice guy and he’s pretty sweet as well. I like him better than Cole that's for sure.Something happened which left Cole and Jordan move into Cole’s father’s home and, well... you can just imagine how this goes. I was so excited when they moved in becausedraaaaaama. And there is definitelyplenty of drama and angst.The first time Pike and Jordan meet, I definitely feel their connection and from then on, I was already rooting for them! And the sexual tension between the two...?HOT DAMN. It was really great. I have no words to explain how sexy it was, to be honest, haha. And, like, Cole who?Speaking ofCole... Ok, so I’m going to rant here just a little bit because I have a lot of emotions when it comes to this guy and I need to get it out. So there are someSPOILERS AHEAD...---this bitch... i fucking hate him. For real though, he was such an asshole and super selfish. His excuses were that he was lazy and he felt that he was not worthy and some shit like that. AND I AGREE. I’m sorry I’m being a little mean but he deserves it. He does not deserve Jordan at all. And he definitely is not worthy of her. I don’t care that he kind of redeemed himself at the end. What he did to Jordan? Unforgivable. Imma just list out the things he did to her:- Forgot to pick her up on her birthday because he was too busy partying and cheating on her for the first time behind her back! and went to jail on the same night and got evicted from their apartment.- He was barely there when she needed him, always partying and cheating. always makes her do all the chores in the house because he has a hangover or is out partying.- When she doesn’t want to sleep with him or be intimate, he barely resists and blames her for all his shit and how he’s not good enough for her (because he clearly isn’t) and how she has her shit together (because obviously, she does as she’s not lazy and she’s also not a loser like him. Harsh, I know).- and when he promised he would pick her up from her work at, like, 2 in the morning, he doesn’t. Instead, he sent one of his friends to pick her up, and guess who picks her up? HER ABUSIVE EX-BOYFRIEND, who he is still friends with. Like tf? And that’s not the worst thing. He basically kind of forced her to sleep inside the bar because why in the world would she get inside the car with that asshole?- and he sleeps with this bitch, Elena, for God knows how long, on their bed and in their pool on the nights that she was working late. This is probably the reason why he can’t pick Jordan up anywhere because he’s too busy cheating behind her back.- he never fixed her car, which he promised to do...- he never made an effort to apologize to Jordan for all the shit he put her through!!! And he got mad because his father and her got together at the end? Like, I get it, it's weird as hell and I would be uncomfortable too but for real though? Does he even have the right? He screwed up so many times and instead of Pike and Jordan being sorry, he should be the one who is sorry. I honestly don't think he deserved their kindness, and when they forgave him so easily, I was just so frustrated because I wanted him to literally EXPLAIN himself and APOLOGIZE to them for being a shitty son and boyfriend. But did he do that?nooooooooo. Instead, he ran away, barely made an effort with both of them, NEVER said sorry to them, and made himself look like the victim. ugh. I literally feel no remorse for him.All he ever did was party, cheat behind Jordan's back, and spend all the money they were saving up on booze and partying. So, I’m glad she ended up with Pike.There was no Cole and Jordan at all. There never was. Like, what kind of friend/boyfriend remains friends with their girls’ ex who used to hurt them physically? A stupid guy, that’s who. I just wished someone kicked his ass or he grovelled more because I love drama lol. I don’t care about the reasons as to why he is how he is. They’re honestly not good enough and pretty pathetic.ANYWAYSI’m done with my rant about Cole. As you can see, I love the guy to pieces.If you loveforbidden romance,slow burn romance,FANTASTIC sexual tension, a hero who is quite older and is super mature and charming,STEAMY AS HELLsex scenes, andangst, then this is the perfect book!I recommend 100%, 10/10
This was perfection... Well, for me anyway. 💖 Exactly what I needed right now, and going onto the favorites list.

Oh dear… Let me grab a metal shield!I am so disappointing, people! This is not going to sound nice but it's my most sincere opinion.Cutting to the chase:.) The angst factor I was expecting from this was completely missing..) At some point I felt it was dragging out as I had a feeling I read this typical story line before, (good girl from trashy caravan park, drunk father and stepmother, striptease job offers, slutty sister and so on)..) I found the characters development flat and the chemistry between the MCs meh..) There were a few eye roll moments culminating in the final scene, when he goes to the motel looking for Jordan. Dear Lord! That was so cheesy!.) Cole was a douche the whole story until the end when he suddenly had an abrupt change of heart that I didn’t see coming. It felt bizarre. When had he grown up?.) I never got to understand why a mature girl such as Jordan was putting up with him. That sad little story about the dead friend didn’t work with me, it was not a strong enough reason.And here is where I make my point about the lack of angst, the kind of angst Penelope Douglas had done in the past in grand style. The boyfriend was a little shit and I couldn’t wait to see Jordan kick his butt. The dad was a very good man. Too good to my shameless taste.Summing up, it all ended up a bit tasteless, like food with no salt.I was expecting Birthday girl to be heavy in the feels but it turned out as just another book I will forget about it in a week.Peace!

*for some reason i gave this 2 stars. well, i was too generous for no fucking reason. let me fix this.There's no way you can convince me that a 19yo is the right fit for a 38yo guy... like no matter what you've been through, you're still in your maturing age lolAs I said before, this book seems like it's about a string of poor decisions that you make when you're horny. These two absolutely weren't made for each other. I can't count the number of fits Jordan had throughout the whole story. And the worst part of it is that you can't blame her. Or maybe you can, but it is something you would expect of her. She is fucking 19.What you shouldn't expect is Pike's tantrums. Like... maybe get a grip? you're literally 38 years old. why are you acting like a spoiled, jealous brat? It just doesn't make sense to me. I can see the attraction and the sexual tension between them, sure, but nothing beyond that.Also, everything was so convenient. Her boyfriend treated her like trash and wasn't even around for most of the time. I wish she had written this with him being attentive and caring, but it would make Jordan a son of a bitch for getting together with his father (but the drama though, it would be amazing and much less boring). penelope basically took the easiest route out with this plot.I think this fits into that box that most romance books close themselves into: everything is about them, and even though the characters go through things outside of the relationship, it's very dull and boring.I also wish authors would stop writing lead female characters who have no friends, just a project of a boyfriend. It's so fucking weird. If she had a girl friend to slap her in the face and tell her to break up with her shitty boyfriend and go get her shit together, everything would be easier. I'm telling you. people need FRIENDS!!!
ok this was fun ngl 🤚🏻 RTC maybe

book.title: Heartless
book.author: Elsie Silver
book.description: Working as a nanny for the world's grumpiest single dad should be simple...except she can't keep her eyes off him. And he can't keep his hands off her.Cade Eaton is thirteen years older than Willa Grant, and he barely looks her way, even though she's living in his house for the summer. That is, until she gets him into the hot tub one night for a game of truth or dare. Then, all bets are off--and so are their clothes.Cade is gruff, a little rough around the edges, but broad-shouldered ranchers with calloused hands and filthy mouths are this city girl's kryptonite. So who is she to resist?But it's in their quietest moments together that he softens. It's in the unexpected way he takes care of Willa that she realizes his hardened exterior is just a façade. And it's when she watches him go all sweet with his little boy that she starts to fall for him, whatever the consequences.Someone once convinced Cade that his best wasn't good enough. But Willa has never felt more cherished than she does in his arms.Her contract says this arrangement is only for two months.But her heart says this is forever.

reviews.text: ➸3.75 stars“and i realise in that moment maybe i am heartless after all, because the beautiful girl with the copper hair grinning back at me right now is the one who stole it."when willa said “i’m in my cowboy era” yeehaw, me too!! 🤠i picked this book up when it first came out but dnfd it half way through because i wasn’t really feeling it but i’m so glad i picked it back up because i did enjoy it more the second time around!! maybe i am a sucker for a single dad romance after all.➳cade eaton:a single dad who had to step up at a young age and take care of his family and run the family farm since his dad fell apart after his mother’s death. the way he was a goner for willa from the very start. i loved his grumpy and broody exterior but he’s a major softie at heart for his girl and his kid and he is fiercely protective. i loved that we got to see both of those sides of him. a couple things that did put me off tho was his inner thoughts about willa.. it was giving a prepubescent teen boy. why was this man thinking with his dick majority of the book 😭 like i don’t need to know that ur dick is getting hard whenever willa walks in the room. like he constantly says she’s young (she’s a grown 25 year old woman btw) but you’re literally horny for her 24/7.. make it make sense. like he was constantly sexualising willa, like we get it she’s young and hot. i did enjoy seeing his development from the start to the end and that he finally opened his heart up to love again after being hurt and that he showed his vulnerable side. and his dirty mouth?? had me going absolutely feral 🥵➳willa grant:「“save a horse, ride a cowboy.”」when her show horse is injured and her brother closes the bar for renovations where she works at, willa finds herself with a free summer. willa is constantly taking up new things which makes her feel lost in life, she needs something to help ground her and lead her in the right direction, enter luke and cade. cade needs a nanny for the summer and her best friend summer asks her if she will nanny for her soon to be brother-in-law’s son and she is in. as a city girl through and through and who also loves boozy brunches with my girls, willa was easy to connect with. i loved how she never failed to put cade in his place. the bond she shared with luke was just so heartwarming to witness, it was no doubt my favourite aspect of this book. the pool party scene (iykyk) truly iconic and was very much justifiable!! she should’ve thrown hands with that kid’s mother too. i love that willa takes no shit from anyone and that she lives life carefree and that she just wore her heart on her sleeve. she’s a total firecracker, completely unhinged and incredibly loyal and i loved her for that.“you are pretty fun. he worships the ground you walk on.”“as all men should.”some minor issues i had:the overuse phrases!! like we got it the first 20 times cade is always “scowling” and willa “rolled her lips” pls stop 😭i wish we got to see more of cade’s relationship with his son but he was way too busy being fixated and horny for the nanny lmaooo.even though this was a slow burn it relied heavily on insta-lust. there was an instant infatuation and desire between them from the get-go. literally right from the very start when he saw her at the cafe and didn’t know who she was, he was getting all horny for her and her curves. i would’ve also liked to see more of how willa and cade’s connection developed. it was only about them having sex after they finally got together ugh. i literally got the ick when they done it at luke’s 6th birthday party because he could’ve literally walked in on them.i did find the writing to be pretty cringe at times like:murder me with your dick, please, sir.my kitty is too sore from riding your massive pole.ANOTHER ICKKKK. how do you write that with a straight face.— overall, this was a fun read. although i had some minor issues, they didn't detract from my overall enjoyment — but tbh, i do feel like i’m being a little generous with my rating bc i was contemplating rating it 3 stars 🫣 anyway, let me go eat the rest of this series up!!「chestnut springs series」#1 Flawless —2.5 stars#2 Heartless —3.75 stars#3Powerless—3 stars#4Reckless—4.25 stars#5Hopeless—1.25 stars───♡─────────────aaa this was such a fun read. i definitely enjoyed this one sm more the second time around!! rtc—re-entering my cowboy era 🤠

CADE. FUCKING. EATON. D.A.D.D.Y. The grumpy dirty cowboy that you are… shouldn’t be legal.I’m ruined. I simply will never ever recover from this man. The way he had my soul LEAVING MY BODY the entire time. Goodbye. Dead. Deceased.No because it is the way that this book had me absolutely FERAL! For a moment there I thought I might actually need to be put under surveillance because I was suffering from a severe case of giggling and kicking my feet syndrome!!! I couldn’t stop. My boyfriend kept calling me out for how hard I was blushing. MIND YOUR BUSINESS.Elsie silver miss ma’am YOU KNEW EXACTLY WHAT YOUR WERE DOING WRING THIS MAN! I now have a thing for fictional single dads and I’m never coming back from that. Now listen, I like my fair share of spice don’t get me wrong, but it’s never been something that has significantly added to my overall opinion and enjoyment of a book as a whole… UNTIL NOW. The way this women writes spice has me knowing at the bars of my food damn enclosure. My toes almost fell off from how hard they were curling. Everything about it is perfection no fucking notes. It’s filthy yet tasteful at the same time idfk but I know the bar is now unreasonably high.Speaking of filth. The MOUTH ON THIS MAN!!! I was about ready to internally combust. How in the hell is anyone going to live up to him???? My kindle highlights need to go to church. I fear my book boyfriend roster is in serious trouble right now. Help.Okay something I was missing from book 1 (even though I still ate it up don’t worry) was I felt like Rhett and Summer were just lacking a bit of chemistry and didn’t feel that attached to them as a couple. But Willa and Cade… WHOLE OTHER STORY OH MY GOD IM OBSESSED WITH THEM! They own a piece of my heart. The single dad nanny trope was done so well I didn’t expect I would love it as much as I did.WILLA>>>>> don’t even start with me I’m alsoSo obsessed with her! He firey, gives zero fucks, takes no shit, and will go to war for the people she loves personality was EVERYTHING!! I genuinely want to be friends with her so bad 😭😭 her relationship with Luke melted my heart. Cade being so irrecoverably enraptured by her from day 1 squeezed my heart to bits.“I think I can’t get you out of my head, no matter how hard I try. I think you’re too damn tempting and that I’m too damn complicated. I think you smell like him, and I can’t fucking stand that.”“I’ve watched you with my son. I’ve watched you, period. I’ve longed for you. I went crazy tonight thinking of you out with Lance. I know in my bones that I won’t want to let you go at the end of the summer, but I’ll take what I can get. Because you’re too fucking special to pass up. Fuck my promises, that’s what I was going to say.”“You want it all, Willa? The house? The babies? The ranch? You want me, Willa? Good. Because I’m sick of holding back with you. You’re not going anywhere. You belong here, with me.”

when i say i hate men… never ever do i mean this lovely man right here. i’d run through a fucking wall to get to cade eaton🫠cade really puts the EAT in Eaton cuz tell me why Cade was EATon my ass through these pages?! there’s something so real and poetic about the grumpy, scowling, quite, lonely single father and the witty, loud, carefree woman. that dynamic will always be famous, mark my words 🫣this book has rotted my brain levels never seen before. Willa was a shot of espresso. i wanna emphasize my gratitude towards her descriptions of Cades body, physique, mannerisms, and outfits throughout this book. she is me and i am her. i am no better than a man with the thoughts i had running through my head in those romantic moments. her relationship with Luke was so mfing cute. i love that fictional kid to pieces. the way Cade crumbles in her presence should be studied. i wanna hear him whimper 😪

a man has enough money and brains to let me live whatever wild ass career dreams i want while giving me babies on a nice ass ranch? willa mighta had the right idea with skippin panties under those dresses, just sayin.

Obsessed with this book and Cade & Willa. We've got SO MANY great tropes: single parent, small town, & grumpy x sunshine. Plus cowboys and a gorgeous ranch setting!I think Willa and Cade are my perfect romance book characters. Willa is sweet but also feisty and speaks her mind. Cade is grumpy, initially closed off, and rough around the edges, but once you break those walls down, he is insanely sweet and thoughtful. One of my favorite things about the single-parent trope (outside of cute kiddos) is that we instantly know the single parent is kind, caring, and responsible. So even while Cade was being grumpy, we see his sweet side with his son from the beginning.👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻Round of applause for Elsie Silver👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻 I cannot wait to read the rest of the books in the series!!

give me anage-gap, single parent, grumpy-sunshine, small-town romancewith an adorable 5 yr old kid and my heart is wide open↠Willa(h) &Cade(H) meet-cute was interesting to say the least when her panties dropped out of her purse and Cade picked them up for her. It only got better when Cade realized that he picked up his son’s nanny lingerie up. 💀“Willa.” Cade says my name like it’s a demand. “You need to wear proper undergarments while you’re at work. You can’t be dropping them out of your purse around a child.”I plaster on the sweetest smile I can muster and turn to look back over my shoulder. “I’ll be ready for your inspection tomorrow, boss.”↠ we have a heroine whomade the Hero begfrom the beginning quite literally!! After Cade acted like a dick, Willa called him out on his behavior and was ready to quit the job. She knew that he needed her more than she needed this job and that’s the moment I fell in love with her. 🥰“Beg.”“Pardon me?”“You heard me.” Her lips don’t even twitch. She’s not joking at all. “Beg.”↠ Cade and Willa’sbanterhad me smiling like a fool. They had the perfect grumpy-sunshine dynamic where it was way too easy for Willa to rile Cade up. Their text exchanges were some of my favorite moments.Cade:Willa, I’m working. If everything is okay, we don’t need to chat.Willa:I put my panties on.Willa:Are you ignoring me?Willa:I figured you’d be proud. Day one and I’m knocking all the rules right out of the park.Cade:If I pay you more, will you stop texting me about this?Willa:Probably not. I don’t need the money. I’m just easily bored, and poking the bear is fun.↠ a fun fact about me, but I’m in love withpetty heroinesand guess who is a petty heroine? Willa fucking Grant. Willa pushing a child into a pool on his birthday, after he embarrassed Luke (Cade's baby, but Luke is mostly mine) is peak literature. Willa Grant 🤝 Nico Russo being petty people pool pusher is the kind of idols my teenage self needed to see years ago.Willa might be a bit of a psycho—after all, she did just push a child into the pool—but the more time I spend with her, the more I feel like she’s my psycho.↠ THATFIRST BLOWJOB SCENEWHEEWH 🥵 idk if I read too many romances already that I’m at a point where I skim most sex scenes, cause the all written the same, but I didn’t skip one single line when reading this BJ scene. There is just something so good about a stuck up man losing his control and go feral. Cade x Willa BJ porch scene will live rent free in my head.“Keep talking like that and I’m going to fuck the filth right out of your pretty mouth.”I arch a brow at him as goose bumps break out over my body. The air between us sizzles. “Excuse me?”He swipes the back of his hand over his mouth, like he’s pulling away the filter that’s been there all along. “You heard me, Red. You keep barking at me like that and I’m going to put you on your knees, open those strawberry lips, and fuck your face just to shut you up.”↠ Cade Eatonyou little grumpy catwith the biggest heart. His love for his love and how much he cares for all the people around him were his best attributes. I only wish him the most happiness that Talia bitch couldn’t give him. Her loss, but Willa’s win. 🙏“Red, don’t marry a man who rolls his eyes at you.” “You roll your eyes at me all the time.” Fuck, I need to stop doing that. She deserves better. “Don’t marry me either.”↠ last but most importantLUKE EATION ILY PRECIOUS BOY. cutest kid who has my heart. 💖💕🖤♥️“Willa!” Luke’s quickly forgotten guitar rests on the step as he tears across the front lawn toward me. “I’m so glad you’re back!”CONCLUSIONHeartless has only proven once again why the single-parent trope is and will always be a smash for me. That trope has never disappointed me so far. I didn’t like the first book in this series that much, but after knowing the trope of Heartless and getting to know Cade and Willa a bit in Flawless and seeing how they were complete opposite and I KNEW they were going to be my favorite couple in this series.Comment to add after just finishing the bonus epilogue for Heartless: omg I love Cade, Willa, Emma and Luke all so much. my family, my heart. why are they so wholesome. Everybody go read this book now! 😩┍━━━━━━━━»•» «•«━┑CHESTNUT SPRINGS SERIES#1 Flawless – 2.75 stars (Rhett x Summer)#2Heartless– 4 stars (Cade x Willa)#3 Powerless – 2 stars (Jasper x Sloane)#4 Reckless – 3.75 stars (Theo x Winter)┕━»•» «•«━━━━━━━━┙

I am obsessed with this book. I got lost in this book. There is nothing heartless about Cade Eaton. I mean, come on:"You want it all, Willa? The house? The babies? The ranch?"YES CADE SHE DOES. WE ALL DO. 🤠🖤🐮👨‍👩‍👦🦖🧑🏽‍🌾🌶️🥵Cade and Willa's story is best described by Summer as follows:"It's two people who were both a little lost until they ended up on the same path and walked together for a while."Ok, so. Basically next to the dictionary definition of "cowboy" there is just a picture of Cade Eaton. That's it. He is the literal ideal cowboy that you picture reading a western. He's also unproblematic. Drama free. DOESN'T F*CK ANYTHING UP with Willa! Just so solid and sturdy, though at first to a fault. Anyways, I don't even usually like books with the single-dad trope or the nanny trope. But this book??? Maybe it's just Cade and his batman-cowboy persona, or maybe its just how well-written this story is. It doesn't feel like you're reading some cheesy story about a dad and a nanny. Its deeper than that, more realistic. Willa is this fiery, free-spirited redhead who is sort of lost and doesn't know what she wants from life yet. She's never nannied or babysat in her life but agrees to help watch Cade's son Luke (who is simply the sweetest funniest little man ever) for the summer to pass the time. Cade is big and broody and jaded, hardheaded, stubborn, stuck in his ways, doesn't trust others and doesn't smile. Enter our spunky Willa girl and expect both of their worlds to catch on fire.Think:spiiicy slow burn, him trying to pretend he's not falling for her, her hella flirting and teasing and generally driving him crazy, the sweetest moments between little Luke and Willa, the slow domestication of firey Willa and the undoing of strict Cade, sexy hot tub nights with truth or dare, rodeo events, slow dancing in the kitchen, a possessive and caretaking cowboy daddy, and fun cameos from Summer and the Eaton boys.Flying through this series. I loooved this even more than the first one. I thought the spice was much better (something about Willa and Cade just felt really authentic and raw), the bantering was even more perfect, and if I hadn't already fallen in love with Chestnut Springs and Wishing Well Ranch I am now totally a goner. This book was so sweet and lovely. I don't necessarily need another book about Rhett and Summer, but I'd do terrible things for a sequel where we see more of just Luke and Willa and Cade living their happy ever after together. Hoping to get a little of that in the next two books. Anyways, Elsie Silver is SUCH a good writer and this book had zero flaws.

4.5 Stars ☆"And I realize in that moment maybe I am heartless after all, because the beautiful girl with the copper hair grinning back at me right now is the one who stole it."I am definitely in my cowboy era and don't plan on leaving anytime soon. Well, at least until I finish this series. Chestnut Srings should be a real place because I want to go visit now.Heartless is the second book in the Chestnut Springs series, and ya'll, it did not disappoint. This small town, age gap, single dad/nanny, cowboy romance hit the spot with its banter, sexual tension, and humor. This had me giggling and kicking my feet throughout. It has now easily become my favorite in the series so far.Cade is a rancher who will be busy for the summer and is in need of a nanny for his son Luke. He has turned down all of the applicants as he didn't like them and is now desperate. When Summer suggests her friend Willa that has come to visit. Now, seeing that Willa is the woman that he had an awkward meet cute with at the local coffee shop earlier that day, but what other options does he have. It's when he sees Willa interact with his son that he decides to give her a chance.Cade Eatondamn this man is a grumpy, protective, broken selfles caretaker that indeed is so much daddy. The man who is always responsible and serious has a dirty dirty mouth that I was eating up. The way that he is so soft and sweet with his son and Willa and helping her with overcoming her insecurities in the sweetest of ways had me in a swoon worthy trance.“Thank you. Thank you for being the first person in my life to put me first, to give me options. I’m not sure I deserve that gift, but I know that I’ll never forget it for as long as I live.”Willa Grantis a breath of fresh air with her sunshine, strong, sassy, and fiesty personality. She had some insecurites but didn't let them define the badass that she is. I love that she tells it like it is and gives herself pep talks when she feels uncomfortable. One of the best things that she did was push a kid into a pool for picking on Luke. This had me downright laughing so hard.“Beg.” “Pardon me?” “You heard me.” Her lips don’t even twitch. She’s not joking at all. “Beg.”Luke EatonI absolutely loved and adored this kid so much. He kept things light and had me cracking up at some of the things that came out of his mouth. It was definitely never a dull moment when he was around. The relationship that him and Willa had was heartwarming to witness.“Hey, Willa?”“Hey, Luke,” I reply dryly, since the be quiet part obviously didn’t register. “Sometimes I wish you were my mom.”Cade and Willatheir relationship blossomed slowly from their push and pull from each other to chemistry that was undeniably palpable. They were the definition of opposites but fit so well together. Some of their moments were straight fire.“You want it all, Willa? The house? The babies? The ranch?” I nod again because it’s all I can do. I want all that. With him. “You want me, Willa?”“Yes.” I cry out against the soft fabric, nodding frantically as he slams into me. I want him so badly it hurts. “Good. Because I’m sick of holding back with you. You’re not going anywhere. You belong here, with me.”I found myself not being able to put this book down. It had me wanting more with every turn of the page. I also enjoyed Summer and Rhett so much more in this than their book. They had the best group text that I wanted so badly to join in on. The ending was just so sweet and wholesome. It makes me want to be a part of this big family.Favorite quotes and moments“Eaton. You grumpy motherfucker. You just laughed,” I blurt. “Yeah, Red. I did.”“Daddy Cade, you’re a whole lot prettier when you smile.”"Willa might be a bit of a psycho— after all, she did just push a child into the pool— but the more time I spend with her, the more I feel like she’s my psycho."“Keep talking like that and I’m going to fuck the filth right out of your pretty mouth.”“Excuse me?”“You heard me, Red. You keep barking at me like that and I’m going to put you on your knees, open those strawberry lips, and fuck your face just to shut you up.”Did you know I fell in love with you before I fell in love with your dad?”~~~~~~Pre-review:It's time for the grumpy single dad cowboy, Cade 🤠 and to find out if he really is daddy 🤭🤭

4.75 stars“I realize in that moment maybe I am heartless after all, because the beautiful girl with the copper hair grinning back at me right now is the one who stole it.”I’m officially and wholeheartedly in my country-boy era because they just do it better than any other fictional men. What the hell kind of crack did Elsie put in this book that made me devour it in just two days?! This was the funniest, swooniest, and sweetest book—so adorable. I’m so in love with Cade, Willa, and Luke and their sweet little wholesome family.Heartlessis my comfort book, and I can read it over and over again. I loved the story so much, and it’s adorable because of Willa and Luke’s precious, chaotic moments. And it’s sexy because Willa and Cade are fire. The pacing was a little slow in this one, but the whole storyline was constructed perfectly from the start to the most endearing ending. Even though the nanny and single-dad trope is such a typical plot in the small-town romance genre, Willa and Cade made everything about this book perfect with their top-notch banter and scorching chemistry.I quirk a brow in her direction. “You know the rule, Red?”“You wear the hat, you ride the cowboy.” She winks at me, looking fucking adorable wearing my hat.Willa Grantis fire. If attitude were a person, she’d be it. A bartender turned nanny for a hot single dad who’s also a rancher. She’s carefree, funny, loyal, and has a witty sense of humour with a very sassy mouth. She’s such a badass but also a little insecure about her future and herself. This girl is a firecracker, a hurricane who melted all the walls Cade built around himself as protection—just by earning his trust. And she’s the girl who pushed a kid in the pool because he bullied Luke. I wanna be badass Willa Grant so badly right now. I loved her from the very first chapter, and the urge to leave everything behind and be Willa Grant is so strong right now.“Willa might be a bit of psycho—after all, she did just push a child into the pool—but the more time I spend with her, the more I feel like she’s my psycho.”Cade Eatonis a hot cowboy, a rancher, and a single dad to the most adorable kid. He’s grumpy and a little infuriating—he doesn’t know how to make jokes or even communicate sometimes—but he’s the most thoughtful, selfless, and caring person I have ever seen. His actions speak louder than his words, and the things he did for his family as the oldest child, as a single dad to Luke, and as the most amazing person Willa needed—were everything. And we all know he’s also known for his dirty, dirty mouth, which is a threat to my ovaries.“Daddy Cade, you’re a whole lot prettier when you smile.”Cade Eaton’s dirty mouth and Willa Grant’s sassy mouth are the most dangerous combination in this world.Cade and Willa are opposites in every way. While Willa is fire, Cade is ice—but the moment they met, there was always this undeniable electric chemistry and connection between them. No matter how much he tried to push her away or tried to hate chaotic Willa, he was always drawn to her. And she’s the only one who thawed his icy heart. They complement each other so well that I can never imagine one without the other. From their first meeting—when she dropped her panties—to the moment she asked him to beg her to stay, everything about them was epic. Their forced proximity, the slow burn, the push-and-pull relationship made the sexual tension between them palpable, and that hot tub scene was chef’s kiss. They both knew what they wanted, and they took it however they wanted. They are mother and father for real.“What are you doing?” I grumble as I wipe at my watery eyes, no doubt smudged with mascara.“Taking care of you,” he replies without even looking my way. His tone says that I just asked him a stupid question.“That’s fine. I can take care of myself.”“I know you can, but you don’t have to because I’m here to help.”The way they both cared for each other and tried to be there for each other when needed—it was beautiful. Cade had trust issues because of his ex-wife, but Willa proved to him that she was the one he needed. She’s his comfort person and his little troublemaker. Willa was insecure about herself, but Cade made her feel like she was the most amazing, perfect person—that she could be anyone she wanted, and he’d still love her. He’d leave all the post-it notes for her forever. They are so perfect and everything, I love them so much.“You want it all, Willa? The house? The babies? The ranch?”I nod again because it’s all I can do. I want all that. With him.“You want me, Willa?”“Yes.”“Good. Because I’m sick of holding back with you. You’re not going anywhere. You belong here, with me.”Luke Eatonis the most adorable, funny, and goofy kid. Always running around and doing chaotic things. I freaking adore this boy so much, and he deserves the best—he deserves Willa and all the happiness in this world. I’m glad he has Willa, who loves him more than anything.“Did you know I fell in love with you before I fell in love with your dad?”His eyes light at that. “You did?”I nod, pulling him close with an arm around his shoulders. “I did.”THE BEST THING IN THIS BOOK WAS THE FAMILY DYNAMICS.It was beautiful to see Willa slowly but surely become a part of Cade and Luke’s small world. Willa and Luke were perfect, and I wanna protect them and never let them go. The way she treated Luke was so, so endearing. And I support Willa throwing bully kids in the pool. She understood that kid and his father and gave them the love, comfort, and family they deserved. They are all so precious.“I’d do it for everything she’s given me in such a short time . . .A love Luke has never known.A reason for me to smile again.A person to talk to after so many years of silence.A love I’ve never known.”HeartlessandRecklessare my absolute favourites in this series because Cade Eaton and Theo Silva supremacy. I love this series with all my heart. The ending was perfect, with a very cute little surprise. There was a little drama at the end, but it wasn’t stretched for chapters and chapters—everything was perfect. I’m so happy for Cade, Willa, and their sweet, wholesome, adorable family.

5 starsI’ve read most of Elsie Silver’s books and I’ve enjoyed them all but THIS is the book! This one is by far my favorite book of hers and I’m positive it will be on my favorites list this year!Heartlesshad so much I love in a book. Single dad… check. Grumpy hero… check. Forced proximity… check. Age gap… check! I swear it’s like she wrote this one just for me!Cade Eaton is in need of a nanny for his son, Luke, while he works on/runs the family farm all summer. He can’t find anyone so Summer has her best friend, Willa come to town and work the job for the summer. Cade is not excited about this. Willa is young, thirteen years younger than his 38 and does she even have experience with kids? Since Summer vouches for her, he gives her a chance but he is unsure.Willa doesn’t have much experience with kids, but being with Luke all day and playing is too easy and fun. Their relationship (Luke and Willa) is completely heartwarming. I smiled so much seeing those two together. Seeing Luke and Willa together makes Cade come around a little quicker too.Cade was burned by a woman and he’s just here to focus on his kid, work, and family. He doesn’t want a relationship but there is just something about Willa. A grumpy hero who is soft with his son and starts to thaw for his nanny… seriously you just can’t go wrong with this book. The sweetest scenes, the family stuff that I adored, and the steam was top notch. I love Elsie Silver’s writing style and she killed it with this book.This series is shaping up to be fantastic. I loved Rhett’s book, I loved Cade’s book, and soon enough (hopefully) we’ll get Jasper and Beau’s books and I couldn’t be more excited. I’m loving being in the ‘Chestnut Springs’ world and I never want to leave.Heartlesswas truly perfect for me- there wasn’t a thing I didn’t love. I’ll be recommending this one to everyone!

“And I realize in that moment maybe I am heartless after all, because the beautiful girl with the copper hair grinning back at me right now is the one who stole it”The amount of times this book made me look like a fool by blushing, laughing and swooning is wild lol this was my first single dad romance and it exceeded whatever expectations I thought I had. It was humorous, steamy, heartfelt andgave me all the good feels!I honestly had the best time reading this and it feels like my favorite already4.5 starsWilla & Cadewere exceptionally the best and the soft moments besides the sexual innuendos/ witty banter between them delivered a pleasant and fun time (it’s safe to say they’ve won me over).Willafelt like sucha reprieve from other fmcswith her personality in being strong willed + loving with a spine and herunhinged sense of humormade it impossible not to love her!! She was soft and fierce to her core and her pep talks when stressed were sending me (she’s very much my spirit animal).Bonus points for pushing that kid into the pool. I reveled in her ability to be so blunt in saying what’s on her mind, putting anyone in their place and just who she is as a person that I was able to connect to. My petition to be included in the group chat is quite embarrassing but we’d be such good friends hahaNowCade.. the best grump yet such a charmer and so soft with Willa. The fact that he was communicative and supportive of Willa knowing how she is and what she needed had my support and vice versa. This man had such a filthy mouth and knew how to use it. I mean, you know that famous quote“save a horse, ride a cowboy?”Yeah, It was meant for him 🤠 now the build up to their relationship felt entirely entrancing & the tension here fueled me that I felt like I was drowning in it. This book was mostly all humor, horny moments paired with some soft moments to which worked for me. Anyways, certain scenes left plenty to the imagination and all I’ll say isdaddy cade indeedlmao I think he’s myfavorite Eaton brotherwithout reading beau’s book and don’t ask me how I know—I just know itAs forLuke,I absolutely adored this kid and his relationship with Willa 🥹 their scenes warmed my heart and I loved how protective she was of him. Also, I quite liked Rhett and summer here and the text threads with everyone >>I know there’s bonus POVs for Harvey but I still think he needs his own novellaI saw this as more than a “cowboy romance” given certain factors in the storyline with the characters internal fears— in particular Cades complex background and their mutual understanding of the needs of one another. It all ultimately by the end made this feel a little deeper which i liked and it was overall such an enjoyable time and i loved it. ╰┈➤5 stars!heartless by elsie silver was such a great story! i thought that this would be underwhelming, but oh boy, was i wrong!౨ৎ willa.this woman was made to be a mom even without really the experience of handling kids. some people are just really meant to be parents even without them knowing. i love her loyalty, and the way it was showed throughout the book. the way she stood up for the people that means a lot to her, even if they’re present or not, is truly admirable. once you earn her trust, she’d truly always have your back. moreover, i feel like her struggle as a kid who wasn’t pressured by her parents is often overlooked, but i truly think that putting a little bit of pressure on kids really gives them the direction they need. it shows that anything too much or too little is bad.౨ৎ cade.this man is such a daddy, and i mean that both literally and you definitely know the other one i’m talking about. everything about this man is so hot, and i’m not even kidding! i truly can’t imagine how hard it was for him to be the dad of his brothers and sister when his mom died, but i know for sure that it made him think that it’s just his role, which isn’t the case because he deserves to put himself first before others, and i’m glad willa showed him that.overall, this book was sooo good! i swear i wasn’t expecting much because again, the first book ended up being underwhelming for me, but this book exceeded my expectations! i'd definitely recommend this book to anyone who loves to read about a single dad who lives in a small town + they're grumpy but it just really makes him hotter!・。・。・。・。・。・。・。・。・。・。・。・。・pre-read:a single dad and small-town romance? consider me freaking excited to devour this book

It follows two characters that seem to come from different worlds and it ends up being a love story full of heart.
