book.title: Part of Your World
book.author: Abby Jimenez
book.description: A refreshingly modern fairy tale and instant New York Times bestseller that Love Hypothesis author Ali Hazelwood hails as "an uplifting, feel-good, romantic read."After a wild bet, gourmet grilled-cheese sandwich, and cuddle with a baby goat, Alexis Montgomery has had her world turned upside down. The cause: Daniel Grant, a ridiculously hot carpenter who’s ten years younger than her and as casual as they come—the complete opposite of sophisticated city-girl Alexis. And yet their chemistry is undeniable.While her ultra-wealthy parents want her to carry on the family legacy of world-renowned surgeons, Alexis doesn’t need glory or fame. She’s fine with being a “mere” ER doctor. And every minute she spends with Daniel and the tight-knit town where he lives, she’s discovering just what’s really important. Yet letting their relationship become anything more than a short-term fling would mean turning her back on her family and giving up the opportunity to help thousands of people.Bringing Daniel into her world is impossible, and yet she can’t just give up the joy she’s found with him either. With so many differences between them, how can Alexis possibly choose between her world and his?"Abby Jimenez’s words are like fairy dust... they sprinkled humor and warmth all over my life. Pick up Part of Your World if you’re looking for an uplifting, feel-good, romantic read—and for a beautiful reminder that we should always try to live the life that makes us the happiest." --Ali Hazelwood, New York Times bestselling author of The Love HypothesisBook of the Month Club Best Book of the Year finalistGoodreads Choice Awards finalistBookPage Magazine Best Books of the YearBooklist Best Romances of the YearSheReads Romance Book of the Year Award nomination

reviews.text: I’m just slapping my head so hard! Why did I wait so long to jump into this MARVELOUS JOURNEY? I’m telling you, my friends: this is the BEST ABBY JIMENEZ BOOK! Absolutely her best work!I cried and laughed so much, and sometimes I did both at the same time! The book brings out so many different emotions you’ve buried inside and helps you set them free!A workaholic, responsible ER doctor’s path accidentally crosses with a small-town mayor when her car gets stuck in a ditch. Blame it on a mischievous raccoon!Alexis comes from an ultra-wealthy family of world-renowned surgeons but differentiates herself by becoming an ER doctor. At age 37, she has suffered enough from the emotional, verbal, and physical abuse of her ex and her own family.After a car accident, she finds herself in a small town called Wakan. (Wakanda forever! Okay, I wrote that to see if you’re still reading!) Daniel, who saves Alexis from the ditch, is a caring sweetheart and definitely a dreamy book boyfriend! Yes, he’s younger than her, the mayor of the town, an efficient carpenter, and runs his grandparents’ B&B. The guy checks all the boxes! I don’t know how Abby Jimenez creates the best hero characters in her books. Each one of them is exceptional. And luckily, this time Alexis is also easy to root for with her resilience, honesty, and genuineness.As Alexis spends time in that small town, taking a break from her responsibilities, extra busy schedule, and burdens from the past, she realizes she can find happiness in a place and with a man she never imagined before.I have to admit, some parts of the book truly hurt me. Reading about the traumatic experiences Alexis has been through was really painful. I felt so sad, angry, and heartbroken. The angsty parts were truly realistic and hard to digest!But like the sun coming out of the clouds, the sweet and promising love story in the book warmed my heart!I love this book so much! I am absolutely giving it five small-town, amazingly adorable hero, second-chance, emotional stars!Special thanks to NetGalley and Forever (Grand Central Publishing) for sharing this amazing digital reviewer copy with me in exchange for my honest opinions.medium.cominstagramfacebooktwitter

4.28!"Grace costs you nothing"This book certainly feels like a part of my world 😌✋🏻 it was so well written and so well paced because the way I flew through this one 😭 it was so fast paced and so enjoyable that I just stopped everything and binged it as much as I could and let me tell you how fun it felt to be so immersed in a book specifically one that i had hopes for. Jimenez really did make this book worth my time. I would say, having read the second book, this is better in terms of writing and pacing and that is probably what made it connect with me so much 😗Alexis gives off the perfect 'Alexis from schitts creek' vibes at times because she just had that aura and it made her pov so much more enjoyable. Abby did such a mega job describing Lexi's complex life with her family and it made me absolutely furious with how she was treated. Her mother was the definition of a doormat with the way she kept her husband walk all over her and I absolutely hated how Alexis couldn't rely on her father and it made feel so bad for her because most girls have this strong bond with their father and that support really means a lot to them because I do see my sisters and I see how much secure they feel knowing that if things don't work out my father will support them and to see Alexis quittingn an emotionally abusive relationship with a man who cheated on her btw only to be constantly judged and belittle her into thinking that her ex could never be wrong and she was the one in the wrong hurt 😭 because how can you do that to your own daughter, he was so controlling, he basically made all the important decisions of her life and the worst part was the he wasn't even there for the occasions. You know the little moments that you wish to celebrate with your family- yeah Alexis never got that because her dad was an arrogant bitch and her mother a weak doormat.Daniel was perfect and the Greenest flag ever and he was definitely everything Alexis needed. He was the kind of guy who would go out of his way to help others. He was also very passionate about the thing he loved done *cough* Alexis *cough* 😭 no but besides that he actually was. Daniel and his baby goat were the highlight tbh 🤭 I absolutely loved his patience and good he waited for Alexis and created an environment where she felt comfortable!The romance was one of the nicer parts of this book. It was healthy and cute. I liked how it was closed door as well. I think it helped with the angst...I loved slow burn but this one was a one night stand turned into strangers with benefits so I wasn't expecting that I just liked that we didn't get insta love 😌✋🏻 both of them were so well fit and I totally got where Alexis was coming from because I saw how her parents reacted to her brother's gf I knew they would react the same way with Daniel 😔 and I also saw why Daniel felt betrayed but I loved loved loved how they got back together 🤭Overall, it was an amazing read 🩷___Reading this for just for the summer 💫-Abby Jimminez please blow my mind 🙏🏻•Buddy reading withRoxy

5 stars. Heck, 6 stars. I have found a new all-time favourite!“I wondered distantly if we’d known each other in a former life and we’d found each other again. If that was why he felt so familiar…”I’m not really going to tell you what this book is about. The blurb will do that for you. But I’m going totryto tell you how it made me feel, try, since I think that’s the most interesting part when it comes to book reviews. To review the impact a fictional story left you with. Real-human-feelings type of impact for a fictional world.I think this book just became the blueprint for what all romances should read like, should feel like. It read like an absolute dream for me and I don’t know how I am supposed to move on from this story, knowing that this realm exists that I can never be a part of? I don’t want to move on. 😩“But grace costs you nothing.”From the town, to the main characters, to the side characters, I loved every part of it. I was rooting so hard for Alexis and Daniel, it’s not even funny. They’re not even real, but they might as well have been.That speech that Alexis gave towards the end? I can’t tell you how proud I felt. I don’t even know whether she recognised her worth by the end, but I sure as fuck did. The woman is incredible! 🥹And Daniel? Don’t get me fuckin’ started. 😭 Abby Jimenez had no right to create this type of hero. None at all. Cinnamon roll to the core. He was someone that was just sogoodand honourable. Real life men, real life people could never. They’d fail in comparison to him.These two characters who are seemingly worlds apart, fit and made sense like you wouldn’t believe and I loved following them on their individual journey as well as the one they had together.I’m just blown away, tbh. I’m finding words difficult, simply because it wouldn’t do this book justice.So, to end it, my favourite aspect to this book (alongside everything else I’ve already mentioned) boils down to this: I feel like it emphasised that found families can sometimes trump the ones you are born into.*Trigger warnings for emotional/narcissistic abuse from an ex and her parents.There’s also a side character who’s experiencing domestic abuse*

Now THIS is the rom-com world I’ve been waiting for! Hallelujah!After a series of cynical (and not very fun) reads, what a pleasure it was to spend time with the wholehearted characters Abby Jimenez brought to life in Part of Your World. Just as I was starting to wonder if I’d outgrown the romantic comedy genre, her fourth book (but first for me) helped me realize there’s still some rom and a whole lot of com out there to be enjoyed.Part of Your World takes the opposites attract trope and gives it a good shake-up by (gasp!) making the woman almost 10 years older than the man. That’s right folks, she’s 37 and he’s 28. She’s an ER doctor at a prestigious hospital in Minneapolis, and he’s a carpenter/mayor/B&B manager at a small town called Schitt’s Creek, whoops, I mean Wakan (the Dakota word for “magic”). He wears denim, she wears Dolce & Gabbana. She comes from a family that puts the “greed” in well-pedigreed, and he comes from a self-centered teen mom. Opposites, to be sure.One thing they have in common though is obligation, and it’s those obligations that become the obstacle to their happily ever after. As they wrestle with them (and each other, ifyaknowwhatimsayin), readers are treated to many humorous lines and scenes, a baby goat in pajamas, and a pig named Kevin Bacon.Jimenez has sprinkled a little pixie dust over the story by integrating subtle nods to various fairy tales and Disney movies. Be on the lookout for Cinderella scenes and Little Mermaid moments. Also be prepared for the underlying serious theme of domestic abuse - both emotional and physical. A few of the beauties in this book have dealt with some beasts.Part of Your World might have been slightly more successful for me if it had been a tad less spicy in the s-e-x department, but that’s a personal preference and at least none of the C-words I hate so much were used. There are a few f-bombs, but that’s a-OK in my book. Or should I say world? ;)4.5 starsMy heartfelt thanks to the author and Estelle at Forever for the gifted copy to review. Now available!!Blog:https://www.confettibookshelf.com/

4.5 Stars ☆"Grace costs you nothing."This did start off as insta-love, but don't let that deter you because I'm not a fan of it and ended up enjoying this. The story progresses into more, even touching on some heavy real-life topics. This is my first Abby Jimenez book, and she portrayed emotional and mental abuse well, showing the realness of dealing with it.Part of Your World is the first book in the POYW series. It is a small town, age gap(she's older 37 and he's 28), opposites attract romance with tension, witty banter, laugh out loud moments and scenes of abuse that had me feeling angry and shedding tears.Alexis was on her way home late one night from an out of town funeral when she swereved to miss a raccoon and put her car in a ditch. While she was waiting for the towns tow truck, Daniel stopped to help pull her out. She goes to the only open place in Wakan the towns bar and runs into him there. Deciding to help him win a bet with his friends as a way to thank him.Daniel Grantis thoughtful, generous, and selfless, and I swear he is the sweetest. This man has the biggest heart and will do anything to help those who need it. He had me swooning for him so hard with all the gestures that he did for Alexis. He has his struggles, though, and it mad me so mad to watch him go through them. We see him suffer with financial struggles, but he tries not to show that he is struggling to save one of the things that means the most to him. Guys, he takes care of a baby goat that wears pajamas for a friend, sigh.“I like to read. It’s the only way I get to live somewhere that isn’t Wakan. I read three, four books a week. A lot of audiobooks. That way I can work and read at the same time.”Alexis Montgomeryis smart, sophisticated, and grew up being privileged. She went through both mental and emotional abuse with her ex and didn't realize that she deserved better until she met Daniel. My favorite part of Alexis was watching her grow and realize that her own family had also been abusive while growing up. Always doing the things that were expected of her by her parents and doing what truly made her happy but to please them. She started to see it all so clear once she was treated the way that she deserves by Daniel. My heart broke for her so many times, but in the end, she broke away from it and did the things that fit her life for her and not for anyone else."Nobody can save you until you’re ready to save yourself.”Daniel and Alexisare the complete oppostie of each other. She is an er doctor at a prestigious hospital, and he is the mayor of the town who runs a B&B and is a carpenter. When they are together, though, the sparks fly. They fit so well together and really do compliment each other in ways that no one has before. Their love for each other and the trials that they went through together felt so real.“Have you ever seen anything so perfectly beautiful?”“Yes…”“You.”I fell in love with the found family in this. It's the way the people of Wakan all look out for one another and are a big family. Some of my favorites were Doug and Brian as they brought humor with them that had me laughing out loud so much. I adored Liz and felt so bad for everything that she was enduring. Bri was the only friend that Alexis could count on, and that was true to her.This took me through so many emotions that I wasn't expecting. The ending was a bit predictable, but I still loved it and was happy with the outcome. The only question I'm asking myself is, "What took me so long to read this?" I'm excited to read the next book and see Bri get her happy ending.Favorite quotes and moments"Sometimes what you have to give is enough. Even if it’s a rock instead of a diamond.”"Love follows you. It goes where you go. It doesn’t know about social divides or distance or common sense. It doesn’t even stop when the person you love dies. It does what it wants.""There’s something more final than forever. It’s never. Never is infinite.""Sometimes family isn’t what you’re born into. Sometimes family is found."~~~~~~Pre-review:I've seen so many good things about this! Plus, I'm hoping for some cute and cozy vibes 🥰
It’s been a minute since a book has made me feel this giddy and emotional while reading. I want to go back and annotate literally everything. I’m. In. Love.

the crazy high average rating is definitely what drew me to this book. my experiences with AJs novels have been so-so, but a book that currently has a 4.62 rating has to be better than just mediocre, right?and luckily this is!the relationship between alexis and daniel is super sweet. yes, it starts off with a massive case of insta-lust, but i think the story progresses in a really nice way that shows how genuine feelings develop between the two of them. it feels real. dont get me wrong, the story is super predictable, but its honest, so i liked that.my only complaint would be the balance between the fun, cute, and lightheartedness of a rom-com and the serious, heavy topics of real life. i think romance books incorporating more of the serious topics of relationships is a good thing, but sometimes i pick up a romance to escape real life, and i thought this story had a bit too much seriousness and prevented that escapism at times.but honestly, it wasnt a deal breaker. i had fun and thought this was an improvement from my previous experiences with AJs books!↠3.5 stars

“Grace costs you nothing.”Hey, so this was definitely giving a bit of “insta love,” but it’s okay because there was so much more to this book than just the romance.Alexis Montgomery comes from a long line of world-renowned surgeons. She and her twin brother are expected to carry on this legacy, except maybe they don’t want to do it exactly in the way their family members did. As she was headed somewhere, because I really don’t remember where she was going, her car ended up having a problem. Luckily for her, Daniel shows up and helps her. Daniel comes from a local town, where he rents out a family house to visitors. They probably didn’t expect to see each other again, but as fate would have it, they see each other again, and “things” happen. How does their relationship develop from there?I thought this one was really amazing. I wholeheartedly could not put it down and finished it on the same day I started. Let’s start with the romance. Alexis and Daniel were really really really cute. Like I said from the beginning, it was definitely giving insta love but they still developed their relationship over the course of the book. Alexis and Daniel live different lives in regards to money, so it was really nice to see how they learned from each other through tasks and conversations.“Don’t let them decide the life you’re going to live.”A huge part of this book was abuse, both physical and emotional. Jimenez does a great job of highlighting how emotional abuse is just as powerful as physical abuse. I want to especially give a high five for revealing that these kinds of abuse can be among family members. My heart hurt for Alexis so much throughout the book. Except for her twin brother, best friend, and Daniel, I literally hated everyone she was surrounded with. How could someone survive with that much toxic energy? It was very realistic to portray this struggle that she had and how she slowly overcame them one at a time. How does she do this?Of course, Daniel also has his own individual struggles, not abuse, but more on the financial side. I was scared that we weren’t going to see any struggles on Daniel’s side, but we did, even though it felt at times that Alexis' problems were shown more. Daniel is pretty much another illustration of fictional men being amazing. I could smell husband material from the first lines about him. He’s 10 years younger than Alexis, and she made this a pretty big deal. I thought we are in a time when age isn’t a huge deal, but apparently, not everyone thinks this way.Overall, there were some funny lines here and there, and the pacing was great. It wasn’t really smutty though, which I was kinda surprised by. I’m also surprised that I’m surprised since it’s not like the rest of her books were that smutty. I believe Jimenez has written four books so far, all of which I’ve read. Her characters have so much chemistry. It is something she has definitely mastered. I personally think this is her best one yet!!

LOL 😭♡.ᐟalexis and danieli didn’t feel any connection between alexis and daniel because their relationship often felt one-sided. it was clear how hard he was trying to make it work, but alexis, on the other hand, was so sure it wouldn’t that she didn’t even bother trying 😪 like babes, at least SHOW SOME EFFORT 😭♡.ᐟother charactersugh, everyone else, except for bri and the people in wakan, was insufferable. i wont even bother with the details 🤦🏻‍♀️ˏˋ°•*⁀➷overallit’s such a bummer that my first abby jimenez book didn’t work out for me ☹️ alexis frustrated me so much that i just couldn’t enjoy the book 🥲💔all that aside, shoutout to Youssra for buddy reading this with me! i’m glad at least one of us enjoyed 🥹💖pre-read 🌃my first abby jimenez book! i have no idea what to expect, so i’m going in with zero expectations ~tysm to everyone who commented/voted on my poll 💖 i’ve decided to read this series in order ^_^impromptu br withYoussraYAYAYAY

➳ eugh ☆“love follows you. it goes where you go. it doesn’t know about social divides or distance or common sense. it doesn’t even stop when the person you love dies. it does what it wants.”buckle up my besties. i am about to be a hater piece of shit. if you love this book, do not take this too seriously. this is no where near the worst book i have read, but it was not memorable or impressive either. i am going to structure this differently than my usual reviews. i am going to list aspects i liked about the book, and then aspects that completely flopped for me.⌞characteristics i liked⌝✎ the narrators! julia is my favorite narrator of all time & zach is my second favorite romance narrator.✎ the setting was pleasant & the vibes were lovely.✎ the cover is pretty!✎ i liked certain side characters' development.✎ daniel is a GREEN flag.⌞characteristics that made me irate⌝× *alexis* is a whole buffoon.× embarrassing representation of women in the medical field.× atrocious writing.× hook up in chapter four???× dialogue tags???× poor pacing of the story.× useless third act breakup.×tellingvsshowing.× why is his name daniel???× stereotypical plotting & story telling devices.× unwanted obsequious adults.× repetition with lack of payoff until the end.× jarring conclusion.× pathetic deportment from developed individuals.× how are you gonna pull this tomfoolery and not give me smut???overall, i have read four books from this author, and they are ALL so similar to each other. i am tired of her writing overtlyperfectmen, with very unlikeable and puerile women. everybody is too grown to be acting like these circus clowns. pack it up.

— 4.5 stars ★“Sometimes family isn’t what you’re born into. Sometimes family is found.”I was truly captivated by thisPart of Your Worldfrom very first chapter. Abby Jimenez, what did you put in this book? This is my first book by AJ and I am obsessed with her writing and the characters. I am not a fan of insta-love but Abby has a true talent of writing insta-love stories because it was just so perfectly written. This book is a mixture of hilarious, tender and heart-warming moments. There were moments where I was sobbing but then the next moment I was giggling and smiling because Alexis and Daniel were so precious. This is also the best reverse age-gap book ever written.This book is like the biggest warm hug— like those comfort cardigans you love to wear on your good and bad days. AJ beautifully blended the heavy issues and emotional moments with refreshing and swoon-worthy romance. Abby tackled heavy subjects like emotional and mental abuse, domestic abuse and manipulation in a realistic way and showed us how people deal with their situations differently.“Love follows you. It goes where you go. It doesn’t know about social divides or distance or common sense. It doesn’t even stop when the person you love dies. It does what it wants.”The small town moments and the found family trope was so precious in this book. I loved the way they all were there for each other and how they all were just a big found family. Daniel's friendship with his Doug and Brian and how Alexis was welcomed and loved by his people. Everything was so sweet and tender. There were a lot of nuanced things in this book like the concept of Wakan being a magical town and how it will keep what it wants was kinda cute, it added the modern fairy-tale vibes to Daniel and Alexis's story.➸ Daniel GrantThe man of my dreams. He's just so perfect that no words will ever be enough to describe him and how much I love him. He's so down to earth, appreciates the privileges in his life, has the biggest heart ever, always helping his people, and there is nothing complex about him at all. You read about him and you'll see exactly what he shows you and says to you. He's good, kind, thoughtful, charming, selfless, knows how to love and take care of a woman without even asking for anything in return. I mean he spent two hours down by a river to find a perfect heart shaped rock for his girl, he adopted a war trained dog, he takes care of a baby goat in pyjamas. Aside from this, he also has his own struggles — financially and he tried his best to save his legacy but he also tried not to show his struggles to anyone because he's the one Wakan people depends on. He never pressured Alexis for anything just only ever wanted her time and I did not liked how he was treated by her in this book. Also, did I tell you HE READS AND COOKS!“Even though I didn’t fit into her life, she fit into mine.”➸ Alexis MontgomeryThe woman who belonged to a complete different world than Daniel's and who's rejected the idea of dating until she met the one. She's intelligent, kind-hearted, has lived a very luxurious and extravagant life. My heart broke for her so many times and all the emotional and mental abuse she went through for years by the people who loved her only to criticise her for her own choices. I liked her character development and how she came out of her toxic relationships with her father and her ex and how it made her realise that she was never the problem. Daniel helped her see love and life from a different and clear perspective which helped her understand her true potential and how there's so much to life than just carrying on a 125 years old legacy. Alexis is great and I love her but what I didn't liked about her was how she treated Daniel. There were so many things she could've done differently and that would've made things easy for their already complicated relationship.“It’s amazing how someone can touch you, even if you only know them for a moment in time. How they can change you, alter you indelibly.”╰┈➤ Daniel and AlexisDo I mind that they fell for each other way too soon? Absolutely not because when you know you know. They did started of as friends with benefits but I loved the way they slowly started to get to know each other. They are perfect for each other but their different worlds and lifestyles are not perfect for their reunion. Daniel is the best in their relationship who was ready to give up his town, his legacy, his family just to make a future with her in her city because she means more to him than his 125 years legacy in Wakan. He's truly a gem. Daniel gave Alexis everything she never properly had in her life. She needed love, care and someone who can make her his first priority, someone who won't emotionally abuse her, someone who can accept her as she is and Daniel is that person to her.“How do you do that?” she breathed.“Do what?” I asked, my voice husky.“Know how to touch me.”“I pay attention to you,” I said, kissing her collarbone gently. “How you feel matters to me.”I loved the way he made her feel normal, gave her the normal life she never had, told her all the little and handy things she thought she could never learn, how he always gave her the time and space and his love. He accepted her even without knowing about her extravagant lifestyle and her 125 years legacy. I understand Alexis choice and why she felt she could only protect Daniel by not including him in her luxurious and sophisticated life and she did her best but she also betrayed him so many times by not trying to include him in her life because he only ever wanted her. Daniel deserves so much better and someone who is proud of who he is and I think Alexis could've done better by his side.“Have you ever seen anything so perfectly beautiful?” I breathed. I turned to Daniel. But he wasn’t watching the petals anymore. He was looking at me.“Yes…” he said quietly, holding my eyes. “You.”The endingwas dreamy and no I am not kidding. The third-act breakup was not needed but it was reasonable and it broke my heart because Daniel did not deserved to be treated like someone who's not good enough for the world Montgomery's lives in. He was ready to give up his everything for Alexis and still it would never be enough for her father. Alexis's father is the worst and he should be in jail for the way he emotionally abused his kids and his wife for years. What I liked was how Alexis accepted Daniel in her life and how she didn't give a shit about anyone's reaction and just waited for Daniel to come and walk in her life with his head held high. The epilogue was the cutest ever!I highly and wholeheartedly recommend this book to every hopelessly romantic out there 💌“Kiss me, Daniel Grant.”He looked scandalized. “Right here? In front of everyone?”“Right here. In front of everyone.”

⊹ִֶָ 𓂃⋆∞ / 5⋆𓂃ִֶָ ⊹💐“Love follows you. It goes where you go. It doesn’t know about social divides or distance or common sense. It doesn’t even stop when the person you love dies. It does what it wants. Even if what you want is not to be in love.”💐THIS. BOOK.is so beautiful, funny, and amazing🛐🛐 it had me smiling and giggling THE WHOLE TIME! Also, I was screaming, crying, melting, and kicking my feet. My heart was shattered in some scenes but healed in others. I connected with this book since chapter one, and I loved every single part of it💖 it was my first book by Abby Jimenez, and I’m so happy that I decided to finally read it. Thanks to that, I just foundmy new favorite romance book💗 after reading it, my standards are so high that I just know that it will be extremely difficult for other books to reach them.🌹 𝓢𝓹𝓸𝓲𝓵𝓮𝓻𝓼 𝓐𝓱𝓮𝓪𝓭 🌹❥𝒜𝓁𝑒𝓍𝒾𝓈❤️ ˖⁺. ༶ ⋆˙⊹🦋“I had to drown first.And I was finally,finallyready to save myself.”🦋This woman has my whole heart💗 she lived in privilege her whole life and had the pressure of continuing her family legacy even though it wasn’t what she wanted to do with her life. She wanted an out, but she felt that shecouldn’t disappointher family more than they already were. She needed to feel accepted by her family, friends, coworkers, and everyone in general. Maybe she didn't know how to do basic stuff like cooking, cleaning, etc., but we must keep in mind that she was literally conditioned by her parents since she was a child to fulfill the expectations of her family and to have everything handed on a silver platter.But she was raised like that.That was her world- she didn’t know anything better. She was taught that all she had to do was to study, get a job with a high position in the medical field, surround herself with influential people, and marry someone "worthy" whom her parents accepted. She didn��t have the mindset to see something else outside this world until she ended her abusive relationship, went to therapy, started to set boundaries for her parents and order her priorities, and when she met Daniel. Then, she started her journey of self-discovery,self-awareness, and personal growth. I was so proud of her and her character development.❤️‍🩹“Daniel empowered me, instead of stripping me. Lifted me up instead of keeping me low.”❤️‍🩹❥𝒟𝒶𝓃𝒾𝑒𝓁💚 ˖⁺. ༶ ⋆˙⊹💗“She felt like the sun. Like she was the reason for everything. Like I’d always been waiting for her to get closer and bring me to life.”💗Where should I start? THISS MAN!🛐 he’s so thoughtful, generous, sweet, caring, respectful, gentle, adorable. He has: the most charming and selfless personality, tattoos, dimples, and the cutest dog and a baby goat (it’s his friend’s, but he helps him a lot taking care of it).🤍“Nothing could convince me this woman wasn't made for me to love. I think my soul recognized hers the second I laid eyes on her. Our bodies knew it the very first night.The power she had over me terrified me. But it also gave me clarity.There is a peace in knowing the one thing you can't live without. It simplifies all things. There was her, and then there was everything and everyone else. And only she really mattered.”🤍Since I first read about this man, he captivated me. There’s justsomethingabout him that always had me smiling, melting, and kicking my feet. Was it his charisma or how he took care of Alexis? Was it how humble he was or how openly he was to love Alexis despite their differences and the odds of not being able to fit in each other’s life? He is the sweetest person ever. He made Alexis feel safe, beautiful, powerful, heard, seen, helpful, worthy, and happy. Daniel helped her heal from her past relationship which was mentally abusive. He helped her raise her self-esteem, and always encouraged and supported her. Since the beginning, all he wanted was to worship her and make her feel comfortable. Daniel made her his priority and was willing to give up everything just to be with her. Andd he didn’t doubt that not even once.🛐😭“I'd give up my life here to be where she was if she'd have me. I'd give up my house and this town and all the people in it. If she was at the hospital eighty hours a week, I could be there when she left and be there when she came home. Make her breakfast, take her lunch, take her dinner. I could pick up the slack. She wouldn't have to do anything, it could be all me this time -I'd go to her. It didn't have to end.”😭🛐❥𝐵𝓇𝒾🤍 ˖⁺. ༶ ⋆˙⊹☀️“You don’t have to feel like this. You literally don’t. Quit. Leave. Pick him. Pick yourself.”☀️This gorgeous woman💕Alexis' best friend and the only one she truly deserved. Not like Gabby or Jessica, the superficial and hypocritical “friends”. Bri is the type of friend who is so genuine and the one who will tell you all the hard truths, even if you don’t like it. She was always there for Alexis when she needed her the most, and she helped keep her grounded. She was so helpful for Alexis to recognize her true feelings and take those brave steps to organize her priorities and follow her dreams, not her parents. Also, she helped Daniel to calm down, avoid misunderstandings and reunite them. I love her <3❥ 𝓣𝓱𝓮 𝓼𝓶𝓪𝓵𝓵 𝓽𝓸𝔀𝓷🌻 ˖⁺. ༶ ⋆˙⊹💝“Sometimes family isn’t what you’re born into. Sometimes family is found.And I’d found mine in Wakan.I’d found mind inhim.”💝I’ve said it before, small towns romance books have a soft spot in my heart❤️‍🩹 there’s something so special, sweet, and cozy about them. Wakan didn’t disappoint at all. The people there weren’t just simple neighbors. They were a family. A strong bond and found family that won my heart. All of them were so special. They had me laughing so hard and melting, and they were the sweetest and the most supportive people ever.❥My thoughts💭 ˖⁺. ༶ ⋆˙⊹➴every single chapterwas magical🦋✨ this book hooked me from the very beginningwhich normally is very hard for me to achieve.Part of Your Worldis my new favorite romance book💜 I felt a rollercoaster of emotions and enjoyed every single word of this book.➴ I hated so much Alexis' parents. I could say so many things about them, but I would never end. Her father was a narcissist who only cared about prestige and was mentally abusive to her. Her mother never stood up for Alexis or her twin. They mistreated her and didn’t care for her well-being or happiness. She deserved so much better, but I’m so glad she found her own family in Wakan, Daniel, Bri, and her twin.➴ the third act breakup broke MY HEART. I felt like I was the one who was drowning instead of them. THAT is how empathetic I was toward this book.➴ when Daniel thought Alexis was embarrassed of him? SO HEARTBREAKING💔 he just wanted to be supportive, love her, and just be there for her. He was willing to give up EVERYTHING- his house, work, town, and follow her to another city just to be with her. Thissss man😭💗➴ this was an insta love, and you know what? I didn’t mind that one bit because I truly felt all the chemistry since the beginning. They were so opposites, and they lived in very different worlds. Even though, Alexis and Daniel had a beautiful, comfortable, and palpable connection since the moment they met and as they got to know each other and formed a relationship. They had insecurities, fears, wounds, and all the odds were against them. But they found a way to mix their worlds and be together despite the obstacles. They helped each other bring out the best of them and be the best versions of themselves. They became each other's priority and were willing to give up everything to be together. They just wanted to live their lives doing what they wanted and loving each other. And they achieved it. No matter how many differences they had, that didn't stop them from following their hearts and fighting against the odds to be together. I'm the happiest with their happy ending💖❥Other quotes just because I love them✨I didn't like that others had to take care of me. I wanted to know how to take care of myself. I wanted to pull my weight and learn to be self-reliant so that when I did depend on someone, it was by choice and not necessity. And it was Daniel who was teaching me how.“I see you. I would save you if you were drowning.”“I pay attention to you. How you feel matters to me.”“Grace costs you nothing.”He was in a black tux. One hand on the railing, and every woman in the room looking at him- but he was looking atme.“Have you ever seen anything so perfectly beautiful?” I breathed.I turned to Daniel. But he wasn’t watching the petals anymore. He was looking at me. “Yes…” he said quietly, holding my eyes. “You.”He pulled out something and put it in my palm. It was a rock…“It’s shaped like a heart,” he said. “Took me two hours to find it.”It wasn't flashy. It cost him nothing but his time- but that was the gift. Daniel didn't have time. That was his most valuable commodity right now, and he'd given it to find me this?She said she didn't see a future with us. That our lives didn't fit. I knew there were things I could never give her. At best, I had about as much to offer as my damn dog, companionship, and entertainment…But I could love her better than anyone ever could for the rest of her life.That, I knew. And if there was even a fraction of a chance that might be enough, I was going to take it.Anyways, I will forever recommend this book to absolutely everyone. I hope you all enjoy it💜🦋🥀“There’s something more final than forever. It’s never. Never is infinite.”🥀

I’ll not go into the plot, and will keep this review short and sweet. It’s no secret I’m a curmudgeon (lol) and my eyes tend to roll a lot.But every now and then I need a rom-com (as long as it’s heavy on the com).Did I have to set aside believability? Of course. Did I care? Of course not.This was a delightful rom-com with compelling characters and a conclusion that wasn’t entirely predictable, but yet, perfect. I loved these characters. And, there’s the adorable dog!After more than a few disappointing reads, this was a delight start to finish. Funny and sweet, I highly recommended for fans of the genre.Yet, there is substance, but the author dealt with it with sensitivity. It’s nice to be reminded of the best of humanity.The narrators, Julia Whelan, and Zacchary Webber were perfect for their roles.

It’s no secret that Abby Jimenez writes some of my favorite Romance books….Although I only read a few a year, you can BET that one of them will always be whatever Abby has published! I am always so excited when I see that she has a new book available to request.Sure, this genre has a formula that leads to happily ever after-but that doesn’t mean it’s easy to write.In romance you MUST like the characters, you MUST enjoy their dialogue, and you MUST feel the chemistry!! 🔥In addition, you absolutely MUST feel the angst! Theirs and yours-as you become worried that one of the leads will let the other get away, causing you to scream at that character to “go get your person”!NOW!!All the boxes are checked here!And, once again, Abby Jimenez adds an “extra layer” to her stories by weaving another important topic into a sub-plot-this time, “domestic violence-both verbal and physical abuse“, providing resources after the epilogue.This story introduces us Alexis Montgomery, who has been tapped to carry on her family legacy of world-renowned surgeons. (Think Meredith Grey of Grey’s Anatomy) She and her brother, Derek, have been groomed since childhood to be the next generation to lead their prestigious hospital into the next century and then Derek bails out.Not their first choice, but nonetheless, now their only choice, their parents want Alexis to marry the right man-Neil-the head of surgery-have the right friends-the horrible Jessica and Gabby, and fulfill her destiny-Despite the fact that she has she realized that she doesn’t want any of these things.Late one night, she is driving home from an out of town funeral, and she has to swerve to avoid hitting a raccoon-ending up in a ditch. Daniel Grant, a very tattooed, blue color, (and younger) man, offers to tow her out, and afterward she runs into him again at a nearby bar -which is the only place in town that is open this late with available food and rest rooms.She decides to help him win a bet against his friends as a way to “thank him” for his assistance.Despite the fact that their lives are as different as night and day, their sizzling chemistry is undeniable, and will make Alexis question if money does buy happiness and if if she has the courage to live life on her own terms.As she tries to reconcile how she feels, we hear from both Alexis and Daniel, in alternating chapters, and I enjoyed hearing from them both equally.Thankfully, Alexis has one true friend in her corner, Bri, and we should ALL have a friend like her-a friend who will tell you what you NEED to hear, and CHEER YOU ON, as you go for what will make you happy!If you have enjoyed this author’s past work-you will enjoy this book too!AVAILABLE NOW!!One last thing—I was excited to learn that in 2023, it will be Bri’s turn in the spotlight and it will be her chance to find happiness! ❤️ My calendar is already marked!!Thank You to Forever for my gifted copy! It was my pleasure to offer a candid review!

2.5 stars💕 Contemporary rom-com💕 Age gap (The heroine is 9 years older)💕 Friends with benefits to Lovers💕 Small town💕 Tear-jerkerAbby Jimenez is officially my favourite contemporary rom-com author. Her writing is so good. Unique, realistic, engaging, emotional and heart breaking. I teared up while reading this book. It was so freaking good!But I can't, I won't give the book more than 3.5 stars. Because here is the things...Our hero Daniel Grant is an ultimate Cinnamon Roll. He was an adorable teddy bear I wanted to protect at any cost. Daniel was soooo perfect.I really do need a man like him in my miserable life. I'm in love with Daniel Grant.But...The heroine Alexis was a stupid, selfishbitchperson. I can't believe she's thirty seven. Come on! She's so unreasonable and dumb. And also a huge pushover. She wanted to please everyone but Daniel. She was in full control of their relationship. She did whatever she wanted, whenever she wanted. She never did care for how Daniel might have felt. I understand her complication. Alexis was emotionally abused by every man in her life, no one cared for her feelings. But that didn't give her the rights to hurt Daniel who was literally an Angel. And man! Did she hurt him! She hurt his poor heart again and again and still Daniel was willing to fight for their love. I hated how awful she was to him. Absolute worst. I am so angry at her.Alexis doesn't deserve Daniel Grant, she never did.Then again, I would have overlooked all Alexis's faults and flaws if only the couple had good communication between them. They had zero to no communication. Alexis went through a lot. She should have shared her problems and feeling with Daniel. Instead she decided to hide things from him.Where was the chemistry? Nowhere to be found. They had good sex. They were good poeple (as individuals). That's all. No chemistry or bonding whatsoever..Meh...

April 5th: No blurb yet, but I literally don't even care what it's about, I just want it now.November 1st: A huge thank-you to Forever and Netgalley for the advance copy. This book...GAH. I laughed, I cried, I swooned. I don't know how Abby Jimenez does it, but I am awed by her talent. Full review to come closer to release date.

Part of Your Worldis out of this world!😍💥‼️It's easily my favorite rom-com so far this year. I wish I had more stars to give!In the middle of nowhere between Cedar Rapids and Minneapolis, Alexis avoids a raccoon on the road and ends up in a ditch. Luckily, a handsome Daniel drove by and helps tow her Mercedes out.This isn't instant love, but close. Their encounter on the road sets off an unlikely romance between two people who are polar opposites, but their chemistry is off the charts! I soo love them together.Daniel Grant is a carpenter, and mayor, and runs a B & B in the tiny town of Wakan, MN.Dr. Alexis Montgomery is an ER doctor from the big city.I love their flirty connection, witty banter, and laugh-out-loud humor. I love the animals, Daniel's circle of friends, and dialogues with some risqué. 🤭There's a scene that I listened to THREE times because it was immensely hilarious. On a slightly serious part, there's an underlying emotional abuse message in the story.Part of Your Worldhits all the right notes and gives me huge satisfaction~adding to my favorite shelf (the first rom-com!). The romance is hot ~ 3/5 🔥 scale.🎧💓Is it normal to have a voice crush? If so Zachary Webber is mine!

WHAT is Abby putting in this book??? I can’t even tell you why exactly, but I could not put it down. I loved the writing, I loved the storytelling, I loved the characters & the setting & everything about it! I don’t usually love a romance, but I just loved this book :) 10/10!

Where this book has been my whole life!!😭🤧🥹And this one should be the last book of Jan, i think?! 18th!------------------------------------Updated in 01/29/20244.5🌟This was somehow my first book ofAbby Jimenez! From this moment on, I’ll be buying everything she writes, I'm not even joking!I didn't want to write a review, but it would be injustice to this book, not that I'm a good reviewer by any means. lol you get the point!!!* ˚ ✦“ Have you ever seen anything so perfectly, beautiful?”“Yes…”“ You.”✦ ˚*︵‿︵‿︵‿︵︵‿︵‿︵‿︵︵‿︵‿︵‿︵-About the plotIt follows Alexis journey where she happens to find her self in a small town where she knows no one and doesn't even plan to stay for long!until she met this guy called Daniel who is almost 10 yrs younger than him that changes what she had planned for her life all along!Both MMC and FMC are expected to continue their family legacy with zero intention of leaving their town or letting anyone down in the process!: ̗̀➛‘There's something more final than forever.it's never. Never is infinite.’The story flows so effortlessly that before you know it, you reach half of the book.I can't normally find humour in these novels funny due to cultural differences I have with them, but there were times I was crying out of laughter!!: ̗̀➛‘All we had was our love for each other. That's all we had.None of the other parts worked or fit or made sense.But I didn't need it to make sense, because for me, the love was everything.it was all I needed.But it wasn't enough for her.’There are many situations where characters talk about domestic abuse, and you can see how wisely and thought through they were written, so please be aware of the warning!: ̗̀➛“Nobody likes assholes… .” Sometimes that's just what you think you deserve”: ̗̀➛“Nobody can save you until you're ready to save yourself.”︵‿︵‿︵‿︵︵‿︵‿︵‿︵︵‿︵‿︵‿︵-Audiobook10/10. Definitely recommend it.“ Thank you for reading this review “

At this point, I'll read anything Abby Jimenes writes.The men in her stories are perfect. They are the goodest boys. The kindest, most wonderful people. And I love them. Daniel has my heart! His only competition right now is Jason fromThe Happily Ever After Playlist, another of Abby Jimenez's books.I am in love. That is all.I need to go cry about the fact that men like Daniel only exist in fiction.

3.5 StarsHmm...Well, I enjoyed it.But...Hugely ripped-off style in the form of KA biker-speak and the chin lifts...Hugely simplistic in a lot of ways...Hugely forgettable in a lot of ways...?Perhaps.Regardless, a decent way to pass an evening and good enough for me to grab the second one. You know I can’t deny a 19-year age gap, people.

Admittedly I didn’t get very far into the book, but the bit I read seemed unreal and dated. Like King was channelling a 1970s movie character and I legit expected him to break out his comb to do a Danny Zuko.Parallel to that, the Alpha male thing is reminiscent of Kristen Ashley who, by the way, is one of my least fave authors.Anyway, it’s a DNF for me.

2.5 starsSeriously, this got me laughing so hard. I didn’t know what to expect from this, but for the first few chapters I was just laughing? I mean sorry, but I just couldn’t take them seriously AT ALL!!Eighteen years old boys don’t act like that. They just don’t. It’s just not believable. Throughout the book I had a hard time trying to figure out who was the teenager and who was the adult. As for the heroine she was kinda insufferable and kinda drove me crazy. The whole thing just seemed so unreal to me and I couldn’t take the characters or the whole plot or even the “forbidden” aspect of their relation seriously at all. I was laughing the whole time and I’m like for real?? Even every time he called her “babe” (which happened at least three times per page) i’d just start laughing. It just kinda made me cringe so bad.Their romance was so freakin insta-lovey. This is definitely a me thing, I just don’t like romances that are so insta. I need them to take their time before they start having feelings. Insta love romances just don’t convince me. Even though I have issues with this book and the pacing was kinda all over the place, I think I’ll still continue the series because I heard that the second and third books are better and they seem more interesting.

3.5 starsI was a good woman who had fallen in love with the ultimate bad boy. Now, I just had to figure out what to do about it.After reading and becoming obsessed with ‘Welcome to the Dark Side’, I wanted to start at the beginning and read King and Cressida’s story. Though I didn’t love this near as much as Zeus and Lou’s story, it was an enjoyable story.Cressida is a teacher and stuck in a loveless marriage. She married an older man when she was eighteen, and now eight years later, she’s done. She just needs that push to leave. When she sees King across a parking lot, it’s the motivation she needs to leave and start over.King’s dad, Zeus, is the prez of the Fallen biker club and King is an eighteen year old high school senior. King doesn’t look or act like a teenager. He’s mature for his age, sometimes even more mature than Cressida. King and Cressida start a relationship before she finds out he’s still in high school or as young as he is, but she’s in for a huge surprise when she finds out he’s her student.There was some push and pull in this one from Cressida which I understood, but after a while I was like come on girl! King was fantastic, though. I loved that he was alpha, devoted, and even poetic. I can’t wait to continue on with this one. I’m loving this series!

4 Unadulterated Lust Stars ⭐I wanted to be a part of it. To stand beside the blond king and be his rough and tumble queen.It was love at first sight, but it would take months until they would properly cross paths or give into their feelings. And it would be done. It would be too late, lines would be crossed, and she would never be just his teacher…Cressida Irons spent years in a marriage she’d been groomed for. By leaving her ex-husband, meant leaving her family as well. Starting over in a new town with a new job was meant to give her a fresh perspective. What she hadn’t expected was to instantly fall for a man destined to be a king. A man who happened to be her student.My breath left me all at once, but I didn’t care to get it back. I’d never breathe again if it meant seeing that man with that smile made especially for me.King Kyle Garro was born with a preordained path. As the son of the president of the Fallen MC, his role was written. But beyond that he was gifted with beauty and brains and a penchant for poetry. He knew instinctively that Cressida would be his, even if the object of his affection needed a little convincing.“You’re too much for me,” I whispered through my tightening throat.“I swear, you’ll be bored in a week. I’m no fun.”“I just gotta corrupt you a little and, just sayin’, I’m lookin’ forward to that part.”This was essentially a story of entrapment in the most delicious circumstances. And let me assure you that no breathing heterosexual woman could resist King. With that filthy mouth and a soul that yearned for his woman, Cressida never stood a chance.“You think just ’cause you’re the teacher, I gotta follow your rules?Well, you’re wrong, babe. Between you and me, I’m the fuckin’ authority figure.If I gotta fuck you in the library to show you what’s what, I’m only too happy to fucking oblige.”Lessons in Corruption is a refreshing take on the MC trope. Although gritty in parts when the focus was club business, it wasn't the sole focus. This was a true romance. It was poetically beautiful, raunchy and breathtaking to watch the interactions between King and Cressida. And of course, we meet the infamous Zeus Garro which only makes you salivate for the next book in the series…

NOTE: This coming year I have set a challenge for myself of reading one book a week that takes me out of my comfort zone. A book that may include one (or more) tropes (scenarios) that I avoid like the plague. Cheating. Older women/younger man. OW drama. Just to name a few. Nothing is off limits. I am taking a HUGE step out of the box I usually live in and charting a course into the unknown (HaHa). My reviews for these books will differ slightly from my usual as I will focus more on whether the author wowed me despite it having content I avoid. That said, I will go into each book with an open mind and an open heart and who knows, maybe I will discover a new favorite author on the way!~THIS BOOK INCLUDED THE FOLLOWING TROPES (SCENARIOS) I USUALLY AVOID~-- Older Woman/Younger Man~WRITING - PLOT - PACE~So apparently hell has officially frozen over because I LOVED this book. L-O-V-E-D it, and I usually HATE the whole "older woman/younger man trope." That said, King acted WAY older than he was supposed to be and the heroine acted younger than 26. Both of which I am sure helped me forget the age difference. On top of that, this was very similar to a KA book. The writing. The way they spoke. Certain things that take place. And since I adore early KA, I loved that aspect of this book as well. The writing was fantastic. The pace was perfect. The plot was super engaging. Last, it was all wrapped up in a very goodhappy for nowending.~HERO~KING...I adored KING. He was smokin' hot. He was sexy. He was smart. He wrote poetry. He was alpha. He knew what he wanted, and he went all out to get it. I felt a little pervy lusting after an 18-year-old, but like I said he acted older so I am just going to pretend he was (lol). King rates up there as one of my favorite book boyfriends ever.~HEROINE~CRESS... liked Cress a lot as well. She was a teensy bit annoying sometimes with how judgy she was and all the push/pulling she did. But I did like that she always apologized straight away and knew the way she was acting wasn't fair or right.~SECONDARY CHARACTERS~There were SO many awesome secondary characters in this book. I LOVED Daddy Zeus. I loved so many of the MC brothers. I loved Cress's students. I loved the biker babes. I loved Officer Danner. I sort of even liked Cress's brother Sander and I am hella curious to see what this author is going to do with his character especially after reading book two of this series.~HEAT LEVEL~This was pretty insta-lust, but it takes a bit until we get to the sexy times (50%). That said, this couple has an excellent on-page chemistry, and when we do get to the sex scenes, they are super hot and plentiful.~ANGST LEVEL - SEXUAL HISTORIES~Low to Medium. There is a bit of push and pull from the heroine. I am not usually a fan of that, but it didn't overly bother me here. I kind of got where Cress was coming from. The student/teacher thing wasn't as big a deal as I thought it would be. Of course, we have some drama from a rival MC that causes some angst. Neither were virgins. There was a bit of OW drama with (I guess) some MC chick that took King's virginity, and she and heroine get into it a bit. It is never really made clear if the stuff she was telling the heroine actually took place or not, so I am going to assume it did. There was also some OM drama in the form of Cress's husband (some to be ex).~IN THE END~I ended up loving this book WAY more than I ever thought possible (lol). In fact, I would go so far as to say that out of the very few older woman/younger man books I have read, this was by FAR my favorite. Of course, again, I am chalking it up to the characters not really acting the ages that the author assigned to them. But honestly, who cares? I loved it. I will probably reread it. I can't wait to read book three. Therefore yours truly is going to give this one two HUGE thumbs up

4.5 StarsI’m the kind of girl who likes her mafia romances light on the mafia. Im pretty sure I avoided MC romance because I wasn’t into the gritty douchery, so I was hesitant to dive into this. But I am happily surprised that it’s light on the asshole side of bikers. To me this is just the right amount of dark and gritty. I know I love me a dark ass read but sometimes I just want to be swept off my feet by an alpha male that’s not a total dick.I love that this is very loosely based on Paradise Lost.“It’s not funny like that. It’s ironic. Paradise Lost is supposed to be about the fall of man from Eden, the fall of Satan and his angels from heaven, about their follies as they compare to the grace and power of God. It’s God that’s supposed to be the hero, the perfect character, but it’s Eve and Satan that we empathize with the most.”I think it’s ingrained in all women to want a bad boy at least once if your lifetime. More likely, all the damn time.“You are such a rebel without a cause,” I teased. His eyes lit as they raked me up and down. “I got a cause, babe. Gettin’ you on the back of my bike and gettin’ you to stay there.”Why did I put off reading this? It’s oddly both so adorable I can’t stop smiling and so smoking hot I’m sure I’m on fire.“Don’t be afraid to scratch or bite me if you get scared,” he said over his shoulder. I didn’t have to look at his face to know he was smiling. “I won’t get scared,” I lied. “Don’t be afraid to scratch or bite me all the same,” he retorted.I’ve never hidden the fact that I’m a huge fan of the teacher/student trope but this time for the first time it was a cold glass of water in the face. The dynamic is all wrong and instead of it being hot it just feels like an enormous bump in the road.And then, King had to go and totally prove me wrong. It doesn’t matter that she’s the teacher, it doesn’t matter that she’s 8 years older. He’s the fucking boss.My personal apple preference is a pink lady. If you’ve never had one, I highly recommend it. And his apple efforts were fruitless. 🤣😋🍏🍎Omfg I thought I adored King, but Zeus? I think I’m in love.In all honesty sometimes Cressida annoyed the crap out of me. I’m all for a girl speaking her mind but damn. She can be horribly disrespectful, especially when he’s done something nice for her. Plus don’t even get me started on her lack of loyalty. She’s severely lacking. But, at least she turned into a badass.Overall the writing is so well done, including the poetry. The cast of characters both good and bad are fascinating and absorbing. You are so sucked it you can feel their blood run through your own veins. And even though Cressida isn’t my favorite h, she still had a spark worth knowing. And last but not least, King is pretty much everything.
