THATS THE SOUND OF THE POLICE 🚨🚨 everyone say it with me: PEDOPHELIA. if you read this book and liked it I’m seriously concerned about you because the fmc is 15, FIFTEEN and the mmc is 32 THIRTY TWO!!! excuse me but what the actual fuck- how the actual fuck did this get published

With all the controversy surrounding this book...there was no way I wasn't going to give this one a try. First of all, because I am of the opinion that I myself decide what I want to read and no one else gets a say... and secondly because I am very curious by nature :PI used to be a gymnast myself in my much younger years...and I actually trained a whole lot. Back then a slap on the ass by my coach was just the way things were. I have no idea how things are now, but I am pretty sure there is no more slapping teenage girls butts (and there shouldn't be).This book goes way beyond some ass-slapping though. I am sure most of you have read reviews and the warning that comes with this book, but just in case you didn't... There is sex with a minor, and as far as I am concerned he does abuse his position as her coach.I know that Adrianna tried her very best to seduce him, but in my book...he is still the adult here and blaming a 15-year-old is just madness, no matter how deviant she is acting. Not to mention the way he goes about it. I don't think any adult woman should take the shit he dissed out, let alone a 16-year-old girl.That said... I do think this story was very intriguing, though I have to say that Adrianna did get on my nerves quite a bit. It just gets harder and harder for me to relate to the girls in YA books and as much as the 18 years olds annoy me... 15/16-year-olds are even worse.I have no idea if I am gonna finish this series, but I probably will in time... and no that doesn't mean that I condone rape or statutory rape or sex with a minor or whatever you want to call it. It just means that I am a reader and I read fiction... any kind of fiction I feel like reading.

Condom doesn't exist in this book's world, just saying.Anyhow, what a hard book to talk about. She was 15 when they started getting involved, while he was 32, so yeah problematic. I'm still trying to figure out my feeling as I'm writing this down. Let's talk pros first.I did appreciate the detailed background on the whole gymnastics area. You can tell there's a lot of research was done on this part. Actually, that's the only pro. Huh.To cons:Cheating. I might've considered chopping his penis off a bazillion times.It was kinda boring.Despite the whole gymnastics thing, I did find it a bore reading about them. I know nothing about gymnastics, so my imagination and constant googling was a tad exhaustive for me.Worst hero ever.I hate... no, loathe the hero. Loathe him. I have so many choices of words for this child fucker but I'll refrain. Just know I don't want to talk about him again.Toxic relationship.I know it's a taboo relationship, underage one at that, but damn if this couple doesn't have one toxic relationship, where it all boils down to getting the forbidden fruit and, to be honest, extortion.In the end, the whole underage thing didn't matter that much to me (I knew what I was going into after all), when I could see so many problems with the relationship they have. All in all, problematic but a definitely unique story to tell.

I'm going straight to hell for reading that.She's 15/16, he's 32 and her gym coach.It was intense and it made my head spin. She's a child by law, yet she's living away from her parents in her own apartment. Does that make it ok? Hmmm. In this, you can make excuses for him, but if she were my daughter I'd be removing his bollocks with an axe. Fortunately for our Russian former Olympic gymnast coach, this is fiction.I've struggled to put this one down today, it's compelling and raw, hot as fuck and full of risky behaviour and some scenes that had me reading with my arse clenched.The author is a former gymnast and that really comes through in the detail, not just of the moves, but also in the understanding of how it feels to push your body to extremes, to have that passion and craving for success. That detail really enriched this story, made it about more than just lust.Unfortunately it ends on a cliffhanger and the next part isn't available yet. Normally I wouldn't even start a story if I knew that, but once I started reading this I couldn't put it down again so now I've got an anxious wait for part two.Recommended, but heed the author's warning at the start - this won't be for everyone.

4 starsss cause I have no morals when it comes to fictional, sexy ass russians. i also have to preface this by saying that balance isn’t going to be for everyone!! it pushed many of my boundaries, and I didn’t think i had any to be pushed lol. this book can be highly controversial regarding the subjects which are at hand. That being said, this is a work of fiction, and would I honestly feel the same way about this dynamic in real life? fuck no. But nevertheless, I enjoyed the fuck out of this book. LIVE:http://amzn.to/2c3ghzH5 “I’m high on Kova” Stars.I started Balance expecting just another hot book about a Gymnast coach and his student. What I got was a deliciously “romance” thrill-ride that didn't let me breathe until the very end.This book was a challenge for me. It went against two topics I believe in. But Lucia’s exceptional writing, plus her mind blowing knowledge about gymnastics, made me forget about it most of the time.To say I didn't hesitate to keep reading the moment I realized how forbidden the story was, would be a big fat lie. But again, Lucia’s captivating words made me got completely caught up in all the red burning passion that flows through this book.Adrianna Rossi is a fifteen, turning sixteen girl. A heroine who’s as fierce as she is vulnerable. One that knows what she needs to reach the Olympic glory, her ultimate goal.“Gymnastics was everything to me—it was my heart and soul, the air I breathed. It was the one thing that allowed me to be me.”Dreams don't work unless you take action. So in order to make hers come true, Adrianna moved 3 hours away from home to train at World Cup. Determined to reach her end she trained day in and day out for it. Nothing would bring her down. Not even Konstantin Kournikova, her deliciously forbidden new coach.There were a few moments where I dislike Adrianna’s behavior because I believed that she was being selfish. She knew that what she was doing was wrong. And even though the author gave her a level of maturity that made me forget about her age, at the end of the day she was just a teenager.“…he looked like the perfect package, if there ever was one.”Now, let’s talk about KOVA. The big bad wolf. But the questions is: Does he really is the bad guy? Don't forget that lust can overpower even the strongest man.KOVA!!!*Breath in-breath out*Just the name brings out so many different feelings in me. The man was beyond confusing but endlessly fascinating, even intoxicating. All of his back and forth was giving me whiplash. But I wouldn't change a thing about him.“Ria, you may be my undoing.”And you may be mine, KOVA!I wanted to fuck him all the while I bit his fucking head off.I can’t say that I didn't hate him at some point, because I did. He was a real bastard but in a twisted way I understood his motivation. So the hate just longed like 10 min. I’m not proud of my feelings for him. But just like Ria, I got caught up to an unhealthy level in his net. One I don't want to escape.I wish the author gave us Kova’s POV in the next book. I think that reading his thoughts would be very interesting and not just because I wanted to know all of his lustful thoughts but because I want to know how he feels about the situation he had put himself in.“His presence dominated the air surrounding us. Why that made my heart race faster, I wasn't sure.”"A perfect storm of tension and heat”That’s how I would describe the chemistry between them.Theirs was a can’t-eat-can’t-sleep kind of attraction.And Forbidden or not. The obvious connection between them couldn't be denied. It was there. There for you to feel it. To experience it. To make it yours.The devotion to the sport and the mutual respect for their ambition and focus, was what drove them together.Was it “morally wrong”? YES. It was obscene. But nauseatingly addictive and I LOVED IT.“We were balancing on a fine line and we both knew it.”They knew it, I knew it and you will know it. But consequences be damned.Raw and intense, yet sensitive and touching. It will keep you hooked till the very end. Forbidden Romance at its finest.This is a book where people will have to go with an open mind. This is a forbidden romance, one that is not easy to digest. If you are not keen on this genre, avoid it. Or give it a chance just like I did and get ready for one of the most torturous but delicious rides of your life.You have been warned.

book.title: Tis the Season for Revenge
book.author: Morgan  Elizabeth
book.description: Abbie Keller thought that Richard Bartholemew Benson the Third would be her forever. In their four years of dating, she never doubted that she would end up with his grandmother’s engagement ring on her finger. Sure, she had to change a few things about herself to fit that mold, like dyeing her hair, dressing more conservatively, and finding golf enjoyable (honestly the most difficult of the changes), but she was sure that at the end of it all, it would be worth it.That is, until he leaves her crying outside her apartment wearing a Halloween costume, having broken it off with her because she’s just not serious enough. She was just fun, he tells her - and now that Richard has becoming a partner at his law firm in his sights, he needs to focus on work.So she does what every girl does when she’s broken up with: she calls her friends, gets drunk, dyes her hair, and formulates her plan for revenge. It just so happens that the universe supports her efforts and gives her the perfect match to prove to her ex that he made a huge mistake: his boss.Abbie starts dating the founding partner of Richard’s law firm, Damien Martinez, with one thing in mind: convincing him to invite her to the huge annual Christmas party as his date.But when the relationship starts to become something more than casual dating and Abbie sees that the tough New York lawyer has a soft side, will she be able to follow through with her plan of deceit?Tis the Season for Revenge is a contemporary, grumpy sunshine, age-gap, Christmas romance.It is a full-length romance with a Happily Ever After that features sexually explicit material and profanity. This book is intended for 18+

reviews.text: If you like Legally Blonde and Christmas vibes, then this is the book to pick up.Plot- 3.5/5🌟Spice- 4/5🌶️✔️Revenge trope (duh)✔️Age gap romance✔️Insta loveOh wow, I did not expect to enjoy it this much especially the second half. It was a rollercoaster of emotions- anger in the beginning, joy in the middle, a mix of disappointment and anxiety in the last 20% and a sweet delicious ending in the last 5%.Guys when I tell you the smut is so juicy, I mean it. It's really toe curling potential tbh and I'm not downplaying it at all.My only problem with the book (which is why I don't rate it 5 stars) is the FMC and how insta lusty/lovey it was. I'm sorry, I know I was supposed to like her but I couldn't. She was annoying and full of "facepalm" worthy bad decision maker. I did have sympathy for her but she was toopinkfor me.Wait that makes me sound like a borderline Richard. Dang it.I also like my romances to be a slow burn which was another miss for me in this one.I loved Damien. The man is full of charisma, personality, loyalty, passion and all things perfect. Like he did not go wrong anywhere and I loved how utterly fictional he was lmao.Overall, this is a pretty good Christmas read and one that will make you swoon with the sweetness and hot with the spice.Also, *pats myself on the back* for completing the reading challenge.

This is a book I KNOW I’ll reread forever. It gave me everything I wanted and more. Ugh, it’s too good!Every chapter would leave me with such a giddy feeling. My body rejected this book like that scene from Alien.✨This read like Tessa Bailey books, which I loathe entirely. So safe to say it was just like not for me. The writing style, the hero, the heroine, the words used. I’ve never really experienced the ick quicker than I did with Damien. But then again Abigail tried her hardest to get me to tap out. I nearly DNFed at 32% but people don’t like when someone DNFs and writes a review so here we are.In terms of writing style, it just felt like a lot. It took 18% for them to meet, and when he finally said “hi” she went on an inner monologue for FIVE pages. And then she was like “oh hi.” She did this a lot.For your displeasure, I compiled some of my ick words, including the number of times I read them. Not numbered: the amount of times I nearly broke down in tears.“!” - I can’t search the exact number of exclamation points but good! god!“yeah” - 113 - and of those 31 are “yeah?” when he wants to articulate a thought. It’s all that man says somebody get him a thesaurus and somebody get me the iodine, yeah?“Baby” - 66 - yes he called her baby 66 times, including at least three times on the first date. My personal hell. He also likes country music so maybe this was a clever homage to the Cars classic, Route 66.“Christmas” - 50 - while mentioned half a hundred times, this book was very much not holiday vibes. She just wanted to go to a Christmas party that happened at the end of the book.“Honey” - 15 - which she called him on the first date!good god this was nearly as bad as baby“Good girl” - 13 - 10 too many for one book bc it loses all impact“Gurgle” - 2 - well gurgle just isn’t a sexy word and that coupled with all the babies…I don’t like“Fuckuva” - 1 - well he found the thesaurus and then birthed this monster“Reese Witherspoon and Luke Wilson” - 1 - leave them out of thisAnd a partridge in a pear tree.⭐️.5/5 🌶️🌶️🌶️.75*/5*the language noted above made me hate the sex scenes, of which there were manyNote: A conversation involving STIs used the word clean.

3 starsIt was a very realistic, NON-REALISTIC book I've read lol 🤡 Some aspects of this book were very relatable, and this book was a perfect balance between cheesy romance and the sad, brutal truth of life! I have a really big rant coming on a serious topic though....╰┈➤ ABIGAIL KELLER & DAMIEN MARTINEZ:This is about Abigail, who got her heart broken byRichardDick after 4 years of a relationship because for him, 'she wasn't enough.' To overcome the insecurities and hurt he gave her, she decided to take REVENGE, and the perfect revenge plan was created when she was matched with Damien, the boss of Richard, on a dating app!Abbie— her character in this book was more than the pink sunshine-y vibes she was said to have! What I really concluded from her entire personality is that she had attachment issues, and A LOT (and not saying that randomly, her childhood was bad too because of parents). Her character didn't annoy me, and her emotions were kinda relatable in many aspects, but I can't really say that she got character development and turned into a strong person — it was more like she found a perfect man, and life goes on and on.Damien— he was actually amazing! His type of people are just the best ones! The ones who make you feel comfortable and confident, who don't judge you for being yourself, the ones who wouldn't make you feel insecure! His character was all good, and he was just fun to be around, if you can say, and umm he's also someone who would set their boundaries and respect others too! I loved how he made Abbie feel and listened to her. ✨╰┈➤ Rants:—RELATIONSHIP TRAUMA IS REAL!I liked how everything about the trauma is explained in this book. The way Abbie realized each and every messed up thing her ex made her do, now that she's not wearing rose-tinted glasses! When you're so in love with someone who doesn't like you being yourself, you sometimes compromise so much just to please the other person that you lose a part of yourself, and you're not the person you used to be. You start hating yourself, the things you do, and second-guess your choices because the other person made you feel less about yourself. All these emotions were present here.I mostly relate to Cam and Kat because as a friend who had this experience with a friend, I can 100% say it's terrible, not just for the person going through this, but for the friends as well. Personally, I felt bad and regretted that I couldn't do anything for her, and even though you warn them, you just have to stay quiet because they are the ones in love, and they won't see what you're trying to tell them unless they realize it themselves.I related to this aspect of the book because I've seen all this comparing and having no self-esteem shit in real life!— Abbie kept comparing everything Damien was doing with what Richard used to do and kept overthinking the worst-case scenarios and didn't allow herself to be HERSELF! The standards set by Richard were so engraved into her mind that even though she hated them, she hated herself more for following them.She was insecure, had no self-esteem, and felt bad because even though you do everything for the other person, 'YOU'RE NOT ENOUGH' for them. It gets super toxic that some days you sit and think that maybe it wasyouwho did something wrong that they left you because.— The one-night stand and enjoying the date part are real, but having full-on obsession and attachment is kind of a stretch.... so yeah, this book is falling into INSTA-LUST category 😭I mean, on the first night, Damien started saying YOU'RE MINE and MINE MINE MINE again, and it was too much for me? They're not serious for commitment but exclusive in their seeing each other, but they actually had so much sex in only weeks, and some scenes were cute and over the top nice things, but everything was too rushed! It was just too quick for Abbie to forget about the 4 years and fall in love quickly again!The insecurities remained, but you can't completely get over a person that fast, even I know that lol 🥹, so the relationship development was really quick!— In the end, when Damien found out about everything, he didn't act all petty and angry but handled things a bit more maturely, and it didn't cause a third-act breakup.But to be very honest, what Abbie did with him was pretty fucked up! Imagine dating someone just to get back at your ex — to make them regret that they lost you and you're worth it — but INSTEAD of falling in love like Abbie did... what if you don't? What if you try but just couldn't fall for them and end up hurting them just as badly as you were hurt?It's a book, and I shouldn't care much, but I just couldn't help it! I wouldn't like it if this happened in real life. This was pretty bad.╰┈➤ FINAL THOUGHTS:Would I recommend it? Yes. It's good for reading in one sitting or if you're looking for a book just to pass your time! But it's pretty forgettable! 🤷🏻‍♀️The nickname RUBIA (blonde) couldn't be more original! 💀

i’m going to make a very bold claim but… tis the season for revenge is THE holiday romance of 2022.if you are a legally blonde, rom com, age gap, banter, or rich hero lover, this is an absolute must read.i literally read this paperback (which we all know is slower than reading a kindle) in 3 hours.abbie gets broken up with by her boring and controlling boyfriend of 4 years on halloween but when her friends come over and they start drunkenly swiping on dating apps, abbie spots her ex’s boss that he always complained about and vows that getting with him would be the sweetest revenge. except, he turns out to be a perfect boyfriend ok I’m obsessed with damien.i loved abbie so much. she’s elle woods-inspired in that she’s a lover of all things girly and sparkly but her ex coerced her into dying her hair darker, toning down her appearance, and toning down her personality. and we’ve all been there when a dud tries to bring us down to make themselves feel better.daddy damien has one date with abbie and is like, bring on the sparkle baby because you love it and i love you. he’s so thoughtful and hotttttt af. SO many cute gestures, i went through two things of book tabs and this book is only 280 pages.this book is EVERYTHING: funny, spicy, and so sweet and romantic. and it reads so so quick <3

This is absolutely my new favorite holiday romance!! At halloween, Abbie's boyfriend of four years breaks up with her for not being serious enough. Later that night, Abbie matches with his boss on a dating app and creates a plan with her friends to date the hotter, older, and richer boss and show up to her ex's Christmas party on his arm. What she doesn't expect, though, is a super sweet man who puts her first and who she starts falling head over heels for. You know the closer they grow together that things are definitely going to blow up when he learns why she really started dating him, but that actually didn't bother me. I loved seeing how sweet Damien was and how he truly fell for Abbie. He was a super successful and serous lawyer, but he knows how to treat a woman and wanted her to know her worth. This is a hot age gap romance and I just loved everything about it! It definitely has Legally Blonde vibes but at Christmas and it was just perfect!

2.5 ⭐️ To be honest, this just wasn’t for me. I’ll be the first to say my issue with her is personal to me - strong and resilient is my preference when it comes to a leading lady and the constant repetition of statements like “I’m so fucking stupid” and “I’m so pathetic” was more than I could bear.Yes, she got fooled by a man, but so have many other smart and beautiful women. That doesn’t make her stupid, that makes HIM stupid.Stand up girl and know your worth.

3.75 stars“I am absolutely wild about you. You are my sun and my moon, and I will be yours. You completely consume me.”This was such a sweet, fluffy, and adorable book. I enjoyed it a lot. All those sweet and domestic moments between Abigail and Damien were so cute. This is the kind of book where you feel butterflies dancing in your stomach because of the adorable moments between Abigail and Damien. The plot is fast-paced. There is banter, small acts of love and care. The side characters were amazing too—I loved Kat and Cam and how they were always there for Abigail. Their friendship was perfect.Abigail’s shitty ex-boyfriend broke up with her on Halloween night when she was dressed like a bunny, and that dickhead told her she was just for fun and that she was too much. And then all three girls (Abigail, Kat, and Cam) got together and made a revenge plan for the bald ex-boyfriend. All their revenge ideas were hilarious. Abigail’s dating profile matched with Richard’s boss on a dating app, and she decided, “I’m gonna fuck his boss.” I know this woman is dramatic, but I liked her over-the-top personality. They started dating and falling in love, and they both decided it wasn’t serious. But who were they kidding?“What brings you joy, Damien?” she asks, her voice low and soft.“Right now? It’s standing right in front of me.”Damien Martinezis just so perfect. He’s sweet, caring, kind, thoughtful, funny, and the most adoring character who knows how to treat his woman. He never let Abigail question anything about herself. And his sweet little acts of love were everything. He made Abigail feel cherished, appreciated, loved, and beautiful. He’s perfect for her. And he calls herNaranjaandRubiaand so many other nicknames. And he sure knows how to use his mouth perfectly. He’s such a dirty talker, and it was so freaking hot.“Boys might not, baby. But men? Men love to make a woman scream their name while their head is between her legs.”Abigail Kelleris all pink, sunshine, and sparkle, and I’m happy she’s not trying to change herself just because a man thinks she’s too much. She was a lot sometimes, annoying, with how she acted in this book. I liked her character growth, though, and how she finally decided to accept herself as she was. Because fuck those men.“Because the reality is, he wasn’t enough. And he’s right: I am too much. I am too much for him because he always should have deserved less.”Abigail and Damienare both just so freaking adorable and sexy. I don’t know who said that Damien Martinez is a grumpy man because it didn’t feel like that. Some scenes were corny, but you know what? I ate that shit up. I’ve never read the Age Gap trope because I thought it wasn’t my cup of tea. But this was my first book with this particular trope, and it didn’t disappoint—not even a bit. I loved Damien and Abigail’s relationship and how Damien always made her feel loved and comfortable. It was just perfect. And the spice in this book? Just immaculate and perfect. The only thing that bothered me was the insta-love thing. I mean, they just met, and then Damien took her to his apartment to fuck her. Not that I’m complaining, because that scene was just hot.“You want big, and you want sparkle, and you want extravagance. Not in price, but in love and adoration. And right here, right now, I’m promising to spend the rest of my life giving you that. Say yes, and I’ll make you feel loved and cherished and appreciated until my last breath. Say yes, and I’ll help you paint the world pink. Say yes, and we’ll forever be completely consumed by each other.”“You consume me. I don’t know how you did it, but I have fallen madly, deeply in love with you.”The ending was so cute. They got married, and those chapters were perfect! If you haven’t read the bonus chapter, go right now and read it.If you’re in the mood for a fluffy and sweet romance story with Christmas and Legally Blonde vibes, this book is for you. Plus, everyone needs to meet Damien Martinez!

I’m speechless! This was such a perfect romance book! Especially for this season.I’m so not ready to let them go but omg I LOVED it😭Tropes:- Instant love- Heartbreak- Self discovery- Age Gap- Possessive alpha maleEverything starts with an ugly break up and Abbie’s plan for revenge. She wants to make sure her stupid ex knows what he lost. And she decides the best way to do this is to fuck his boss. But the boss in question has no idea what’s going on. He’s just dtf.Damien is a dream man. He falls for Abbie on their first date. He had no intentions to, just wanted some fun but he starts calling her his soulmate almost immediately 🥺 I love him. I want him. I need him.The words he says makes me think maybe men are allowed to speak after all.“I don’t share. I’m a very possessive man. What’s mine is mine.”“I don’t date. I downloaded that stupid app on a whim and my dream girl dropped in my lap.”“You were listening.”“I always listen to you.”“What brings you joy, Damien?”“Right now? It’s standing right in front of me.”“This is what you get for turning me into a desperate man. This is what you get for consuming my thoughts.”“You make me happy. I’ll do what it takes to prove it to you.”“I want everyone to know I’m the luckiest person alive. I have a woman on my arm who is every man’s wet dream but only I get to play with her.”“I’m falling head over heels for you, and even if you weren’t with me, I’d drag you along.”“You consume me. I don’t know how you did it but I have fallen madly, deeply in love with you.”“Whatever you want, you ask and it’s yours.”I COULD GO ON AND ON BECAUSE THIS MAN NEVER STOPS BEING PERFECTION!!!I loved Abbie too. I could relate to a lot of parts of her. She is amazing, she is unapologetically herself even though a certain someone tried to force her to be someone else🙄Her friends and sister are great too! They are so real with her. I loved Kat the most. Especially when she sees Damien for the first time💀“Calm the fuck down, Kat. He’s just a man.”“That is not a man, Abigail. That is a god.”Honestly, relatable 🤣 You don’t see hot, successful, tall, sexy, smart men in their 40s that often. I’d be drooling too ngl.I couldn’t stop reading, I needed to know what happens next. Every chapter keeps you on your toes. The ending was so well written. Exactly what they needed, these two are made for each other!The spicy scenes were CHEF’S KISS😩P.S. if you haven’t read the bonus chapter, go do it nowwwwww!

3.5 stars“I’m going to date his boss. I’m going to make his boss fall for me, and I’m going to go to that stupid fucking Christmas party Richard never wanted to take me to, and I’m going to show him what a mistake he made by playing Abigail Keller.” 😈lmao, this is one book i want turned into a movie because:1. the“petty revenge on an ex”trope might be tired and unoriginal, but it is also timeless and relatable.2. i want to see theclimax“Christmas party”showdownin technicolor. 🤣3. the friendship in this book is everything ive wanted and more.4. this book is so “Better Than Revenge” by Taylor Swift coded.this book is corny and cheesy the way romance books are, but i would totally get down on my knees ifDamien Martinez(H) crooked his finger at me and called me his dirty little wh*r*. 🧎 🧎 hes so...suave, but when you get him to the bedroom...all bets are off. i love the way he always cups his palm over the h’s neck to feel her pulse. it looks dominant but tender at the same time. 🥰“This was always more, Abigail. I don’t blame you for wanting revenge. If you had told me from the start, I would have agreed. Richard’s an ass. An ass who won’t have a job after the holidays.”mybiggest quibblewith this book is the“lie by omission” conflict(how the h takes advantage of the H to futher her own agenda). 😑 i understand perfectly why the h goes about involving the H the way she does, but it would be less drama if she’d simply propose her idea to the H (he would totally help her). i suppose their romance would have no stakes if it happened the way i preferred.“Every day you showed me what I was worth and that I deserved more than someone tolerating my presence. I deserved to be equal in a relationship instead of an assistant to some man. It took one date with you to see you were nothing like I had been told. It took one visit to Rollard’s to know you were a good man. It took one trip home to realize that you and I were something more.”another thingi object is the timeline. its fast how the h moves on from one man to the next. shes literally dumped by her long term boyfriend one night, goes on a date the next day, and sleeps with her date on their FIRST date (after which she claims the experience is soul-altering/doesnt feel like a rebound). it seems unrealistic, especially considering shes just gotten out of atoxicrelationship. 🤧 there should be baggage & trauma to unpack and therapy appointments to go to.“You consume me. I don’t know how you did it, but I have fallen madly, deeply, in love with you. Every moment of every day is consumed by thoughts of you, planning the future, dying to be with you.”the friendship dynamic in this book is mostly supportive and heartwarming. however, the last minor niggle i have is with one of the h’s two best friends. shes a jilted man hater who keeps projecting her heartbreak on the h. she annoyed me. i would sayTis the Season for Revengeis NOT perfect, but it was a fun read. i think it would make for a great Christmas romcom movie. FYI,both MCs are childfree, which is fairly rare in this genre. 🙌

2.5⭐️/2🌶I’m disappointed i didn’t like this more…Right off the bat, I knew I wasn’t going to be that big of a fan of Abbie’s. I really couldn’t stand her personality but at the same time I felt sorry for her because her ex really did a number on her. But idk something with her didn’t hit right.I really liked Damien. I loved that he showed Abbie what she was missing in a man who treats his woman right. Literally everything he did was very swoon worthy. Especially the scene at Thanksgiving dinner?? THAT was so sweet.I don’t think we got enough… honestly anything? It just didn’t feel like we got to know each of the characters individually.We didn’t really see them outside of each other. Like I wanted to see more of Damien’s day as a lawyer kind of thing. And we get a brief glimpse of that in the end but not anything substantial.Their whole relationship starts out as her getting revenge on her ex by getting with his boss which I loved that. And they both weren’t looking for anything serious but as soon as they go out on one date and they sleep together, those “nothing serious” lines get totally blurred. And that suddenness just didn’t work for me here.When it all is revealed at the Christmas party, I got second hand embarrassment bcos like we all knew it was going to crash and burn at some point. And then i expected her to have to grovel or at least SOMETHING. And nope. She runs after him and explains and all is well. 🫠 like it was cute what he says in that scene but like ??? Idk. If i expect the men to grovel when they mess up, the same goes for the women.I really didn’t like her friend Camille? she just wanted this whole thing to go down to get closure for her fuxked up past which made me feel mixed things. Her other friend kat was alright.Richard is an absolute douche and he deserves to rot and the pranks they pulled on him were absolute top notch 😂Also, the dialogue was meh. I swear if I have to read the exchange :“oh?”“Yeah oh”One more time…im gonna lose it 😂And Damien…every time he asked a question it was most always followed up by “,yeah?” For example:“A little harsh, yeah?” he asks, and it’s a relief.”“Got it, rubia. See you tomorrow, yeah?”There’s a lot more but I think you get the idea. Totally fine, it just became really repetitive.Also, I know it’s been a while since I’ve taken high school Spanish but isn’t “rubia” Spanish for blonde? So my assumption is that it was meant to be used as a term of endearment like “blondie” but what was being used was the word that describes someone’s hair color…so idk that’s just what I substituted every time he called her “rubia”.And i gotta be honest the smut wasn’t that great 😂 it was so cringey to the point where I really debated if this was worth continuing 💀 it was giving p0rno imo 😅
legally blonde if it was deep and if elle woods had mommy and daddy issuesand damien isthe only manever. made me cry🫡

4 stars“Oh.”��Yeah, oh.”‘Tis the damn season!! Also, This was the perfect pick for my “one holiday romance per year” because I’m such a moody reader and too much fluff is too much for me!!This is a book that I know if I think about it too seriously, I’ll see and recognize many red flags. However, I chose to ignore them all for the sake of the holiday spirit and all. It’s a book that is not meant to be taken seriously and I just think that there’s a charm in that.Damien was such a sweet caring character. I loved how he showed her the affection and love she needed. Abigail was all pink and sparkle. Yes, knowing that this whole thing was just wrong -and begging her internally to just confess even though you know she won’t- was frustrating, because Damien didn’t deserve that. Actually no one deserves that. I actually though that the whole thing will be planned between them before I read this.

4 stars“What brings joy should never be embarrassing”Another time I gave into the hype! This was my first Morgan Elizabeth book and it was a good time. Legally Blonde holiday vibes, age gap romance with a heroine focused on revenge.When Abbie gets dumped on Halloween by her boyfriend of four years she's crushed. The way he broke it off was awful and he was a trash human being so when she saw his boss on a dating app, she went there. It was all fun and games at first until real feelings started to develop.I loved watching Abbie come back into her own. Her ex couldn't handle all she was and wanted a watered down version of her. Reminds me of that quote- 'If I'm too much, go find less'. I loved watching her be with someone who not only appreciated her but let her be her. Damien was the best and though this was a bit predictable, it was a lot of fun.

✨✨zero stars✨✨Apparently tis the season for me to read garbage books with cute covers.Tis the Season for Revenge is definitely the worst one though.Really, it fucking sucks.It's a cheap Legally Blonde rip off with terrible characters, an unrealistic as hell love story and the promised revenge that is... just not there.The only thing this book has going for it is the smut and that's so not enough to not make me regret wasting 7 hours of my life on this bullshit.But the worst thing is 100% the characters.Richard the ex-boyfriend is the worst guy you will ever encounter in a book. Like seriously forget Lord Voldemort, Sauron, Dracula and all those other losers.He treats Abbie like his employee/ sex slave, cheats on her, fat-shames, is misogynistic as hell, and not to mention is he a snob who feels like he is soooo much better than literally anyone else and .... do I really need to say more?He made Abbie PRETEND to be his fucking secretary in public for fuck's sake.While I usually love to hate a character, this was just getting ridiculous. He has not a single good quality and it doesn't make any sense that Abbie was with him for 4 fucking years.Every time she had yet another flashback of Richard doing something horrible to her (and there were a lot of flashbacks) I didn't feel sympathy for her. I was fucking annoyed.Richard is a walking red flag in any way, but the worst thing that he isn't even clever about it.I've recently read the Flat Share and while it wasn't exactly my favorite book, I was kind of impressed how manipulative the ex-boyfriend was. There was also a scene that was almost identical to one in this book, so I can't help but compare them:So for context, the heroines of both books love to dress in a fun colorful way and their bf hate itIn the Flat Share, the guy told her:"Are you sure you want to go like this? Everyone there will look way more professional than you, and I just don't want you to feel uncomfortable"In Tis the Season for Revenge, Richards tells her:"What are you wearing? Are you dumb? Go change"Tis the Season For Revenge has the subtlety of a fucking sledgehammer.Anyway, after Richard breaks up with Abbie she goes from beingsooooin love with him to realizing that he is human dog shit in like 3 pages and I was like: oh wow... isn't that a bit too fast?But then I realized that bouncing between 2 extremes it basically Abbie's whole character. She is either super confident that she can do everything or completely insecure about everything, her life is wonderful or it's awful, she thinks her job as a makeup artist is nothing specially or she thinks she's✨ an artist and changing lives ✨.... and that goes on for the whole book like bro please get some help I am concerned for you.(Not to mention that she also always believes what the last male creature she talks to tells her)After immediately realizing how terrible Richard treated her, she and her girlfriends want revenge and that's the part I was most excited for and the biggest letdown, as their revenge is fucking shit.Basically they just put glitter into the vent of his car and the worst thing isWE DON'T EVEN GET SO SEE THATwe just know they did because someone was like"hey man do you glitter a bit?"and Richard is like"yeah, an ex did that"and that's fucking it!?Then it focuses on their biggest revenge plan: Abbie getting it on with Richard's boss to get invited to the Christmas party Richard never took her to because he was too embarrassed to be seen with her.Which is already not the most flawless plan if you think about it, but as we already established: Abbie is not the most stable woman.But that brings me to the next male creature in the book: Damien.I've seen so many people gush about how perfect he is and I have to agree, He really is perfect... and that is why hate him.He always says exactly the right thing at the right time, immediately drops everything to be with Abbie and grants her every single one of her wishes (even the once she didn't know she had. Natrually.)Damien is also a fancy lawyer butOF COURSEonly takes on clients who are poor or abused and have absolutely no money so that he can win for them and ✨justice✨ and so that he can pay his clients rent for the next 3 months without expecting anything in return and is overall so perfect that he barely seems human.Like literally barely human, as he doesn't seem to have a real personality.(Unless coming up with terrible pet names is a personality trait, duh)He also basically invites herON THEIR FIRST DATEto the Christmas party. Which is totally something a real human would do, taking a complete stranger who might or might not turn out to be a fucking psychopath to your most important work event of the year.Sure, that totally sounds like a good idea.If you want to tell me how wrong I am about this book, please note that I only read 60% of it until my sister basically forced me to dnf it after I raged about how fucking awful it is for the 1283x time.Have a nice day.

It was just ok for me. A story about a girl getting petty revenge on her emotionally abusive ex-boyfriend seems right up my alley but I just couldn't vibe with this book. I did love Mr. Damien Martinez though, that man was ridiculously sexy and sweet. Naranja? So damn cute!I think that I could never really get around her using him like that and letting it go so far. (I never buy in to the "I tried to tell him excuse" it's weak)I also didn't realize this is book 5 in a series, so there were so many secondary characters I didn't know and it was a bit hard to keep track.

Okay, I was in between 1 and 2 stars for this. Straight to the point, this was not for me. The plot didn't have anything interesting to offer, everything felt like a random filler, except for the main story of revenge for crappy ex.Even so, I was hoping to love this book because I'm fine with a simple plot IF the characters, dialogue and spicy times are great. But that wasn't the case.Well, Damien was definitely dreamy. The perfect guy (no, seriously, he had *zero* faults to his character). Despite dating many women who apparently look just like Abigail, he falls for her immediately after one date and good sexy times.Abigail.. I wanted to like her and I'm happy Damien treated her well but the entire book, i was cringing. so. damn. painfully.Hear this, I shit you not:"A woman should never make the call after a first date. She waits so as not to sound desperate," I said near instantly and then regretted it almost instantly because it sounded like a bitchy thing.🤡Her self-esteem was lower than the flooor and everything was about how both Richard and then Damien were important rich lawyer men and she was nothing. It annoyed me that Damien was tasked with uncovering her major (majormajor) issues with self-esteem and self-respect.There were a hundred instances of something like this:[ A high-powered man like Damien wouldn't take a train, much less on someone else's schedule. I should have thought of that. ]Gee, who'd take the train, what a peasant 🙉(I mean, of course they drove in the end lol. )[ The reality is when you're dating busy, high-profile men, you should be happy when they take any portion of their time out to be with you. It's something I had taught myself to understand, and the few times I had conversations with the wives of Richard's equally important and wealthy friends, they echoed the sentiment. When time is money, any spent on you should be an honor. ]☠️🙂Then there's the super casual mentioning of sexual harassment:[ "I'm just glad she's not focused on me," Hunter said under his breath, and I scoffed a laugh, because he wasn't wrong - Maggie loves to hit on my brother-in-law, trying to make him as uncomfortable as possible. *cue total change of topic]Funny how it wouldn't sound the same if it was a sister-in-law and a creepy uncle or whoever that Maggie was (a part of the fam, apparently). Just gross, and, just no.☠️☠️☠️Okay, but there's more.While I see others loving the sex scenes, I thought it was a bit bland with the back-arching whimpering - i believe the word 'whining' was actually used - and the 'no, you WILL come from my cock alone'! attitude (srsly, Damien dude, it's possible for that to not be possible for a woman no matter how dominatingly you demand it lmao. you should know better).Also, that 'you dirty whore' was a surprise, as I thought this isn't casual sex lingo? 👀 I mean, to each their own but it just came out of nowhere. I get the sex was supposed to be filthy and your girl enjoys some of that like any other lady but some things were just off.Didn't like the final scene but that's a pet peeve of mine.I get I shouldn't overthink a simple fantasy holiday love story but it was just not believable enough to even get me to immerse myself in the story. Not only that, but it had major issues.Or maybe I'm just not into the rich guy saving poor girl with no self-esteem plot line. It's not charming for me.I saw this author has another book out with a nanny and a wealthy businessman love story (this fmc's sister probs). Looks like she's into a big power dynamic difference. Meh. Am I supposed to swoon if she gets the rich guy in the end who - yayyy - treats her as an equal human being?This was just a disappointment and I'm done now, my soul is free from this book and when i wake up tomorrow, i will have forgotten i ever read it.After forcing myself to re-read those 'cringe highlights', 1 star.

Get back at her ex by fucking his boss. But that plan goes out with window because Damien is the perfect man. He’s supportive, caring, thoughtful, and can dirty talk like no other. Abbie tried to tell Damien about her plan multiple times and he kept being like “tell me later,” which annoyed me. But the FINALE. THE LOVE CONFESSIONS. They made up for it. book.title: Unconditional
book.author: Q.B. Tyler
book.description: It only takes one moment to change the course of someone’s life.One moment to link two souls forever.That moment came when I pulled her out of her darkest hour.The tragedy that threatened to swallow her whole and shatter her innocence.Save her.Protect her.Love her.I vowed.Ten years later, my vows are the same.But I’m differentShe’s differentWe’re different.And the feelings threatening to break free have the power to destroy everything.I’ll stop at nothing to keep her safe, but what if the very thing she needs protection from...is me?

reviews.text: ARC reviews are talking about a hot love story between two people with an age gap but all I see is the story of a guy who falls in love with a child he raised and I feel sick!And they do not want to give the information about their ages. Age gap, they say. Oh come on, tell me what the age gap is.Well, let me tell you, she was 7 when he was 23. That's the age gap.Because he takes her in when she is 7 and raises her, he acts like a father figure.One review says,"It's a real "Daddy" story with great emotion".Another one says"It has delicious daddy kink in it"- yayyyy, how nice... Let me go puke some more!***Correct me if I am wrong and he didn't fall in love with a child he raised because I am talking about it without reading the book but based on two honest reviews. And if it is the case, I mean if I am not wrong about what happens, then, please don't tell me how epic their love is!***P.S. And most reviews say that they don't want to talk about the taboo subject because - I am quoting -we have to read and see!Are you f*cking kidding me???You have to tell what that taboo is so that I can decide to buy it or not. I was able to find only two reviews explaining the taboo matter. hope it increases with time.

4 Forbidden Stars ⭐️Unconditional is the story of Maddie a young girl who lost both parents to a double murder/suicide. Left orphaned she is taken into care by Cal, a junior law enforcement officer who finds her at the scene of the crime.Present day sees 10 years have passed and Maddie is approaching her 18th birthday. Having spent her formative years with Cal, her feelings begin to evolve, creating complications for them both. For many, this book would be classed as a taboo read as it involves a guardian and his ward falling for each other, not to mention a large age gap.Although Cal tries to keep their relationship platonic, ultimately the heart wants, what the heart wants. As they very much love one another, they are prepared to deal with the ramifications once they decide to enter into a relationship.‘Maybe all along I’d been falling in love with her. Not in a sexual way, but in a way that made me love her mind and her soul and her heart. The way that made me believe in soul mates’.This is a very hot and explicit read, so be warned. I really enjoyed this although it takes a strangely twisted turn at the end...

2.5 stars ⭐️‘I want to worship her…but I also want to defile her.’Aiiiighttt, look, I know most of my GR friends loved this one, and for good reason: it's taboo, there's quite an age-gap, and the writing's really addictive.That being said, this book didn't quite live up to the hype for me. The plot was overall a 3-star, but the unnecessary plot twist akadramaat around 90% made it even less enjoyable. Lastly, let me tell you what pissed me off the most...Maddie.This girl spent about 80% of the book crying. Sounds like exaggeration? I wish it was.Allow me to show y'all:I just only hope he doesn’t see the tears forming in my eyes before I turn away.*****I spent the entire night in tears after what happened.*****The tears spring to my eyes just as they always do when I think about what could have been.*****Tears rush to my eyes and my bottom lip trembles.*****Tears roll down her face just as her eyes flutter shut.*****Maddie standing there with tears in her eyes, streaming down her pale cheeks.*****She turns in my arms, allowing me a look at her face that’s red and splotchy. Her eyes are glistening from her tears.*****The tears leaked out of her eyes and slid down her cheeks.*****Her eyes well up with tears before she blinks them back.*****The tears are already in Maddie’s eyes, but they haven’t started to fall.*****I squeeze my eyes shut to prevent the tears from falling when I feel one leaking out of the corner of my eye and slide into my hair.*****My eyebrows shoot to my hairline and my eyes fill with more tears.I limited myself to about 10-12 quotes, but there are around 20 more times that she cries. So basically, this was me trying to get past her crying parts:Me: OMG, JUST STOP CRYING!! 🤬Maddie:‘My eyes well up with tears at the harshness of her tone.’🥺

2.5 starsI wanted to love this book so much...but no one told me the heroine was only 17 during this book...Honestly, if the heroine were at least 20, I would be all on board. However, she's only 17 and we're dropped into the story when she's had a crush on Cal for 3 years and Cal is super attracted to her. Keep in mind he's raised her since her parents died when she was 7. I would have liked to see more of a connection between Cal and Maddie than the physical connection they had. They were literally obsessed with their physical relationship and I didn't believe they were completely and madly in love with each other. It definitely felt like insta-love and I didn't buy that Cal loved her beyond his desire to constantly sleep with her. Considering Cal is 33 and Maddie is 17, I wasn't that big of a fan. There's taboo and then there's TABOO and this just isn't what I like. Even if Maddie were actually 18 when they started their relationship, I wouldn't be able to get over how young she acted and how young Cal acted. She would act so bratty when any woman came around and I just did not see what Cal saw in her. He's a 33 year old chief of police and acted like a teenager too. I need some more maturity in my relationships in books.The ending twist was interesting and helped move the plot along, but I just couldn't get behind the romance. I'll stick with QB's romances with older characters.

I AM NOT BEING JUDGEMENTAL BUT ONLY OPINIONATED!!!See this was strait away creepy and disgusting. A person who has looked after a 7 YO till she turned 17 should not feel lustful about her as he has practically SEEN her growing which obviously means that he has seen her body changing as well.Now if while during the change he start to have those feelings of which she knew nothing about then that's certainly not fair.Also, the man till the time being could neither controlled his feelings nor could understand what's right or what's wrong.I get it that this is a work of fiction but I couldn't read further chapter 1 as it was unethical. But still some people may like it for the very same reason (no offense to them).

Re-read-(7/12-7/14/22)It’s a hard line to blur without coming off entirely skeeevy. I think the writer helped that along by having the timeline go back in forth, in essence giving you a taste of both relationships forming. Both that of a guardian/child and a man and a woman.Lol honestly though, it’s a little skeevy and because I’m a sick bitch, it’s fucking hot. If it’s two consenting adults, I’m good and yeah she was a little under. It’s funny how everyone was up in arms over the Nyla K, but it’s always been a thing.Anywho, the story of Maddie and Cal is pretty beautiful, as a cop he saved her. As a man he eventually fell in love with her. Super sweet really.Until the sweetness was obliterated by pure filth. Holy smokes, these two know how to dick down. It could’ve been repetitive but it was so hot I didn’t care if it was.I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again. When it comes to steamy ass age gaps, QB Tyler is one of the best.Not to mention all the character building, the ensuing drama. All in all, an extremely entertaining read.Original review- (5/26-5/31/20)This book was sexy as hell!The first scene starting on page 58 is probably the best love scene I've ever read. Hot!

TW:the awakening of your daddy issues.This was one of the most delicious taboo reads of all times for me.I was on a cop kick, desperately searching for the best cop romance and I was GIFTED this masterpiece while on my search. Not only my badge bunny dreams came true, but it hit every single daddy spot known to men.Q.B Tyler has such an unique voice, she gives me Penelope Douglas vibes and you know anyone who writes even remotely like Penelope has my heart in a chokehold immediately.Her characters had awesome personalities, her writing flows so smoothly, her sm*t is not even a tiny bit cringy, the way she writes tension is amazing and the plot of this book was also pretty incredible. I was invested from beginning to end.I will 100% be reading more Q.B Tyler for the rest of this year.
This book is so forbidden...And I liked it!!!

From all outward appearances this should have been a hit with me. I love taboo books. I love age gaps. I love hot sexy cops. However, I just could not get into this one. First, the flashbacks disturbed me. Not because I found the present situation disturbing, but I don't particularly want to be reminded about how the hero changed her sheets when she peed the bed as a child. Every time there was a flashback it threw me out of the story. Second, I liked none of these characters. The heroine came across like a desperate brat inappropriately throwing herself at the hero every chance she got. The hero himself seemed wishy-washy and weak. Don't even get me started on Aria. Every time she showed up in the book I wanted to throat punch her and tell her to mind her own f-cking business.So yea...I think it is best I shelve this one for now. (lol)

I read this book because I’ve been on an age-gap kick lately and someone recommended it to me on a Facebook group. I went into it kind of blind. I didn’t read the reviews ahead of time and all I knew was that this was an age-gap romance where h falls in love with her guardian.I’ve read this theme before and I thought I was into it, but this book was just too much for me! The other book that I read, which was along the same lines as this was Little Dove by Layla Frost. I LOVED Little Dove, but there were a couple of big differences between that book and this one. 1) in Little Dove H didn’t raise h from the time she was a little girl and 2) although the taboo nature of that relationship was a part of the book, it wasn’t literally the entire plot of the book.I just can’t wrap my mind around how it would be ok for a man who raised a girl from the time she was 7 years old, and played a DAILY father figure role in her life, to then look at that same girl and lust after her. This was justified by saying that Cal wasn’t Maddie’s father and that he never adopted her. Sure there was no blood between them, but the whole “I never adopted her, so it’s ok” justification didn’t make any sense. It would be like a common law partner cheating and then saying “but we never got married, so it’s ok”: the marriage certificate was a formality, for all intents and purposes, you were married! Same thing goes here: the adoption certificate was a formality, for all intents and purposes Cal was Maddie’s father!The father/daughter relationship between Cal and Maddie was so in your face! Like, you couldn’t forget about it and pretend that it was ok and that they were just two consenting adults having steamy sex. The book would toggle back and forth in time between when Maddie was a little girl to present day. It would go from Maddie cuddling her bunny on Cal’s bed after a storm, to her rubbing up on Cal’s dick. It was fucked up! I actually stopped reading the italicized parts of the book, because they were trips down memory lane that I didn’t need to take! The whole thing was made even more cringeworthy because after they started hooking up, Maddie began calling Cal “Daddy” 🥴.All that being said, I thought this was well written. Even though I was cringing most of the time, I couldn’t put it down. It was like a train wreck and I couldn’t look away!

✨ DREAMY’S REVIEW ✨Deliciously taboo.Forbidden goodness.Age gap.Angsty AF.Sizzling, off the charts steam.A connection so fierce, so strong.My emotional level: 100.Anyone who knows me KNOWS that I love everything and anything forbidden and...UNCONDITIONAL IS E V E R Y THING.QB Tyler has truly outdone herself with this standalone novel! Unconditional fed my NEED for something taboo and oh so forbidden with a little age gap?YES. YES. YES. YES. YES.Cal and Maddie’s story has grab a strong hold onto my heart and STILL hasn’t let go. I’ve read it twice!! I’m still not over them!Never will be! This HAS to be my favorite QB Tyler book ever! She’s really outdone herself with this one!*****Cal and Madeline.They aren’t supposed to be together.Their love is questioned... many times.But their union is strong and goes against all odds. They have a love that doesn’t, shouldn’t be explained.Just be.Unconditionally.“Maybe all along I’d been falling in love with her. Not in a sexual way, but in a way that made me love her mind and her soul and her heart. The way that made me believe in soulmates.”*****I enjoyed the shifts from past and present which helped paint the picture of Cal and Maddie’s ever growing relationship! I enjoyed the relationships Maddie shared with her family, Cal’s family, and everyone around her! The secondary characters also brought life and joy into this book.I loved ALL the tender moments between Cal and Maddie! You were able to see when the lines began to blur, its was beautifully explained! After everything that went on, I NEEDED their HEA.“I haven’t explicitly told her I loved her since we’d crossed those lines, but I hope she knows. I hope she knows that with every kiss, every touch, every moment I risk my entire life just to hold her in my arms in every way I knew I shouldn’t, I hope she knows that I’d give up everything to love her forever.”Their story is well written, emotionally charged, hauntingly beautiful, raw and real, and steamy af!“Your first love takes power over you that never relinquishes. It takes hold of your soul and never lets go.”FIVE Superhero and dreamy stars: 💫💫💫💫💫

I want to give this book more than 5 Stars, because that’s what it deserves.I’m one of those people who will pick up a book because other people have branded it “sick” “too taboo” or words to that effect. This is exactly how I found myself to be reading UNCONDITIONAL. You see, this book IS taboo, it pushes the boundaries and it has that shock factor. Just as well, though, that none of those things bother me. I’m able to separate myself from reality and look passed what might be wrong, and see the story for what it is. And it is fiction after all.Cal and Maddie came out of nowhere and blew me away. I had never read a book by QB Tyler before, but oh, what a way to start. Her writing is raw, it’s emotional and it draws you in so much you don’t want to leave. I read this whole book in one go because I just couldn’t bear to put my kindle down, I HAD to find out how this was all going to end.I know a lot of people hate reviews that say “you need to read it for yourself to see what it’s about” and that’s not quite what I’m going to say, but I do believe that you should go in blind and enjoy the story.Of course, if taboo isn’t your thing, then it’s maybe best to step away now. YES, this book has a fairly big age gap. YES, Cal first meets Maddie when she is 7 and he is 22. YES, he is her legal guardian. YES, they do start a relationship when she is still 17.But UNCONDITIONAL is SO. MUCH. MORE.And as the saying goes...”The heart wants what the heart wants” and Mads and Cal wanted each other. Passionately. Wholeheartedly. Unconditionally.

As far as forbidden romances go, this was quite good. I loved the tension in the beginning, the back and forth, the inner conflict and the slow progression. It was well written and I felt for the characters.Something shifted in the middle though. It got dramatic, way too smutty and it dragged. I love smut, don't get me wrong!!! But somehow (and I never thought I'd say that!!) it got a bit much.The chemistry between Cal and Maddie was great, Ms. Tyler writes FILTH very naturally and even beautifully, but the plot stopped developing at some point - and I found myself skimming. I must admit, I agreed with hero's friend and heroine's mother figure Aria about the distance the characters needed at some point. After Maddie turned 18, everything felt rushed and a bit undeveloped. Then the major plot twist (which I saw coming) happened in the last 10%...Soall in all, the pacing was unbalanced for me, some moments of super easy acceptance felt too convenient and heroine's obsession did not make a mature impression, IMO. She was still young, makes sense, yet if the author wanted to show me how epic and long lasting the love was,I wanted to see some more steadiness and maturity from the heroine. And from the hero too, frankly.Still it was a good read, I did enjoy the writing and will be reading more by the author.

Gross.)◍ Since the first chapters, H and h were embarrassingly horny for each other.Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll Eye-roll◍ The direction the plot took in the last 20-15% was just ridiculous and unrealistic.The smutty sexy times were, for me, the highlights of this book and the only reason I kept reading it until the end. If you are looking for an age gap/taboo romance story, with some light daddy kink you may enjoy it.Just, don't expect too much depth..as I said before

After so many mixed reviews, I decided to judge myself, and I always love a good age gap, taboo romance. So I dove right in with an open mind and read a great story.Unconditional is the story of Maddie, who lost her parents to a murder/ suicide at seven years of age. Cal, the police officer on duty, finds her in the closet, and Maddie instantly attached to Cal.Present-day, Ten years later, Cal is Maddie's legal guardian; Maddie is now seventeen and close to her eighteenth birthday. Cal is thirty-three. Her feeling has evolved towards Cal. Cal tries to keep Maddie at a safe distance and fights his feelings towards Maddie. But once he stops fighting, what a beautiful love story.Unconditional is a steamy book with a twist at the end. Maybe a little over the top. But I still wouldn't change it. This book is not for everyone. But if this book sparks an interest, I highly recommend it.

But the lines were really blurring when they said they were dad and daughter from the start.11)“I can’t wait to get you pregnant.” “Me too.”Oh honey this is the first time he's fucking you.You're a virgin.And the first time ever a man fucks youYou say that?Or fuck you the first time a man fucks you HE SAYS THAT?okay breed kink.12)“Sure you want to spend the rest of your life with me?”“The last fourteen haven’t been too bad.” He winks and presses his lips to mine.Sir a good 60% of those years her age was one digit only and a good 20% of those years you cleaned her up when she would piss herself (literally written in the book)Soooooo my conclusion is that ......we tried but we didn't get there.

Unconditional by Author Q. B. Tyler is a standalone, taboo novel with an age gap.“Kissing you was…amazing. Touching you, feeling you…I can’t get it out of my head.”Well, heck. When I first begin reading a novel, I generally enter being a bit guarded. I want to get a feel for the writing and characters before investing time I can never get back. BUT, there are some books that grab me and make me jump in lust first, throwing all caution to the wind. Unconditional was in this latter category of novels. It didn’t take me too many pages to realize that this was the type of taboo that makes me salivate, makes me want to run around the room and fist pump the air. It makes me want to scream, “YESSSSSS!”, and then promptly sit to get down to “the good stuff”.“I won’t tell anyone. Our secret.”This story was DEFINITELY of taboo subject matter. Author Q. B. Tyler did not skim over the off limits nature of Unconditional, but instead approached it with thoughts from numerous characters on the arguments for and against Maddie and Cal’s liaison. In doing this, she exposed the reality of challenges that might be faced, but illuminated the aspect that mattered most, love. What lead up to the forbidden was so sweet and caring that there was no way you could not make Maddie and Cal crash together in your mind. I loved the different stages they were at in their lives that contributed to the seemingly impossible shift from past to present to questionable future. I also loved the initial thread that wove them together, creating a bond that was one to be cherished.“Trust me, sweetheart, the things I’m about to do to you are not things an eighteen year old boy can do.”Past that tenderness was amazing heat and some never-ending sizzle. Cal and Maddie had fiery chemistry that was SO incredibly hot and tantalizing. Every descriptive act made the scorch turn into a blazing inferno. There were also numerous moments that made me aroused and ready to scream with delight.“I hope she knows with every kiss, every touch, every moment I risk my entire life just to hold her in my arms in a way I knew I shouldn’t, I hope she knows that I’d give up everything to love her forever.”I turned the last page of Unconditional with a HUGE smile on my face, knowing the naughty, needy taboo reader in me was satisfied, again and again, and that the forbidden became a reality.4.5 stars

There was a plot twist at the end, so that's why I rated it with 1.5 stars.Sadly, this wasn't for me. I really tried, but just couldn't. 😬😬
2 STARS!Hot but boring.Yep!

4"It May Be Ick, But I Can't Look Away"Stars!!!My face throughout this book...BecauseSo at the tender age of7 years old, our heroine, Madeline/Maddie, was rescued by the heroic and dashing23-year oldofficer, Cal, after her parents died in a domestic homicide-suicide situation. From the moment he found her hiding in her closet, she clung to this man as though her life depended on it and would trust no one else. Having no surviving relatives, the state felt that it would be in the best interest of the child to keep her with someone she felt comfortable with, thus allowing her to live with this single, young man, of whom she shares no blood relation and bonded so quickly with.I'm not sure in what alternate reality this would ever actually be deemed legally or ethically appropriate, butHEY! I read to escape reality, not abide by the rules of it! So I certainly can look beyond social norms in a fictional setting.So I'm not going to get my panties in a twist over that aspect. of this story.The story continues and really starts off when Maddie is17(soon to be turning 18) and Cal is33. It would appear that virginal Maddie is starting to have a sexual awakening and being in the constant presence of a young, caring man that has the body of a god is really starting to affect the way she feels about him. Cal, too, has taken note that Maddie has grown into a beautiful [very] young woman and tries to limit the kind of interactions they share these days. Before long, though, Maddie, who is convinced that she absolutely loves Cal and will never feel that way for another man, decides that she wants their relationship to change and starts to sexually come on to Cal. He tries to resist, but soon discovers his feelings have also shifted from being a nurturing guardian, to a lusting male who wants to actively pursue a romantic relationship with Maddie. They begin this secret affair and try their hardest not to do the actual deed before Maddie turns 18, but skirting around it proves too tempting. And when another teenage boy shows interest in Maddie, Cal's will caves and he ends up taking her virginity approximately a week before her 18th birthday. There's one problem. Unbeknownst to the couple, Cal's older brother, Henry, stops by the house and hears their first tryst in all it's glory and confronts Cal when he comes downstairs to investigate the noises he hears once they're done.I really liked that the story did at least explore the whole aspect of how people would realistically react to the change in their relationship. I mean, you know there would be a lot of people looking at Cal as if he was a groomer that had prepared Maddie to one day fill these kinds of needs for him. And even though, there's absolutelyNOTHINGin the story that would allude to this being the actual case, you know there would be people ready to persecute him all the same.Now, that's not to say that I think the author portrayed all these actions realistically, but I'm happy that at least it was addressed.There is one thing that I have to come clean with and admit that I didNOTlike about this story. The author chose to continuously give flashbacks of memories between Cal and Maddie as aCHILDthroughout the book. I thought that was a very poor choice on her part. I mean, if you're trying to get a reader to accept this outrageous relationship that has developed between a man and a "woman" that he raised since she was 7 years old, you don't exactly want to highlight the moments between them when she was a child and he was a man as "bonding experiences"!Ironically enough, she didn't really show them talking and interacting that much as "adults" to develop a different kind of relationship either. It was more so a mutual understanding of,"I know I deeply care about you and now I realize I find you really hot and sexually attractive, so let's start banging and be a couple instead"kind of thing.Which brings me to my next thing....the steam in this book was off the charts! I mean, if I were rating on that factor, it would certainly be a 5 star read, which I think kinda tricked my brain into rooting for an otherwise very bizarre and off putting relationship.Lastly, I want to say that the little added bit of drama at the end was very much appreciated and not really expected. It added a little edge to the story and helped to serve the purpose in showing all the characters within it just how much Cal really did love and care about Maddie. Oh and that epilogue! It was soVERYsatisfying, which has not really been my experience with epilogues here lately, so I thoroughly enjoyed it.Final Thoughts:This feels like a story I shouldn't have enjoyed as much as I did, but QB Tyler did such an incredible job in writing it, you couldn't help be captivated by it despite it's very taboo origins. Would highly recommend to those who can keep an open mind when reading fiction. Your taste in reading material does not have to be a direct representation of your moral ethics and character-- and I think we'd all be doing a little better to understand and accept that.Happy Reading!!!
