GIVE ME A ZACH RIGHT NOW PLEASE AND THANK YOU!!This is easily Melanie Harlow’s sexiest book to date!!!I was lucky enough to get to alpha/beta read this beauty by Melanie Harlow. I have forever loved Melanie’s books, but this one will forever hold a special place in my heart.This book is Melanie’s first plus size FMC. And although she is plus sized and we get a background of her not always loving her body as a teen growing up (thanks to being in ballet), she is extremely confident and quite frankly after they mention her life early on her size isn’t even mentioned again. So thus, we have a beautiful story of a plus size baddie getting plowed but an older hot man!As a plus size woman myself, I felt seen in this book. The things that have been said to her in her past? I’ve heard them. The trouble shopping for clothes? I’ve dealt with that. Just wanting to be? I feel that every day. I personally loved how this story was handled, and I hope she does more plus size fmc in the future!Millie is so fun. A lot of her reminded me of myself. I felt myself wanting to hug Millie when she was sad, laugh at her jokes and honestly I just wanted to be her best friend. She has never felt like she would get her Prince Charming… and she doesn’t get him, she gets his father, the freaking KING!Zach.. Zach Zach Zach. If I could bottle him and sell him I would. My man has a dirty mouth, and body and isn’t afraid to use it. He sees Millie and is a goner - until he realizes she is his son’s ex girlfriend!!! He has a lot of past issues he has to work through and I loved how he just can’t let her go without a fight.The push and pull was amazing, loved the spice and although this is a little instalusty it didn’t feel rushed. I already wish I could reread this book for the first time again 🥹Overall, I completely adored this book and I’m sad to see Cloverleigh Farms end!!!5/5 stars3.5/5 spice**arc for honest review**

FIVE OUT OF FIVE FUCKING FANTASTIC STARSWhat an incredible ride this was. You almost always know from the first 1-4 chapters that a book will blow you away & this one did NOT disappoint.I was head over heels for both of these characters, I wanted them to win SO BAD. The chemistry and connection they had together was undeniable and they were so perfect for each other.Can we talk about the fact that Millie is a plus sized FMC and the baddest bitch ever. The details and how this was handled was done so damn well and I personally felt represented.Zach was so down bad for her ISTG he wanted to marry her on site. The fact that their first interaction was him saving her from an awkward and uncomfortable run in because he is just ugh big dick energy and such a gentleman? I DIEI loved watching both of them grow together and separately. This is by far my favorite book by Melanie to date. Idk what kind of crack she put in here but I vote for more please.

"Love—the greatest plot twist life has to offer."I had to jump right into this one after Tease because I was already half in love with Millie. And I needed to know she would find a hero who was good enough for her.Zach was everything she needed."Everything that came to mind was unspeakable. Sit on my face. Put your hand in my pants. Let me rub the tip of my cock on that little dimple that appears in your cheek when you smile."Millie is a wedding planner, and she’s really good at her job. She’s also really good at "fixing" her boyfriends so they can go on and marry someone else. Always the wedding planner, never the bride. And it may not look that way, but she does mind it.Bear in mind that Millie is unapologetically curvy, and I love that. As a curvy girl myself, I love to see this—especially when it’s not self-deprecating."Because it’s been a long time since a woman took my breath away."On a dare from her sister, she goes down to the bar of the hotel where she’s stranded for the night. Unsurprisingly, she’s hit on—by a total douche. Until Zach comes to the rescue. She doesn’t need him to, mind you, but he does it anyway.Because he’s been watching her from across the bar, and he’s not willing to let her go that easily."If I go up to your room, does that mean you’re going to keep your hands to yourself?""No. My hands will be all over your body. My hands, my mouth, and my cock."I mean, what’s one night? One night of indulging with a stranger they’ll probably never see again. OK, he’s older than she is, but does it matter when the attraction is this strong? Let me answer that—nope! And what a night they have!When the morning comes, though, she needs to leave. And you know that they want more."I know it’s wrong, but I can’t leave without kissing you."Fast forward to a rehearsal dinner a week later, with Millie in full-on wedding planning mode. For the wedding of her ex-boyfriend, no less, who introduces her to his long-lost dad. Yep, it’s Zach.And it may be all sorts of wrong for them to be together, but then why does it feel so damn right?"Every night we spent together only brought us closer. Every kiss made it harder to part. Every goodbye was an inevitable reminder that we had no future."They’re unable to let go. Of course it’s physical, because the want between them is scorching, but there’s something else there. The need to belong to someone. The need to know you’re wanted and cared for. The need to be home.So they lie."I’m not sure I’d have deserved you in any life, but I sure as hell would have tried."They make up the time to be together—unofficially, of course—all the while pretending this is all about sex. Except when they start missing each other. And then it’s suddenly so much more.I loved the push and pull. I loved that there was no added drama—it’s just two people who are perfect for each other but who have a lot of baggage to unpack."You’re my north, south, east, and west, Millie Rose. You ground me, you guide me, you inspire me. And I’m madly in love with you."It’s not just Zach’s son. It’s what they plan to do with their lives. It’s not knowing if their plans would fit, but that they most likely won’t. She wants a family. He doesn’t. They both think it’s bad to be sneaking around, but they need to realize why it’s bad.It’s because they want to show how much they love each other."Home to me now is wherever you are, and no matter where I go, I will always make my way back to you."In the meantime, there’s his job. There’s also the new job she found and the new career she’s embracing. There’s her sisters and their advice, and also Zach’s boss’ advice (loved him, by the way).The one thing everyone agrees on? When it’s right, you go for it."When he held me, I was home."I loved how unique their happily ever after was. Like all the puzzle pieces falling into place. And that bonus epilogue? Man, I feel so single.Love really is the biggest plot twist. And sometimes you have to sit back and enjoy the ride.

Do you ever watch a movie trailor and decide that even though the trailor was entertaining, you’ve seen enough and don’t need to spend two hours getting the extra details to fill in the blanks? Well, that’s how I felt about this book —— I would have been content just watching the trailor.The story was fine - very predictable and kind of exactly what I expected it to be… but nothing more. I wasn’t really connecting with either of the characters, but was into them just enough to keep reading. I think my issue was that I didn’t care in the slightest about anything that didn’t involve Millie and Zach interacting with each other — and there was a handful of other side story stuff going on… so I was bored pretty often.I also felt like a lot of the story was repetitive - something happens, then one (or both) of them talks about it to their friend and/or sibling, and then they might re-think about the moment or even talk about it again with each other… it made me want to skim over so much of the book because I didn’t care enough to hear a scene I already saw be repeated again via girl talk or bro bonding moments.I think the story in itself was fine for what it was and deserves a three stars for being a very easy/basic ex boyfriends father romance with a good amount of spice (I’m trying to be nice here… 🥴) — but on a personal level based off my actual in the moment enjoyment… it was more like a two stars because of how much I felt like skimming and how mehh I was about the whole thing. Buttt I’m giving it a three because I don’t think it technically sucked enough to deserve a two.

The one thing that I really like about this author is that she gives you all the feels when it comes to small town romance. You’re going to get swept up in the quaint little town, the loving people, and the thrill of finding that new kind of love and witnessing every moment from start to finish. This is what she brings to every book and this story was no different.Millie Rose has always had big dreams of doing something on her own eventually but being a wedding planner for her family’s business is more than enough for her right now. She’s single, happy with her family life, and is enjoying being in the present. But one night at a hotel bar changes everything and decides to go out of her comfort zone, Millie embarks on something that she swore she would never do. When that moment resurfaces a few weeks later and makes things in her personal life more complicated, she has to decide whether if some things are worth fighting for.I don't want to go too deep in the premise of the story but I will say that I really liked that this was a good forbidden romance. It took it’s time in the important parts and the characters actually spent a lot of time getting to know each other and building something good even though it was done in private the majority of the time. Despite a few bumps in the road that threw their romance off track for a little bit, there was still something that was genuine and deep with these characters and you felt their love. It was slow burn, hypnotic and it made you want to be a part of the family.If you’re looking for a small town eyebrow raising romance, then this one’s for you.

***no rating***I loved this. Until I didn’t anymore.Reading this book at first just made me feel good and super cozy. I loved the writing, the storyline, the characters and overall the whole setting. Not to mention that the mmc is a broad-shouldered,filthy hot alpha malewith tattoos, ex-Navy SEAL and a gentleman.Thecurvy bombshellMillie that is our fmc is the wedding planner at Cloverleigh Farms. Turns out that Zach, the super hot one-night-stand she had a month ago in New York, is the groom’s father at this weekend’s wedding and 15 yrs her senior. Oh yeah and… said groom is… her ex-boyfriend. So you see we havesome pretty hot topicshere. There was no way I was not tempted to read this with a premise like that.But sadly, there was one thing that bugged the hell out of me. It always does.“Wait,” she said between loud, panting breaths. “Do you have a condom?”“No. But I’ve had a vasectomy.” […] I eased inside her, and we both groaned.Uhm yeah… you guys know STDs are a thing, right?Sadly this isone of the very few things that throw me off (and turn me off) so hardthat I always contemplate if I can continue with that particular book at all. Imo romance authors have the responsibility that their characters stay safe when having sex - for the reader’s sake. I tried getting back into it after that because I liked this story very much but it was a struggle and I guess I failed.I meanthese guys were practically strangers to each otherat that point and for me this is the kind of irresponsible behavior that lets me see the mcs, who I’m supposed to love, in a bad light(same as when they would drive drunkenly or smoke around children). An absolute no-go for me.side note:don’t get me wrong. I find nothing hotter in romance than the couple (or whatever) ditching the condoms - but know they are safe in every way, not only regarding unwanted pregnancies.The book was still good, objectively speaking, but after that I wasn’t feeling it anymore.This is the latest release in the series and my first Melanie Harlow book - I really hope this is not her usual MO because then, sadly, this author isn’t for me - I hope it’s not because I’d love to give this author another chance one day.

*Thank you to the Author and Valentine PR for an ARC of this book.*I have been reading Melanie Harlow romances for 3 plus years now and I can honestly say, this is my favorite one. Not only do I feel a kinship with Millie for where she is in her life but I also have a thing for a salt and pepper hero. lols! In all seriousness, I took such joy in reading this story and the romance between Millie and Zach was so beautiful.I kind of had a certain song by Journey going through my head at the beginning of this book. Two strangers meeting in a bar and having a night to remember... then they go their separate ways and don't think they will ever see each other again. They try to keep the memories close and can't help but fantasize about how good it was but they know it will just have to stay that way.But lucky for them, and for us, they are fated to meet again. Only this time... it's the day before Zach's son's wedding. The wedding that Millie planned for him. And oh yeah, she's also his ex-girlfriend. Talk about an awkward but thrilling re-connection. It's the worst time and the worst way to meet again. But when fate gives you a second chance... how can you resist?They are at very different places in their lives. Millie is 32, hoping to start a family and taking on her dream business. Zach is 47 and firmly a bachelor. But they just can't stay away. Their passion and even more so, their hearts, are too involved. Also, Zach is totally blown over by Millie's body and her confidence and is just obsessed with her from first sight. *sigh*Of course this has some good steam and we do start out real strong with the One Night Stand action. Zach and Millie have blistering chemistry and they struggle to keep their hands off each other. However, the intimacy and genuine friendship they built through their conversations was what really stole my heart. I just ached to have them work it out.I just genuinely loved these two SO much. This romance felt more mature and realistic than many I have read this year. Yeah, it's fantastical how they meet and have no clue how they are connected. But their issues and roadblocks are SO real. The conflict has a serious potential to hurt people and I was nervous about how it would play out.I always struggle with 3rd acts in romance. I'm sure authors struggle to write them too, so I understand, but when I love a book like this I start to get that really tight gut as I wait for things to blow up. I am happy to say, this HEA was so beautiful and romantic. As I said at the top, this will be a Melanie Harlow favorite of mine for years to come.5 stars3 on the spice scaleCW: talk of loss of a young sibling

A swoony, spicy small town romance with all the forbidden feels! Tempt had me utterly addicted and staying up way past my bedtime for more of Millie and Zach. A must read for all small town romance fans.

I'm just gonna get right to the chase about what I didn't like:- it was repetitive- issues were solved to easily when they were huge issues that needed more depth to it- all the talk about Millie getting a new job was boring me to death- I hated that a big part of Millies pov was about her weight and her being curvy. I wished that it showed more of her being okay with being plus sized and not the stereotypical being obsessed with her weight, having people comment on it in the past, being shamed for being plus sized like it's a bad thing, and the mom getting onto her about it- the lack of depth of the characters- Zach caring about his age. Dilfs are hot so get on board buddy- to many side things were happening that I didn't give a fuck about- just didn't care about them- hated that it had a ton of tropes I liked but I didn't like the book- didn't believe their chemistry- they fucked more than they had actual conversations
Welp never mind⋆. 𐙚 ˚ Pre Read ⋆. 𐙚 ˚I’m battling this book slump like a toddler refusing nap time—kicking, screaming, and all.

This HAS to be the fastest burn by MH ever…By 9%… 7% when it all begins… I was speechless. MH… girl… I was NOT ready… Jeeezus! There’s no FTB here!! We get every nitty gritty duuuuurtay detail, and I couldn’t get enough 😈.Millie’s (32) at a wedding expo when she decides to unwind by going to a bar and right away notices an older, handsome man, Zach (47) as she walks in. A different man hits on her and he’s not taking the hint; luckily for her, the older man pretends to be her date. They talk and decide to go back to his room to get to “know” each other a little more…Oh… do they ever 😈🥵💦🔥.They leave on good terms, understanding that this was an amazing ONS.And then they see each other again the following month- for Zach’s son’s wedding, who also happens to be Millie’s ex 😅😬🫣.What I liked/loved: possible spoilers—I won’t put 🔥 first (see, I can be a lady 😅sometimes 🤣). The lack of cringing I did. Theoretically, it would seem like I should be 😬 left and right since Millie was in a relationship with Zach’s son. Buuuut… Zach only met his son, Mason, months before. Mason’s mom never told him who his real father was. So Zach’s still adjusting to finding out he has an adult son. I feel like this helped their situation 🤷🏻‍♀️. Plus, Mason’s getting married and Millie’s helping with their wedding. It’s a win win for me 😅—well or course I’m gonna say the 🔥. Oh lawd. Melanie Harlow really said “not enough 🔥 in my books? Hold my martini” and proved us all wrong. Because, these two bring the STEAM in STEAMY… the H in HOTTTTT, the D in DUUUURTAY… the… okay, you get it😅🤣. These two are insatiable 😅. Like, literally lol. I loooove all the times they just breathe the same air and just… A T T A C K each other🔥😅💦. They don’t come to play 💅. It makes the tension so delicious 🤤. And Zach, 😮‍💨, your skills are 🥵. Millie’s a lucky lady—the side characters. I was so happy to see the MCs from the previous books 🥰. I love this entire series. I was silently fan girling lol—the depth. Both Millie and Zach have some baggage, enough to bring in the right type of tension. Sure, they can turn a blind eye to what’s happening, but there are still problems they need to overcome—the sneaking around 😈. These two are stealthier than #orn¥ teenagers 🤣🤣🤣… and they’re SOOOO addicted to each other 😉—the plus size aspect. I’ve done my fair share of plus size FMCs and I loved the way it was portrayed here. As a bigger girl, I appreciated the attention to detail when it comes to the frustration bigger girls feel when shopping (even for a wedding dress). But, I also liked that it wasn’t just about plus sized girls, but inclusivity in general. (Spoiler: Millie opening up a business isn’t only for plus sized women- it’ll end up including all sizes)—on that note, I loved how O B S E S S E D Zach is with Millie. With her curves, with her mind, with her taste 😉😮‍💨🔥—the epilogues. MH does some of the best ones. The extended epilogue on her website is just so perfect 🥹❤️What I wanted more of:—more angst. There was definitely opportunities for more angst. Don’t get me wrong; I like it when it’s angst-free (or low enough to not make me shed tears) as well, but this would’ve felt more complete if it had more of it. Is there angst? Yes, but the potential for more was there. It didn’t deter me from feeling swoony by the end—more of their “after.” We get an epilogue at the end of the book that takes place months later and then the extended bonus one is years later… but I wanted more 🤣🤣🤣 (I’ll never forget the one where they had been married many many many decades and I was bawling by the end lol)Overall, I loved it! 🥰⚠️safety squad⚠️spoilers ahead-no cheating/sharing/owd-omd- the h dated the H’s son… 😅😬 the OM is getting married, but still… 🫣-praise kink/spanking/phone smex/role playing (bonus epilogue)-H hasn’t had sex in over a year/it’s been a while for the h as well-divorced H-condoms/H has had a vasectomy 😒/then no condoms-HEA/epilogues (months later)/bonus epilogue years later (with some surprises 🥹❤️ahem… BABIES!)

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️''Doing the right thing mattered to me. But in that moment, she mattered more''Safety warnings:- H 47 / h 32- No cheating- No OW/OM drama- Loss of loved one- HEASPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️We finally get Millie's story!This story involves Millie who is a wedding planner currently planning her ex-bf's wedding to a girl he was introduced to by Millie. To be honest I thought the ex-bf would be an absolute jerk to Millie or have some horrible actions made towards her, so it was really refreshing to see someone who was kind and was truly thankful for her help and for being introduced to the woman he was marrying.However, that same ex-bf has been looking for his biological father (Zach) who just happens to have had NO idea he had a 28-year-old son and also happens to be the man Millie has a ONS with while she was in NYC.They both cannot stop thinking about each other after that one night and they are separated for about a month. He gave her his business card, but she never used it. You could tell how much that one night had affected them.Imagine the absolute shock and horror when both of our MMC's see each other at the rehearsal dinner. Unable to admit their true connection. I was horrified for them both when they blabbed each other’s name in front of everyone and were grilled about how they knew each other. Obviously they gave the G rated version of the night, but I swear you could hear the panic through the pages.They cannot stand to be apart and their attractive is so prominent. They sneak around and basically have a secret affair, meeting in different cities for a few nights and lying to Mason (his son). Both in fear of what he would think, what people would say and also the massive elephant in the room about the fact Zach does not want to get married again or ever want to have a child. All these things are more reasons why they cannot work and despite telling each other over and over again that it's wrong and they should move on they cannot.I understood their fear and I also understood why Zach was how he was. He never wanted any of these things, but I think he never found anyone that was worth wanting these things with. I'm glad he realised she was it and he wanted to give her everything she deserved and despite the fallout with Mason told him the truth (albeit Mason knew lol he saw them briefly). Also, I had to applaud Mason for never holding a grudge about their relationship - yes he was hurt but it was only because he wanted the truth and was lied to.Another thing was Zach was truly freaking about being someone’s father, it was overwhelming for him since he never imagined it and now he was also going to be a grandpa??One thing I really appreciated was how there was no big dramatic fight scene just for the sake of it. They both truly knew it would be near impossible for their relationship to work if the things that stopped it would forever haunt them. Millie never lashed out or made any childish decisions < so refreshing.Millie was fantastic, she was ambitious, and I loved how caring she was. Zach was the ultimate alpha male - he had a dirty mouth on him that made me blush a few times lolThe proposal scene was really on brand with Millie tbh. It worked so well with her fashion show!PLEASE READ THE EXTRA SCENE! We get to see them around 5 years or into the future. They are married and have two children.

I was expecting forbidden, age gap romance with full drama. To my disappointment, Tempt is confusing. There is not much forbidden romance. Only mild one between Mason and Zach.The attraction between Zach and Millie is... confusing. What is exactly the attraction here? Aside from hot sex. Miss Harlow creates 2 characters that grow apart in her story without real emotional connection.What I like about this book is the sweet, tender moments. I like Zach tenderness toward Millie. I like the sweet dialog between them.3 stars

UTTERLY OBSESSED. Tempt is devastatingly romantic, powerfully emotive, and epically swoony- a pitch perfect love story from start to finish. I devoured this in one deliciously consuming sitting, mesmerized by every word and moment. I simply ADORED this story. It's more than tempting- it's unforgettable and one of my favorite all time Melanie Harlow stories. I was totally swept away.Millie and Zach are EVERYTHING. I could not adore these two more. What starts as an intensely physical connection blossoms into something neither anticipated- and the 15 year age gap isn’t the only forbidden aspect of their tryst. But their chemistry is undeniable and neither can resist- both torn between wanting to give into the obstacles that keep them apart but finding that the urge to give into their connection is far more powerful.Zach- holy crap. He’s a swoony alpha- a mean with emotional depth and a complicated vulnerability, but he’s also in control and demanding and such a dirty talker. He’s like this growly protective cinnamon roll. He exudes edge and dominant charisma, but he’s also really tender and thoughtful. What a transfixing and magnetic combination- sensitivity and alpha. And Millie- oh Millie. She’s my favorite of all Harlow women- her authenticity, her relatability, her fierceness and her gentleness. She’s complex, she’s thoughtful, she’s strong. But she’s also longing for something, questioning her lovability (not because of her size!), struggling to connect her desires with her reality. I mean wow, have I ever related to anything more? The raw emotion that comes from this character is staggering, beautiful, transformative even. Like my own heart was pouring out on the page.Melanie so perfectly harmonized heat and heart in this story. She has such a keen ability to translate real human experiences into her characters while also giving them a specificity, a uniqueness that individualizes them. I really loved the growth we saw in both of these characters- how each confronts their own baggage and fears. Both have a tendency to push others way, to prioritize others instead of themselves. Watching these two figure out their own issues because they’ve fallen into each other, providing a safe space to live into their dreams, to grapple with their demons, to find a way to being more THEMSELVES- well, it was magic on the page. Emotionally rich, sweetly heartfelt, perfectly told. I adored their chemistry- and JAYSUS THE HEAT! THis book is so spicy, I could not get enough!And, while I certainly don’t purport to speak for all curvy gals, this curvy gal feels that Melanie wrote a plus-sized character in such an empowering and respectful way. Millie is sexual and confident, and outside of her aspirations to create a more empowering bridal experience for other curvy women, her story doesn't’ center on her size or insecurities. This story screams body positivity- and I found myself loving my own curves more with every passing page. And I mean HOLY MOLY, the way this man worships her physically and emotionally! Millie is his goddess, and my heart wanted to explode from how tenderly this man cared for her.I am OBSESSED with Melanie’s worlds, and I loved that we got so much content with my other favorite characters (Dex and Winne from ignite). But Millie and Zach, I’m not sure anyone can compare. I fell so madly in love with them- their journey is rewarding, delicious, and so very dreamy. I will not soon forget this magical story.

I stopped reading the Cloverleigh Farms series after book 4.Nothing wrong it the series…I just got distracted by other books and said books just kept getting pushed back into my TBR (You know how it is!) BUT when I saw the words Age-Gap, Forbidden, Small Town romance..let me tell you, I ditched my currently read and jumped right into Tempt. And it was all worth it!Tempt is sexy, really endearing at times, the chemistry between Millie and Zach is freaking FIRE! The heartfelt scenes are beautiful. But in all honesty? Millie and Zach’s chemistry just kills it for me. It’s so passionate!I love a good WELL WRITTEN age gap, forbidden romance and Melanie Harlow gave us such a good one.Grab this one right now!

3.5⭑This is my fourth book by Melanie Harlow and so far none have blown me away. I think they are enjoyable but not the best romances I’ve ever read.I enjoyed the somewhat slow burn this book had. Both main characters have a one night stand and then continue meeting up ending up in a friends with benefits situation.It was fun, fluffy and spicy. I still recommend it bc I know I’m picky with my ratings and other people would probs enjoy it much more than me lol.

I can't seem to get enough of this series and the excellent couple pairings this author makes!I love a good age gap trope from time to time, this one worked like a charm despite the potential "ick" factor —for me— considering the female character ends up having a one night stand with her ex's dad. Myeah. Not my fave premiseBUTthere's a twist that made it not so much tabooand a delight to read.Lovely family dynamics, great banter, plus size representation, PLENTY hot sex but also very sweet moments and heart to heart talks between Millie and Zach. Their chemistry on page is really well done, I lived for the "we should stop this is wrong but it feels so good" moments! How despite their determination to do the "right thing" Millie and Zach were no match for their impulse to be together. The many "this can't happen again" situations made me smile throughout. I felt this was relatively low angst despite the forbidden premise.It's an effective mix of one night stand, love at first sight, missed connection, slow burn, secret romance and age gap set mostly in a small town.I loved the little bits of epistolary romance, I'm a sucker for emails/texts/letters in romance stories, Melanie Harlow spices it up even more with some playful phone sex. Bonus points for the "good girl" praise and "only a bad girl for you" in their bedroom scenes and the "come sit on my face or else" moment.Extra bonus points for MATURE characters. I loved Millie she was amazing all throughout, no drama, not living in her head. She truly was the perfect pairing for Zach. Sigh, I just love a mature—take charge male lead. Low key obsessed with them both.I found myself smiling like an idiot reading the epilogue. My resting bitch face felt like a fraud.This is currently available through KU :)More reviews and book talk at :You can find me here too ☞
adults who actually act their age. Actual mature grown ups who put in effort and try to make relationships work. 🤌🤌🤌🤌

4.5 Stars!(ARC provided by author)Millie MacAllister lives in a small town in northern Michigan where she works as a wedding/event planner at Cloverleigh Farms. She practically grew up at Cloverleigh, and it felt like home to her. At thirty two, she was the oldest of five sisters. Her sisters Winnie and Felicity were both in love, but Millie hadn't found her person yet. Lately she'd been feeling a little unlucky, like nothing was going her way. Then the stars align when she meets a gorgeous, mysterious stranger on a trip, and has a sexy, unforgettable one night stand. It's just the spark of excitement she's needed. To flirt and share such a powerful attraction was exhilarating. They only spent a few hours together, but she hasn't felt this way in a long time. She doesn't think their paths will ever cross again, but fate has other plans.Zach Barrett was a former Navy SEAL that works in private security out of San Diego, California. He recently found out he had a grown son he never knew existed. His son is getting married soon, and he's meeting him for the first time at the rehearsal dinner. He's nervous and then things turn even more complicated when he locks eyes across the room with the blonde bombshell that's been starring in all his fantasies. There was just something about her he couldn't get enough of. Since their night together, he couldn't quit thinking about her. Now he learns not only is Millie planning his son's wedding, but she's also his ex-girlfriend. He's divorced and fifteen years older than her, and the situation is a disaster, but he still wants her. She had an hourglass figure with stunning curves that drove him wild. Could he stay away from her, or will she be a temptation too hard to resist?Tempt by Melanie Harlow is the fourth book in the Cloverleigh Farms Next Generation series. It's a steamy standalone, small town, age gap, forbidden romance full of passion, humor and explosive chemistry. This couple's connection was pure fire and I thought this story was super hot! I just loved Zach & Millie together! I adore this series and need more from this world! <333***NOW AVAILABLE***https://amzn.to/3gw8n7H

Ummm.. I just finished Melanie Harlow'sTemptand am basically obsessed! Clearly, Harlow MUST be psychic because how she knew that Zach Barrett would check all my Alpha Heartthrob boxes and rock my world, Heaven only knows!!! But ladies and Gentlemen...Temptwill give you allll the swoons and feels and will skyrocket to your 2022 best of lists the minute you finish reading it! THIS BOOK IS FIREEEEE!!!!Ok so first things first: Millie is basically my new bestfriend and no one else can have her. You guys!!! This story was SO real and raw, and Harlow did the damn thing with the amazing Millie MacAllister. She was just pure perfection. She was curvy and proud of it. She loved her body, her family, her career and was just looking for that special Dream Man that has eluded her. Enter Zach Barrett. GIRLSSSS. He gives GREATTTT growl. Lord. You will basically pass out. I just can't deal with how much I loveee him. This book is AMAZING! God.. I may even love him more than Jack Valentini. What is this life?! Sigh.READ THIS BOOK, PEOPLE!!! It has HOT passion, fantastic banter and an amazing age-gap deliciousness. You will swoon allll the swoons, die allll the deaths and basically come back wanting more! Millie and Zach are EVERYTHING! 5 stars! ~Ratula ❤️
4.75⭐️ THIS BOOK WAS SO GOOD!! The spice, plot, characters, it was all perfect. I love this series so much. 😭💛

3.5 “Sometimes our hearts don’t communicate with our heads.”I enjoyed this one overall. I sort of had a hard time buying into the fact that an engaged to someone else, 29 year old man would be so weird about his dad dating his ex girlfriend that they’d feel the need to keep it secret for months and months, but also I’ve never been a 29 year old man whose dad was canoodling with his ex, so what do I really know.That said. I enjoyed Millie and Zach. An age gap and two people falling in love despite the fact that they weren’t planning on it with each other. The sneaking around was fun even though they constantly felt guilty for it. I kind of wish that they hadn’t and had just been keeping it secret because they didn’t plan to peruse it and wanted it for them. ANYWAY.Good read if you want ex boyfriend’s dad, age gap, secret relationship, and sort of fling romance all in a small town.

book.title: Irresistible
book.author: Melanie Harlow
book.description: I’m a full-time single dad to three daughters and CFO at Cloverleigh Farms. I don’t have time to fall in love—I’m too busy trying to run a business, keep the red socks out of the white laundry, and get the damn pillowcases on without owing a dollar to the swear jar.Sure, Frannie Sawyer is beautiful and sweet, but she’s twenty-seven, the boss’s daughter, and my new part-time nanny—which means she’s completely off-limits. It’s bad enough I can’t stop fantasizing about her, what kind of jerk would I be if I acted on the impulse to kiss her?(Exactly the kind of jerk you’re thinking.)Actually, I’m worse than that—because I didn’t stop with a kiss, and now I can’t stay away.  She makes me feel like myself again. She reminds me what it’s like to want something just for me. She’s everything I ever needed, but nothing I ever imagined.I’m a former Marine. I should have had the strength to resist her from the start.But I didn’t. And now I have to choose between the life I want and the life she deserves.Even if it means giving her up.

reviews.text: I am hosting a PAPERBACK GIVEAWAY now up on my blog! Clickhereto enter! ;)A sweet, single father romance who resists to admit that being with the one true love of his life will not be a regret for him, nor his family is what Melanie Harlow has created in this masterpiece of a novel...There is love in the air y'all! The kind oflove that makes you swoon, and you end up having butterflies in your stomach because of all the passion there isbetween two characters such as Mack and Frannie.Melanie Harlowhas been an author that I took a break from reading for a while, but now that I am back to reading her books, that same taste from before that includes and oozes sweetness and sexiness all in one package is exactly what I have been missing. This story was yet again,another compelling romance that Melanie is bringing to her readers. The way everything was described just warmed my heart in the best of ways.And of course, anyone who knows me, knows that I can never back away from a single father romance. This beauty was no exception, especially when an emotionally broken single father and his three little girls are involved.Mack and Frannie both work for the Cloverleigh Farms, which is a family owned type of hotel that oozes that sweet and relaxing ambience to it. It is owned byFrannie's parents, and is ruined by her family. Coming from the youngest of four, Frannie understands what it's like to be part of a big family. She has struggled at a young age from heart problems, so her parentshave always been over-protective of her. When her passion of cooking arises, she knows that having an independence of her is a struggle because of her parents. Mack is added to the equation because he works for the family business as the CFO of the company. After the mother of his three little princesses left him to take all the responsibility of them, he has found in a place where is asks for help basically anywhere. Frannie is always happy to help him out, because the crush he has for him is one that has been there from the moment she first met him. FromMack's point of view, Frannie is way too young for him to indulge anything with her. Having that ten year difference is something that I find rather forbiddingly good and satisfying to alwaysread.The relationship his three little kids have with Frannie is one that is utterly adorable!You know how when you come across those cute kids in romance novels and you question "why can't all kids be this way?"Yeah well, that was me reading this novel every time Mack's little girls where mentioned. Everything about them was just beyond charming and so sweet to read. Especially because Frannie has such an understand relationship with them, and with Mack as well. After one stormy and snow night in, Frannie has unintentionally brought herself to stay over at Mack's house after one babysitting night. The temptation is there for Mack.The attraction he has towards this petite, yet curvy young woman is what brings him to his knees to want her more and more.One he has a taste of her however, there is no back away. Though in his mind, he does not seem like the appropriate man to be hers for the rest of her life. He already has three little girls and a full time job that takes so much time of his daily activity, but with Frannie, he learns that having her help makes all the stress come to a free zone. The idea of her leaving is something that never sits well with him;"I watched her leave, feeling that ache in my chest again, and I wished there was a way I could've asked her to stay. I was tired of being careful, tired of missing her at night, tired of feeling bad for wanting her, tired of feeling like I was one person with her, and a different person with the kids. Neither version of me was complete.I wanted to be both at once. Wast it possible?"Overall this was truly a romance novel that shows so many affections towards each individual characters. Not just the important protagonists, but alsothe characters that are related to them. The love that oozes from this novel from all the families what something sweet and caring to read.Melanie has brought yet again, another that conquers the power of protection, love, and care for one another. Obviously this is the first novel in a series she is planning on releasing so you already know that my anticipation for the next novel is already up there. Thank you to SBPR and Melanie for providing a copy of this beauty, that I know all of you will truly enjoy and not regret reading.ARC kindly provided by the publicist, in exchange for an honest review...Follow Me On:Blog♕Instagram♕Facebook♕Twitter

3 StarsOverall Opinion:I think this book was one of the final straws that broke this camel's back. I've been in a massive book funk. Nothing has really grabbed me. Nothing has made me uber excited to read. And I'm over it!I apologize now if the following rant is too harsh. I'm sure many other readers will love this book, and my opinion could very well be because of my funk. That being said, here is a spoiler tag if you just want to skip my ranting:What I really want is for authors to think outside the box and stop giving us such formulaic storylines. I feel like there is a guideline out there that a lot of authors are simply following instead of providing us with their creative juices. While I do appreciate the predictable and fluffy read occasionally, I feel like it sometimes is an insult to my intelligence as a reader when a book doesn't seem that well thought out. I apologize, and again, I want to say that Ms. Harlow is getting the (probably unfair) brunt of my frustration as she is not alone in this.Now on to the book specific rant...The dramatic elements in the book were nowhere near fleshed out and addressed properly IMO. I feel like there were certain aspects that were added for the sake of drama but were either never addressed again or were dropped by the end without proper closure.Here are some specific examples:-The H worked for the h's dad and he was worried about how he would react.There was one scene where the h's dad told the H that he didn't have a son and would like to spend more time with him (during the H's separation with the h) but that was it. We didn't get any reaction from the father about them being together except this vague and somewhat confusing scene.-The H's mom deduced that something was going on with the h and H and was against the H pursuing the h and didn't hesitate in telling him so.This was never again addressed after the scene with the phone call.-The H's ex also deduced that something was going on with the h and H and threatened to never see their girls again if he was with the h.This too was never addressed after the scene with the phone call, and she wasn't even really mentioned again after this. She is the H's children's mother! I felt strongly that we needed some type of closure with her.-The connection between them felt underdeveloped.I understood that they were crushing on each other for a while since they work together. I also understood that those feelings were supposed to have been laying dormant when the story begins, but I don't understand how they jumped from crushes and lust to love. It seemed like a lot of lust and the h stepping in to help with his kids but not much substance to their interactions otherwise. The H literally decides (in inner dialogue) that he loves the h after she gives him a stellar bj!-The H was adamant that he did not want to get married or have other children.He was so adamant that he ended things with the h because he knew she wanted these things. I get that people can change their minds. I get that he was supposed to be scared and maybe that led to him saying and believing such things. But what I don't get is why the sudden 180? Because he missed her and his children told him they knew they loved each other? I simply didn't find it all that believable because we were presented with this single dad character that was spread too thin and often struggled with his self confidence in giving his children what they needed. This might also be because we don't getanytime at all between their reconciliation after their separation and the epilogue to process how his life and views changed.I think what my silly rant is about is that I want authors to respect that we do think about things when we read. Yes, sometimes reading is about mindless escape where a fluffy/low drama book like this one might be just what we need. But! I have had what seems to bewaytoo many 3 star reads lately where I just feel meh about it all. I would like to actively think while reading a book, and get the dramatic elements properly addressed and played out with an ending with some actual closure where I feel that "ahhh" moment with a smile on my face when I finish a book!Okay, sorry about that again. For a low stress/fluffy/sexy read about a single dad with some cute little girls and sweet interactions with the h, this might be your book. For anything deep and meaningful, I sadly think it missed the mark but had the potential to bemore. I'm going with middle of the road "just okay" 3 stars for my rating.Brief Summary of the Storyline:This is Franny and Mack's story. They work together and Franny takes care of Mack's daughters part-time. When his full time caregiver breaks her leg, Franny steps up to help the single dad out. After being thrown together and some sexually tense situations, they give in to their attraction and start a fling. Mack is focused on his girls since his ex is worthless and that makes him unable to give 100% to Franny and a relationship. There is some family and personal drama, some hot sexy times, and some great scenes with Mack's girls...and they get a HFN ending.POV:This alternated between Franny and Mack's POV.Overall Pace of Story:Good until the end. I never skimmed and I thought it flowed well otherwise.Instalove:Kind of. I didn't really understand their progression into love (see above). They had been crushing on each other when the story begins and had some feelings that they built upon.H rating:3 stars. Mack. I liked him, but then he would be too wishy washy for me. I felt like he had such a huge turn around by the end that I wasn't really sure who he really was supposed to be (see above).h rating:4 stars. Franny. I liked her. I appreciated how she wanted to care for the H and his girls and how she was sweet and understanding.Sadness level:Low, no tissues neededPush/Pull:YesThe H pushes away and I'd probably say that the h is the main pursuer of the relationshipHeat level:Hot. They have some hot tension, chemistry, and scenes -- but not so much it takes away from the story.Descriptive sex:YesOW/OM drama:NoThe H does deal with drama from his ex wife, but it was in regards to his children and not about his relationship with the hSex scene with OW or OM:NoCheating:NoSeparation:YesThey are separated shortly (days?), but there was no mention or suggestion of other partners during this time.Possible Triggers:YesThe H is ~10 years older than the h (37/27) and the H is divorced with 3 children from his marriage.Closure:My absolute biggest issue with the book. Not enough closure at all (see rant above)! I would classify this as HFN but others might be fine with it all and call it a HEAthey get engaged in the epilogueSafety:This one is probablySafe with exceptionfor most safety gang readers depending on personal preferences.-Does Nothave cheating-Does Nothave a descriptive sex scene with OW/OM-Doeshave the H pushing the h away-Doeshave a separation between the h and H-Does Nothave abuse-Does Nothave a HEA ending (see above)

*****FIVE STARS*****{ARC Generously Provided by Author}For a moment, I let myself imagine what it would be like if I were younger and didn’t have kids. If I were free to pursue her. Win her. Keep her. Christ, I’d fucking spoil her rotten all the time. She was the perfect combination of sexy and sweet—it drove me crazy in the best possible way. In another life, I’d have done everything possible to make her mine.IRRESISTIBLEis an incredibly entertaining sexy and sweet romance. For those of you who love romances with a single father, you are sure to enjoy Mack and Frannie’s story. I could not get enough of this book and I can’t wait to see what Melanie Harlow has in store for the remaining Sawyer sisters. When I end up loving both the Hero and Heroine equally I know that I’m reading an exceptionally good story. The author makes you fall for Mack and Frannie almost immediately. I felt a connection to them both and was waiting on pins and needles to get to their HEA! And I have to give honorable mention to Mack’s three little girls. They were the most adorable and precocious characters who added to the magic of this heartfelt romance! Great story. Terrific characters. Beautiful book cover! Bravo to Melanie Harlow!Here are my overall ratings:Hero:5Heroine:5Plot:5Angst:4.5Steam:5Chemistry Between Hero & Heroine:5IRRESISTIBLEreleases on February 25th! Make sure you bump this one way up your TBRs!!Amazon Paperback:https://amzn.to/2X8ROAj

’IRRESISTIBLE’is the first full length romance novel in Melanie Harlow’s ‘CloverLeigh Farms' series. In this we meet Frannie Sawyer & Declan McAllister aka Mack.What a pathetic fucking cliche I was: Divorced Dad lusting after the Babysitter. As if a girl like her wanted anything to do with a guy like me-one with three kids and an ex-wife.Frannie is a twenty-seven year old female, her family owns Cloverleigh Farms, where she’s part time receptionist, and bakes delectable macarons, and a nanny for Mack. Being the youngest of four siblings (Sylvia, April, Meg, Chloe) she’s been babied most of her life with an overprotective mother due to being born with a heart defect which puts her at a slighter higher risk of having a heart attack. Due to this she missed out on a lot as a child, in and out of hospital, home schooled and missed out on a lot of things her siblings got up to so she feels at a disadvantage and not as close to her siblings as she would like.Thirty seven year old divorcée Mack is a full time single dad to three adorable daughters (eleven year old Millie, eight year old Felicity, and four year old Winifred) and works for the family business as the CFO of the company making Frannie’s father his boss.If there was a girl off limits, it was Frannie Sawyer.These two have skirted around their attraction for each other for a while, but things go to that next level when after babysitting the girls she's stranded due to a snow blizzard and stays the night, things between Mack and Frannie escalated heatedly.I'm no prince, Frannie. I'm stubborn, I'm impatient, I have a foul mouth and a lot of fucking baggage. And I come attached to three small humans who run me ragged every day.I didn't think I'd love this one as much as I did, going by the synopsis, it just sounded too much like all the other single dad falls for the nanny books out on the market but that's not the case with this one, and for that I am so glad!!I loved this simply for it's no frills simplicity, you could see the scenes unfolding in life, this was just a straight forward, pure romance which made me love this fully. This is a lesson some authors need to learn, you don't need the angst or drama to make a good old fashioned love story come alive, and this is where this author is in a class of her own. I connected with the story, I connected with the characters. The storyline flowed freely, the feels were boundless, the characters were believable and loveable, what wasn't there to love about this!! Highly recommend!!* Teasers used are off the authors facebook page *

Irresistiblewas a very quick read, perfect for a lazy Saturday morning. It contained a lot of my favorite tropes: single parents, age gap and a friends to lovers/forbidden romance. I loved the small town setting and the children. Oh, how I loved the kids, they were adorable!However, I felt like the middle dragged on a little and the hero was incredibly frustrating at times. I understood his doubts about himself and the relationship, but I didn't like how his decisions hurt the heroine so much. I also would've loved to have seen more of a development for the heroine since a part of her storyline was about breaking free from her parents coddling. And the whole 'forbidden' aspect of the relationship was grossly glossed over, making the story fall flat at the end.Rating: 3/5

4.25 ⭐️ me reading winnie’s book before her dad’s is honestly so iconic of me HAHABAHA but seeing her as a lil girl was so cute pls 🥹 anyway, I really enjoyed this! FRANNY IS AN ANGELLLLLL, I already knew that when I read ignite but this CONFIRMED it! Mack being a single dad to three girls sent me 😍 overall I loved the vibes & these books are so easy and fun to read. And they reminded me so much of Winnie & Dex!!!!“Because he loves you! He told us!” <3

I mean, this was quite average. I kept reading it for the sake of reading it and because it was easy. But I didn’t get much out of the story at all. It was cheesy and not in a tolerable way.I appreciated that we got to see a single dad and him raising 3 young girls and the struggles that come along with generally being a parent.But there wasn’t much to Frannie’s personality - or even to his - other than making things easy for him. Yes, I know she started her own business and whatnot but it felt contrived and not genuine?It just didn’t sit well with me. The story lacked depth.

Absolutely loved this one!!!! This author never disappoints for me! I think this one is my favorite to date, and I can not wait to read more from this series!!! Mack & Frannie were so fun and so very easy to fall in love with!!

Have you ever read a book that from the very first page you had an immediate good feeling? Deep down you know that this one is going to have minimal drama, lovable characters, a splash of forbidden, and a feel good relatable storyline that makes you enjoy being in the moment. I couldn’t have asked for a better romantic story and I’m so glad that I took a chance on this one.Being a single dad is not the easiest thing in the world but being a single dad to three girls under the age of thirteen? Someone needs to hand this man a medal. From hair braiding, to ballet class, to arguments over who's turn it is to pick a movie, Mack has seen and done it all for his girls. His entire lives purpose is to support and be there for his kids and everything else is going to take a back seat. Unfortunately that includes his love life. With temptation brewing in the form of his nanny and boss's daughter, will Mack be able to indulge with the woman he’s wanted for over a year?Frannie has always been sheltered throughout her entire childhood. Due to a scary heath condition, her parents has always treated her like she was made of glass and now that she’s a twenty seven year old woman, they still see her as breakable. Being the Nanny to three of most beautiful girls is one of the highlights of her life but having to see their Dad on a daily basis and not combust? Well that’s an everyday miracle that is quickly starting to lose it’s power. What will happen when these two decide to give in for one night only?I really loved the bond that Mack had with each of his daughters and it showed just how much he cared for all of them. He was protective, loving, vulnerable and hilariously animated. I couldn’t get enough of those girls and I loved that their personalities were strong forces in this story. It wasn’t easy at times and there was certainly a lot of screw ups, but every moment was a learning experience and they took it in stride.If you love a hot single dad romance , then I highly recommend this one.

This book has been so hyped for me and it has two of my favorite tropes: Taboo and forbidden. Everyone I know that has read this has loved it, so I went in thinking I would love it too!Frannie is our main character and since she has a heart condition that required surgery when she was little, her parents are overprotective. So overprotective that she was home schooled, not allowed to go to college, and still lives at home at 27. To me, this made Frannie so frustrating. Why did she never want to do anything? Go to college? Have a life? She literally didn't even have a single friend. But she had four older sisters living life and doing whatever they wanted? I get she had a heart condition, but her parents' obsessive overprotectiveness didn't make sense to me.With the romance, I didn't really feel the chemistry between Mack and Frannie. They supposedly couldn't keep their hands off of each other, but I didn't really feel that. Also, the dialogue was pretty cheesy and I felt like this book was just trying to use tropes and cliches and wasn't pulling them off. I was more bored than anything. And there weren't really any stakes with the forbidden/age gap aspect of this novel. Mack works for her father but in no way was the dad ever around to act as a conflict to their relationship, so it didn't really feel all that forbidden.Overall, this was just okay. I didn't hate it, but I definitely was never excited to keep reading. I felt pretty apathetic towards the characters and guess I was just expecting a different romance.

4 starsThis was a great single dad/nanny romance. I absolutely adored the kids and the part they played in the story. Frannie and Mack work together and Frannie plays part time nanny to Mack’s three daughters. They both like each other have denied their feelings for various reasons. Until they get stuck together overnight during a blizzard and things change between them. I enjoyed this story a lot, and I’m looking forward to reading more in this series!Audio book source: AudibleStory Rating: 4 starsNarrators: Teddy Hamilton & Savannah PeachwoodNarration Rating: 5 starsGenre: Contemporary RomanceLength: 7h 36m

4.5 Stars!(ARC provided by Social Butterfly PR)Mack is a former marine living in northern Michigan. He's a divorced full-time single dad to three daughters. His life is laundry, lunches, schedules, and trying to stay afloat without adding any more money to the swear jar. He's doing his best, but most days things are complete chaos, and he feels like he's hanging by a thread. The one place he feels somewhat in control is his job. As the CFO at Cloverleigh Farms he works hard to keep things running smoothly.Frannie Sawyer is the youngest of five sisters. Her family owns Cloverleigh Farms and she works as their receptionist. The highlight of her day is seeing Mack, the delicious single father who has worked for her family for the last five years. She's had a hopeless crush on him forever. After his wife left she became a part-time nanny to his kids. She admired his devotion to his girls, and thought it made him even more desirable. He was unbelievably hot, and she couldn't help how much she wanted him.Mack has spent the last nine months trying to survive being a parent on his own, and Frannie is always quick to help out. She's sweet, genuine, and gorgeous. She had an innocence about her, and had become his go-to fantasy. Lusting after the babysitter was his little secret. As the boss's daughter and his nanny, she was off-limits, but a guy could dream. He knew he could never act on his attraction, but then one kiss changes everything, and he can't stay away.It's an impossible, complicated situation. Mack's girls were his first priority. He was already stretched thin, without room for anything else. He was in no position to pursue anyone, and felt he had nothing to offer. He couldn't give his time, attention, or a future, and it wasn't fair to her, but he hadn't felt this way in a long time. She made him remember he's not just a dad, but also a man. She makes him feel like himself again, and he has trouble fighting their connection. He tried so hard to resist, but soon he's attached, and this thing between them is all-consuming.-Mack was just... everything.-I could hardly breathe when we were in a room together.-Something unfamiliar hummed in the air between us. I could feel it- he wanted me the way I wanted him.-She had no idea what she did to me.-She was that perfect combination of sexy and sweet- it drove me crazy in the best possible way. In another life, I'd have done everything possible to make her mine.-She wanted all of me. Accepted all of me. Even craved all of me.Irresistible (Cloverleigh #1) is a beautiful, sexy, single dad, second chance, forbidden romance where two lonely people find love. I really loved this book, and can't wait for more in this series!***NOW AVAILABLE***https://amzn.to/2U5uYrv

4⭐️“Every inch of you is beautiful. Inside and out.”WHY THE FUCK WAS THIS SO FUCKING CUTE?????????? i've had this book downloaded for a whole ass year but i was never interested in it until i saw the lil goodreads rec shelf and i was like "hmmm? is this my fucking sign?" hell ya it was.now before i say anything else — why does a 4 y.o, 8 y.o, and 11 y.o know more about basic communication than a fucking 37 y.o??????? I WAS DYINGGG at that lil intervention, it was hella cute tbh.i kept wondering why i fucking LOVED mack bc he's the standard hero type? nothing to him to make him special but the second i met him and idk it was weird bc HES HOT YA BUT something was there too, UNTIL i found out he was a military man hehehehhehehehehehhehe i know i pick them right <3 <3 i love me army boys, love at first sight <3all the kids are adorable, i loved the couple. SOOOO MUCH sexual tension, i actually got butterflies from start to finish.def will be reading more of this authors books bc hell that was short, good and spicy. my fucking cup of tea.🌾 cloverleigh farms 🌾#1 irresistible —4 stars#2 undeniable —1.75 stars#3 insatiable —4.5 stars#4 unbreakable —3.25 stars#5 unforgettable —2 stars#6 ignite —tbr#7 taste —tbr#8 tease —tbr

DNF at about 40%?Urgh this was terrible! The heroine was 27 but she acted, and was treated like she was 15! She was also such a wet doormat!The love interest, Mack was only 10 years older at 37, but acted like the age gap was much huger and so taboo. It wasn't. Yes he had kids, but they loved her and she was good with them. Yes he worked with her father, but again... She was 20 freaking 7 years old!Can't be bothered to read anymore, can see where it's going, and frankly I'm glad I'm getting rid of KU, as the books on there generally suck.

⭐️⭐️⭐️.5-⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️''I couldn’t breathe, couldn’t see, couldn’t think.''Safety warnings:- H 37 / h 27- Single father- Child neglect/abandonment- No OW/OM drama- No cheating- HEASPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️I have read the next gen books from this series without having read the parents story and I kept putting it off but here we are! I will be honest and say I will only be reading this book and not the remaining ones. Maybe one day that will change but I am not interested in the other couples.Mack is a single father of 3 girls who has been divorced for about a year, his wife cheated and left the family. She is an absentee parent and is pretty much what you expect a horrible parent to act like without the actual abuse. She doesn't love her daughters but expects utter devotion from them, she makes promises she never keeps and blames Mack for everything going wrong. Blame him sure but why punish your children for that? I'm glad she was just a flea in this story and makes no real appearances in person but all we get from her is phone calls to either lie about visits or belittle Mack.Frannie is the youngest child of her parents, and she has been basically coddled her whole life because of her heart condition. She has had a crush on Mack for years but never has said or done anything. Mack has been interested in her, but we are not told how long he has felt this way, but I am pretty sure he wouldn't of cheated on his wife while he was still married if Frannie showed signs of interest.He is hesitant about being with her because she is the boss's daughter, they have a 10-year age difference, and he is a single father of 3 who she babysits for. This book has a lot of pushing from Mack until he cannot control his need or feelings for her.The three girls were really adorable and made some of the scenes really fun and entertaining to get through. I appreciated how Frannie loved them BEFORE she even knew Mack was interested, she was genuinely happy to be their babysitter.These two had hot chemistry and we find out Mack has been celibate since his divorce. He obviously needs help in raising his daughter and Frannie wants to be that person, but he doesn't seem to want to let her or burden her. They keep their relationship a secret, especially from the little girls but there are two scenes where they are almost caught. Mack was afraid of what Frannie’s parents would say about him dating their daughter but honestly we didn’t get anything from the parents that made it seem they were okay with it and the two scenes we got from each parent was weird enough that it didn’t establish any real feeling about it from them. So, this part was blown out of proportion.We do get the third act breakup after he decides to let her go because he doesn't think he can ever give her what she wants, a marriage or children as he does want either. His mother make it seem like he is not allowed to have someone like Frannie with him right now as it’s too soon and if they break up once the girls find out about their relationship they will be struggling even more.However, his daughters convince him to get her back since he is miserable and they know about the relationship and they are aware he is sad because they broke up, and he goes after Frannie after they plead with him that they love Frannie and want him to be with the one he loves. It was a cute scene.It was a nice small-town romance. There was not much new in this story that you won't find in other books, but it was well done.
Nope. Not feeling it.I think it's overrated. The romance here is nothing more than a helping hand being provided to the person in desperate need of it.Not for me.

This story was so cute and well written. I fell in love, laughed well, and am sad it is over. Frannie and Mack’s relationship is so complicated. There is an age difference, it is taboo, she's the nanny, he's a single father to three daughters! Mack's relationship with his daughters was the most adorable thing I've ever read. It was amazing. It is wonderful to see how Frannie finds her true self along the way and stands up for what she wants out of life. This is one couple you don't want to miss. This book is one that is inspirational and down to earth all at the same time. I highly recommend you get this book. You won't be disappointed!In short:Hero4/5 |Heroine4/5 |Plot (Point, Originality)4/5 |Writing Style4/5 |Steam4/5 |Romance4/5 |Angst-Suspense3/5 |Darkness0/5 |Humor3/5 |Secondary Characters4/5 |Drama-Conflict4/5 |Mystery0/5 |Twists4/5 |PacingSteady |Action3/5ARC kindly provided in exchange for an honest review.

RATING:2.5 stars to Irresistible★ ★ ★➜ Forbidden Love➜ Age gap romance➜ Friends to lovers➜ Part-time nanny➜ Small town romance➜ Workplace romance➜ Single working parentContent Warning/Listing:Heroine was homeschooled due to a congenital heart condition (bicuspid aortic valve) and has had previous open heart surgeries in her infancy. She's described as having been socially limited due to the risks associated with stress to her heart when younger. Parental abandonment, parental overprotection, parental pressures. It's referenced that the three daughters of the male love interest struggle with the abandonment of their mother in different ways. Parental uncertainty/insecurity. Marital conflict (past). Mentions displacement after serving in the Marines. Separation anxiety. Parent guilt. Explicit adult content including descriptive sex scenes, profanity and drinking.

I can feel the need to wax poetic on this story hammering at me. I can be the better person, though. Really, I can be. But will I?Screw it. This tragedy starts with the title. Because nobody in this story isactuallyirresistible, starting with the cranky Mack. If this guy was a Marine, our armed forces are in more trouble than I believed possible. The man is a self-hating coward through-and-through and with a side of delusion that can't be healthy on the battlefield. I mean, I was worried that the entire story seemed to rest on his negative motivation* putting brakes on. And then he starts musing on his reasons and it's all "she'll figure I'm not good enough" and "it has to end because she'll discover I'm not worth it." Apparently, in his world, the stand-up guy who busts his back day in and day out to provide his daughters with love and careisn'ta stone-cold catch. If the author had painted this even a little bit accurately, Mack would be beating women off with a stick. But no, this is Harlowland where powerful, successful guys showing fantastic dad-skills is oh-so-lonely.I stuck through long enough to get to my breaking point because I kinda liked Frannie (though seriously? What is she with that name,ninety?!?). She's kind, cheerful, and thoroughly and unapologetically domestic. The only real downside is if I had to hear macaron pronounced one more time with the emphasis on the Frenchy origins (swallowing the final "n") my eyes were going to roll right out of my head. Yeah, sure, that's an accurate and correct pronunciation. But who bothers? Well, besides everyone in this story. Also, I've had macarons and they ain't all that, even when done to perfection. Mostly, they're hints of a taste, gone way too soon and even stacking them on top of each other or double-fisting them doesn't do more than evoke "that's an interesting flavor, I wonder what it'd taste like if it actuallytried."Anyway, I got to the dark moment and then hate-finished it. Because yeah, of course Mack was going to be the wimpy garbage pile he'd been heading towards from the beginning. At that point, I wanted Frannie to kick him in the goolies and go find someone worth her time. And his daughters, who had been an interesting and nuanced portrayal to that point suddenly turned into high-mountain gurus dispensing the wisdom of the ages to straighten him out. I mean, I get the trope behind the cliché, but I think this author blew through cliché to a new form of authorial excess that deserves a term of its own. My irreverent brain keeps throwing up clishit, but you didn't hear that from me...So yeah, one star for the serious crash and burn. It was coasting because of the plot drag that was Mack, but that dark moment ended all potential for this to come out in the positive.A note about Steamy: So there are a handful of explicit sex scenes putting this in the middle of my steam tolerance. Also, Frannie apparently went to some kind of sex school she's so good at all the things. So much for being a near-virgin with only two previous (explicitly unsatisfying) partners. It wasn't even a "quick learner" thing. She just knuckled down and . . . I'll stop now before this gets out of hand...*Negative Motivations: I kind of hate that the term "negative motivation" isn't widespread, yet. Since it isn't, I'm going to save off this little jag to append to my reviews that feature the term.Jennifer Crusie blogged about ita bit back (or, if that link doesn't work,here's a cache of the original) and it changed how I understand story. The problem with the term is that if you've never heard it before, you'd assume it meant motivations that are harmful or immoral. Not so. What it refers to is motivationsnot to do something. The thing is that many of us are motivated to not do things for a lot of different, perfectly valid and reasonable, reasons. The problem is thatin a storymotivations to not do things are a huge drag on the plot—particularly considering the fact that most negative motivations are overcome by the character simply deciding they don't care any more (or, rather, that theydo careand are now motivated to do the thing). So not only do you have a counter to action but you also have a situation where to overcome it, all a character has to do is change their mind. Which means eventually, the reader is rooting for the character to get over him/herself already and do the thing we want them to do. Conflict drives story. Conflict between a reader and a main character drives readers away from story.

I picked up this book on a wimp and I sort of liked it. I mean, it had potential but the romance felt outdated in my opinion. The main lead was cute but she was almost a dormant. Her only purpose was to provide the food and emotional support to the guy... or she was built that way in the beginning and changed later because the little agency she had felt like an afterthought.The guy was just your typical single father trying his best, and he was an okay dad. He loved her daughters but he was just so underdeveloped (and he had more POVs).The story was pretty cliché but done right, it could've been great. Like I said it had potential. Maybe the rest of the series evolves.

No sé porque en primera instancia le puse 3 estrellas ni porque no hice reseña.Pero aquí vengo a cambiar la calificación porque de todas las parejas e historias es a la que más cariño le tengo.Frannie y Mack son geniales.BUT THE WAY, les recomiendo mucho esta serie.
