This book, though sweet and quite steamy, was too problematic for me to give it a higher rating. The FMC, though 27 which is definitely not a kid, was just way too immature. Honestly I wouldn’t have had a problem with the characters’ age difference except that Frannie came off more like a 20 year old than a 27 year old. There was no way I could believe that these two were right for each other. I frankly felt sorry for her that she was saddling herself with this guy with so much baggage when she literally had not even begun to live her life. Also Mack really treated her badly and she was way too sweet to him. She was basically a surrogate mother for his kids, his chef and his therapist and he came off as an newly divorced dad who was hot for his nanny. Kind of hot sure, but I did not really root for them to have a HEA.If she had been more mature and he had been less of an emotional infant this would have worked much better for me. Ugh.

This book was super cute because of the kids but overall it was nothing special. I do like the single parent trope but I'm not really a fan of the single dad-nanny trope because so far I didn't enjoy the books that I read with this trope.It was an okay read, I loved the kids, the H was as stubborn as the rest of the heroes inMelanie Harlow's books that I've read. I can't believe I'm saying that but I wish it was longer because I wanted more character development and I'd like to see more of them before they got together.If I had to rate it against the other books I read by this author I would have given it 4 stars because I didn't enjoy it as much asMake Me Yoursbut I liked it more thanDrive Me Wild. However, I have to settle to 3 stars because it was just okay.Note:The extended epilogue was really cute but I'm not sure how they'll manage it 😂

This is this type of an age gap romance, which is not taboo (27 and 37), but it makes you uncomfortable. I didn’t have any issue with the writing, it was a fast-page-turner without any big dramas. It’s just the guy who was far from a perfect bookish boyfriend… He didn’t work at all on their relationship and there were times when he treated the girl like a booty call. He disappointed her on every single occasion and the fact that they were still together is only thanks to her being overly-forgiving and easy-going.

AVAILABLE NOW!Hot single Dad. Sweet part-time nanny. Ten year age gap. Sizzling chemistry. All the laughs, all the swoon, all the feels.Grab it now for free in KU!Amazon:harlow.pub/Irresistible-amazonAmazon International:mybook.to/IrresistibleMHPaperback:harlow.pub/Irresistible-pbAudio:harlow.pub/Irresistible-audible

2.637 ⭐️ At this point, Goodreads should just include a sliding scale for ratings 🤦🏽‍♀️This was the hardest book to rate. On the one hand, teenage me would have adored it and drawn hearts all over it 💕 At the same time, that’s exactly why adult me found it a little cringe 😬The premise was interesting—a single dad, ex-Marine who falls for the nanny. Too good to mess up, right? Unfortunately, the entire thing was lacking in depth and the MCs were extremely immature with zero character growth.“You’re too young for me… I have nothing to offer because I’m a single dad with three charming daughters… people will gossip.” UMM, let me stop you right there, Mack ✋🏼 EXCUSES. Real adults figure stuff out. Please grow up.While this book was a bit of a let down, maybe I’ll continue the series to see if it gets any better.

book.title: Nocticadia
book.author: Keri Lake
book.description: A dark, atmospheric tale of deadly secrets and forbidden love.Mortui vivos docent.The dead teach the living.After watching my mother succumb to a mysterious illness, I promised myself two things. I’d find the cure for what ravaged her. And leave the godforsaken city where she abandoned me.Four years later, I receive an acceptance letter from Dracadia University, one of the oldest, most prestigious schools in the country. Nestled on a secluded island off the coast of Maine, it’s rumored to be haunted by the souls of the mental patients exiled there centuries before. Those whose bones are said to make up the island’s white sandy shores.And restless ghosts aren’t even its most daunting peculiarity.Devryck Bramwell, known on campus as Doctor Death, is a brilliant pathologist in charge of the midnight lab. He’s also my devastatingly handsome professor, who seems to loathe tenacious first-years, like me. Except, his dark and enigmatic gaze tells me all the ways he’d devour me if given the chance, and his stolen kisses burn my lips with forbidden jealousy.I crave his authority.He aches for redemption.Together, we’re toxic. Delicious fodder for the prying eyes hellbent on exhuming the rotted skeletons of our pasts.For the dead have much to teach, and it’s only a matter of time before Dracadia’s most depraved secret is resurrected.Nocticadia is a standalone dark academia gothic romance.

reviews.text: Update: IM LEGIT TRAUMATISED MISS KERI LAKE U CRAZY CRAZY LADY THIS NOVELETTE WAS A MASTERPIECE IHY FOR ENDING IT LIKE THAT 😭🤚***WE FINALLY GOT A NOVELLA ON THE SISTER IM SCREAMING WHY AM I JUST FINDING THIS OUT NOWWWWW?!!!!!***I wanna tattoo this book on my forehead and travel the world- that’s hm i love LiliaDevryck 🙏This review is mylong asf and chaoticclove letter to this book 🤭💗The characters, the romance, the plot, the writing, the banter, the SARCASM *violent chefs kiss*It took me like a week to get past the first 12% but then IT POPPED OFF like I was hooked gripped choked fisted EVERYTHING. The way it has plot AND romance is just MWAH chefs kiss. The writing was so easy to follow but also not. Imma reread this in the near future so I can look closer at the little details and not be overwhelmed by everything else.Fair warning — this book is dark asf. There’s gore, explicit detail of said gore and a fuck of a lot of other things. Pls don’t look to this book for something realistic lmao you’re gonna be disappointed. There’s a lot of questionable stuff in here but I didn’t give a shit. It was perfect to me 🙏There were times when I was like Omf i was NOT expecting that to happen. I was shooketh at the thing that happened around the 30% mark like i was legit shaking screaming howling. I had no idea how I was supposed to react but then it happened and I literally couldn’t stop to question my morals anymore 💀😭 love that.This atmosphere Miss Ma’am created was beautifully disturbing.I’m completely enamoured by the detail that went into writing this. I can tell there was brainpower used bc the science terms actually made sense.It made my biology loving heart SING 🤭🩷✨🙏The noctisoma was so fleshed out and the world building was executed in the most amazing way. I cannot get over how invested I was in their study of the specimen, the drama, the conspiracies.The plot was crazy as fuckkk hahaha the last 20% had me going feral.There were some plot twists I couldn’t predict. That made me happy. The ones that I did predict and was proven right for?? My serotonin levels 📈📈📈📈 💅✨THE CHARACTERSDevryck Bramwell, my grumpy sarcastic hot asf dimpled professor KADHUDRJ 🤭🦋💗💞🤌 he has my whole heart ugh the way his inner monologue had me creasing so fucking bad it had me tearing up fr.his sarcasm had me screaming. He’s such a jaded bastard that’s been THRU IT and it just made him funnier bc he hated everything.This guy would literally roast anyone and everyone in his head and I was just pissing myself laughing I can’t- 💀🤣The depth to his character, his illness, the reason he is the way he is had me wanting to cry for him. My heart was aching when he was talking about his past — which was written in SUCH a great way and I commend Keri for that — and the way I could feel the genuine wonder and happiness in him towards the end 🥹💗🫂our mans J/P streak had me giggling and kicking my feet ahdgjdhd-╰┈➤ ❝“I don’t share. If anyone else so much as looks at this pussy, I’ll take pleasure in dissecting him, starting with his eyeballs.”❞AND HE FOLLOWED THRU ON HIS THREATS 🥰 had me eating out of his hands fr.The way he would be so adorable and cute towards Lilia had my heart in a fist. And when he said that he would burn his hard earned work, his reputation, THE WORLD for her with a smile on his face?? Mouth open for this man 🙏╰┈➤ ❝“The beautiful girl with her autumn hair, and eyes that reminded me of both the sea and the sky. And she was everything. The earth, the sun, the moon. The air I breathed, and the tenacious beat that kept my eroded heart pumping.What I felt for Lilia wasn’t healthy. Beyond simple obsession, it was a sickening possession. Savage and rapacious, bordering on violence.“❞*Lilia Vespertine, my love my fiesty precious adorable redhead qween.You know when the fmc says she’s from the rough side of town and she’s seen things, is tough and has fought her way thru life and then BAM the mmc enters and she forgets that she has a spine?? Yeah Lilia is definitelyNOTthat.She knows when to blend in and when to speak up. Omg i absolutely LOVE that she had a spine and literally went out SWINGING lmao it was beautiful to see 🥲 her character development was gorgeous.I loved how she opened up and found stability in Dracadia 😭🫂🩷 this girl didn’t deserve the shit she went thru Omf. She was so passionate about finding the root of noctisoma and I loved to see the relentless drive in her. She never backed away from a challenge. once her mind was made, it was MADE.Lilia is one of the rare strong yet vulnerable characters I’ve come across and I cherish her sfm.I could feel her love for her sister even if we didn’t get to see them spending time with each other (which I’m kinda salty about ngl)The way she was eye fucking Devryck every lesson 🤭🤭🤭🤭 and their heated glances when he’d finally look back?? ugh 💘🦋😩Once they accepted their illicit relationship i was shaking bc i want what they have 😭🙏- the hugs from behind 🤭🦋💘- the hair tucks 💕- subtly caressing the others hand when passing each other in public 🧍‍♀️🚂- them studying together at night 😭💞- the witty banter 🙏- the slowburn 😩- the SPICE?!🤌🔥 OMFDS had me rolling my eyes back and fisting my sheets; it was toe curling crack fr. I’m an addict atp.The steady progression in their relationship was so natural and they didn’t go around saying the L word the moment they felt it. The layers from them first accepting what they felt, to mutually acknowledging how they felt, to giving in, to trying to protect themselves, to going all in and then having all those small momentssprinkle in a fuck lot of trauma bondingeventually exploding into soulmatery of the highest level 😭🔫The way Keri lake wrote these characters makes me wanna kiss her. Give her a big ol smooch. She clearly understood the assignment 👑*The next part of the review is gonna be for all my ZA lovers- YOU ARE GONNA GOBBLE THIS BOOK UP SO FAST KABDHGDLiliaDevryck were so BlueLance coded and I acceptttt this variant 😌💅✨the characters are so fricken similar OMFDSSS it was like the BlueLance fanfic i never knew I needed. Everyone has to read this book just for that reason alone 🙏Exhibit A:╰┈➤ ❝“I don’t like interruptions. This isn’t a fuck around class. Know that I’ve failed more students than I’ve passed.”“With all due respect, I believe that’s the failure of the one teaching.”❞➥ you cannot tell me that’s not something bluelance would say in books 1-3.Exhibit B:╰┈➤ ❝“The next time you decide to eavesdrop, perhaps you might opt to be more subtle.”❞➥ SCREAMINGGGExhibit C:╰┈➤ ❝“No one touched what belonged to me without repercussions.My class.My requirements.My student.Without a doubt, Lilia Vespertine was going to be a massive headache. But she wasmyheadache.”❞➥ Lance is that you?? 🤭🦋💞Exhibit D:╰┈➤ ❝“They had no idea the girl was brilliant and witty. Too damn smart for her age.Too damn beautiful to be seen as something so simple and entertaining.”❞Exhibit E:╰┈➤ ❝“Don’t fuck with me, Miss Vespertine. You want to play hardball? Know that mine are made of steel.”“That sounds painful.”His lips twitched as if he were trying to hold back a smile.❞➥ I’m convinced that’s BLUELANCE in an alternate universeExhibit F:╰┈➤ ❝“It was a wonder he hadn’t rubbed all of the hair clean off his skull, the way he kept running his hands over it.“❞➥ pls that’s definitely something Lance would say 💀Exhibit G:╰┈➤ ❝Calling her beautiful was like calling the sun lukewarm. She’d blazed like the hottest part of a flame in that dress. And fuck me, I’d felt the heat.❞➥ plssss I’m gonna die I need aLance disguised asDevryck 😭🙏Exhibit H:╰┈➤ ❝The crease at the corner of his eye hinted at the slightest smile. Man, the guy was stingy when it came to them.❞➥ that’s so darcy coded helpExhibit I:╰┈➤ ❝“Have you always been so damn stubborn?”“Since the womb, I’m afraid.”❞➥ It reminds me of the scene when Lance was like why are you so stubborn, let me protect you and Darcy was like no I can protect myself 🤭🤭🤭Exhibit J:╰┈➤ ❝“It was there that I began to wonder if what I felt for Lilia was something more than I cared to admit.I dared not slip into those thoughts, though, becauseI knew fate and the world didn’t give so freely. “❞➥ that’s so Lance coded 😭😭And one last parting quote:╰┈➤ ❝“Are you jealous?”The dark smile on his face sobered to something serious, and he pushed a stray hair behind my ear.“Jealousy is a callow schoolboy’s emotion that ends in hard feelings and bloody noses. What I feel for you, Miss Vespertine, woulddestroylives.”❞➥ THE BLUELANCE BRAINROT IS REAL*I’m still reeling from the ending omf hahaha the scream I scrumpt at that last chapter- 😭 I need a book on Caeden rfn I beg 🙏Lmao if ur still here after my long asf review, get on this book rfn bestie! 🤭𝗦𝗶𝗱𝗲 𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲:The funny thing is that I wasn’t even gonna write a review for this lmao. i never realised I had the words in me but here we are 🫂 this is by far the longest review I’ve ever written Omg 😭𝗦𝗼𝗻𝗴𝘀: 🎧My Nocticadia Playlist𝗕𝗼𝗼𝗸𝘀 𝘄𝗶𝘁𝗵 𝘀𝗶𝗺𝗶𝗹𝗮𝗿 𝘃𝗶𝗯𝗲𝘀:Zodiac academyGothikanaDevils night seriesbut sfm better─── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ──Update:someone pls teach me how to breathe again🙏 THIS BOOK ALTERED THE CHEMISTRY OF MY BRAIN IDK WHO I WAS BEFORE I READ THIS 😭***I’ve been wanting to read this book since it first got announced pls be amazing 🤞***Nov 2022 —DARK GOTHIC ACADEMIA ROMANCE?? TEACHER x STUDENT?? DOCTOR MMC?? AGE GAP??Where do I sign?!!!

“That I would kill for you without a beat of hesitation, or remorse. And yet, at the same time, I could be reduced to nothing more than a pile of ash without you. I’m weak for you, Lilia.”4.5 stars ✨Read if you are looking for Blue Lance vibes✔️ Gothic romance✔️ Dark academia✔️ Forbidden romance✔️ Professor x student romance✔️ Age gap✔️ Slow burn✔️ Angsty✔️ Only one bed✔️ Secret society✔️ Smuttyy (4/5 🌶️)✔️ "Touch her and die"Damn I don't read a lot of gothic romance but this one sure peaked my interest. I went in with the expectations that the main characters in this have to live up to Blue Lance from Zodiac Academy and boy, they absolutely did.Thefirst 40-45% of the book was really slowin my opinion so I was a tad unsure then but after that the way it picked up 🤌 I did not want to put the book down at all.Theplotwas super intriguing. NGL the prologue had me confused initially but things started making sense soon enough. Can tell how much thought and work the author put into this book, absolutely worth reading. So kudos to her 🙌 Although it's romance but the sub plot was so gothically pleasing to read. Of course it's unrealistic(I sure hope so)but the way it was pulled off 🤌Lilia and Devryckare EVERYTHING 😩🥵 They are so perfect for each other. I liked how both of them had perfect balance in the story and one didn't overpower the other. The chemistry and heat is off the charts. And thank goodness Lilia is not dumb.Devryck Devryck Devryck Devryck Devryck Devryck Devryck Devryck Devryck 🥵😩❤️Say hello to my new book boyfriend. Man's so hot and dreamy I'm not even exaggerating. He was making me swoon so much it's embarrassing. I loved his jealous, possessive ass.Theangst and slow burn was delicious.The spice was oof 🥵🥵🤭🤭*fans self*Read to find out 👀 One thing I really appreciated was that Lilia did not kept things (majority of the times) from Devryck which didn't cause any unnecessary conflicts or drama. I liked she was open about the problems she was being put through as it gave them time to resolve those things and the fact that Devryck reassured her EVERY. DAMN. TIME. 🤌🤌🤌🤌Need me a man like him.Theendingwas satisfying and am so glad I read this. Possibly one of the best books I've read this year so far but let's see if that changes. I rate it 4.5 stars instead of 5 cause of the initial pacing. If you push through it, am pretty sure you'll end up loving the book.Recommended.Note- There are a lot of gore and vivid descriptions, some real creepy and strange. There's also violence, and abusive situations towards the FMC (not caused by the MMC).~•~•~Cause apparently it gives Blue Lance vibes 😌
if devryck asks me to bark for him, I'd literally get on my knees and start woof-woofing 🫣
She was good, but she wasn’t great. Absolutely loved the first 80%, by the last 20% I was like pls hurry tf up. Also he never said I love you back to her?!? Like HELLO?!

♾️ STUNNED. Might be one of my fave books of all time. I would poison myself with Noctisoma for Professor Bramwell or to reread this book all over again. Zero questions, seriously just give me all the noxberries I’ll eat them from a bowl idgaf. This needs to be the STANDARD for a contemporary dark gothic romance. Equal parts plot and romance, but heavy on both. It’s daaaark, haunting, beautiful, horrific, romantic, spicyyyyy, thrilling, symbolic, and all-consuming."It was my mother, my refusal to accept her death, to accept what the world had taken from me, that had brought me to Dracadia in the first place. And it was there that I'd faced death head-on. So smitten, I fell in love with him--his abrasive heart and blood-stained hands."🧪💜☠️🪱🐦‍⬛🖤✨🥵LITTLE MOTH. Picture this: Dracadia, an elite ivy league-type university situated on a small island off the coast of New England. Collegiate vibes on a gothic campus straight out of cambridge and a haunted history rife with secrets, death, and Native American tribal lore. A mysterious disease called Noctisoma that exists only on the island. Secret academic societies where members wear plague masks. A smoldering sexy young professor nicknamed DOCTOR DEATH with black hair and copper eyes. Said professor is also brooding (bc duh) and ignores everyone but you (touch-her-and-you-die vibes…..literally). Midnight labs where Doctor Death lectures about this creepy as hell disease through tanks filled with swimming black worms and purple moths that turn black when infected. Apparent suicides in women that, on second glance, all appear to have links to black worms......including the FMC’s mother.So. LITTLE MOTH aka Lilia who is incredible writes a paper at her community college in Massachusetts about a "fictional" disease, only she bases it on what she actually witnessed during her own mother's death. After submitting it, her professor recognizes the disease as the one that Dracadia studies and recommends her to Dracadia's dean. She's then whisked off to Dracadia, which is also her dream school, where she meets Professor Bramwell, aka Doctor Death, aka the professor who specializes in this disease, aka the dreamiest devilish alpha 30ish year old mad scientist smokeshow. NOW picture this: a slooooowwww burn romance with Doctor Death. Secret trysts that grow spicier and spicier and spicier until the entire jar of spice is simply dumped all over you. Again, I would risk Noctisoma if it meant having Prof Bramwell. The romance is not insta-lust or insta-love but authentic and real. Lilia and Bramwell are each other's moths and flames. They are each other's toxins and antivenoms, one's life to the other's death. But its not just that one of them embodies one trait--at times, Lilia is Bramwell's toxin and medicine, and at times Bramwell is Lilia's life and her death. They contemplate each other in a deadly way. 🧪💀💜Perfect writing (like so good even your english prof would agree). Perfect dark gothic romance. Perfect professor-student romance. Perfect academic setting. Perfect creepy af disease plot. Perfect spice that makes you want to go back to college and do it a bit…..differently. Perfect suspense and WTF is going on?! vibes. The last 20% is such thrilling high stakes chaos that I had to catch my breath. I'm reeling from how much I'm obsessed with Lilia's and Bramwell's story. AND its dual first person POV between them, and being in Bramwell's mind when he starts falling for Lilia is...something else.I will never recover from this until Keri Lake agrees to write a sequel. Even if its just the day to day boring lives of Lilia and Bramwell. I will read about them doing f*cking laundry and washing dishes together if it means I get more. One million stars.

♾️ ⭐️s Hands down my favorite read of 2024 so far. OH. MY. GOD. I need this book INJECTED INTO MY VEINS!!! Keri Lake, where have you been all my life?!!! This book was sheer perfection, I wouldn’t change a single goddamn thing!The gothic world, the academic setting, the tension, the spice, the banter, the writing, the characters, the romance, the plot….. ABSOLUTE MASTERPIECEThis book is DARK and TWISTED and really made me question my morals, and I ate up every last word on the page like a starved feral animal.I seriously have never read ANYTHING like this before. The atmosphere was so spooky and haunting, and the world is so immersive I felt like I was watching everything take place through the characters eyes! The plot was absolutely INSANITY! I seriously could not predict the plot twists at the end and the last 20% had me pacing back and forth with anxiety, I LOVED IT! The science nerd in me also went crazy for this, even if they are studying a completely fictional disease, Keri Lakes attention to detail made it feel so realistic! The perfect balance of plot and romance that had me quite literally incapable of putting this book down.Professor Devryk Bramwell aka Doctor Death… DONT EVEN GET ME STARTED ON THE FUCKING MAN! I don’t even know how to properly articulate how much I love this grumpy, traumatized, dark, sarcastic, smart, dimpled man. His inner monologue was everything to me, I never wanted to leave his head. He’s so unserious and sarcastic and unintentionally hilarious, I was giddy every time he opened his damn mouth. He has such a hard exterior and lets his guard down for no one because this man has been to hell and fucking back. His past made my heart hurt but I absolutely loved the way Keri Lake told the story of his childhood and upbringing, GENIUS. The words this man uttered were a cross between absolute poetry and down right filthy. How possessive and obsessed he gets with Lilia had me SWEATING. He literally had me giggling and kicking my feet the whole time, one of my new favorite book men of all time. I will never get this man out of my head. For example….“The wealthy possess an insatiable appetite for the rare and priceless. They stare because you’re the only thing worth staring at.”“You’re a sickness inside of me that begs never to be cured. Infecting me with this unshakable craving for things I shouldn’t want.”“She was the warmth of the sun on a cold and rotting corpse. The first breath after a lifetime of death.”“Her name was a dirty scalpel plunged deep inside my chest, past crumbling bones and decayed flesh. It pierced the only organ I vowed could never be touched again and infected me with insufferable greed.”“I don’t think I’ll be able to stay away from you after this. But let me be very clear, I don’t share. If anyone else so much as looks at this pu$$y, I’ll take pleasure in dissecting him, starting with his eyeballs.”“I have lived a lifetime in death–a cold existence in an endless void. It wasn’t until you came along and cast the first ray of light that I felt a pulse of life. A pull that I couldn’t resist. It was you who dragged my heart from this insensate slumber. And it’s you for whom it beats now. It’s a fucking wreckage, though. Scarred and caged by ravaged bones. But it belongs only to you.”Lilia, I couldn’t love this girl more if I tried! Her character development and growth brought tears to my eyes. She is a fucking FIGHTER! It’s rare to find an FMC that is damaged and vulnerable, but also has the strongest backbone and doesn’t let anyone else fight her battles, especially not a man. I adored how she fought for what she truly deserved and never backed down despite the odds being against her on multiple occasions. I loved how she found solace in Devryn but also never backed down from the challenges he presented. This girl must be protected at all fucking costs.Lilia and Devryn honestly, one of my favorite fictional couples of all time. There’s just something about a professor student relationship that DOES SOMETHING TO ME like nothing else! The tension, the constant push and pull, will they won’t they???!! Gets me REELING! The definition of soulmates. One truly does not exist without the other.- the heated glances from afar- The longing- The angst- The banter- The hair tucks- The stolen touches- The most scrumptious slow burn- THE FUCKING SPICE!!!! Don’t even get me started, some of the best spicy scenes I have ever read im actually getting hot just thinking about it.For my Zodiac academy lovers, they are so BlueLance coded it’s not even funny.I could rave about this book for HOURS ON END!! I now have a gaping hole in my heart that will never be filled. Please read this. I’m not the same person I was before I new this masterpieces existed.

── 4 stars ୨ৎ꒰ spoiler-free review ⊹₊ ⋆⊹ ࣪ ˖ "that i would kill for you without a beat of hesitation, or remorse. and yet, at the same time, i could be reduced to nothing more than a pile of ash without you.”┆ ⤿ 🦇 ⌗thoughts⟡ to preference, i am not a dark romance girlie by any means. every time i try to get into the genre, it always ends up being too dark for me (i know, how paradoxical). however,i really enjoyed this one.⟡ this book follows lilia vespertine, who leaves her hometown to attend dracadia university to investigate the stange, mysterious death of her mother. this university specializes in studying the parasite "noctisoma", which lilia believes may be the reason for her mother's passing. while she's there, she begins to learn secrets while engaging in a forbidden romance with her professor, whose leading the noctisoma studies.⤿ ⌞side note.while it's forbidden + an age gap trope, this takes place in a college setting and the fmc is 20 years old⌝⟡ to begin, i really liked thewriting of this book.when i read books, i love for there to be beautiful descriptions and alluring writing, and this book delivered just that. the way keri lake perfectly transmitted me into this world was so amazing, and i struggled to put this book down at night or to do my daily tasks because it was so good. i would love to read this book during the fall time, because the dark academia vibes really would be perfect for a foggy autumn day.⟡ i also really liked how this wasmore than a romance. there were spooky, thrilling aspects to it, as well as mysteries being unsolved and other things. getting to learn more about this disease with lilia was so fun, it really felt like i was in her shoes as she was discovering more about it. same with her family history, and getting to learn about the weird things involving her mother and the university was interesting as well. there were times where i felt horrified as well,HINT : THE WORMS 😭overall, it was just a good expierence and i'm surprised more people aren't talking about it on goodreads (at least from my mutuals)⊹ ࣪ ˖ “calling her beautiful was like calling the sun lukewarm. she’d blazed like the hottest part of a flame in that dress. and fuck me, i’d felt the heat.”┆ ⤿ ���� ⌗characters⌗ ┆lilia verspertinedespite what i've seen in some reviews about her, i genuinely enjoyed her character. she was such a badass, and she was actually not as aggrivating as i thought she would be. a pattern i've noticed with dark romances is that the fmcs are portrayed often as a 'helpless, damsel in distress who needs saving from her murderer boyfriend', which is always just so boring and dull 🙄 luckily,lila wasn't that way at all, at least most of the time. she was empowering and dynamic, and seeing her drive to learn more about her mother's death was inspiring to read about. we love a motivated queen. also the scene where she's filming herself in the classroom.... i don't need to say anymore 🤭⌗ ┆devryck bramwella mannnn 🫦🫦 i actually loved him so much, he's not my usual kind of book boyfriend but i'll take him. him being her professor and being older than her just screams lana del rey, and he was so ultraviolence coded (specifically shades of cool). while i do like the golden boys better, he definitely was giving with the"touch her and i'll kill you" vibes and i genuinely loved it so much. the scenes where he's teaching but won't stop looking at her live rent free in my mind. i loved men who have a soft spot for their girls <3the scene where he calls his lawyer to immediately have her video on that strange website taken down after she posts it, saying she can find money a different way because he didn't want other men watching her 🫣 if you know, you know⌗ ┆devryck + liliateacher x student is one of my least favorite tropes ever,i think i'm still traumatized from lolita/my dark vanessa LOL.but it was, of course, done right this time. forbidden romances are always amazing, and them sneaking around this school because it's prohibited was so fun (but also thrilling) to read about. all the little stolen glances and kisses throughout the classes and in-between them>>> and they were such a power couple once they got together, i was rooting for them so hard⊹ ࣪ ˖ “you don’t have to be scared. i will never hurt you, lilia. in fact, i’ll rain hell on anyone who ever hurts you again.”┆ ⤿ 🗝️ ⌗final thoughts⟡ i really liked this book and i would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a spooky, dark academic gothic romance (that's so much more than a romance 🤭).⟡ my one problem with this book, though, was the last 80% were very rushed but somehow dragged at the same time? there was just too much going on, and i think we could've left without the secret society stuff/the stuff with bramwell's brother. otherwise, i think this book would've been a 4.5 stars! i will definitely be reading more titles from this author in the future <3started ➤ june 9, 2025finished ➤ june 14, 2025

– 3 starsI’ve finally succumbed to the pressure after reading in several reviews how Lilia x Devryck is being compared to one of my favs Darcy and Orion from Zodiac Academy. After readingNocticadia, I can safely say that the comparison is a reach 🧍‍♀️ besides the student-professor trope, age-gap and forbidden relationship aspect of both couples I don’t really see similarities.No one touched what belonged to me without repercussions.My class.My requirements.My student.Without a doubt, Lilia Vespertine was going to be a massive headache. But she was my headache.---not really a review, but I wanted to share some thoughts here and there and they might include spoilers---💀 the beginning is painfully slow .. I was waiting forLilia(h) andDevryck(H) to finally interact and be on the same page and it takes FOREVER. 21% of an almost 700 pages book until they cross path and 23% until they have a conversation… it was really testing my patience. I’d even say that until 50% of the book liliadevryck have maybe a handful of interactions .. they don’t have that many scenes together until she gets to be his assistant in his lab. Literally Mina and I complaining every 5 pages when Lilia and Devryck would meet:💀Lilia Vespertine(h): she was a decent heroine for the first half of the book... there but not super annoying .. so basically the bare minimum, but I take it. But then in the last 40% she started to annoy me, and she became such a hypocrite. Accusing Mel of being a snake when she was the first person who sold out Mel’s name to a certain someone for information, but then woe is me when Mel did a reverse uno card on her lmao. I don’t even like Mel, she is a huge cunt and deserves what is coming for her, but it’s the principle what Lilia did wasn’t great either so stop acting like she didn’t do something bad too.“The wealthy possess an insatiable appetite for the rare and priceless. They stare because you’re the only thing worth staring at.”💀Devryck Bramwell(H): I like him, but I don’t quite love him. Professor who doesn’t like anyone with a tragic backstory, all the potential for a 5* hero was there, but Dev never reached that potential, and I can’t tell you why. Every time I see his name, I hear the “you were good I’m waiting for you to be great” sound in my head.“To answer your question about why I didn’t leave you in the gardens that night, I find you to be an intriguing annoyance.” “I guess that’s appropriate, coming from a man I find to be brilliantly antisocial,” I quipped.💀 at 30% there is the first interesting scene when Lilia masturbates in class during Devryck’s lecture, films herself and gets caught by Professor Dearest who confiscates her phone and gets obsessed with that video as he should cause that shit was hot 💅 the scene without context might sounds cringe, but it was so good, kudos to Keri Lake tho most of her smut scenes in this book were pretty well done. The sex scenes are prob my fav part about Lilia and Dev’s relationship 🏃‍♀️“You’re the fever in my veins,” he said through clenched teeth, as he drove into me with furious determination. “An incurable madness I can’t shake. Fucking you is the only thing that keeps me sane, Lilia.”💀 the dark academia gothic vibes were def there, while I thought that the writing and description were very pretentious, it’s on par with the dark academia and topics that Nocticadia covered, but I couldn’t help but feel like Keri Lake wanted to shove the fact down my throat how smart she is by putting in how much research she did with all the medical and biological terms she threw around💀 the plot of Nocticadia is all over the place I wouldn’t even know how to begin to explain all of it. Within the mystery plot were several other mysteries woven in and the last 20% of the book was a whack show plot wise. Miss Lake did too much that I didn’t know half of the time what was going on…💀 the epilogue got me very intrigued. I’m waiting for Lilia’s sister book with a potential other teacher/professor hero that I already like more than Dev from what little I’ve read about him on screen 👀Conclusionall in all,not a bad book, but not great for me either. It was too plot heavy and it didn’t have enough couple scenes imo, even tho the smut was very good, but if you're not a character driven reader like me this book should work a lot better. The book should have been 200% shorter as well, cause books that aren’t fantasy that are more than 500+ pages are a crime to humanity.

4.5 stars ⭐️⤷ spoiler-free review!! ⋆౨ৎ˚⟡˖ ࣪omg this book was SO. FREAKING. GOOD. I LOVE DARK ACADEMIA AND GOTHIC ROMANCE (this is definitely my alter ego speaking), AND THIS BOOK DELIVERED. i’ve never read anything quite like it, and i kid you not, i was completely hooked from the very first chapter.that said, i didn’t rate it 5 stars for a few reasons:1. chapter 25-28 made me kinda uncomfortable and gave me second-hand embarrassment 🤦🏻‍♀️😭2. the author seemed to lose focus on the plot at one point because lilia and devryck could not stop smashing each other.3. the last 20% of the book felt underwhelming. while the author masterfully established a slow, suspenseful, and deeply eerie atmosphere along with an intriguing plot, the conclusion felt rushed due to the rapid-fire revelations, leaving me unsatisfied. on top of that, the last chapter and epilogue felt so unnecessary, and i was left with so many unanswered questions.ᡣ𐭩.ᐟlilialilia is strong and independent, i’ll give her that, but she’s certainly not the wisest 😭ᡣ𐭩.ᐟdevryckdevryck is hot and all, but i didn’t really swoon over him 👀 i’m sorry but he’s nothing like orion, and no other professor can compare to my man lance. i loved how he shared his backstory as if it were story time though.ᡣ𐭩.ᐟlilia and devryckthe tension and slow burn were executed really well, but i honestly couldn’t feel their connection beyond their constant lust for each other. i picked this book up hoping for a good ‘professor x student’ romance, but the plot was so captivating that i ended up not caring much about the romance 🫢“I both hated and craved the addicting way he’d hooked himself into my veins and fed me with this unbridled and reckless desire. It went against everything I was. Everything I believed. And yet, it was everything.”“She was the warmth of the sun on a cold and rotting corpse. The first breath after a lifetime of death.”“After I lost my mother, and Bee went off to school, I kind of spiraled into myself and learned how to find solace in loneliness. It became my home. The only place I felt safe. Then I came here. And I met you. And I learned that loneliness was a choice for me. This place is more than just a school to me. I suppose I could’ve kept to myself, as I always have, and remained another forgettable face in the crowd. And maybe Gilchrist wouldn’t have bothered. Or maybe she would’ve and I’d have just gone back to my old, lonely life, and had all the answers I’ve yearned for. Answers that don’t really matter anymore. The decision was made the moment I met you, though. To stay here. I choose you.”“I choose you. And believe me when I say, you’re hard to forget, Miss Vespertine.”“You don’t have to be scared. I will never hurt you, Lilia. In fact, I’ll rain hell on anyone who ever hurts you again.”“Don’t hide from me, Lilia. You are no less beautiful than that day you walked into my classroom and stole my fucking breath.”“I have lived a lifetime in death—a cold existence in an endless void. Never feeling. Never knowing the warmth of touch. Every unfulfilling breath a suffocating reminder of how hollow I’d become. It wasn’t until you came along and cast the first ray of light that I felt a pulse of life. A pull that I couldn’t resist. Don’t ever hesitate to touch me, Lilia. You’re the only one who can. It was you who dragged my heart from this insensate slumber. And it’s you for whom it beats for now.”“I hadn’t come to Dracadia with any notion of falling in love with my professor, or Death, as some had referred to him. Perhaps that was the nature of the world, to take so cruelly, then swoop in and blindside us when we least expected it. There was an implicit truth in the dead teaching the living, though. It was my mother, my refusal to accept her death, to accept what the world had taken from me, that had brought me to Dracadia in the first place. And it was there that I’d faced death head-on. So smitten, I fell in love with him—his abrasive heart and blood-stained hands. The dangerous and erudite professor, with fiery eyes and cold steel flesh. We hid away in shadows, stealing kisses under midnight stars. He taught me passion and courage, and to seize what I wanted by the teeth. And in return, I taught him to feel again.”overall, i ate this book up, and i had a great time reading and discussing it with kendall. i love how our thoughts and opinions perfectly aligned 🤭pre-read 💀🦋:‘professor x student’ is my new favourite trope and i miss bluelance sm but can’t read book 4 of ZA yet so i’m hoping this one will make up for it 😭🤞🏻update: impromptu br withkendallbecause she's currently reading it too 🖤

5★”Mortui vivos docent. The dead teach the living.”╰┈➤read this if you like:★ dark academia★ touch her and 🪦★ professor x college student★ forbidden romance★ slow burn + spicy🥵★ gothic★ morally gray mmcREAD THE TWs!!!!!!!!reread 15/03/2026:i loved it like the first time! this is one of my all time favsfirst read 08/10/2024:OMG the way i devoured this book!!!the perfect read for the spooky season, couldn’t have chosen a better one to start.”I forgot the world outside the gates of Dracadia. If Heaven existed, I'd found it in those ancient, dark hallways, under cold misty skies with autumn's wet leaves sticking to the soles of my boots, in the scent of coffee and old books. And him. My moody and devilishly handsome professor.”➥lilia vespertinefinds herself with a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity: attending Dracadia university to study and uncover the truth behind her mother’s death. dracadia is on a secluded island, it's rumored to be haunted by the souls of the mental patients exiled there centuries before. those whose bones are said to make up the island's white sandy shores. And restless ghosts aren't even its most daunting peculiarity. but then there’s also the handsomeprofessor devryck bramwell, a pathologist with as many skeletons in his closet as lilia.the dead have much to teach, and it's only a matter of time before Dracadia's most depraved secret is resurrected.ˏˋ°•*⁀➷Lilia & Devryck💜🖤these two made me question my morals ngl. but the romance between them was*chef fucking kiss*!!!lilia is smart and brilliant, she asks lots of questions (to which devryck pretends to be annoyed by, but he’s actually a proud fucker); she’s passionate and absolutely strong. she knows when to fight and when to flee. she’s curious and cunning.her passion is what drove her to dig into her mother’s death cause. she’s no fool, even tho people have always claimed she was crazy. she knew something wasn’t normal the day her mother passed away, so she wants to uncover the truth and hopefully find a cure to help others.devryck (aka doctor death) is morally gray, perpetually annoyed by people and a total bastard—but i love him for it. he uses sarcasm and black humor all the time, making him unintentionally funny.he’s a man of his word: make no mistake, if he makes a threat, be sure he’ll follow through.his past and his illness made my heart ache for him.together, they are the perfect duo.they share the same interest in science and you can definitely see how their love for each other grows all throughout the book. from their first glance to their first kiss, everything felt so natural and inevitable because they clearly couldn’t stay away from each other.i LOVED the dynamics between the two of them. the tension and banter, the angst, the heated glances, the stolen kisses and slow burn… TRUST ME WHEN I SAY THIS BOOK ATE AND LEFT NO CRUMBS!aaa i loved them so much!!!”She was the warmth of the sun on a cold and rotting corpse. The first breath after a lifetime of death.”overall, i’ve been hooked on this book since page one.the academic setting, the dark themes, the tension, the writing—everything was so fucking good.the intricate plot and the final twist left me with my jaw on the floor.this is the perfect book if you want something spooky, creepy and disturbing but also hot and mysterious.this is truly one of the best book i’ve read this year. i have no criticism about it bc everything was on fucking point. i’ve never read anything like this and i’m definitely picking up more of keri lake’s books bc i love her writing.DEFINITELY RECOMMENDED!ALSO, we have a novella about bee??????? omg????? this is insane! brb i have to read it asap!🏃🏻‍♀️💨onto the next💋Without a doubt, Lilia Vespertine was going to be a massive headache.But she was MY headache.In a normal human being, it might’ve triggered an urge to get the heck out of there, because what the hell kind of person would’ve been caught on a rocky cliff, in the woods, with a man who carved corpses at night?“That is the tragedy of women, isn’t it? We deny ourselves beauty for the sake of misleading men.”Again, the thought of Professor Bramwell studying my puke somehow seemed worse than him handcuffing me to a bed inside what must’ve been a prison at one time. Crazy, but that was my state of mind right then.Calling her beautiful was like calling the sun lukewarm. She’d blazed like the hottest part of a flame in that dress. And fuck me, I’d felt the heat.“Says who? I happen to get very attached to my fictional boyfriends.” Yet another frown. “Boyfriends?”“I read a lot.”If the man buried his compliments that way, or softened his insults behind handsome smiles and poetic words.Curiosity and trepidation clashed his eyes, and he skated his palm down my cheek, pausing to rub his fingers together. His thumb caressed my bottom lip, and his hand moved to my hair again, where he let a strand slip through his fingers, eyes alight with fascination. “I feel you.”Lilia. Her name was a dirty scalpel plunged deep inside my chest, past crumbling bones and decayed flesh. It pierced the only organ I vowed could never be touched again and infected me with insufferable greed.Could it have been more perfect? Could I have known another soul so intimately as in that moment, with the two of us wound so tightly together? Entombed by rapture and the shame of knowing we’d breached the forbidden boundary and there was no going back.“Jealousy is a callow schoolboy’s emotion that ends in hard feelings and bloody noses. What I feel for you, Miss Vespertine, would destroy lives.”Over her shoulder, she shot me a smile–one so fucking beautiful, I wanted to frame it. Capture it. Study the alchemy of it. How wonderfully intoxicating one simple expression could be.I couldn’t fucking do it, because all I could think about, all that mattered to me, was the girl. The beautiful girl with her autumn hair, and eyes that reminded me of both the sea and the sky. And she was everything. The earth, the sun, the moon. The air I breathed, and the tenacious beat that kept my eroded heart pumping.It was in that moment, I believed him when he said he belonged to me. Like a vast ocean claimed by a single grain of sand.Burrowed deep into the roots, it blossomed from the wounds of my broken heart, tearing through the stitches that burned with memories of those who’d tried to hurt me. Sometimes, the pain was too much to bear. But sometimes it felt good, because it meant that I was capable of feeling something.

4 stars"I would kill for you without a beat of hesitation, or remorse. And yet, at the same time, I could be reduced to nothing more than a pile of ash without you.”This book follows Lilia who after watching her mother succumb to a mysterious illness, vows to find a cure and escape the city where her mother abandoned her. Four years later, Lilia is accepted into Dracadia University, an elite school on a secluded island in Maine, known for its eerie history and rumours of hauntings. There, she meets Devryck Bramwell, a brilliant but distant professor who runs a midnight lab. Despite his aloofness, an undeniable attraction sparks between them, leading to a dangerous and forbidden relationship. As both struggle with their pasts, they uncover dark secrets about the university—and the deadly science that ties them together.➸This book was honestly one of the creepiest, most traumatising, and most disturbing reads I've ever encountered... but somehow, I LOVED it?! I'm still shocked by how much I enjoyed it, especially since I’m not usually drawn to gothic themes. But this one completely hooked me. I couldn’t put it down. The slow burn was expertly done, keeping me on the edge of my seat the entire time. The plot—especially the scientific aspects—was fascinating, and I was surprised that it never felt boring. This was my first Keri Lake book, and I’m definitely not disappointed. The dark academia gothic setting was spot-on, totally immersive, and the spice??? So HOT. The tension, the angst, the plot—everything came together perfectly, and I genuinely enjoyed every twisted moment of it!Liliais a character with a lot of drive. I really appreciated her passion for finding a cure for her mother’s illness—her determination to uncover the truth was honestly inspiring. She’s strong-willed, independent, and takes on challenges headfirst, no matter how daunting. That said, there were times when I found her decisions frustrating. She often rushed into situations without thinking them through, and honestly, it gave me secondhand embarrassment. 😬 Those moments made it hard to fully connect with her at times, especially when she could’ve avoided trouble with a bit more caution. Still, despite those missteps, she’s a solid fmc overall. Her strength and determination carried her through the rough spots, and even though her judgment wasn’t always the best, it was clear her heart was in the right place.Devryckis the perfect mix of brooding, brilliant, and commanding. I couldn’t get enough of him! He radiates this intense "I'm in charge" energy, and then there’s his FILTHY mouth. 🥵 But what really hooked me was his "touch her and 🪦" attitude. I loved how possessive and jealous he was. 🤭 There was something dangerously protective about him that was absolutely magnetic. And his sarcasm? 🤌🏼 His sharp, witty remarks brought just the right amount of dry humour, making him downright irresistible!IlovedLilia and Devrycktogether. Their chemistry was off the charts. The slow burn between them was absolute perfection—every subtle glance, every word, every touch, kept building the tension and making me yearn for more. When the spice finally kicked in? 🔥🔥 Every interaction, no matter how small, was charged with energy—each moment felt like it was pulling them closer. The way their relationship unfolded had me hooked from start to finish!The plot of this book really went CRAZYY in the best way.The atmosphere was dark, eerie, and utterly immersive— it was the perfect backdrop for the unfolding mystery. Every twist and turn kept me hooked, and there was never a dull moment. The story is packed with mysteries that had me constantly guessing, always eager to uncover the next . I was completely drawn in by every plot detail, and even the moments of suspense that seemed to drag on just made everything feel that much more intense. The disease plot, in particular, was so intriguing. It was disturbing as hell, but so fascinating and fun to read about, even though it definitely made me squirm a bit.What I didn’t like:- That one scene where Lilia filmed herself in class—good grief 💀. I was cringing so hard. Like, why WHYY did she do that?! I was practically fighting for my life reading that part.- And then the ending. I know this book is hefty, but the way things wrapped up felt way too rushed. I know it’s crazy to say that about a 700-page book, but it’s true. After all the tension and mystery that had built up, everything was solved way too quickly for my liking, and it left me feeling unsatisfied.Overall, this book completely exceeded my expectations. It seamlessly blends dark academia, suspense, and a touch of gothic romance into something thrilling and unforgettable. Despite a few cringey moments and a rushed ending, I thoroughly enjoyed it! I’d recommend this to anyone who enjoys gothic settings, dark academia, slow burns, and a professor x student dynamic with darker, more intense themes. Just be aware, though, that this book is really dark, with graphic depictions of gore and abusive situations.

Honestly, I should be ashamed of myself. I've been adding Keri Lake's books to my tbr for years and have listened to more than one enthusiastic friend fangirl over her, but did I pick up any of her work? No. Why? The world may never know why I do the things I do. I certainly don't. All I know is it was a big mistake. I could have been in Keri Lake heaven all these years, because let me tell you, I definitely was this week! My expectations couldn't have been higher after all the aforementioned waiting and high praise, but she blew those expectations right out of the fucking water!Nocticadia is a forbidden, gothic, age gap, dark academia, slowburn romance with horror/sci-fi vibes. It's set on the fictional island of Dracadia where our heroine, Lilia, is invited to attend a prestigious university on a full scholarship. Up until that invitation, Lilia is barely surviving, working long hours to pay rent and half of her sister's expensive boarding school tuition while managing a couple of community college classes on the side. Her dreams of earning a degree and providing a decent life for the only family she has left seems far out of reach, so when this seemingly too good to be true opportunity presents itself along with an additional way to afford her sister's schooling, she takes it despite her skepticism and any other concerns she may have.Devryn is an aloof, brilliant, and hot as hell professor at Dracadia University with a deadly reputation. He's studying a peculiar species of worms Lilia suspects are responsible for her mother's strange death, so she makes it her mission to become his lab assistant by any means necessary despite the rumors responsible for his nickname around campus, Doctor Death. Devryn and Lilia's chemistry in and out of the lab is intense, and before long, the sexual tension is just too much to bear.Devryn and Lilia's story gave me everything I could have hoped for! It's mysterious, atmospheric, intriguing, chilling, and macabre with a healthy dose of naughty student/teacher taboo. Both characters have interesting, tragic backstories that will break your heart, and if you're into sexy, morally gray antiheroes and smart, strong heroines, you'll absolutely adore these two.Nocticadia is a slow moving, beautifully written romance that is worth every bit of the journey. I usually devour books I'm loving as quickly as possible, but with this one, I actually prolonged my reading experience, because I wanted to live in this world for as long as I could. It's so well done and unique that I know it's going to be hard to find something that will give me similiar feels and that I'll enjoy as much as I did this one.Gothic romance fans, this one's for you!P.S. I would love a book with Lilia's sister and a certain someone, especially after that ending!!
this was actually so good.RTC

"You're a sickness inside of me that begs never to be cured. Infecting me with this unshakable craving for things I shouldn't want."4.5 Stars (rating up bc the BLUELANCE vibes were GIVING!)I'm OBSSESED with this book! It was Dark Academia at it's most fun and macbre and I loved all of it! Four years ago Lilia witnessed her mother die in a gruesome and perplexing way but no one believes her instead believing it to have been suicide. But Lilia knows what she saw that night and finding the answers to what illness afflicted her mother and perhaps saving others from the same fate becomes her mission in life, so when the opportunity to attend Dracadia University, the premier school that studies the same parasite the she suspects killed her mother she jumps at the chance. In order for Lilia to achieve her goal though she must get close to the Professor everyone calls Dr. Death. Prof Devryck Bramwell is taciturn, unyielding and she also can't take her eyes off the alluring teacher but with rumors swirling around him and several murders and disappearances, coming to Dracadia may have put Lilia in a world of danger."You've infected every part of me, and I can't stop. I can't stop this obsession."Aaaahhh! This book was so much fun! The dark vibes, the academia, the CHEMISTRY between the main characters and the mysteries!! All of it was so good and captivated my attention from the first chapter. This book does skirt the line of horror and can be a little GORY at times but I delighted in it and all the biology heavy references and scenes were fascinating! I loved all the mysteries and plot twists, I guessed most of them but it didn't take away from the exciting pace of the storytelling. But the relationship between Devryck and Lilia was heart of this book and I am infatuated with them! For my ZA fans, YES, this is SOOO BLUELANCE coded!!! The writing was beautiful and the plot was intelligent, I did think it was a little slow at a certain parts when it leaned heavily into the science but it makes up for it later in the plot."A strange pull tugged at my chest. I couldn't explain it. Even as abrasive as he was, I found him utterly captivating. Magnetic."Lilia-Determined, intelligent and doesn't put up with any tomfoolery. She was such a fantastically written fmc. I loved her perseverance and how she always was steadfast to draw her own conclusions instead of allowing other people to influence her decisions and opinions."She was the warmth of the sun on a cold rotting corpse. The first breathe after a lifetime of death."Devryck-GAH!! THIS MAN!!!! He is grumpy, brilliant, bossy and he has such a FILTHY mouth!!! I LOVE HIM!! He also is a bit unhinged but it's all forgiven bc Dr. Death is just too HOT! I really loved his story and how it all played out, it was heartbreaking and created an immediate attachment to his character. The way he is so possessive and protective of Lilia tho! 🫠He was PERFECT! He was deliciously Lance coded all the way down to his bourbon and the dimple in his cheek!Fave quotes 🤌"What separates monsters from good men is only a matter of perspective. In your eyes, I'm a sick fuck for what I've done to you. But I, on the other hand see you as a parasite.""I reached down into the bottom drawer of my desk and grabbed a bottle of bourbon, along with two glasses."OH HELLO LANCE! IS THAT YOU?! 🤭"My grouchy nature serves a purpose Miss Vespertine. Unfortunately, you seem to have some inexplicable resistance.""And because I'm a selfish prick who has to love with the fact that I cannot have you to myself, I'm going to ruin you so that any boy who comes after me will leave you deeply unsatisfied, and you will be left fucking your own fingers, desperate to remember the time you had your professor's face between your legs.""Good fucking girl.""There's a violence in my blood. This rage that twists in my gut, and it makes me sick. I'm fucking sick when it comes to you.""That I would kill for you without a beat of hesitation, or remorse. And yet, at the same time, I could be reduced to nothing more than a pile of ash without you. I'm weak for you, Lilia.""I will bleed out everyone of your demons until you feel safe."...........................................................................................................................................................When my bumblebeeIqrasays BlueLance vibes and read this now, you do what she says! Give me the dark and twisty goodness!!! 🤭

𝚆𝚎 𝚍𝚎𝚗𝚢 𝚘𝚞𝚛𝚜𝚎𝚕𝚟𝚎𝚜 𝚋𝚎𝚊𝚞𝚝𝚢 𝚏𝚘𝚛 𝚝𝚑𝚎 𝚜𝚊𝚔𝚎 𝚘𝚏 𝚖𝚒𝚜𝚕𝚎𝚊𝚍𝚒𝚗𝚐 𝚖𝚎𝚗.”𝙏𝙧𝙤𝙥𝙚𝙨┊🏹› 𝕋𝕠𝕦𝕔𝕙 ℍ𝕖𝕣, 𝕐𝕠𝕦 𝕕𝕚𝕖› 𝔻𝕒𝕣𝕜 𝔸𝕔𝕒𝕕𝕖𝕞𝕚𝕒› ℙ𝕣𝕠𝕗𝕖𝕤𝕤𝕠𝕣𝕩𝕊𝕥𝕦𝕕𝕖𝕟𝕥› ℙ𝕠𝕤𝕤𝕖𝕤𝕤𝕚𝕧𝕖/𝔸𝕝𝕡𝕙𝕒𝕞𝕒𝕝𝕖› 𝕊𝕝𝕠𝕨 𝔹𝕦𝕣𝕟› 𝔸𝕘𝕖 𝔾𝕒𝕡› 𝕄𝕪𝕤𝕥𝕖𝕣𝕪› 𝕊𝕖𝕔𝕣𝕖𝕥 𝕊𝕠𝕔𝕚𝕖𝕥𝕪જ⁀➴ I just want to give a special shout out to the detail, and writing that went into making this book exceptional. The attention to detail and style made this book the perfect mix.‧₊˚ ┊𝙷𝚎 𝚠𝚊𝚜 𝚖𝚘𝚘𝚍𝚢, 𝚕𝚒𝚔𝚎 𝚛𝚊𝚒𝚗𝚢 𝚍𝚊𝚢𝚜 𝚊𝚗𝚍 𝚋𝚒𝚝𝚝𝚎𝚛 𝚌𝚘𝚏𝚏𝚎𝚎. 𝚂𝚎𝚗𝚜𝚞𝚊𝚕 𝚠𝚑𝚒𝚜𝚙𝚎𝚛𝚜 𝚒𝚗 𝚍𝚊𝚛𝚔 𝚌𝚘𝚛𝚗𝚎𝚛𝚜 𝚊𝚗𝚍 𝚝𝚑𝚎 𝚜𝚕𝚘𝚠 𝚋𝚞𝚛𝚗 𝚘𝚏 𝚏𝚒𝚗𝚎 𝚠𝚑𝚒𝚜𝚔𝚎𝚢. 𝙰 𝚝𝚘𝚛𝚖𝚎𝚗𝚝 𝙸 𝚋𝚘𝚝𝚑 𝚑𝚊𝚝𝚎𝚍 𝚊𝚗𝚍 𝚠𝚎𝚕𝚌𝚘𝚖𝚎𝚍 𝚊𝚝 𝚝𝚑𝚎 𝚜𝚊𝚖𝚎 𝚝𝚒𝚖𝚎.𝑭𝒊𝒏𝒂𝒍 𝑻𝒉𝒐𝒖𝒈𝒉𝒕𝒔જ⁀➴ Finishing this book left me feeling sad! I plummeted into this world and now I must leave, and I wish I could have spent more time entrapped in this universe with 𝘋𝘦𝘷𝘳𝘺𝘤𝘬 and 𝘓𝘪𝘭𝘪𝘢. This book was phenomenal, I𝙸 𝚠𝚘𝚞𝚕𝚍 𝚛𝚎𝚌𝚘𝚖𝚖𝚎𝚗𝚍 𝚝𝚑𝚒𝚜 𝚋𝚘𝚘𝚔 𝚊𝚗𝚢 𝙳𝙰𝚈 𝚘𝚏 𝚝𝚑𝚎 𝚠𝚎𝚎𝚔. 𝚁𝚄𝙽 𝚍𝚘𝚗𝚝 𝚆𝙰𝙻𝙺 𝚝𝚘 𝚛𝚎𝚊𝚍 𝚝𝚑𝚒𝚜. It was also slightly reminiscent of one of my favourite books of all time “𝑫𝒂𝒓𝒌 𝑵𝒐𝒕𝒆𝒔”, so being able to read something that hit that mark like that book made me beyond happy.‧₊˚ ┊“𝚆𝚑𝚊𝚝 𝚝𝚛𝚞𝚝𝚑?” 𝙸 𝚊𝚜𝚔𝚎𝚍, 𝚖𝚢 𝚟𝚘𝚒𝚌𝚎 𝚜𝚑𝚊𝚔𝚢. 𝙽𝚎𝚛𝚟𝚘𝚞𝚜. “𝚃𝚑𝚊𝚝 𝙸 𝚠𝚘𝚞𝚕𝚍 𝚔𝚒𝚕𝚕 𝚏𝚘𝚛 𝚢𝚘𝚞 𝚠𝚒𝚝𝚑𝚘𝚞𝚝 𝚊 𝚋𝚎𝚊𝚝 𝚘𝚏 𝚑𝚎𝚜𝚒𝚝𝚊𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗, 𝚘𝚛 𝚛𝚎𝚖𝚘𝚛𝚜𝚎. 𝙰𝚗𝚍 𝚢𝚎𝚝, 𝚊𝚝 𝚝𝚑𝚎 𝚜𝚊𝚖𝚎 𝚝𝚒𝚖𝚎, 𝙸 𝚌𝚘𝚞𝚕𝚍 𝚋𝚎 𝚛𝚎𝚍𝚞𝚌𝚎𝚍 𝚝𝚘 𝚗𝚘𝚝𝚑𝚒𝚗𝚐 𝚖𝚘𝚛𝚎 𝚝𝚑𝚊𝚗 𝚊 𝚙𝚒𝚕𝚎 𝚘𝚏 𝚊𝚜𝚑 𝚠𝚒𝚝𝚑𝚘𝚞𝚝 𝚢𝚘𝚞. 𝙸’𝚖 𝚠𝚎𝚊𝚔 𝚏𝚘𝚛 𝚢𝚘𝚞, 𝙻𝚒𝚕𝚒𝚊.”

✩ 4.75 stars ✩What to Expect:➼ Student x Professor➼ Age Gap➼ Slow Burn➼ Mysterious Island➼ Forbidden Romance➼ Researching a Deadly Parasite➼ Secret Societies➼ Gothic Atmosphere➼ Dark Academia➼ Duet Narration➼ Dual First Person POVPetition for all audio to be duet narration because DANG, this was perfection! 🤌🏻 I feel confident we’ll get a screen adaptation for this one! It already has that cinematic quality and I think it would appeal to a wide audience. I’ll be impatiently waiting for news! 🤞🏻I rarely enjoy dark/gothic romances, so I was really trusting the process with this one and man did it deliver! I’ve said it before, but books that are described as “dark” or “atmospheric” usually put me to sleep. This though… THIS is the exception. 🙌🏻 Such a well crafted and compelling story with EXCELLENT pacing. Every bit of the atmosphere the author created enhanced the plot rather than just making it feel dark and dreary for funsies. The MMC… trust me, you will be OBSESSED! 🥵 He’s dark and twisty, but so good to our girl! Murder, mystery, plot twists and reveals that will keep you on your toes… There’s so much I could stay about this book, but I think this is one you just need to read for yourself. And I do mean NEED! Don’t skip this one friends! Even if you’re like me and this isn’t your preferred genre, make sure this gets added to your TBR!✼　 ҉ 　✼　 ҉ 　✼　 ҉ 　✼　 ҉ 　✼　 ҉ 　✼　 ҉ 　✼Pre-read:Tell me why my brain wants this to be Nocti-daddy-a😆😆But really though… is this scary? 🫣≪ ◦ ❖ ◦ ≫Connect with me onInstagram

2.5⭐️ you just didn't speak to meI think this was supposed to be some kind of slow burn dark academia/fantasy and Devryck and Lilia were not bad main characters but they and their lab activities weren't interesting enough to keep me engaged for 700 pages, to put it mildly it was bordering on torture 😬Usually I'm obsessed with age gap, professor-student to lovers but this was soooo drawn out when the smut finally came the thrill was gone and I had literally fallen asleep 😭

book.title: Right Man, Right Time
book.author: Meghan Quinn
book.description: Running into your ex is never ideal.Running into your ex who is now dating your nemesis is a literal nightmare.That’s how I found myself in a sheer panic, reaching for the closest guy at the bar and kissing him. And to my delight he was easily the most attractive man I’ve ever seen.The only problem is, when I tried to walk away after thanking him for his help, he stopped me with three life-changing words . . .“You owe me.”That’s how I found myself, a college girl, in a fake relationship with a professional hockey player ten years older than me.At first it was easy.He helped me with my internship.I helped keep his ex away from him.We were becoming fast friends.It wasn’t until he started staying the night at my dorm did the rules of our arrangement become complicated.Now my feelings are involved and even though I kissed the right man at the right time, I’m worried he’s just waiting for the clock to run out.

reviews.text: It felt like the female mc was written by a man…

listen….this was cute at times and it did have some funny moments but overall so much of it annoyed me.one thing that will throw me off every damn time is women being pit against each other. so having catty exes and the typical mean girl at work drama was so upsetting to me.i’m all for a good age gap romance but when it makes sense!!! for instance i love ✨birthday girl✨ and that’s a way bigger age gap than this but it just felt like this one wasn’t it. a professional 30 year old hockey player w a 21 year old college student who lives in a dorm just didn’t add up. what do they even have in common? idk.i felt like a lot of it was trying too hard too. it didn’t seem like natural conversations and encounters. it all felt very forced.i did love the found family and seeing the whole friend group again!! they’re all so funny and fun to read about which is why this is a 2 star for me.

Meghan is my favorite RomCom author for a reason!!! She’s so damn funny and also happens to have some panty melting spice that never misses! Her men are so swoony & Silas is no exception. Whew. It seems like I am in my hockey era & I am loving it. Who knew sports were so fun 😛What starts off as an impromptu kiss to make her ex jealous & awful Coworker think she’s in a relationship, ends up being one of the best arrangements Ollie could’ve imagined. She found the right man to kiss at a bar; Silas is a stud muffin who happens to be a very famous hockey player that is needing some loving!Ollie & Silas are such a great duo. Their chemistry was great right off the bat & THEIR BANTER >>> Silas is so sweet but wayyyyy dirtier than I expected. A Jacob’s ladder!? COME ON 💦💦💦 let’s be real that’s the main reason we all want to read this book. And god it was hot. he could get it…all day every day BABBYYYYYY. Ollie is hilarious and doesn’t hold anything back. AND HER GBF, ROSS, IS AMAZING. He is the #1 bestie we all need.Let me tell you, the audiobook experience is one you do NOT want to miss. Daddy Jason Clarke & Stella Hunter are such a perfect duo. And Jason brought Silas to light in the best ways whew. And the duet narration is just so perfect.YOU NEED IT. SHE NEEDS IT. HE NEEDS IT. THEY NEED IT. So just get the book ;)Spice: 🌶🌶🌶.5Tropes:Hockey romanceFake datingAge gap

2 starsBoth MCs were unlikeable.The H was wishy washy and too into his problems if that makes sense. It just took away from seeing him fall for the heroine. His constant self doubts and his obsession with his ex were an absolute buzz kill. And another observation is that no matter what he says about how much he is over his ex in terms of his love/affection for her it wasn’t believable. In his case his actions spoke much louder than his words. He was always observing her whenever she was close by, obsessed with her beauty and grace but later claims it was just him being disgusted by the fact that he ever loved her so much. Mmm, ok, sure. Lie to yourself buddy. Also what really sealed it for me was when the 3rd act break up happened and he sympathised with his ex and reassured her that he will make sure that her job won’t be in danger. This is the same woman that cheated on him for months and played him like a violin for years. I’m sorry but his devotion and obsession with her is too much regardless of how many times he tells himself and others that he is over her.As for the heroine. Gosh. I wish authors would stop trying to make the women they write “quirky”and”oh so different”. She was either immature or too pushy. The only qualities that really did shine. It’s one think to be consistent and to try to persuade someone into a relationship if you like them and you know they like you too, but at some point, give it a break and reassess. And I’m also of the opinion that she had a lot of growing up to do. As a person. She may have found a career but I don’t feel like she is a grounded person.The age gap here was obvious and not because of their respective places in life but because their personalities just didn’t fit. Maybe in a few years they would have had better chemistry overall but at this point their selling point as a couple is the fact that they are attracted to each other sexually.
it is time to ban the third act breakup from romance books

Dnf 26%Should I have given this book more of a chance? Probably. Will I? No.The MMC seemed fine, seems like every other basic white male celebrity athlete character I’ve read.The FMC … holllllly fucking shit I cannot go one more page reading about her. Possibly the most annoying character I’ve come across this year.I do love age gap romances when done well but you could really feel the age difference in this. The FMC was just so immature - I think it was trying to come across as funny and “not like other girls” lol but it was just very tiring and frustrating to read.This is just not for me

If you want to make a ten year age gap work, don’t write your h as an insufferable immature brat, middle school vocabulary speaking child.Ollie is just yuck. Silas is eons ahead of her in life experience and maturity.

DNF @ 41%The moment she went on a word-vomit lie about how they met, I mentally checked out. That was so stupid, and that kind of stupidity in a character is unredeemable for me. Especially when in the following fight scene, she acted like the victim when he was rightfully upset. Just because he fucked up too doesn’t mean her fuck up gets erased. That kind of logic is infuriatingKnew I was done when most of their conversations started to revolve around sex and physical features/attraction. Of course I want them to be physically attracted do them, but how about some emotional development as well? I don’t need to hear for the sixth time how ‘hot’ her boobs are 🙄

Yet another laugh out loud read by Meghan Quinn… that also had me in tears.“𝑰 𝒘𝒂𝒏𝒕𝒆𝒅 𝒕𝒐 𝒕𝒂𝒍𝒌 𝒃𝒆𝒄𝒂𝒖𝒔𝒆 . . . 𝒃𝒆𝒄𝒂𝒖𝒔𝒆 𝑰 𝒅𝒐𝒏’𝒕 𝒘𝒂𝒏𝒕 𝒕𝒉𝒊𝒔 𝒕𝒐 𝒃𝒆 𝒂𝒏 𝒂𝒈𝒓𝒆𝒆𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝒂𝒏𝒚𝒎𝒐𝒓𝒆. 𝑰 𝒘𝒂𝒏𝒕 𝒖𝒔 𝒕𝒐 𝒃𝒆 𝒓𝒆𝒂𝒍.”Silas and Ollie were such a cute match. From the moment they met, it was instant lust. Yet they tried to ignore it and made an agreement to help one another by fake dating. The best part to me was how oblivious Ollie was to the fact that Silas was a well known hockey player. It made their connection so much better. (In my opinion.)The banter between these two was superb. Even the banter between Silas and his teammates, they had be cackling. This has got to be one of my favorite reads of the month.Ollie knows what she wants and does what she has to in order to get it. That’s my favorite quality she has. I love how honest she is as well.Silas is a broken man, after his trust is completely broken by someone who he loved so deeply, it takes him awhile to open up. But his self awareness about everything made him such a lovable character from the beginning.This is not only a romcom, but a story about learning that not everyone will hurt you, it’s okay to trust people and let them in. How important it is to open up about what’s hurting you and remembering to hear someone out when something happens.I loved every second of this book.I wish it never ended.so damn good.TROPES;Age gapHockey romanceFound FamilyFake Dating

Right Man, Right Time - Meghan Quinn⭐⭐⭐⭐.75"𝗜'𝗺 𝗹𝗼𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗺𝘆 𝗺𝗶𝗻𝗱 𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂."Listen, when it comes to baseball and hockey MQ can do no wrong. I stand by that. I was looking forward to Silas so much because a hurt, "I'll never love again" grump is the BEST kind of story. And Ollie DELIVERED. I absolutely adored these two and can't wait to get my physical copy."𝗛𝗲 𝗵𝗮𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗮𝘁𝘁𝗼𝗼𝘀 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗰𝗿𝘂𝗳𝗳𝘆 𝗵𝗮𝗶𝗿. 𝗕𝗿𝗼𝗮𝗱 𝗦𝗵𝗼𝘂𝗹𝗱𝗲𝗿𝘀. 𝗪𝗶𝗰𝗸𝗲𝗱 𝗹𝗶𝗽𝘀. 𝗥𝗼𝗴𝘂𝗶𝘀𝗵 𝗲𝘆𝗲𝘀. 𝗧𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼 𝗱𝗼𝘂𝗯𝘁 𝗵𝗲 𝗮𝗹𝘀𝗼 𝗵𝗮𝘀 𝗮 𝗽𝗶𝗲𝗿𝗰𝗲𝗱 𝗰𝗼𝗰𝗸."Not only were the MCs incredible, but the side character Ross was amazing too! I loved reading about him, the hockey boys and the little bit of the other MMCs from other books *cough JP cough* I had a small issue just surrounding the conflict and how the instigator or 'bad guy' wasn't really in it through out the book a decent amount in the beginning and then not til the end. I felt we need a tad more sprinkled in. And even though I found it to be predictable it did NOT drag on and was resolved rather quickly which I think is awesome.𝗜'𝗺 𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗼𝘂𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗜'𝗹𝗹 𝘀𝗮𝘆 𝗼𝗿 𝗱𝗼 𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘀𝘁𝘂𝗽𝗶𝗱, 𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗲 𝗜 𝗱𝗼𝗻'𝘁 𝗸𝗻𝗼𝘄, 𝗮𝗰𝗰𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗹𝘆 𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗽 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗳𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝗵𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗳𝗶𝗿𝘀𝘁 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗼 𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗹𝗮𝗽. 𝗢𝗿 𝘀𝗮𝘆 𝘀𝗼𝗺𝗲𝘁𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗲...𝗰𝗮𝗻 𝗜 𝘀𝘂𝗰𝗸 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗰𝗼𝗰𝗸 𝗮𝘀 𝗮𝗻 𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝘁𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿?Ollie is a FORCE. I absolutely adored her. She was hilarious, relatable and knew what she wanted in bed...mainly Silas 🥵 Silas...grumpy, sad, never wants to love again Silas. All he needed was a little fire cracker!! And when he calls her by her full name *SWOOON* The tension was so damn good it had my sweating and when you hit the spice?! *fans self* This is book 3 of the series and so far my favorite! I can not wait to see who we get next!!"𝗨𝘀𝗲 𝗺𝗲, 𝗳𝘂𝗰𝗸 𝗺𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀. 𝗠𝗮𝗸𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝘃𝗼𝗶𝗰𝗲 𝗵𝗼𝗮𝗿𝘀𝗲."

3.5 StarsOpinion BreakdownThe Good:The premise. The sexy times. The side characters. The zoo story 😂The Not-So-Good:The h. While I didn't hate her or anything, I did find her somewhat annoying at times. I don't know if that was just the author trying to portray their age gap, or something, but I wasn't feeling her. I also thought the Candace character was a little too OTT in her vindictiveness.The Bad:The ending. It was so abrupt. It was right after the conflict resolution and there was hardly any grovel -- which I do think was necessary after certain words were expressed. I also struggled with the H and how he was with the ex. I just didn't like it.Overall:This was a cute read. I am a sucker for the fake-to-real trope and add in an age gap and an athlete?! I'm in! I didn't really read reviews before jumping in, in hopes of not setting my expectations too high. It was enjoyable and got quite a few laughs out of me, but there were some things that make it so it won't find it new home on my favorites shelf.Brief Summary of the Storyline:This is Ollie and Silas's story. Ollie is in a bar when she finds out that her nemesis and ex are now a couple and she walks up to the closest guy alone that she sees, Silas, and kisses him, pretending he is her new boyfriend. While Silas was completely thrown off, he thought that maybe she could return the favor to get his buddies off his back about his ex that is now working for their professional hockey team. They form an agreement to pretend date, form a friendship, lines get crossed, have some sexy times, and stronger feelings start to develop...and they get a HFN ending.Point Of View (POV):This alternated between Ollie and Silas's POV.Overall Pace of Story:Good until the abrupt ending. I never skimmed and I thought it flowed well otherwise.Instalove:No. They become friends first and then develop stronger feelings.H (Hero) rating:3.5 stars. Silas. I liked him but I didn't like how he didn't allow the h to communicate in their conflict (but he did listen to his ex).h (heroine) rating:3 stars. Ollie. I wasn't too sure about her. She was a little too immature for me in the first half, but I felt like she came around and actually matured as the story progressed...so there is that I guess 🤷🏻‍♀️Sadness level:Low, no tissues neededPush/Pull:Yesthe H pushes away a good bit and I'd say they both pursue the relationship at timesHeat level:Hot. They have some hot tension, chemistry, and scenes -- but not so much it takes away from the story.Descriptive sex:YesOW (Other Woman)/OM (Other Man) drama:Yesthe H's ex is around and causes some drama (the h's ex is too but only mild jealousy)Sex scene with OW or OM:Not with mcsthere is a scene with the H's ex and a woman (man watching and masturbating) at beginning of storyCheating:Not with mcsSeparation:YesThey are separated shortly (week/s?), but there was no mention or suggestion of other partners during this time.Possible Triggers:Yesthe H was cheated on by his ex and he witnessed it (on page), mentions of potential verbal abuse (h's dad - off page), and the H is ~10 years older than the h (31/21)Closure:This ends super abruptly right after the conflict was resolved. I need much more to call it anything but a HFN ending.they're living together and have shared "ILYs"Safety:This one should beSafefor most safety gang readers-Does Nothave cheating-Does Nothave a descriptive sex scene with OW/OM (with mcs)-Doeshave the H pushing the h away-Doeshave a separation between the h and H-Does Nothave abuse-Does Nothave a HEA ending
