4 fake-relationship starsAnd to find out he has a Jacob’s ladder? Good LAWD, I’m in!! (Extra points for it 🤷🏻‍♀️, because, well, I’m intrigued 😮‍💨).Also, the audiobook is duet narration 👏🏻 my FAVORITE kind.The prologue opens up with Silas coming home from a game and finding his girlfriend cheating with two other people. He breaks up with her and makes it a point not to trust easily anymore- no more serious relationships (not to mention he spent a decade with her).Two years later, a random woman comes up to him and asks him to pretend to be her boyfriend so she can save face in front of her ex who’s dating her nemesis. Silas (31) decides to go along with it and kiss Ollie (21) to help her out. She tells him she owes him.When he finds out his cheating ex is going to be working with the Agitators, he decides to take Ollie up on her offer. He needs her to fake date him so he can show his ex he’s moved on (and so she doesn’t try to do anything with him) and, in return, he’ll help Ollie on her journalism assignment that she’s required to do for the internship she’s doing, since she has to write about hockey players, the sport, and the Agitators.But, both of them start developing real feelings 👀What I loved/liked: Possible Spoilers—the 🔥. MQ delivers. We get sooo many teasing scenes 👀. The sauna scene was 😮‍💨 and nothing even happened 😅. That’s the best placement of towels I’ve ever seen 🤷🏻‍♀️. Ollie’s very open smexually and Silas appreciates it 💁🏻‍♀️. When she sits on him at a hockey event and gets him… “excited”, he has to get her back in her dorm room. And oh LAWD 🥵. A hand necklace? A commanding “swallow”? 🫠- yeah. That was me 💦. It’s on the slow burn side- home run happens past 70%, and it was sooooo worth it 😅💦😉—cereal eating FTW: that was her bday present and it was good🥵 he eats his breakfast well 😮‍💨 even when she reciprocates, he has to reciprocate back again😉🤷🏻‍♀️🔥.—duuuurty mouths. On both. Obviously, I love his more 😉 thanks the LAWD that he does a lot of it—Jacob’s ladder. It needs its own bullet 🫠 I should add that neither are afraid of using toys 😅. Fiiiine by me 💁🏻‍♀️(I’m whispering this… but I feel like it would huuuurt 🤣😅)—the 🤣 moments. Omg. I laughed and snorted MANY times 😅. I know many found their “meet cute” stupid and too convoluted, but I was cracking up 😭. The way the narrators acted during this scene made it even better 🤣😅. Even the text messages between Ollie/Ross and Silas/Posey had me giggling. The last MQ I read before this was Runaway Groomsman, so I appreciated the laughs (since I wasn’t laughing much in that one)—many options ❤️ Silas’s amazing at giving Ollie options- from her sandwiches, to her coffee, to her creamers, and I found it super cute 🥹—how JP Silas is 😅👏🏻. I loooove when the MMC gets jealous, and here, Silas get jealous and possessive 😈 MINEMINEMINE 💁🏻‍♀️—I’m a suckaaa for a man in a suit 🫠 whyyy? I don’t know 🤷🏻‍♀️ I just know I was here for all the times Silas takes off his jacket… and well, more after 🥵—the side characters. I loved Ross 🥰 he’s such a good friend. Not to mention Posey and Pacey. The way Posey tells Sarah off was award-winning 👏🏻. I really love all of these characters ❤️—previous books’ MCs appearances from other series. Besides the other characters from this series, we had the Cane brothers (all three delicious men) plus Ryot and even had mentions of Canoodle, CA (Runaway Groomsman). I definitely found myself smiling and feeling like a fan girl 🤣—the OW smackdowns. Since there are TWO ow (one is Silas’s ex, the other one a coworker that’s dating Ollie’s ex), I luuurved when they got put in their place 😮‍💨. I normally don’t like the FMC taking out the trash, but I liked it here 👏🏻What I didn’t like/wanted more of: SPOILERS—lack of a grovel 😑. Look. I get it. The “betrayal” scene (not OP-related) was tough. Silas felt betrayed, but he’s so upset he doesn’t let Ollie explain herself much. The way he treats her was HARSH 😬. The male narrator did an amazing job being pisssssed in the audiobook, so for Ollie to not let him stew in his regret juices when he finds out the truth irritated me 😑. She loses so much and she takes him back so quickly. Some might find what he “does” to get her back enough. But not me 😐😑. I wanted more. I DID like that we see how much he’s suffering, but I wanted to see more of it 🤣. I will say- I looove how Ross stands up to Silas and tells him off 💁🏻‍♀️that’s why I love him 🥹—more of an epilogue. It was too quick for me. What happened to Roberts? To Candace? To Sarah? Before the end of the book, it’s implied in the Jock Report that there’s going to be repercussions for what happened, but we don’t see it. It left a bitter taste in my mouth 😐😒. As for Sarah, I hope she gets an incurable disease 🤣—fart face? Really 🤦🏻‍♀️🙄. It was suuuuuper immature! I have an 18 year-old niece and she wouldn’t have ever mentioned that as a possible nickname 🙄 and Ollie’s 21—how does Ross and Ollie know Silas has a d piercing??? Ross’s like “I bet he’s pierced”Like…Hooooooowww?? Is there a universal d piercing sign 🤔? Do they walk a certain way?Because, if that’s the case, then I’m gonna be VERY observant of men I see walking 🤣👀… don’t be surprised if you see me ogling men’s gaits 👀.I also found it intriguing how Ollie and wasn’t intimidated by it 😅. I’m glad Silas found his match 🤣Overall, I loved it ❤️⚠️safety squad ⚠️-no cheating*/sharing-cheating: the H was in a relationship and caught his previous girlfriend (OW1) cheating on him-OWD: the H’s ex (OW1) is working for his team. She causes some drama. The H doesn’t want her, but there was a quick minute where the h thought he DID have feelings. OW2 works with the h and causes lots of drama in her job- she’s also in a relationship with the h’s ex-OMD: the h’s ex works with her and is in a relationship with one of her colleagues-hand necklace/dual dirty talk/phone sex/edging/spanking-HEA/epilogue

DNF. I got about 25% into this book and could not take anymore. It was over the top silly and I couldn’t make myself like Ollie. At all. Silas was a thirty one year old man and there is no way he would’ve wanted to be with someone as young and immature as Ollie unless he wanted a trophy girlfriend.

the writing in this was ass god bless 🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏🙏 and it was riddled with nonstop, mind-numbingly stupid clichés seen at levels not even ao3 could touch. i can certainly see why it’s a goodreads hit, but we need to take a closer look at the tiktokification of the romance genre! stat!

WELL…this book has quite the opening scene 😳 and for once it didn’t particularly make me want to read on 🫣“Better watch out, man, feral beast incoming." 😈Honestly, this book took a lot of warming up to. I think I set my expectations too high after the first 2 books. I just didn’t feel that insta-connection that I did with the previous characters 😕Did I read on? YesDid I enjoy it? YesWas it fun? Boy, yeah!It definitely picked up as the book went on BUT it did lack a little something…Rewind ⏪ 2 years: Silas had a tragic start to the book…finding his future fiancé cheating on him 😢Fast forward ⏩ to the present: We suddenly met a new character - Ollie“My name is Ollie. I’m in a really tough spot, and I’m so sorry, but I’m about to kiss you because I need to save face in front of my ex-boyfriend, who is now dating my nemesis. If you don’t stop me in three seconds, I’m going in.”Ollie is such a boss! And before you know it, we have a fake dating agreement on the table 🤭I am happy to say that this story definitely grew on me. I found Silas a bit hard to warm up to. He has a very hard, cold exterior that is difficult to break through. But, I did like Ollie; mostly because she knew how to stick up for herself and communicate what she wanted/needed. For 21, she was very mature, extremely loyal and honest."I’m feeling like I have a touch of whiplash with you. One second, you’re incredibly sweet and helping me through this odd arrangement we have, then the next, you ignore me completely, and now you’re back to the first guy.” 😅Silas is very much shut off, afraid of feeling again. He takes things very seriously, holds his cards close to his chest, and never shows weakness, but somehow Ollie manages to loosen him up and allows us to see his true self - the man he is past the high walls he’s erected over the years to protect his heart.Despite a rather drawn out beginning, I really enjoyed watching this relationship mature. Once they finally got over the fact that they both actually had feelings for each other, the story was much more enjoyable. Ollie was definitely the star of the story, for me. She was the one that brought all the fun as well as breaking down Silas' walls.Although this wasn’t my favourite book in the series, I still had an enjoyable time reading it. It had a lot more spice than the others novels which is the main reason I didn’t enjoy it as much; it was just a bit OTT for my liking and I didn’t feel as though the relationship was as genuine as the others ❤️‍🔥 However, there was still a lot to like about this story. I enjoyed seeing the other characters from the series (and a cameo from another series). I am very much looking forward to the next book…“And there is no doubt in my mind that we have a bright future ahead of us because she’s mine. I’m hers, and it all started with a surprising, soul-changing kiss.”💋💫3.5 🌟🌟🌟💫

4.5 starsThis was fake dating perfection! I really loved how the hero and heroine meet and how they end up fake dating in order to benefit both of them. It was fun how she had no idea he was a hockey star and I loved the age gap and how they dealt with that! He knew she was so much younger, but that didn't stop them from having insane chemistry and getting along so well! The heroine did act a bit too immature for me at times, but I still loved their connection and, for the most part, I loved them together.I did think this was a little too long and dragged just a little in the middle, but it was so good! I loved the hockey and how the heroine would play games with the hero since he was using her to make an ex jealous. And she was a journalism major, so things got really intense at the end with their futures on the mind. The conflict usually makes me annoyed, but I loved how it played out and how they got their HEAs!The audiobook narrators were also perfection. I definitely recommend picking this one up!!

Great characters and terrific duet narration!The following ratings are out of 5:Romance: 💙❤️💚💜💖Steam: 🔥🔥🔥🔥Story/Plot: 📕📗📘📙World building: 🌏🌍🌏🌍🌎Character development: 😊😅🥹😟🥰Narration: 🎙🎙🎙🎙🎙Narration Type: Duet NarrationThe heroine:Ollie Owens- She is nearly in her senior year of college, majoring in Journalism and has been working as an intern all summer. At the beginning of the summer her boyfriend broke up with her and she just found out that he is now dating her work nemesis. In response, she picks a random guy and tells them he is her new boyfriend and they have been having great sex. She goes over and asks him to pretend to be her boyfriend.The Hero:Silas Taters- He is a star right wing on the Vancouver Agitators pro hockey team. He finds his girlfriend cheating on him with both a woman and a man, in his bed no less. He finds out she has been cheating on him for a long time and kicks her to the curb. However, months later when he finds out that she got a job with the Agitators, he is disturbed to come across her.The Story:when a beautiful girl asks him to pretend to be her boyfriend to make her ex jealous, he plays along, then tells her she owes him. He decides to make her his fake girlfriend so when he has to go to a work outing where his ex-girlfriend will be, it will be easier for him. Ollie sees the benefit of being fake dating and the two come to an agreement, though they specify some rules, including no sex. The two get to know each other and really like each other despite their ten-year age difference (she’s 21 and he is 31).I really liked the fact that these two got to know each other pretty well before they got intimate. There were parts of this book that were hilarious, especially the strange story that Ollie comes up with about how they met. It isn’t long before the chemistry between them heats up and it isn’t helped by all of their talk about sex. Ollie is pretty funny and seems a lot more grown up than many 21-year-olds. I really liked her attitude and fearlessness.This audiobook was told in multiple points of view via duet narration and was narrated by Jason Clarke and Stella Hunter. These are two of my favorite narrators. Jason has a deep smoky voice and is great at showing emotion through his voice. He sounds so sexy, especially when dirty talking and during the intimate scenes. Stella has a youthful, soft voice which is extremely pleasant, and she sounds perfect for Ollie.Blog|Goodreads|Facebook|Instagram|Pinterest|BookBub

Meghan Quinn is the only author that can literally make me laugh out loud while reading. I absolutely love her writing and when I heard she was writing the 3rd Agitators book, I was so excited.This is the 3rd in the series, but reads as a standalone. The other two do not have to be read in order to read this one, however, you will get some easter eggs/mini spoilers from the other books. I also liked the littler tidbits sprinkled through-out of her other books.You start off by learning why Silas and Sarah break up and then immediately jump 2 years forward meeting Ollie. Ollie is a 21 year old college student, who is out with her friend Ross finding out what their project is for their internship. Her nemesis, Caroline, comes in and you find out that she is dating Ollie's ex. In a complete lapse of judgment, Ollie goes up to guy and kisses him saying that they are dating, that guy is Silas Taters.There was instant chemistry and lust between Ollie and Silas but they ignore it and decided to help one another by fake dating. The banter between these two was phenomenal. Ollie is a strong FMC, she knows what she want and does what she needs to get it. I loved seeing the growth in Silas has he deals with his past and really opens up to Ollie. I couldn't get enough of these two, I read this in one day.I recommend this one if you love hockey romances, fake dating, age gap, slow burn, rom-coms.Overall: 4.5Spice: 3

i got got like a total sucker because i saw a tik tok about the male character and the fan cast used was hot. that’s what i get honestly…i had completely forgotten i said i’d never read this author again just last year, but here we are… at the risk of sounding like an absolute hater, not everyone should write age gap romance.to summarize, this was one of the most juvenile, immature heroines i have read in awhile. i am tired of authors trying to write sassy, quirky heroines when half the time it falls so flat. for 21 this character read very childish, almost as someone play-acting adult. but the colloquialisms and mannerisms of actual early 20-somethings also weren’t there so the character oddly felt aged up in a way, which compounded with the emotional and mental immaturity of someone the age she was written to be, made for a weird character. it was contradicting.for age gap romances to work i feel like there has to be a similar level of maturity where both characters trend on the side more emotional maturity; or at least a challenge from the younger to the older character’s maturity that makes them question themselves and kind of puts them on a more even playing field. here i found neither happened. they were similarly mature but that’s because the hero was a jackass. the hero being 31 didn’t come across other than the geriatric comments because he was such an emotionally immature ass whose most memorable trait to me was being horny for the heroine.i would have at least enjoyed the romance if i felt there was any but i just don’t like this author’s style of writing romance. to me it felt like there was very little development or buildup to the characters getting to know and liking each other let alone loving each other. it felt insta love to me and i felt like once it got sexual there was little effort into the romantic side outside of them fucking like bunnies. the central conflict was underdeveloped until it suddenly wasn’t, to add end of story drama that felt forced and random. weaving in the conflict into the storyline frankly isn’t that hard but when all your characters do is fuck and talk about c*ck and c*nt… in fact, more time was spent recounting the stories about how side characters met and fell in love to kind of get you to buy-in to the series but that made me more annoyed because if i wanted to read those books i would.idk i wanted to like this when i got it on kindle but this was such a waste of my time i ended up skimming once i hit the 50% mark to avoid a dnf this early on in the year.also it’s 2023… we can stop it with the ageist comments toward the “evil” ex in age gap. something about decade younger heroines having these gotcha moments with the antagonists where they essentially attack them for being old feels just silly and catty at this point.

Ollie is just the worst basically a child, Silas is a 31 year old man and she’s 21, look I don’t love age gaps but if done right I can be able to enjoy them but this one was just frustrating because she was such a brat and immature, Silas was fine your basic white hockey player, I liked his character when he appeared on the last 2 books and wanted a good story for him, sadly if didn’t happen, you don’t know how disappointed I am right now

I adored this one!!! Right Man, Right Time is another hysterical, heartwarming rom com by Meghan Quinn. When Ollie needs to prove to her ex and his new girlfriend she’s over him, she randomly picks Silas Taters at bar and kisses him. After chatting, the two of them realize that they could help each other by pretending to date.Fake dating will forever be a favorite trope of mine. The fake intimacy in public paired with the mutual pining and lust??? I melt. Every. Single. Time.Silas was so hot. (I mean, he has a Jacobs ladder piercing, so if my review doesn’t convince you, that alone should). Ollie is a spitfire and I also adored her sm. She’s loud, unapologetic, and fiercely loyal.I loved the slow burn fake dating friends-to-lovers arc. Usually in fake dating they immediately jump to the lusty phase, but Ollie and Silas truly formed a friendship first, and it was really refreshing.Since this is a 4.5 read for me, I do have to say the one negative drawback for me was the OW drama. Ollie is not only dealing with Silas’s ex, but she also has an arch nemesis at her internship, who happens to also be a woman. I don’t love pinning women against each other, and there’s multiple points in the book where not only Silas is comparing Ollie to his ex (always in a positive way, but still), but the two women are in direct conflict. The subplot with Ollie’s nemesis also wasn’t super enjoyable for me (considering she’s also now dating Ollie’s ex).Aside from the OW drama (cheating isn’t the conflict), I adored this book!Read if you like✨ hockey romances✨ fake dating✨ friends-to-lovers✨ 🍆 piercing✨ emotional rom coms

SCREAMING OVER SILAS!!!! I really loved him and the character development he went through. But Ollie was a super cool female character that I really loved! I loved the banter. I was literally laughing out loud listening to this book. Which never usually happens. But the age gap? The fake dating? Everything was soo good!!

Right Man, Right Timeis a standalone hockey romance by authorMeghan Quinn. It is rom-com, told from both POV with a HEA.Tropes:✔️ sports/hockey✔️ age gap✔️ fake datingRight Man, Right Time follows Silas & Ollie. Silas is an NHL player whose heart was badly broken two years ago and now his ex-almost-fiancée has started working in his team’s marketing group. Ollie is an ambitious 21YO journalism major who is resolute to make a name for herself.Ollie is also determined not to let her Caroline, her nemesis, get the better of her. She’s already the boss’ favorite and dating Ollie’s ex. So when Ollie goes out with her BFF and stumbles into Caroline and her ex, Ollie kisses the first guy she sees and asks him to pretend he’s her girlfriend to save face.It turns out Silas Taters wants her to return the favor. The two are forced to spend time together, real feelings for each other start to develop but will they be enough to overcome the Silas’ trust issues?Even though the chemistry between Ollie and Silas was off the charts incendiary, they establish a formal (on a napkin) business transaction notarized (with a kiss) and include a no “whorehouse” policy. As you can see, the banter between them was extraordinary.Ollie is my new favorite heroine! She is strong, witty, smart, confident, she knows what she wants and isn’t afraid to get it and did I mention strong? Her fearlessness worked well with Silas, who is 10 years her senior. She was unapologetically ambitious and determined to succeed, which made her a refreshing and inspiring protagonist.I truly hurt for Silas, and appreciated how he opens up to Ollie, he was a relatable and sympathetic character. Watching him grow and change throughout the book was a satisfying journey.Adding to the enjoyment where the secondary characters. Starting with Ross, Ollie’s BFF and Silas’ teammates as well as cameos from characters from another of MQ’s series.Overall, "Right Man, Right Time" was a delightful read that combined heartwarming romance with hilarious shenanigans. If you're looking for a fun and engaging rom-com with a sports twist, I highly recommend giving this book a try.★ Hero: 🏒🏒🏒🏒🏒★ Heroine: 👤👤👤👤👤★ Plot: 📚📚📚📚★ LOL moments: 📚📚📚📚📚★ Romance: 💕💕💕★ Sexiness: 🍆🍆🍆🍆🍆★ Overall Rating: 4.5★ I received an ARC of this title from the author. The excerpts are from that copy. I am voluntarily reviewing this title.★See all my book edits on Instagram

I mean Jason can read the phone book and still be 🔥🫠 And Stella us just so fun and flirty💕 Best shower scene ever 🥵Gosh I wish someone would’ve throat punched Sarah😤 luckily for us Ollie puts her in her place. I love that Ollie has zero fucks to give when it comes to Sarah!💕fave quotes📖“Best pillows I’ve ever seen.”“Where did you get them?” Posey asks. “Target?”“Target?” Hornsby scoffs. “These are West Elm quality.” “Target has great quality pillows, you jackass,” Posey replies.—-omg these guys are the best! Holy hotness and adorableness! Right Man, Right Time was MORE than right- it was pitch perfect! Perhaps my favorite Meghan Quinn story to date. Add in some of my favorite tropes- fake dating, age gap, and hockey...well, I'm a goner for this book. Charming, swoony, epically hilarious, and ultra sexy- I could not put this down! The banter and chemistry - ah, I'm so madly in love with these two. I loved them together, totally addicted to their dynamic. And all the hockey bros made the best appearances. I love this world- and I never want to leave it. Meghan's signature wit paired with her endearing charm wove together perfectly for a story that delivered all the feels and all the heat. I was smitten, charmed, shamelessly grinning, sighing and swooning, crying and screaming- and laughing, there was SO MUCH LAUGHING! Both characters were quirky and flawed, in true MQ fashion, but most importantly they were LIKABLE and relatable, too. You rooted for them- I loved them individually, I loved them even more together.The dual narration of the audio was amazing. Both narrators brought the essence of these characters to life with ease- plenty of emotive expression, perfect comedic timing, and the vocal chemistry on point. This book is perfect for audio, especially in the dual format- the banter, the texting, the flirting, the sexy times- oh my, it translated perfectly. Kudos to the narrators, this is by far one of my favorite romance listens. I loved this story- an instant re-read and truly the best of Meghan Quinn.

ETA: I take back what I said in my original review. Fuck Silas. Why the hell did he trust his cheating ex over Ollie??? LIKE NOOOO FUCK YOU VERY MUCH. You shoulda grovelled your ass off buddy. Ollie deserves better AFUCKINGMEN.Gotta admit it, my girl Ollie's got some serious lady balls on her. The way she was so brave with being vulnerable with Silas? Yeah, I could never, so just for that she deserves a huge round of applause. As for Silas...I know how hard it can get when you have trust issues. All it takes is one second for one single person to break your trust. But it takes a whole lifetime for you to find that person who'll help regain that trust. And that was Ollie for Silas. This book was the perfect blend of spicy, swoony and emotional moments and I absolutely LOVED it! I'm looking forward to reading Eli and Penny's book as well as the Cane Brothers' stories eeeeeepppp!!! Fyi, I gave it a star less because I feel like the ending was a bit abrupt and I would have liked to see Silas grovel more.
I was set to give this book a higher rating but that epilogue left me unsatisfied. 😤

**Audio Review**Donkey pervert for the win! Right Man, Right Time by Meghan Quinn was a HOT, hilarious age-gap hockey romance that I devoured from start to finish. Meghan proves she is the queen of the slow-burn by creating all that delectable steamy tension that eventually combusts and will have you fanning yourself, hard. (Yes, lady!) Silas and Ollie’s romance starts out fun and flirty, and then erupts (So spicy!) and grows into so much more!Stella Hunter and Jason Clarke narrate the audiobook in duet and are simply aural delicious magic every time they team up! Stella captured Ollie’s bold, witty, and spitfire persona perfectly showcasing her budding confidence and undeniable love and devotion for Silas. Jason brought his signature enticing growly tone to an emotional performance (Hello chills!) of broken, broody yet lovable Silas which made him even more irresistible! (Swoon!) Not to mention, Jason’s portrayal of Ross, Ollie’s reliable gay best friend, had me grinning from ear to ear. Stella and Jason both bring the HEAT by portraying Ollie and Silas’ yearning and burning magnetic chemistry!Overall, I LOVED this book! If you are a fan of a hockey romance with a hunky grumpy hockey star, a sassy firecracker journalism major, fake relationships, getting back at your exes, dedicated besties, fabricated doctor appointment meet-cutes, family skate dates, zoo adventures, dorm sleepovers, oral birthday presents, steamy sauna spice, texting mishaps with teammates, Jacob’s ladder piercing, all of Quinn’s signature angst, and a HEA that will hit the RIGHT spot, then you will love Silas and Ollie’s romance. I always look forward to more from Meghan Quinn.

book.title: Hush Hush
book.author: Lucia Franco
book.description: A new, seductive forbidden romance that caters to Manhattan's elite...I knew the rules:Never reveal my true identity.Play the game, give the illusion.Don’t get close to the clients.The dark and glamorous lifestyle of the rich and shameless open my eyes to a lavish world of sin and wealth, and a man I can’t have.A man I desperately want—James Riviera.We're treading a fine line as we live the ultimate double life until we make a startling discovery that tests both our loyalties.I only had to follow the rules, but rules are meant to be broken.

reviews.text: HUSH HUSH:is a steamy full length New Adult & College Romance Novel by new to me author Lucia Franco.5+++++ STARS!! 2019 FAVOURITE BOOK!!This book is undeniably unputdownable and unique in every sense. This is not just your ordinary romance. It's insanely addictive and extremely intense.This isn’t going to be everyone’s cup of tea. And that’s ok too! But as you can see I loved it!!STOP THE BUS!! HOLY SHIT THIS WAS HOT!!🔥🔥This is going to be one of the hardest reviews I've ever had to write up!! First off that synopsis is soooooo freaking vague it's laughable, which just adds to the whole mystery of this story. One piece of advice, go into this one blind, you'll thank me for it later!! But before jumping into this leave your morals at the door because this will shove at all your comfort zones like no other book!! For me this is truly how a forbidden book should be written!!Once I started this I couldn't put it down..Like I literally couldn't..I even told the partner to go fishing (says me never)!! Just so I could read this in peace, without any interruptions!! I devoured it, Inhaled it, Loved it!! This book was beyond scorching, everything about our silver fox James got me hot, bothered and combusting!!We're introduced to college student Aubrey Adams just shy of her twenty first birthday and her bestie Natalie. Aubrey is attending Fordham University on a full scholarship, majoring in Developmental Sociology, she's had to claw her way through life, coming from nothing, losing both parents tragically and raised single handedly by her ageing grandmother, life hasn't been a bunch of roses for our heroine.Between nannying and washing and folding strangers clothes at the local laundromat and scrimping every penny she can to send to her grammy, she struggles to keep her head above water in the daily rat race of New York City. Until her bestie comes up with a solution to all her money woes..But at what cost?? Is she about to sell her soul to the devil??Her bestie makes unbelievable money at a high end establishment at Sanctuary Cove which caters to the elite. There are three rules that must be abided by: 1: You keep your personal life separate. 2: You never reveal your true identity 3: You NEVER get close to a client. Because realistically these men will never leave their wives.It's here that she meets a silver haired fox James Riviera, a much older distinguished gentleman, a top notch lawyer with a heavy New York Accent with a body to die for, tattoos marring his body. The attraction between them is undeniable, it sizzles right off the pages. But he's a married man. He's as forbidden as one can get. Will she walk away or will she take just one taste??* Teaser used is off the authors facebook page *

5 Shameless Stars ⭐️Now this is the book, the kind that will blow your mind even if it at times you are perturbed with what you are reading. Believe the hype, Hush Hush was sensationally scandalous and fanbloodytastic!"Yeah. I'm an angel with black wings born with no morals or modesty, and legs for days that spread like holy water."I will remain vague because as a reader you will want to fully experience the magic and debauchery that Lucia Franco has managed to weave. Because if you enjoy the following tropes you need to grab this immediately....*Age Gap Romance* Scorching Sex Scenes* Taboo Themes* Morally Questionable* Breathtaking & ForbiddenAubrey Adams is a 21 year old college student burning the candle at both ends. Coming from a life of very little she uses brains, sass and hard work to accomplish her goals. Studying and holding down two jobs is no easy feat when trying to support herself and her cherished Grammie. Sometimes a girl just deserves a break. But how far does one go before they sell their soul?James Riviera, richer then god with sex appeal to match shouldn’t want a girl young enough to be his daughter. Between laughing, cringing, panting and crying, this was definitely by far one of my favourite books ever 🔥🔥

ON THE RADAR!Stand-alone.Blurb:Book Blurb:A new, seductive forbidden romance that caters to Manhattan's elite...I knew the rules:Never reveal my true identity.Play the game, give the illusion.Don’t get close to the clients.The dark and glamorous lifestyle of the rich and shameless open my eyes to a lavish world of sin and wealth, and a man I can’t have.A man I desperately want—James Riviera.We're treading a fine line as we live the ultimate double life until we make a startling discovery that tests both our loyalties.I only had to follow the rules, but rules are meant to be broken.

So, this review took me much longer than expected. Complete, utter disappointment isn’t accurate enough to describe my thoughts or feelings on this.Hush, Hushis horrendous to read. Sex workers are shamed and criticised; drugs are abused to cope with sexual situations that border sexual assault, with the acts glamourised to somehow excuse everyone’s behaviours; everyone’s emotions are belittled over and over; constant lies and secrets infiltrate every scenario because the characters are too childish to function somewhat normally for two seconds. It made me so uncomfortable to read this toxic representation of supposed romance.I’m disheartened by this hero and heroine’s characterisation, and some low rated reviews on this “romance” because they perpetuate an enormous societal issue: slut shaming. Everyone, it’s fucking 2019 (incase you forgot), though we continue to criticise sex workers and women’s sexuality, fiction or reality. Sex workers become “others” without morals, dignity or integrity and somehow that allows us “normals” to have authority to ridicule someone’s life choices? How do sex workers differ from us, when we both effectively sell our time and bodies for labour in exchange for money and goods? Our word choice carries connotations that affect others’ understanding of messages and approaches to subjects. Calling the heroine a slut, whore or prostitute (derogatorily) almost every chapter dehumanises her and distances us “normal women” by forcing us into bad girl versus good girl contests. Referring to female sex workers as prostitutes isn’t uncommon, but this gross trend enforces a solemn reminder how terrible our understanding of this industry is.“It's time to pregame,” she says dropping a pill into my hand. “We're celebrating this birthday in true New York City fashion—with a few shots and some Molly.”Substance abuse is an issue within most societies and the author’s casual, unaffected approach to opioid reliance and glamorisation of said usage without significant commentary is distressing. Furthermore, the reliance is encouraged because the heroine isn't comfortable with fucking strangers—there’s no dubious consent here. She doesn’t want to fuck everyone, consumed by anxiety despite her actions, but she’s forced into sitautions that push her into washing down percoset with wine because she’s desperate to earn quick money. It is more worrisome that there’s limited discussion on soft or hard limits with clients—filling in some questionnaire isn’t enough when you don’t know your client, or even meet them beforehand. I can’t even begin to explain the dangers that surround this scenario, especially as violence against sex workers is at an increase.“The universe is cruel for putting us together, but I don’t feel bad because what I feel when I’m with you is what I’ve been wanting all along, I just never knew it until I met you.”Aubrey Abrams is dull and vain; too self-absorbed, floating within a conceited bubble to consider how her constant lies belittle everyone’s feelings, or how her selfish actions make an impact on their lives in irrevocable, significant ways. She’s a compulsive liar, an unapologetic cheater and slut-shamer extraordinaire—a shit human really with little, if any, regret. For example, when Aubrey’s asked to be exclusive with a specific client, in exchange for millions of dollars, she accepts but without any intention of honouring the conditions: to stop dating her boyfriend and escorting. However, when the truth is unveiled, she has the audacity to accuse this client of acting unreasonable and controlling. She’s unconcerned with hurting this client and rather than feel remorse for cheating on Daniel, her boyfriend (who admitted to having been cheated on before), she’s more concerned about Natalie’s reaction. Zero self-awareness, much?I know I should look at the dissolution of our clandestine agreement as a blessing in disguise, but it’s also undeniably heartbreaking. We were hopeless from the start.Rude doesn’t begin to cover the horrid nature of James Riviera, an abhorrent hero with a sexist attitude used so casually, it is outright sickening. James’ unfortunate loveless marriage doesn’t condone his continuous blatant cheating, an act fuelled by an unloving wife with a hate for kink; nor does it excuse his blasé usage of sexist slurs like slut or whore to belittle Aubrey, an escort who doesn’t adhere to his conditions. I find it ironic that James reiterates the values of family, but does a shit effort at following through with said announcements of significance by cheating on his wife (she isn’t innocent in this, however it doesn’t excuse his behaviour) and lying to his daughter.Hush, Hushcould’ve delivered a narrative that dismembered misinformed criticisms of escorting created and supported by sexism. Instead, it shames women’s life choices and sexuality; glamourises substance abuse; condones cheating (there’s a massive lack of self-reflection on this) within a myriad of various other issues that makes this a backwards narrative. Sex work becomes a convenient excuse to purport a forbidden age-gap romance and create a suitable enough reason to introduce drama.Hush, Hushalso had the slowest introduction between the two leading characters and was filled with useless conversations that held no plot importance whatsoever—it was boring filler. I’m sorry.Follow me on:Blog|Instagram

Hush, Hushgave us a seductive, forbidden world and a taste of what’s it’s like to have everything your heart's desire and still at the same time have nothing at all. Money, power, sex, desire, betrayal, you name it…. And this story had it.Twenty one year old Aubrey is your average College girl. She has a best friend who adores her, a grandmother who would give her the shirt off her back, and she’s working two jobs to help support herself and her grandmother. Thinking about her future and what she’s going to do after she graduates, Aubrey knows that the financial road is going to be a long and hard one. When she’s presented with an opportunity to make a lot of money, life as she knows it will never be the same especially with the one condition that’s required of her: Leave your morals at the door.I’m going to be really honest right now and say that not only did I struggle with this book, I struggled with rating this as well. I had a hard time getting into this one up until I reached the 57% mark and most of it had to do with the heroine Aubrey. I couldn’t connect with her on any level and I found her annoying if I’m being honest. Some of her actions not only disappointed me, but I was also angry and the only saving grace for me, was when the hero was introduced and a secret was revealed.I’m a huge fan of this author and even though I was expecting something different with this book, I have to say that she really kept me intrigued towards the end and I have to give her credit for that. I didn’t love this one as much as I was hoping to but what didn’t work for me, might work for someone else.
A reread that was necessary. Forever will be my favourite book.

This one is hard for me to rate. While I didn't love the story and can definitely see why people have problems with it, I was completely addicted and actually wanted the couple to find their HEA. The ending did wrap up wayyyyy to quickly and I didn't like how hypocritical he was with her line of work (he was a client, but then he wants her to quit and demeans her for her line of work). There is also cheating in here and I didn't think the cheating on her part was necessary considering she didn't even like her boyfriend. I don't mind cheating that's done for a reason (if that makes sense? It has to be believable in the book (NOT THAT IT'S OKAY IN REAL LIFE)). In here, the heroine just dragged a different guy along and hurt him, which I found very selfish of her. But, like I said, I found myself actually rooting for them as a couple and wanted them to work. So, yeah, my feelings are very conflicted about this one!

I absolutely LOVED this book. Yep, that's right. My morals actually couldn't give a damn less about some of the more over the line elements. Lucia Franco hit this one right on the head. Having a background with Manhattan and the powerful there, I was almost suspicious how she nailed the way a certain echelon of the city truly does work.But enough about that - I'm too all consumed by James Riviera, the sexiest Silver Fox I've ever come across! I loved how commanding he was, I loved how suave he was, I melted over his tattoos and their meanings. I had absolutely no problem with the age gap - when you find someone that is the one you connect with that's the most valuable commodity there is. And Aubrey and James connected completely.Aubrey is the heroine here. She's on a full ride at Fordham and rooms with Natalie, her absolute bestie. Where Natalie comes from a rich family and doesn't struggle, Aubrey tolls away working at a laundromat and as a nanny and scrimps by to make ends meet. She also tries to take care of her grammy, the only family she has. While Nat refuses to take money from her family, she makes out really well as a 'shot girl' or so Aubrey believes. As Aubrey struggles more to make ends meet, Nat finally reveals the true source of her income and presents Aubrey with the same working opportunity she has.And that is how Aubrey comes to meet James Riviera. He may be 52 to her 21 but the two of them have insane attraction and connect immediately. And although this story would be controversial for many, I absolutely rooted for these two to end up together. James is sex on a stick and kinky to boot, a complete alpha with a touch of sweet on the side.I loved his tattoos and how they were a record of memories and moments in his life."Memories are priceless to me. Maybe years from now you'll come back here, and it'll spark a reminder of our time together. You may not remember everything that happened this day, but you'll feel it in your heart. Money can buy a lot of things, but it can't buy moments, and those are priceless."But as delicious as James and Aubrey's storyline is, there are some massive surprises in this one and some huge obstacles that I honestly didn't see a way out of. Aubrey digs herself into too many lies, complications and too many overlapping stories and when the author throws the twist in, well it all results in one hell of a messy situation. I liked Aubrey in a sense for James but I fully admit she made a lot of wrong turns and if the author didn't have me so fully, I might not have forgiven her for some of her 'mistakes'. To say they were pretty big ones would be an understatement!This is a book that it's best to go in blind to. It'll pull you in and then take you deeper. At least that was my experience. And because of my absolute blind love for James and massive crush, I'm absolutely biased and have to give this book *****5 stars x 2! Here's to all the moments and memories. And a silver fox I won't forget.Note: This book won't appeal to everyone. It crosses more than one line and if cheating is a trigger for you then this definitely isn't the right book.

3.5 StarsOverall Opinion:This was enjoyable. I actually like escort type stories (or f-buddy to more), so this wasn’t too far out of my norm. It has cheating — so beware those of you that have that as a hard limit. I thought the pacing felt off and the ending was not nearly enough. The conflict was resolved in the epilogue!! I thought I saw that there is an extended epilogue or a novella that goes along with this that I’m going to look for because I’m rather frustrated with how little of closure we actually got. However, I would recommend this for anyone that likes a forbidden romance with an significant age gap that has no problem with cheating.Brief Summary of the Storyline:This is Aubrey and James’s story. Aubrey is a struggling college senior that cares for her elderly grandmother financially. When her best friend and roommate offer her a chance to make obscene amounts of money quickly, she takes her up on the offer and becomes a high end escort. Three clients in and she meets James and she cannot forget him. They connect instantly and James starts a strong pursuit of Aubrey. There are some major road blocks in their way, lots of lies being told And revealed, and some hot sexy times...and they get a HFN ending.Point Of View (POV):This was told in Aubrey’s POV. I think it would’ve been nice to get some of James POV to understand his feelings a little more tbh.Overall Pace of Story:Alright. It felt slow at first, it picked up, and then ended abruptly. It was also pretty long, so I think it could’ve been edited better (at least the beginning) tbh.Instalove:Kind of. Instant connection but not really the fluffy instalove that I find annoying.H (Hero) rating:4 stars. James. I liked him. He was pretty hot and I liked how he cared for the h.h (heroine) rating:3.5 stars. Aubrey. I liked her. She was kind of a mess at times and it did really bother me how she dealt with the Daniel situation though.Sadness level:Low/moderate. I shed a few tears but no tissues were needed.Push/Pull:YesThe h pushes away and I’d say that the H is the main pursuer of the relationship.Heat level:Hot. They have some hot tension, chemistry, and scenes -- but not so much it takes away from the story. Some bondage, spanking and light D/s dynamics.Descriptive sex:YesOW (Other Woman)/OM (Other Man) drama:YesThey both are with other people and this causes jealousySex scene with OW or OM:YesThere are a few scenes with the h and OM (one including the H in a threesome)Cheating:YesThe h has a boyfriend and the H is marriedSeparation:YesThey are separated a few times shortly and one time for ~2 years. For the short separations they both were with other partners and it isn’t specified about during the long separation (though it is hinted that they weren’t with anyone else)Possible Triggers:Yes *BIG SPOILERS*Prostitution (on page), mfm threesome (on page), the H is married and the father to the h’s best friend and roommate (on page), loss of a loved one to cancer (on page), and the H is 52 while the h is 21.Closure:This ended too abruptly for my tastes and with what I would call a HFN ending. I think there’s another book to give us better closure that has already been released.Safety:This one is probablyNot Safefor most safety gang readers-Doeshave cheating-Does Nothave a descriptive sex scene with the h and OM-Doeshave the h pushing the H away-Doeshave a few separations between the h and H-Does Nothave abuse-Does Nothave a HEA ending

3.5 starsI must say that I really enjoyed this book and the story. The sexual tension was good and the scenes were pretty hot. James is smoldering sexy and he knows how to control and command a scene without making it feel wrong or humiliating. The writing has a lot of sass and surprisingly a lot of heart. There is really good development. There are really nice secondary characters as well.I really only have two complaints about this book. Aubrey... I actually wasn't all that fond of her, I didn't hate her or anything like that, she just was an ok kind of heroine to me. Secondly, the plot was a typical billionaire alpha hero-struggling college student forbidden age gap romance. I just expected something more original from my favorite author; I truly enjoyed it, nevertheless.If you’re looking for something extremely hot with a nice bit of kink, I'd definitely recommend this.In short:Hero5/5 |Heroine3/5 |Plot (Point, Originality)3/5 |Writing Style4/5 |Steam5/5 |Romance4/5 |Angst-Suspense3/5 |Darkness1/5 |Humor1/5 |Secondary Characters4/5 |Drama-Conflict4/5 |Mystery0/5 |Twists3/5 |PacingSteady |Action3/5**ARC kindly provided in exchange for an honest review.**

5 RED STARS!Warnings: lots of sex on drugs, prostitution, cheating and buckets of tears from crying.I'll give you a heads up. This book has been a roller coaster ride, one I don't regret at all. By rating it 5, I do not support young girls and women selling their bodies for a living, escorting men their dad's age and drinking, smoking or doing drugs. The point of reading isn't to judge characters for what they do, but rather understand where they are coming from and why they make the decisions they make. I don't like the lifestyle these girls live, but it's a sad reality for many people. Instead of scowling at it, I tried to comprehend their deeds.This book had been on my recommendations since forever but I never felt like reading it. As opposed to what I thought, it's not a typical club setting. I was on the edge of my seat and never had a dull moment. The story is about a girl, Aubrey Abrams, who works her arse off to support herself and her Grammy, the only family she has left. Her friend, Natalie, introduces her to the world of prostitution and she can't refuse because all the luxury she ever dreamed of could be on her fingertips with the money on the table and also, her Grammy's life could get easier. The story escalates from there.The things I abso-freaking-lutely loved about this book are as follows:🍇The leads don't meet as soon as the book begins its course. That's when you know the writer is really putting efforts into the story and not skipping to the adrenaline.🍇In the beginning, the way Aubrey and Natalie's bond is set down is just amazing! Never had a friendship been so well-written in romance books. The first time I wasn't hurrying for the leads to meet.🍇Aubrey doesn't agree to escorting as soon as the offer is on the table. She takes time to think, contrary to 'naive' girls who agree readily. Her thoughts were so well put, I couldn't find an error.🍇The way leads meet is simple, yet one of the best encounters I've come across.🍇There were soooo many things beautifully written! For example,"He's the flame on the tip of the arrow that's aimed right at my heart. I want to burn alive with him."Also, when James talks about moments and memories, it screams maturity.🍇The introspective side of Aub was executed well, especially towards the end. The second half holds a special place in my heart. After all, we all appreciate characters who are ready to look back at their mistakes and be a better person.🍇JAMES. THAT'S IT.Any more and I'll be giving spoilers.I don't agree with what Aubrey did. She played with feelings, but it's easier said than done. I understand why she made certain decisions. In the end, all one wants to feel is loved and cared for; especially a prostitute who wants to live a normal life without guilt eating her away.My heart goes out for James. He didn't deserve what he got, but he could have stopped the lines blur further. They were already in too deep ig.Honestly, I like age gaps that seem practical. A 20+ age gap is not really my thing, but I was able to ignore it with this one.I wonder if Madame Christine is gay lol. I wish she didn't put James and Aub in such a vulnerable position. But how else would the story move forward?The escorting world is not as glamourous as it is shown. But it's fiction in the end. Everyone wants to live in a dreamland, so I won't deduct stars for that.I'm honestly in love with the author. Her writing style was perfectly elaborative, with good twists. Definitely a must read for romance and erotica lovers. You'll learn and go through a lot with this one. I freaking cried for a book in a long time, so that says something. The cover seems perfectly in accordance with what we get to see in this book!HAPPY READING!P.S.- The songs that will go very well with the book areillicit affairsandivyby Taylor Swift.P.P.S.-I'm sorry for many things that happened in the book, but more so for Daniel. It's gonna be tough to recover from that.

Not sure where to begin. This wasn’t bad, but it also wasn’t good. It starts super duper slow. The hero is not even introduced until about 30%. The sex is hot, it’s smutty as hell. And I loved that the heroine loved sex and was not ashamed to have lots of it, with and without the hero. Other than that, this was a hot mess. Everyone, including the 50 something year old hero sounds like they’re teenagers. I laughed a lot at some of the ridiculous dialogue. The twist I saw it right away. And the author desperately needs a good editor/proofreader cause there’s tons of spelling and grammar errors.I don’t really care much about her being an escort, I’ve read some amazing books before where the h is an escort. Or even the age difference, I guess it is kinda yuck but to each, at least she’s not underage. I just felt the book was all over the place and lacked quality. The dialogue (and the plot for that matter) was haphazardly put together, there are so many plot holes and contradictions, the characters lack depth and were all unlikeable, except for the grandma. But Aubrey, James, Natalie, Daniel were all meh and jerks so I couldn’t connect with them or felt anything for them. The ending is also very HFN, there isn’t really any indication of how they will really fare in the future.This one is also triggers galore so if you’re easily triggered by things like cheating, OM, OW, etc. then this is not for you. I most of all found it entertaining for what it was, a smut-fest, nothing more, nothing less. Without the hot sex, this would be zero stars. This is my first book by this author and I’m not really sure I will try her again.

I can't even right now.I. CAN'T. EVEN. RIGHT. FUCKING. NOW!THIS BOOK! holy shit!I had no idea what I was getting myself in to before going in and with me being so picky when it comes to my books, I just didn't know what to expect.It went beyond my expectations and so much more. I mean this book was EVERYTHING!!When I wasn't reading it I was thinking about it non-stop. Aubrey and James were always on my mind.Honestly I could gush about this story and characters all day.I love forbidden and I absolutely love an age-gap books and Lucia Franco has by far written one of the best one.I swooned, I laughed-out-loud, I needed a cold shower, I laughed some more, I swooned even harder and I ugly cried. Those last chapters were so hard to read! 💔Lucia not only wrote the most swoon worthy hero in James, but she wrote one of the BEST friendships ever. Aubrey and Natalie are FRIENDSHIP GOALS and I was so invested in them both that when shit hit the fan my heart broke for Natalie and their friendship.One of my favourite books of the year and one I will never forget. Did I mention how much I fucking loved it???I'll say it again, I'll even scream it...I LOVED IT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!(P.S. I need some more delicious age-gap/forbidden goodness from this author ASAP!)(PPS. And yes I have read Balance. I finished it the other day and I'm going to continue with the series after I read Say Yes)(PPPS. Natalie needs a book! She needs her HEA!)(PPPPS. I MISS IT ALREADY!!)

Oh boy! This book completely wrecked me, and I wish I could give it more than 5 stars. It was absolutely nothing like I was expecting, yet it was everything I never knew I needed. While I felt it was slightly slow to get going, once it did, there was no stopping. I stayed up until 2am this morning to finish it because I simply couldn’t tear my eyes away.Aubrey and Natalie were best friend goals, and I adored them both; they bounced off each other so effortlessly.The scene where Aub goes for her interview is insanely hot, and it honestly had me blushing at certain points! *fans self* Lucia Franco certainly knows how to turn up the heat the extra notch.When James enters the story, well, that’s when my interest really piqued. He was all I had hoped he would be...and yes, he was married, but everything isn’t always so black and white. That man oozed confidence, both in himself and sexually; he may be in his 50s but he still had all the moves. The chemistry between him and Aubrey was insane and at one point I had to get up and open a window!I absolutely adored the writing and just found myself not wanting the story to end. HUSH HUSH is 100% going on my Top Reads of 2019 list!!

4.5 STARSOutside, on the streets, you're a woman with no filter who can hold herself up to anyone, which I fucking love, but in bed, I think you need someone to make you forget your name. I can do that. I want to be that man for you. Between the sheets, you should be saying, 'Yes, please, more, harder'—my fucking name and nothing else."I read this book in a desperate attempt to take myself out of a weeks long book slump and boy did it deliver. It delivered on everything I needed and some things that I didn't even knew I needed.First, I can't believe this is my first Lucia Franco book, and it sure as hell won't be my last, because WOW.If you follow my review, you know I love me a sex positive FMC. I love a woman who unapologetically owns what she loves and doesn't shame it or try to find a name or excuse for it. And I absolutely loved Aubrey. With only her grammy left as living family, Aubrey struggles to make ends meet with several jobs to get her through school. She's focused on school and graduation and has no time for men outside of an orgasm they can offer. She lives with her wealthy best friend, who lives a luxurious life style that she shares with her friend. But little does Aubrey know how she affords those things when she says she doesn't want to touch the trust from her family.An exclusive escort for the rich and entitled, in a secret club who only the elusive few 1% can afford offers a solution she never knew she would entertain. But the money is too good to say no to. But then she meets a client who makes her want more. And when she learns who the client really is, everything is turned on it's head. But by then, she's far too deep in.I have to admit that as much as I loved Aubrey, I disliked her relationship with Daniel. While I get why she clung to him, and she really opens up about it in the end, I also disliked it. It was especially frustrating knowing how Daniel shared why his previous relationship fell apart with her and she still made the choices she did. To be honest, the cheating in this book didn't bother me. I knew what I was going into, and I knew this book would be deeply rooted in the gray.I loved the forbidden romance and age gap between Aubrey and James. I loved his dominance as much as I loved her hidden fragility. When I tell you I devoured this book, I mean I devoured this book in one sitting. I couldn't stop reading. I was immediately immersed and obsessed. And while I didn't love some of the choices these two made, I simply loved them too much to care.Find me on:

What a heart wrenching ride!It is true that what doesn’t kill you makes you stronger, but Lucia tested my limits with this book..in a good way. I must admit at first it was touch, but I pulled up my big girl panties and trusted Lucia. She did not disappoint. I love James so much! Of course I didn’t approve of things both he and Aub did, but these kinds of books are the reason I’m a reader. If I wanted something fluffy and safe then I never would have agreed to this book, but I knew any story in Lucia’s Head would take me to those heights that I crave as a reader. Can I just say the steam factor on this one is off the charts—HOT. So so good!!“I need this man something fierce. "James?" "Hmm?" My jaw trembles. "Make love to me?" "Sweetheart, I've been making love to you all along."

WHAT A BOOK!Warning! Please don’t read this book if cheating and high price escort aren’t your thing because you’re just gonna hate it.But for the morally corrupt this is our cup of tea! My gosh, this book is amazing. It’s a favorite of mine.James Rivera is an enigma. He’s this suave 52 year old man with a big dick and a fat bank account. He also has a taste for the rougher aspect of sex. Too bad his wife doesn’t feel the same way.Aubrey/Valentina is hot, young and just so happens to be a high class escort who prefers one night stands than relationships. That is until she meets Mr. Rivera himself and his shocked with the connection they have.The silver fox and his pretty young thing are constantly torn between what is right and what their heart desires. I simply love this book. It’s a favorite of mine. Please read!

book.title: Nero
book.author: S.J. Tilly
book.description: PaytonRunning away from home at 17 wasn’t easy. Let’s face it though, nothing before, or in the ten years since, has ever been easy for me.And I’m doing okay. Sorta. I just need to keep scraping by, living under the radar. Staying out of people’s way, off people’s minds.So when a man walks through my open patio door, stepping boldly into my home, and my life, I should be scared. Frightened. Terrified.But I must be more broken than I realized, because I’m none of those things.I’m intrigued.And I’m wondering if the way to take control of my life is by giving in to him.NeroThe first time I took a man’s life, I knew there’d be no going back. No normal existence in the cards for me.So instead of walking away, I climbed a mountain of bodies, and created my own destiny. By forming The Alliance.And I was fine with that. Content enough to carry on.Until I stepped through those open doors, and into her life.I should’ve walked away. Should’ve gone right back out the door I came through. But I didn’t.And now her life is in danger.But that’s the thing about being a bad man. I’ll happily paint the streets red to protect what’s mine.And Payton is mine. Whether she knows it or not.

1 DNF StarI'm sick of booktok for hyping up stories like this. Insta love, cringe, dumb heroine. She literally gets "wet" because he steals her body wash and gives her money for it. They both acted too immature for their ages too, and nothing was believable.

Yes, he’s a psychopath. A stalker. A murderer.But underneath all of that, he’s a teddy bear. 🥰There’s just something appealing about men that have a skewed moral compass.Fictional or otherwise… 🌚I mean, this was decent. Is it the quality dark romance that I’m looking for? No.Was it a good, unhinged time? Yes. It was entertaining enough and I definitely want to keep reading the series.
You can't convince me this wasn't written by a horny teenager! That being said, I will absolutely be continuing with this sinful series😌🤭

This was unintentionally really funny - this had me cracking up because it was so weird and sometimes cringey. The insta-love was very strong with this one. The character introduction was so wild, and it never really recovered for me. The mafia world-building was incredibly weak; I had so many questions. It was a bit repetitive at times, too heavy on (repetitive) inner monologue, and lacked substance.It was an easy read though.

⭐️⭐️⭐️.5-⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️''For you, I'm f*cking certifiable''Safety warnings:- H 40 / h 27- No cheating- No OM drama- Slight OW drama- Stalking- Violence/d*ath- Child abuse/neglect- Kidnapping- HEASPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️SPOILERS BELOW ⬇️I've been seeing this book everywhere and I put aside the other books on my immediate TBR and jumped right in.It had all the elements needed for a dark romance with an MMC that is jealous/possessive and unhinged. I think the first 60-70% was the strong parts for me but it went slightly downhill when the predicting plotline happened. Jaded ex hook up and villain get together to destroy the H/h.I did think the way they met was hilarious, I laughed too hard when Nero just showed up in her apartment to avoid the cops (through her open balcony) and sat down watched tv with her for a while and ate her popcorn? haha it was too funny.She lost her virginity to him after years and it happened pretty quickly into the story. You get her reasons why even if you view this book as having insta-love/lust.We got a sad backstory from Payton, she suffered throughout most of her childhood until she was almost 18 in the hands of her stepfather while her mother did nothing to stop the abuse. She ran away from her home when she overheard her stepfather sell her virginity to a friend for 600 dollars and how it would happen once, she was 18.Nero got the information he needed from Payton and went ahead to kill the stepfather and it was satisfying to see it happen. I kind of wanted to know the fate of the mom once she lost her husband and her home, but I guess we can safely assume she is fucked. The way he told her and presented the dead guys ring was right in the middle of sex and later that same ring was melted, and Nero created a necklace for Payton (she has no idea lol).The OW drama that I mention in my warnings is connected to Nero pretending with her ex hook up who is a paid escort to distract the men who attend his birthday party. He sees Payton at the party and puts his arm around her waist (HATING IT) but that is all that happened, he is absolutely devoted to Payton but of course she has massive insecurities and believes he used her.We meet King who is basically Nero's best friend/brother from another mother and their friendship was everything. We don't really know who his FMC will be, but we got bits of his background. Comes from a rich family and he is 44. Can’t wait for his book!

"Keep your doors closed, Payton. Leaving them open could be seen as an invitation."Nero.One name. Ruler of the underworld. Hades.Head of the Alliance.But one night and one open balcony later, and there was a new obsession.Payton Vawdrey.She didn't know it but by leaving her balcony open she changed everything.Because now Nero is entering her life.And he will do anything to get what he wants.And what he wants is Payton."I'm not letting her out of my sight. Not giving her a chance to run. Not that she'd get far."If I have said it once I have said it a Bajillion times - PLEASE READ THE ALLIANCE SERIES BY S.J. Tilly!!!I categorize these books as "dark fluffy romance" and I cannot get enough of them - this is my first time doing a reread and I can already tell you by the first chapter I knew it was not going to be my last 🌹🌹🌹"Greed outweighs my guilt every time. And when it comes to Payton, I'm greedy all the way through."Nero and Payton are the OG couple that started it all and I remember how obsessed (no pun intended) I was with them and I am so happy that has not changed one bit!! Unhinged to the max, obsessed while somehow being so fluffy is how I would describe "Nero" and I wouldn't have it any other way 🖤🖤"And I know I've done nothing to deserve her. But I'm keeping her all the same."Nero is the literal definition of "looks like could kill you would kill you" but for Payton he is definitely "looks liks could kill you is a cinnamon roll" and isn't that we want in the end?
