3.5⭐️ did I have a good time? Absolutely. Was I giggling and kicking my feet? Absolutely. Would this have been a million times better without the insta love trope? Absolutely. But I still ate this up in less than 24 hours so take with that what you will.Don’t let the lower rating fool you, I know I’m about to devour this series. I just have high standards when it comes to dark mafia romance and this was lacking in the angst and tension department because well.. insta love. Payton was also just a little bland to me.But boy oh boy do I love me a stalker and I was OBSESSED with Nero! This man… sat. The way he is such a red flag but also such a lover not golden retriever at the same time. Takes touch her and die to the next level yet also drinks honey coconut lattes because they’re her favorite drink even though he hates sugar… I can’t.

when I ☠️ put on my 🪦 wish I was stalked by nero.Ok but for real…. I didn’t know what to expect with this book, BUT OH MY GOD WAS IT FUCKING EPIC.This book was so easy to become addicted to. Once I started this book I knew I wanted to avoid all responsibilities until I finished.From the first sentence it was fast paced, easy to get invested in the plot and the characters. It was the perfect length with no unnecessary drama that didn’t add to the plot which I LOVED.Okay so this book starts with Payton in her apartment after another long day working at her local coffee shop when she notices a figure standing on her balcony… where she left the door open…. enter nero *literally*.Nero is hiding from the cops when he stumbled upon Payton alone in her apartment. As soon as he gets a whiff of her sent and sees her innocence he becomes addicted.I mean he literally stole her soap and left a $100 bill because BOSSSSS.I have read a certain very popular stalker romance which I despised but this has a taste of stalker romance and it is done PERFECTLY!If loving nero is wrong then I don’t want to right…..Red flags? All I see is green…Ok you get the point, but seriously I am OBSESSED with Nero🥵So we got-dark/mafia/stalker/billionaire romance (chef kiss combo… thank you S.J)-touch her and you ☠️ vibes-virgin fmc-curvy fmc-AGE GAPPP AYEEEE!!!!I loved seeing Payton learn to love her body🥰Also the EPILOGUES🥰😭 & when I saw who the next book is about….trust me once you read this book and see you’re going to go feral…. I ALREADY CAN’T WAIT!!Thank you S.J for the ARC & introducing me to my new book boyfriend <3All opinions are mine!!!

DNF @ 19% - I think this author just isn’t for me. I was hoping that I might be able to hang but it’s all just too Insta. There’s no depth to any of the “feelings” - no real chemistry. There are some elements I liked but they all happen too soon and without real feelings and knowledge behind them to make them swoony. No substance. She seems desperate for any kind of care and affection and just latches on to a random person who happens to be the H. And I have no idea why he does anything 🤷🏼‍♀️ This author spoon-feeds every little thing and in turn takes all the suspense out of the book leaving me BORED. Why are we in the hero’s head so much? He doesn’t have anything interesting to tell us. There’s no tension built that his insight would help to relieve. His pov isn’t all that distinguishable from the h. Which is weird considering how different they are. Why is a grown man who heads a mafia-like organization such a simp over someone he doesn’t know? Where’s his chill? Why is she so stupid? Virginity doesn’t actually leach brain cells so I can’t figure it out.This just isn’t for me. This was my issue with all the other books I’ve tried by this author. We don’t mesh, sadly🤷🏼‍♀️ I wish I was as excited as all these arc reviewers 👀

Whyyyy was this story so fun?! Absolutely ridiculous, completely ott, but also HOT and wildly entertaining.Meet a guy.Have sex twice.Fall in love.Payton’s messy, scrappy life collides with Nero, dangerous, ruthless, and absolutely obsessed, and the result is pure chaos, but in the best way. The action scenes are intense, and the romance scorching.Tropes/themes:Mafia romanceAge gap (40H/27h)Ott obsessive mmcNot a manwhoreVirginal fmcLight stalkingForced proximityPublic sexPraise kinkGreat side charactersHealthy sized fmcThat scene at the concert...You have to suspend disbelief (no seriously), but that’s part of the charm.
nero made me want to be a lesbian.
the MMC was an absolute white crayon and had a charisma of an itchy sweater and the FMC sounded like someone who chews the remote buttons, I am not apologizing this book wasn't for me.
i am tired of reading books that make me feelnothing.

Full of ramblings and spoilers‼️---- Nero (Hero, 40) ----He was a freaking creep, invaded her home, sat on her couch and ate her popcorn like he has all the time in the world, flipped through her mail, stole her body wash, stole her dirty undies, then paid 100 dollars (he paid 100 for every undies he stole) and stalked her.He tried to fight off what he was feeling, but then his obsession ultimately won. He kept saying it would've been bad for Payton if people knew she was associated with him.On his birthday party, he brought one of the escorts (Nikki), whom he (often or rarely, it wasn't clarified) used as his date to drive away the attention of his potential enemies from Payton. But seriously, I can't wrap my head around that. I mean, what the actual fvck, dude? That was just despicable 🤢 Can't he even find someone else that he didn't fvck to be his date, or, I don't know, maybe he shouldn't have brought a date at all? After all, it was his birthday party, so he could do whatever he wanted. That petty move, though probably well-intentioned, still pissed the heck out of me. But to be fair to this jerk, he wasn't aware that Payton would attend that party. He never gave her any invitation, not because he didn't want to but rather to keep her safe.Then he got mad when Payton ran away from the party. I mean, whose sane person wouldn't be furious to see that? Nikki was all over him, and he had his hand on her waist, squeezing it, and she was there the whole time by his side while he was entertaining his guests. Being the perfect date and arm candy, ugh!As expected, he followed Payton to her apartment to teach her a freaking lesson. Obviously, his punishment was in the form of sex; cue eye roll. Then, while he was fvcking her, he admitted to Payton that he used to fvck Nikki and called her, wh0r3(Nikki was an escort, but the name calling still didn't sit well with me). I mean, if this conversation isn't vomit-inducing for you yet, I don't know what to say anymore. He also confessed his history with other women and told her that they meant nothing to him(they were still doing the deed here, btw). Is this supposed to make Payton or the readers feel better? Because istg my blood level just spiked up to the stratosphere, and this admission has seen me red everywhere. Gag!I can't believe that he didn't listen when she begged him to stop, not just once (mind you), because she couldn't take anymore of his god awful confessions. It was nauseating and infuriating, tbh. He didn't even think about how Payton would feel about all of the things he just said; he was on a roll, admitting all of his promiscuity.He was mad, not at her fully(yet a small part of him, I think did)but mainly at the situation she put herself in unknowingly. So then he decided for her, ordering her around to pack her effects and move in with him. She definitely refused to do so(probably to show that she has some sort of a backbone)but readers very well knew that weak heroines won't win; they never do with alphahole heroes. 🤷‍♀️ I'm having a freaking whiplash here.The way he also handled the person who gave Payton the invitation made me furious.Nikki was the one who sent the invite. For the record, she was an utter witch. She didn't learn from the first time with Nero, so I don't even feel sorry for her conclusion. Apparently, she was in love with him, wanted to be with him, and wanted to ruin Nero and Payton's budding relationship. The way he talked about Nikki was so degrading and insulting, like he was grossed out so much by her. His view of her and sex workers was outright condescending, as if he didn't use her service for God knows how long. I hated his chauvinism, and he's holier than thou attitude. Being an escort was Nikki's job, and I would never discriminate against it, not in fiction but more so in real life. But him fucking around like it was his job? That was what should be downright disgusting and be frowned upon.I can't track anymore how many times I asked the good Lord to give me more patience to endure this horrible MMC. He made me feel so enraged for the majority of this book.Although the majority of the story, he was an insufferable jackass. He sure had some of his good moments.The way he handled Arthur, Nikki, and Mikhail. How he never crossed the line about human trafficking. His surprised dog for Payton. The tracker on her necklace—I'm 50-50 with this, but in this case, this was very helpful. How he encouraged her to design their home. How he helped her build new friendships and gave her dream wedding---- Payton (Heroine, 27) ----I don't know what to feel about Payton. She annoyed and frustrated me, and she made me roll my eyes so hard. But on the other hand, I also felt sorry for her for all the pain and abuse she went through. No person should experience those things, and they were truly awful.However, she made it so hard to root for her. I get that because of her trauma, she's afraid of men. Yet I don't know why she threw away all the caution out the window for Nero. They didn't even meet in the most conventional way. He freaking B&E in her apartment because he was running away from the police. That should be the first sign she should not entertain him anymore, yet she keeps hoping that he will come back, and she even opened her balcony door to send a sign that she's waiting for him. Whyyyyy Payton? 🤦 I don't understand, for some reason, why she wanted his presence when, for almost a decade, she tried to ignore men altogether because of her trauma. Then, after one night of his unplanned or accidental visit, she was now okay, wanting attention from a stranger she didn't even know.I really dislike it when the heroine is a pathetic sorry of a character. She was weak, spineless, and innocent,(that made her a bit dumbass). Her inner monologue pissed me most of the time(at times it was unnecessary too long), and all throughout the book she becomes this puddle of goo when Nero is around. I want to shake her so hard to scream at her, "Girl, get a grip on yourself!!!"But I have to say, at least, that I did like her partially when she ignored Nero in the coffee shop; I think that was the only time she grew a backbone.Then it was back to being a pathetic mess during his birthday party. She saw Nero with Nikki, then got drunk after he purposely ignored her presence. She planned to get away from the venue, but then freaking decided to drink another two glasses of champagne (I think?). She was already drunk, and according to her, she needed to get away as fast as she could, and she needed a clear head. So why did she add more alcohol to her system? Anyway, she was successful in running away from the party. She got back to her apartment, packed her stuff, and planned to skip town because she was hurting. It was certainly not a well-thought-out plan, yet I get where she was coming from. I was actually hoping for her to succeed with this attempt,(just to decrease the life span of the Nero by 10 years worrying over her, I'm petty sue me 😈)but no, with the alcohol consumption she had and the never-ending crying she displayed, ultimately she passed out. Ugh, such a disappointing turn of events.Anyway, she was a nice person, spineless and pathetic, but a good person nonetheless. She cares about Nero, animals, and her bodyguards. The guilt she felt at the end was also a great exhibit of how she encompasses a massive heart with so much sympathy for other people.---- Overall ----Don't even get started with me on their first time together. He was very aggressive with her, and when he realized she was a virgin, he decided to take ownership of her "forever." Also, what happened to the condom? They didn't even discuss it. That was so stupid for Payton, tbh, having sex for the first time basically with a stranger and not using any protection. 🙄 That scene wasn't hot and sexy at all; I was cringing inside while reading that chapter.Then the moment they said, "I love yous", totally cringe-worthy. It felt so forced for both of them. How did they even know if what they felt was love? They barely connected after the night of B&E by Nero. He left her alone for weeks but kept stalking her; he even added cameras inside and outside of her apartment to spy on her. After weeks of Nero avoiding Payton, she indirectly invited him to her apartment, and then they had ONS. Days passed again after that. Then, on a jealous fit because Payton was going to attend a concert, he followed her there and gave her an@l (just the tip, btw). Then they met again at his birthday party.See, they met or had sex merely for a few days (3 days total, I think), and they didn't even speak about important things. Nero was just giving her crumbs of information about him when he knows everything about her, and you're telling the readers that all of a sudden they're dropping the L word? Totally unbelievable!There were a lot of steamy scenes here, but I never enjoyed them for some reason. It didn't make me feel hot and bothered. And honestly, I don't have a clue as to why I was like that.The writing style was fine, I guess; it never seemed or came off as juvenile. But I don't know; there's nothing really great about the plot. It was kind of predictable about what would happen in the story. Maybe others will be cool with this; unfortunately, this didn't work out for me. It was underwhelming, but others might find this good for them.I can't keep track of the number of times I felt urged to DNF this one to just put myself out of this misery, because this was so terrible with unlikeable MCs. I should give a tap to my shoulder for putting up with this atrocity of a book.Booktok overhyped this one, and it's not even worth it, imo. I'm sure the author put so much effort into this book. But I really can't stand reading this; I only pushed myself to do it because of the book challenge I set for myself on Goodreads. Gooosh! Give me back the almost 4 hours I wasted reading this 😭
i do NOT know why all the girlies on tiktok are eating this up bc i again, could not.
3.5 starsTouch her and die vibes... except it's fr. You touch her and Nero will kill you. A bit over the top/instalove and unrealistic, but it was an easy read and a good time.

I don’t mind instalust but in Nero it was just too much for me. Nero and Payton’s connection felt very surface level as did the story. I wasn’t interested in either of them and the instalust didn’t help. Not much happened in this story.

the definition of man OBSESSED.payton and nero meet when he appears on her balcony while he’s running from the cops, a meet cute if you will. while he’s obviously dangerous and he knows he should stay away from her, he can’t and continues keeping tabs on her until he says screw it.this was giving haunting adeline & darkverse vibes but definitely lighter even though it’s a dark mafia romance. nero was so so obsessed and i eat that up every single time. he had no right to be so hot and freaky. payton was a curvy heroine, we love, and they balanced each other out so well.i couldn’t put this down and i cannot wait for the rest of this series.

DNF @46%Didn’t like the chapter style. Also, the music venue scene was strange and not ok for me. How to you try an*l for the first time in front of hundreds of people in the dark. I’m sure others may have found it hot but it was a no from me. 😳

I have heard such amazing things about this series, so I was so excited to finally pick it up. As someone who loves mafia and has read a lot of mafia romances, though, I found myself wanting so much more mafia and being a bit bored by the plot. Nero, the hero, sees the heroine and is immediately obsessed. He's in the mafia and is a bit unhinged, and the heroine doesn't now whether to be excited or terrified. This was a fun dynamic, but she was very much the virginal heroine who really just went along with what every other character tells her. I like my heroines in mafia romances to have a bit more agency and personality than what we got from her. As for the plot, there were a lot of slow parts until the end where we finally got some action. I was more passively listening to the audiobook for a majority of the story and wanted something more exciting for a mafia romance. I'll definitely continue on in the series, but I was a bit disappointed with this one.

Ok but this was funnnn 😏 I really enjoyed this. Nero is oh so perfectly possessive. There were so many “you are mine” moments and I loved it.The only thing I didn’t love was the fmc- but I Definitely want to read the rest of the series!!- dark- Mafia- inexperienced fmc- Age gap (27:40- Mmc- One night stand🌶️🌶️🌶️🌶️

Man, the first 80% of this was extremely mid. I needed more plot, more mafia business. I'm all for the smut, but when I read mafia, I want the street wars and dirty trade deals, the shady meetings. 🤷‍♀️ Does anyone have some recs for mafia books like that?

Another man I am DEAD over!!! Love at first site is what Nero sees when he's at Paytons open door there first meeting was intense. She's apart of his world its tense at first he's over protective lots of drama and miscommunication at one point and even heartbreak.But he does make up for it and tiny grovel for what he did. it was okayThere are many times I'm into insta everything, but this one needed more for me because of their backgrounds and ages...And the BBS drove me crazy 🤣🫠Nero (40) is running away from the cops when he enters Payton's (27) balcony and decides he's gonna chill and watch a movie with her while eating popcorn 😅.Now. I know what you're thinking. Shouldn't shecall the cops? 🤷‍♀️Yeah. You would think she would 🤷‍♀️but alas, she doesn't 🤣. This girl decides to fallasleep 🤦‍♀️ while a stranger is next to her on the couch🫠. After he's entered her second story balcony.Nothing to see here folks 🤣.It alllllll makes sense 🫠😅🤦‍♀️After he leaves her (cause yes. she really IS asleep🫠), he decides he actually wants her for himself 😅. Hewants her so much that he even steals her body wash so he can smell her on him 👀.Payton can't stop thinking about him. She's a 27 year old v who has stayed away from men because of the abuse she endured when she was young at the hands of her stepdad 😑.But Nero's gonna make sure she's okay. Which means he has to think about her safety and wellbeing ❤️ even if it means he has to step away from her.He quickly finds out that it's hard for him to stay away 👀What I liked: possible spoilers—how OTT Nero is ❤️ and JPPPPP 😮‍💨. I especially loved—retroactive protection 🫶. Someone did Payton wrong in the past and he’s gonna take care of him 👏🏻—the comeuppance 😈. Yup. I love me some good revenge 😮‍💨—the 🔥. This had a decent amount of seeeeeexy times. The first happens at about 21% and they continue until the second epilogue 😮‍💨—the times I laughed 🤣. I found myself cracking up in the scene where they just met 🤣 and other times too. I mean. The audacity 😮‍💨🤣—the side characters 👀 I can’t wait to read King’s book 👏🏻—the texts when he’s not with her 🥹 awwww Nero 🫶 you’re just a soft, squishy marshmallow when it comes to Payton ❤️—the epilogues ❤️ love seeing their HEA 🫶—hurt her and dye 👏🏻😈What I didn’t like/wanted more of: SPOILERS—the owd 🤬. Oh my gosh this angered me 🤬. So Nero doesn’t want people to know about her, and when it’s his bday party, he has his previous f buddy/escort as his companion instead of Payton 😒. Payton goes to said party and finds the ow touching him and he makes it a point to grab the ow by her waist when he sees Payton arrive 🤬 and she’s very hurt ☹️. But the thing is this- you would THINK she would get the f outta there.Naaaahh 😒Why would you think that 🙄?Because that would ONLY make sense 🤦‍♀️No. What happens is she gets drunk at this same party and then eventually goes home where she knocks out 😒. And guess who wakes her up? 🙄Did you guess?Yup. Nero. Which leads me to…—BBS 😒. OH. EM. GEEE. this was something else 😑. Let me get this straight. It was one of the hired ones he used). I did NOT find this swoony 🤬. I found it 🥴—no safety talk 😒. It’s clear Nero uses hired women for his pleasures, so why doesn’t Payton ask for a clean bill of health 🤦‍♀️. Seriously Payton 🤦‍♀️ his d is not clean (hey look. I rhymed 🤣)—what happened to her mom? She needs to get her own comeuppance too 😑Overall, I liked it. She has her hand on him and when he sees the h, he puts his hand on the ow to bring her closer knowing the h is looking. It makes the h insecure. I’m just a girl waiting for a mafia boss to walk in through my patio door and take care ofmyproblems. And now I’m a mafia romance convert. Accepting all recommendations.▹My ⭐ Rating:★★★★.25 out of 5▹Format:📱 eReader─────────────────────────○★○ What to Expect from This Book: ○★○–About:Payton’s just minding her own business one night when a man in a suit walks in through her open balcony and sits down on the couch to watch TV with her. But she’s used to laying low around men. But when he sets his eyes on her, she can’t escape his protection—even when it costs her.–FMC: Payton (aka “Sweet Girl” or “baby”).A woman in her late 20s/early 30s just trying to make ends meet without causing too much trouble for anyone. Wants the simple things in life, a dog, sunshine, laughter and doesn’t want leering eyes from men or anything to do with her step-father.–MMC: Nero.A self-made mafia man in his early 40s who runs a tight ship. Morally gray (sometimes morally black) but a guy with basic morals at his core. Will stop at nothing once he has his mind set.–Location:Minneapolis, MN (and a brief scene in Fargo, ND!!)–POV:Dual first-person–Spice:6 open-door explicit spicy scenes (dubious consent and more than “vanilla”)–Tropes:mafia millionaire MMC, “poor” FMC, strangers to lovers, virgin FMC who has never been kissed, he washes her hair, touch her and die, age gap–Content warning:parental neglect, mild degradation (which might be a kink for some), graphic on-page violence, death, kidnapping, past emotional trauma, physical abuse, dubious consent, stalking, minor blood play, car accident/ambush, watching someone die–Representation:nothing noteworthy─────────────────────────↻ ◁ || ▷ ↺ 1:00 ──ㅇ────── 4:12Now Playing:Shadow of a Doubt by Sonic Youth╰┈➤ ❝From the bottom of my heart he was looking all over me, you take me and I’ll take you, you kill him and I’ll kill her❞─────────────────────────★○ If You Like the Following, You Might Like This Book ○★➼ Darker romances where there’s danger, a little bit of violence (not between MCs), and a hefty amount of stalking & spice➼ Emotional hurt/heal (my fave)─────────────────────────⍟»This or That«⍟Character Driven———✧————————Plot DrivenFast Burn——✧—————————Slow BurnSweet——————————✧—SpicyLight/Fluffy——————✧—————Heavy/Emotional─────────────────────────🎯 My Thoughts:I’ve seen this floating around social media and Goodreads for about a year now and I was on the fence if mafia romance would be my thing or not (for no reason other than I wasn’t familiar with it), but lemme tell yous o m e t h i n g: it’s for me.I love that it’s borderline romantasy because in no way would I allow a man to act like Nero and get away with it in the real world, but in this fictional world? I would crawl across glass if this man asked me to and then I’d let him take care of me (and then do it all over again).Was it over the top at times? Sure. But I read to escape the realities of the cruel world we live in, SHARON, so if he’s going to assign her a body guard just so she can go outside for fresh air then I couldn’t care less! HE LOVES HER.I will be finishing this series, though I’ve heard books 2 and 3 are perhaps a little darker/redder flags. But I’m here for it.Send me more recs.____________Pre-read thoughts: i’ve never read a mafia romance before, but im luteal and feeling dark so why not start now.

I didn’t think I’d enjoy this one as much as I did… but from chapter one, I was hooked. It’s fast, it’s addictive, and it’s way sweeter than I expected from a dark romance.🎬 The Set-UpPicture this: you’re exhausted after a long day, curled up with popcorn and a movie… when a mafia boss climbs onto your balcony, plops down on your couch, and starts eating your popcorn🍿That’s how Payton meets Nero. No slow build—this book kicks the door (or balcony) wide open. And it’s hilarious🖤 NeroTo the world: ruthless, lethal, untouchable.To Payton: protective, vulnerable, and completely obsessed.He’ll burn down a house without blinking, but he’ll also:🐾 rescue a stray dog for her🛁 steal her rose body soap🧃drink her favorite latte even though he hates sugarThat mix of brutal and tender got me every time. Dangerous, yes—but for Payton, he’s all in. Watching that devotion unfold was my favorite part🌹PaytonShe’s not the “badass” heroine type. She’s soft, a little clueless, deeply lovable — and she’s a survivor. After an abusive past, she still craves love, safety, and (yep) a touch of danger.What I loved? She chooses Nero. She isn’t tricked, she isn’t blind—she wants him just as much as he wants her. Even when a couple of scenes had me raising an eyebrow, their chemistry made it work.🌶️Vibes: Dark romance with just enoughsoftness to balance the edge.😮‍💨 Spicy? Yep🔪 Dark? Honestly lighter than I expected🙌 No third-act breakupFinal ThoughtsIf you like your heroes dangerous to the world but hopelessly devoted to one woman, Nero delivers. It’s obsessive, over-the-top, and somehow still heartwarming..And yes—I’m already ready for King’s book, because if this is how the series starts, I’m definitely sticking around.✨ TropesMafia romanceObsessed MMCAge gap (13 years)Curvy virgin FMCStalker-but-soft MMC“Touch her and you die” vibesSeries Ranking1. Hans — Book 4 (5⭐️)My review2. Nero — Book 1 (4⭐️)3. Dom — Book 3 (4⭐️)My review4. King — Book 2 (3⭐️)My review

Story: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️Trope: mafia, instaloveAngst: 😱😱Smexy: 🔥🔥🔥.5Fantastic read! SJ Tilly definitely did not disappoint with her first foray into dark romance ♥️. Well written with a great storyline that moved really well. Relatively low angst and characters that compliment each other in the best of ways. Nero is the perfect overbearing, possessive, and protective steamroller and Payton never stands a chance, lol. Payton is used to handling everything herself, so having someone sweep her off her feet and take control like they mean it feels good. The chemistry between them is wonderful and the story is truly sweet despite having “darker” parts. Truly can’t wait for King’s book!⛔️ My bookshelves can sometimes be spoilers
I've had enough.

Is this what we're doing now? Writing books around tropes and whatever gets the youngins on Booktok all hot and bothered? I can tell because I can almost see the checklist this author was going off of. The result was a hero running around doing all the things villains do, but none of that has any impact if the relationship isn't developed and the characters are cardboard cutouts. Payton was like Bella Swan with even less personality. She was there for Nero to say things like "I'd kill for you" and to be wet at all times. I won't even go into the plot because it's non-existent. The pacing is so weird, and violent scenes are so drawn out for shock value.This should have been my jam. All of it sounds great on a blurb, even quotes out of context sounded amazing but put the insta lust and inexplicable insta love (seriously, what did he see in Payton?), the baffling sex scenes (her legs are hanging off the bed but she's on her stomach? Like straight out? They are having anal sex in public while they are both standing up but he's super tall and she's...not🫨 ) the ILY's come around halfway thru and they have had sex twice and maybe like one conversation. Development seems to be a thing of the past, and I'm super depressed about it.⭐⭐/5🔥🔥🔥/5TropesInsta everythingCinderella vibesStalker hero

book.title: Inked in Lies
book.author: Giana Darling
book.description: He moved in next door.Handsome as sin, older in a way that meant forbidden.He had tattoos on his hands and wickedness tucked in his grin.I was a goner as girl to a man they called Casanova for a reason. He would never love me, at least not the way I needed him to.I tried to move on. But I couldn’t turn my back on him or The Fallen MC.So when they needed me most, I offered the only thing of value I could use to help them.My body.And when helping them meant putting my life on the line, Nova had to decide just how much he was willing to do to get me out alive.A standalone romance inThe Fallen Menseries.

reviews.text: Is this my new favorite of the series!? It might be! From the start, I was obsessed with Lila and Nova's relationship. I cried TWICE before we were even 120 pages in and I just wanted my babies to be happy. While there is a 12 year age gap and they meet when Lila is 6, I never felt like lines were crossed or things were inappropriate. Nova and Lila didn't really live together (they overlapped maybe a few months in the same house) and no relationship begins until Lila is 24. I loved how Lila was trying to move on and Nova could not get over how much he wanted Lila, no matter how many times her tried to push her away because he thought he could never be good enough for her. THE ANGST. It was perfect. Then, we had a whole plot-line happening about the club and people who were enemies of the club that Lila was trying to infiltrate and take down. Things got crazy near the end and I literally couldn't put this book down. I read this in one day and I'm so sad it's over. I was just obsessed with the romance and needed Lila and Nova to finally agree they could be together. I've already jumped into book six because I just love this world and these characters so much!

This one was just okay for me. I had my expectations already lowered seeing as Nova was the hero and I just didn't expect a lot from him, but I was still super excited because I love this series and I love Giana's writing. But though I'm super grateful to have finally been able to read it, and I still cannot wait for the next book, hoping it's Priest🤞, this story just had too many of my pet peeves for me to fully enjoy it, making it probably my least favorite in the series.First, the good stuff. I LOVED the backstory. I think it was probably my favorite part of the book, and it took up a good almost 30%. Any scene between Lila and Dane though especially, was my favorite to read about, and I definitely may have cried at some of those scenes. Very emotional packed scenes in the backstory that I felt like we kind of needed more of in the present time. Then the last 10% of the book I also loved super hard. It was filled with the Nova I wish we had earlier, though he was still really sweet and funny at times, I needed all those loving scenes in the end to have happened way earlier, it was just a bit too late for me. And lastly, literally any scene with Zeus is always gonna be my favorite. And then Giana just had to make it even better by giving us some more Tayline and Cyclops whom I'm also obsessed with, so I'm so hoping we will get a full length book for them someday!Now onto the bad, which sadly, was quite a lot for me. First, this whole unrequited love trope where the female pines literally the ENTIRE story, is SO not my thing. Lila was just constantly chasing Nova, and it started to feel like more of an obsession than love. This also made it seem like Nova cared about Lila considerably less than she did for him and that he was only with her to assuage her feelings for him. The only good thing she did was ignore him for two years and get engaged, but even that was so lackluster because it was all off screen and the fiance was literally in the book for like 10 pages. Her being engaged making Nova jealous or something could have added so much more material I needed to show me that Nova actually loves Lila, because I just wasn't really feeling it at all.And Lila here, although she tries to act tough, just didn't have enough self respect for me to want to root her on. At around 27%, there is a scene where she catches Nova having sex with a woman, and instead of being jealous or anything, she starts touching herself and gets off to it. Nova sees her, slams the door in her face, finishes having sex with the other woman, then goes back to Lila and tells her to pretty much get over her crush. I actually disliked Lila more there than I did of Nova, because does anyone really find it sexy when you see the love of your life banging some other chick? Literally all Lila knows how to do is to chase after Nova, forgive him for anything he does, and constantly soothe his ego, while she's the one getting trampled all over because "he's not worthy of her" and he uses his past as a clutch. When she finally grows a backbone and tells him off, something happens where Nova saves her, so pretty much all is forgotten afterwards, and the "groveling" legit lasts only a couple pages. I was really hoping she can give as good as she gets and would show some backbone, but nope. Apparently her loving him excuses him for anything he does and has done.Then we have Nova. Sweet, sexy, manwhore Nova. He was... Okay. At times I really liked him (mainly in the backstory again), but most of the time...eh. He was almost a bit too "brotherly" or something towards Lila for me. Yes the sex scenes were hot, but I just didn't feel any chemistry or a show of feelings from him except friendly feelings. He hardly showed any possessiveness or jealousy, and was fine showing her off pretty much naked. He disliked PDA even with Lila, except for public sex apparently, making her pretty much no different than any other girl he has sex with. I felt like he actually had more friendly feelings towards her than like actual loving feelings. He just, wasn't enough for me and his love fell totally flat. And the OW drama that comes with the territory of him being a manwhore was bleh. There wasn't necessarily a lot, but his overall character just didn't make up for any of it at all. And his whole "no labels" thing just sounded kind of like an excuse to continue his manwhore ways (though he didn't). One thing I did like that he did though was that he is only ever with Lila sexually since the time they first kiss.The plot itself was also just okay for me. Giana is a fantastic writer, and I do enjoy reading about the ongoing plot, but I just feel like we have been reading the same thing for about 3 books now, just in different POVs. We did finally get somewhere in this one though which is good. But most of it has just dragged on for me with how repetitive it all became. Also, quick add, but I notice it more as the series goes on. But why does literally everyone sound and speak the same way? I get they're all apart of an MC and we can expect for at least the MC members to talk a certain way, but the heroines are all sounding the same too, exactly like the men. Even Cressida who was a teacher started sounding like it, then Lou, and now Lila. Not really a fan of it.Also, definitely some timeline issues. Their ages all kept changing by a couple of years, or they would say what their age is but it just wouldn't add up to what the other person's age is supposed to be. For example, in one chapter Lila said how old they are, but the next chapter Nova said how old Dane is and it was a couple years older than what Lila first said which doesn't make sense because Dane and Nova are supposed to be just about the same age. Or another chapter Nova said he was 19 but I could have sworn he just said he was 17?? It was literally impossible to keep track of anyone's ages.Overall, this was just an okay book for me, though I kind of have been expecting that since we heard Nova and Lila are the MCs in this one. I'm still super excited to read the next books in the series though, and I'm kind of glad we can get Nova out of the way now. I'm so hoping it's finally gonna be a Priest and Bea book, I have a feeling they might be my next favorites, but I would also love a Lysander book too. Or Wrath, but I'm hoping it's not gonna be a dead lover trope and that she's still alive🤞 Either way, I can't wait for the next one!SafetyOM drama - only for a couple of pages, but was with him for 2 years. It ended pretty quick.OW drama- On screen almost threesome/bj but is interrupted. On screen sex scene where the heroine watches at around 27%. Ex-lover/therapist/manipulator comes into the picture wanting to get back with Nova, which we then find out she was preggo with his baby but had an abortion, the whole situation with her is probably what has messed up Nova so bad. Then at around 85%ish, Nova kisses a woman he has slept with before in a ploy to falsely seduce and distract her.H manwhore/h has only been with her fiance Only with each other after kissing for the first time.P.S. NO THREESOME SCENE!! YAY!!

'INKED IN LIES'is the fifth book in Giana Darling's 'The Fallen Men' series. It can be read as a standalone. Spoken in 'Dual Perspectives.'I haveFINALLYpopped my Giana Darling cherry!! I've been wanting and watching this author for a while now but I've just never found the time to pick up a book and read it, until now, now all bets are off, I'm going to make time to read the previous books in this series because god damn this book gave me such a nostalgic feeling, back to when this genre used to be a #oneclick for me above all else.For me it was all about the cover🔥Holy heck he's all kinds of hot!! Then the synopsis had me needing this in my life, then finally jumping into it when it landed I was completely enthralled. Throw in a bit of an age gap and I was all in..I loved this from start to finish and everything else just fell into place, the feels, the swoons, the heartbreak, the love I felt for our heroine and hero was instant..Imagine finding your soulmate at the age of six? Because this is exactly what happened to Lila when the new family moved in across the road from her family. Lila didn't have the best of life, her dad continuously had unsavoury men coming in and out and she barely saw much of her mum, but her one constant was her brother. He was her protector, the one person she loved fully and looked up to above all else.Until the boy next door, the boy who was eleven years her senior also took it on himself to protect her when her brother couldn't and would go above and beyond what was expected of him to make sure she was safe. When her parents meet an unforeseeable end and sister and brother are split up into foster care Johnathon vowed to bring them back together again.As Johnathon is tagging a pub creating memories to send a snap shot to Lila its within these walls where his whole life changes when he meets for the first time men from the chapter 'The fallen men.'They take him under their wing and Nova (Johnathon) is born, he'd finally found the one place where he fitted, even though he had a family that loved him and vice versa he didn't feel like he really fitted, until now. He'd found his own people. His parents fold under much begging from Johnathon, to foster Lila and her brother, it was beyond Lila's wildest dreams, she finally had stable people looking after and caring about her.But when Nova introduces her to the Fallen Men and they also take her under their wing she's finally added to her family.Lila has always loved Nova even before she knew what love was, even though she knew that love would never be reciprocated because he always saw her as the little girl he had to protect.But love always has a way of choosing you. Their story wasn't an easy one to read, it had its ups and downs, some dark times but when you least expect it Nova's eyes open and he sees the woman Lila has become.This ticked so many boxes and I'm so glad I took that chance and read this because all it took was that first chapter and I was hooked. Now I need more..

5 starsShe was as free as the wildflowers she loved so much.My resolve weakened further, a hairline fracture breakin' through to a fault line runnin' the length of my willpower.I was going down fast.Inked in Liesis the fifth book in Giana Darling's 'Fallen Men' series and by far the most angsty.Jonathon 'Nova' Booth and Lila Meadows have known each other for most of Lila's life. All of her best memories involve Nova and he's been there for her more than anyone else. She loves him fiercely, but for so long, Nova's never let himself see Lila like that. But all that is about to change.These kinds of books are my absolute favorites. The ones that keep me on the edge of my seat, give me all the feels, and have a couple that's irrevocably in love with one another. Giana has a real talent for making her romances feel so real and meant to be. Nova and Lila especially, it's obvious that they were meant for each other and I can't imagine either one of them with anyone else. Sigh. Their romance was stunning and so beautiful.With each book I read in this series I get more and more obsessed with The Fallen world. This is by far the best MC series I've ever read and though this book didn't quite top Zeus and LouLou's (I mean... is that even possible?) It's definitely a close second. I absolutely cannot wait to jump into the next book in this series and I hope Giana never stops writing them!

4 Property of Nova Stars ⭐"He planted flowers all over my body, inside the cage of my ribs, in the fertile soil of my heart, yet he never noticed how they bloomed just for him."Even as a 5 your old, Lila Davalos intuitively knew her parents were bad. She'd seen things that most wouldn't witness in a lifetime. During the hardship she found solace in the beauty of flowers and her much older brother Dane, who was the epitome of goodness.With the arrival of the Booth family to their shady neighbourhood, the new neighbours would become a godsend, and their home a haven.Johnathon Booth was fortunate to be born into a family rich in love and support. Moving to a new house he formed an instant friendship with both Dane and Lila. Even with all that he had, he was a restless adolescent with a penchant for mischief and art. As for girls, they were like moths to a flame. They came easy. But at 17 years old, Johnathan and his art were discovered and taken in by the Fallen MC family; and Nova was born.And just like that, a kid who had never been lost in his whole fuckin’ life felt like he’d finally been found.With Nova and Lila being unlikely allies (a teen and a child) It was only natural that she was welcomed into the fold. It was within these club walls that she also grew into her own person. A young woman with unrequited feelings towards Nova that could never be. He made sure of that."You think this is ’bout you? You’re fuckin’ wrong.This is ’bout you wastin’ your life pinin’ for a man who’s never gonna give you a second look ’cause it’s not his right to catch your eye, and it sure as fuck isn’t in him to take care of a girl like you."Remaining friends, their relationship becomes strained. Yet, things change when Nova becomes privy to a plan of Lila endangering herself for the club. He takes it upon himself to wedge himself firmly back into her business. Nova is past denying himself, and will lay claim his way. He is determined to remind Lila who she belongs to.Inked in Lies owned me from the first page. I couldn't put it down. The drama, the intensity and emotions experienced were gripping. I adored the entire back and forth from past to present to what made Nova and Lila who they are today. And their love affair was both beautiful and heartbreaking to watch. The chemistry was off the charts hot. However, I'm not a huge fan of the MC trope so I possibly zoned out during club business. 🖤

★4.5★"You feel this? This is weight of a life. I took it with one squeeze of the trigger.”Inked in Liesis a standalone complex and gritty MC Romance featuring a love that spans decades! Lila is a sweet five years of age when she first meets Nova the man who was destined to change her life. Lila loves flowers, her older brother, and Nova! She will do anything to escape the ugliness that exists in her home. With violence, drug addicts, and heartbreak, she cannot help but find solace in the sweet and loving family that lives next door. When circumstances bring Lila and her brother to a breaking point they end up moving in with the Booths. Nova becomes her older foster brother and all of a sudden things begin to get more complicated than ever. As time passes, the connection between Lila and Nova transforms into so much more than brother-sister love. Although Lila wants nothing more than for Nova to profess his love to her, Nova feels unworthy of his flower child’s love."I knew there were real monsters in the world. I’d been born of one."Told in multiple POV,Inked in Liesis a beautiful blend of taboo, action, suspense, passion, and raw and emotional love. The journey that Nova and Lila take is difficult as they both have baggage to unpack along the way. The dialogue is rich with color and Ms. Darling is a true artist painting a picture full of vivid colors of real-life love, loss, pain, and joy! The spectrum of emotions I experienced while reading this love story left me speechless. Although I adored the complex plot and rich character development, some of the individual choices made by both Nova and Lila caused my blood pressure to skyrocket! The action is intense and the love and loyalty within the brotherhood of the club make the supporting characters unforgettable." I could feel the roots of him tug hard around my heart, urging me to go to him."Overall,Inked in Lieswould appeal to readers who enjoy their heroes rough around the edges. With a tender romance full of challenges every step of the way, Lila and Nova prove once again that love conquers all. This is my first Giana Darling book and I can say with complete certainty it will not be my last. In fact, I have already loaded my kindle with the other books from this series. If there is one thing that I shy away from its angst and yet, I would reread Nova and Lila's story again and again just to relive each and every moment!For more reviews/reveals/giveaways visit:

3.5 Flower Child StarsThe book was well written and the plot was also good and all but there was just this one thing that kept on bugging me so much and because of that i couldn’t enjoy Nova and Lila together. This might completely be a me problem or maybe some of you might agree with me on this.PlotLila was brought up in a house, where her father was a popular drug dealer, her mother was hardly present as she was busy with her shifts in the diner and the only one she basically had was her brother Dane, who she loved more than anything. Until one day 17 year old, Jonathan Booth (Nova) and his family moved in next door and five year old Lila fell in love with him and his family and since then dreamed of becoming a Booth.After one horrific night, Lila (13) and her brother ended up in the care of the Booth family. Since then they have been together, taken care of each other as a family even after Dane passed away during his military service.The first 25% of the book is about the past, how Lila and Dane met the Booth family, their bonding and how Nova met Daddy Zeus (I literally screamed) and was initiated into the Fallen MC.Fast forward to when Lila is 21 years old, Nova confronts her about her “crush on him” and tells her to get over him. So that’s what she does, for the next two years she hardly talks to him, finds another man and gets engaged.Now in the present Zeus is falsely accused and is in jail thanks to The Ventura’s and Lila decides to do something about it as they have been coming after The Fallen MC for far too long. She decides to go undercover and find information on them to finally bring them down for good.I had no great love like Zeus or King, or my girl, Harleigh Rose. I had nothing to lose and so, wasn’t it only fitting I be the one to take the risk? If it didn’t work. If I died… At least I wouldn’t leave behind a love like theirs who would never recover from my death.CharactersLilaMy heart literally broke for her. Minus the scene where she was watching Nova fuck someone else, I really did sympathize with her in the start. Being in love with someone for 18 years, almost her whole life, a man who saved her life, who has always been there for her, it’s not easy. And let’s not forget what all she witnessed since such a young age because of her negligent parents. And then the loss of her brother Dane, Nova had been her rock.And when Nova told Lila to get over him, she did try. It’s just Nova idk didn’t let her go? He kissed her once and couldn’t ignore her anymore and went and fucked her. Kept on fucking her but didn’t want to put any “labels” on their relationship. (Obviously they talked and all too lmao, they have been best friends for so long the only new improvement was the fucking.)Lila didn’t deserve that AT ALL. This is what I couldn’t ignore and this is why I just couldn’t enjoy Nova and her being together. Lila has been in LOVE with him for EIGHTEEN YEARS.And I was––I’d always been––just for him. It only took him eighteen years to notice.Lila deserved the world. And yes Nova was her world and she was happy he was finally paying attention to her but she just gave in so easily to him. Uggggh like she got her heart broken, the least he could have done is chase after her or grovel a little bit. Something that he did TOWARDS THE VERY END OF THE BOOK, like the last chapter I think?”I’m gonna fight for you,” he repeated. “The way I fought for you as a girl. I’m sorry as fuck I ever stopped and I swear to God, I won’t again.”But it didn’t really matter at that time because he already got the girl. However I will point out that towards the end he did reveal something really shocking and adorable and my heart did melt. Also the Epilogue.... CUTEEEEEEEEEEEEEE!!! But I still stand by my words, he should have groveled a little bit for her affection.NovaDon’t get me wrong, I don’t hate Nova or anything, he had his own struggles in life, being the black sheep of the family, he never felt like he was good enough, to everyone he was just a pretty boy and he had his own reasons for never acting up on his feelings for Lila and all.Years of restraint, of tryin’ not to see the woman Lila was bloomin’ into, of knowin’ I’d never be good enough for her even if I was allowed to look, and I finally broke like cheap plastic.But again how easily he got her and then the “No labels thing” pissed me off, that woman understands you, you are basically her entire world, like how have you not put a ring on her yet!!“I don’t think the only thing you have to offer the world is your handsomeness. You are the kindest, loveliest, most misunderstood soul I know, and it breaks my fucking heart because the person who misunderstands you the most is you.”Again this might be a complete MOI problem. Idk. Like I don’t blame Lila also for giving in so easily because if I put myself in her shoes I’m like wouldn’t I do the same thing? After waiting so long and finally getting the attention I wanted? But at the same time I’m like I would prefer a little groveling after all the waiting I have done you know? Is it weird I’m bringing myself into this? 😂😂😂😂The reason I’m focusing so much on the label is because Lila needed it. Even when they were kind off together she was still not sure if he was completely hers and it was just so sad to read about it. You got the man but you don’t have him completely? But I guess that’s the whole premise of the book with regards to Noah and Lila. 🤷🏽‍♀️AnywaaaaaaayyCAN WE TALK ABOUT DADDY ZEUS AND LOULOU”Can I come in?” Loulou asked from the doorframe, holding two squirming babies on each hip with ease like some kinda maternal goddess. “Angel is acting up and I’m pretty sure it’s because she wants her daddy.” “Sure it wasn’t you wanted that, little Lou?” Z asked with a wicked grin that made Lou laugh and trip forward to smack a wet one on his mouth.Yup, still love them.Wanted a Danner and HR moment too but it’s okay. I’ll just read Good Gone Bad again for thatLet me just point on again and say, that the book was overall GREAT, the problem I had can be completely a me problem, if you have been in love with the series so far, I would still recommend to read this one! Because the over all plot regarding the enemy was amazing!

Inked In Lieswas the forbidden gritty raw romance that I never knew I was desperately craving. These characters were flawed, perfect, real, and they showed the kind of love that some of us will spend our entire lives searching for.At five years old, Lila knew that she was in love with her new neighbor. When life gave her family a tragic turn of events which made the family next door her new foster family, her bond with the handsome teenage only grew. As the years begin to go by and Lila is now an adult, those feelings that some have passed off as a silly crush, has only intensified and with passion like this, it’s only a matter of time before you combust.A forbidden romance with Unrequited love is a combination that can not only lead to drama, but it can change the dynamic of so many things. Friendships can be put to the test, heartache will be around the corner, and the passion can seem as if it might dwindle out before it even got the chance to fully blaze. This story gave us the highs and lows of a couple that fought tooth and nail for each other in their own way. I didn’t always agree with their tactics, and I certainly wanted to shake a few people but you couldn’t deny the love and the bond that these characters had with each other.If you’ve read any of the Fallen Men books, then you’re going to fall heart first in love with this one. Nova brought the swagger that is legendary in this author’s heroes and Lila was the ultimate heroine. She was strong, fierce and she knew exactly what she wanted and she had no problem fighting to get it. This is another addition that is not to be missed and I can’t wait to see what else this author is going to come up with.

They first meet when Lila is only a little girl and Nova quickly becomes her everything. His family becoming Lila's escape when all she's known in her young life have been liars and criminals. Lila's childhood is anything but pretty or easy. It's full of loss, pain, and lies. But what starts as a bit of hero worship soon bleeds into something so much more as Lila grows older.Nova is the pretty playboy of the Fallen Men. He's quick to smile and isn't above using his good looks to get what he wants. He's perfectly content spending each night with a different woman and has no illusions that he would ever want more. But not all is as it seems with Nova. He's a true wolf in sheep's clothing. His easy smile hides some very dark demons. Lila has always been like a foster sister to him but there's also something more that pulses between them when she gets older. He knows he can't be what he wants and he pushes her away, cruelly and bluntly. Theirs is a dance of push and pull that will drive you to the brink of madness at times, but it hurts so good!There's so much that happens in this book; secrets unravel with so many twists and turns, it will leave your head reeling. The romance is gritty, frustrating, and oh so delicious. Lila, even if she does forgive Nova too easily in parts, is still a force to be reckoned with and I really loved her fire. Nova was impossible not to love, even when you wanted to hate him. His decisions in parts had me wanting to shake him and rip my hair out. But there's just something so addictive about this man.hight="50" width="50" />Loving someone shouldn’t be contingent on them returning that affection. Loving someone existed apart from reciprocation.I loved the story and the gritty feels of it. Lived for it. But it was missing some polish. There were some minor continuity and timeline issues. There were also some very minor characterization issues. For example, 17 year old Nova sounded exactly like 30 year old Nova biker, which wasn't a huge thing in the grand scheme of things, but still noticeable. The other thing was all the fallen men sound the same. same intonations, same turn of phrases and lack of constants. Don't get me wrong, the biker speak is great, and I loved the Kristen Ashley feel of it in parts. But in the same breath, it was tough to read when it was also in their inner monologues. Like I would have to re-read sentences 3 times before it finally clicked what they were saying. Sorry, y'all, I simply couldn't shut off my picky brain long enough to look past it. Le sigh. This series has a very strong KA feel to it, which I love. But the lack of constants and the blurring of words like "all of the" for:"allathe brothers" or "you think you can handleallathat" became a bit grating for me.Clearly I still really enjoyed the book so please take my nitpicking with a grain of salt. Because it's just that; nitpicking. The story was great and I devoured it. AfterWelcome to the Dark Sideit has now become my second favorite book in the series. If you love gritty MC, it's definitely not to be missed. Giana is a talented author that can weave an addictive tale full of twists and turns that will have you glued to the pages.ARC courtesy of author in exchange for an honest reviewFind me on:

Well this is what I get for my high expectations. Warning-- this will get ranty.Prolonged, agonizing torture. That is what I felt throughout the book. I just wanted it to end. LikeHOW. MUCH. MORE. PAINmustIthis girl endure? Before she breaks apart completely. It was both sad and exhausting to read. Nova, man, I was all hyped up, ready to see some major groveling. Him kicking ass. Fight for his Lila. Unfortunately, he didn’t quite conquer his demons or find his balls until the end. Such a shame.EIGHTEENyears of anguished and unrequited love (or so it appears) for Lila. But at the end, it was kinda simple how he accepts it. He was fortunate she was strong and never waver in her love.Nova spent the majority of book lost in his own pity party. Running from his feelings. Because of something that happened with an ex-lover and how he perceives himself—he wanted to spare Lila the pain. Didn’t felt he deserve or was worthy of her love. So instead, he cruelly rejects Lila and denies what he feels. REPEATEDLY. Nova was also the cause of Lila’s breakup. Yep, it sucks that the first time Lila tries to move on, he had to pull a dick move just to show the power he still has over her. That pissed me off so bad. How selfish. He couldn't commit, but he'd wreck her relationship? I LOVED how Zeus calls him out on it“Don’t fuck with Lila, brother,” Zeus warned. You gotta know the girls’ had feelings for ya since she was a girl. She’s tryin’ to move on. Yeah, that Jake is a pansy-ass, but it’s her choice. You playin’ games with her ‘cause you miss havin’ her undivided attention? Don’t do it. She’s been through enough in her life.”For all the heartache he put Lila through, I kept waiting to see the grand, epic moment. Of how Nova would redeem himself. I wasn’t as impressed as Lila but hey, she finally got her wish and dream guy. As for his reasons not to pursue Lila, I felt he used it as crutch to ward Lila off and punish himself. So many wasted years, especially when he reveals she's the one for him too. Oooh, really buddy? Thanks for the front seat to all your sexcapades and sex tales. Fun times. And Lila, lord, this girl deserves a medal. Even when she says she gives up on Nova, she didn’t hesitate to defend or protect him (literally fighting his ex). Her loyalty is admirable but draining. I wanted her to stop being such masochist, a glutton for punishment. Nova toys with her like a fucking puppet and she plays into his game. I wanted to judo-chop him so bad for the things he would say or do to push her away."I don't got any flowers or romance for you, and being honest, I never fucking' will. Get that through your head, yeah? I don't need another pathetic girl trailing' after me looking for scraps"."This is 'bout you wasting' your life pinin' for a man who's never gonna give you a second look 'cause it's not his right to catch your eyes, and it sure as fuck isn't in him to take care of a girl like you".Does this deter Lila? Somewhat. Lila knew her worth. But she still remains in his life. If not as a lover, then a friend. She valued their connection and relationship too much. It was too strong and important for her to throw it—him all away. I beg to differ, but that’s just me. And for Lila, Nova's always been her hero. Her protector and family. After tragically losing most of hers. Still it wasn’t easy watching her inevitable heartbreak as Nova lives up to his man-whore-extraordinaire reputation. Poor girl Lila even walked in on him screwing someone. And yes, she stayed to watch. Andof courseshe got turned on. That's a prerequisite for these scenarios, don't cha know."The woman groaned in ecstasy as he ground into her at the end of each thrust. For an awful desperate second, I thought about grinding against the doorframe, needing to feel how he might feel against her. Instead, I tweaked my nipple with my free hand and choked off a moan."Bleh. I am still stunned that any heroines still get aroused when witnessing these scenes. I will never find that appealing. As for Nova's reaction"He just stared at me. He gave me nothing. Instead, after a moment, his gaze cut to woman curling like a satisfied cat on the bed, and he smirked at her, giving her exposed ass a swat before he moved toward the door."Nova, you colossal asshole. Wish Lila had given him a taste of his own medicine.So yep, this book was definitely a flop to me. Though I am probably in the minority as other seems to love it. What can I say, I’m a tough critic. But like I stated in Danner’s book, I wanted to see Nova prove himself and fight for Lila. Did we get that? Eh. It was kinda opposite to me. Nova had a couple possessive, jealous moments. And he rescued Lila from her uncover mission. But most of the book it was Lila doing the fighting. Putting herself in danger. Being a martyr. And still assuring Nova of his good heart. That he is capable of receiving and giving love. So hurray, Nova accepts this, as well he should. She sacrificed a lot for him. Also, I kept hearing rebel, dark horse, but I'm like heh? What was the big insecurity or big struggle he had to overcome? What was Nova rebelling against? Feeling different than his brothers? Nova had a pretty good life early on. Far as I can tell. Wonderful family and support system. All the girls he had no issues picking up. (I could have done w/out that almost threesomes, thanks.)

4.25 Stars 🌟Inked in Liesis the 5th book in the Fallen Men series and even though these books follow different couples, each book contains storylines about the MC and carries on throughout the entire series so I highly suggest reading them in order especially since this book contains major spoilers for each book as the timelines match up. Inked in Lies is Nova & Lila's story and like each book in this series it's an age gap romance with Nova being 12 years older. We have met bothLila Meadows and Jonathon 'Nova' Booththroughout the series and going into this book I knew it would revolve around unrequited love with a slight forbidden aspect.Lilahas been in love with Jonathon Booth since she was 5 years old and he moved into the house next door with his family. The first 30% of the book is heavily focused on their back story and you get to see some of the most important and tragic parts of their past that are constantly bringing them together, with Lila falling more and more in love with Nova and Nova growing a more familial and protective type of bond with her. We also see a snipet of when Nova first joins the Fallen MC and how Lila gets her name. I loved every minute of their backstory with parts of it even breaking my heart but it helps give you a real understanding of how and why Lila loves Nova so deeply and unconditionally.Jonathon 'Nova' BoothOH MY this boy, man, manchild, manho whatever you want to call him, I love him, I love him and oh did I mention I love him. Was he perfect? No but he was real, raw and stayed true to the character we have seen glimpses of this entire series. He was his usual playful, fun, protective, carefree self with just the right amount of alphaness and possiveness. I loved that he had a sweet, caring and softside of himself that he only resevered for Lila and that she seemed to bring out in him whenever they were together. The deeper we get into his story the more we understand some of his reseverations and even though there were times he frustrated me with not always knowing the right thing to say but I felt his actions really spoke for when his words failed.I personally thought Lila could've pushed back a little more and given him a taste of his own medicine but I truly don't blame her for wanting anything and everything from Nova as I'm sure I would've been the same. I don't even know what exactly it was about him that made me fall so hard but I did. I enjoyed getting to see Nova through Lila's eyes and this was probably my 2nd favourite book in the series withWelcome to the Darkside (book 2)still being my favourite. There were parts of the story that I found slightly predictable but that didn't really bother me at all.I only recently discovered this series and I'm a little obsessed. I have throughly enjoyed all the books and have loved getting lost in the world of the Fallen Men and I'm so excited to see whose story is next and I'm secretly hoping its Lysander & Honey and I'm hoping we find out more about Ares because he is definitely one of my favourite characters in this series aswell as seeing some of the unresolved storylines being completed.《book aesthetic made by me. I do not own the pictures, all found via pinterest》

Nova and Lila’s story is an angsty one, It’s a friend's to lovers slight age-gap (12 years) MC romance and their journey together begins when they are practically children.Nova or Johnathon as he was then called moves in with his family across the road from a young Lila and her older brother Dane.They are the children of a local dealer and from the minute their fates collided there was a connection.An older Nova took the young girl who had grown up too soon under his wing becoming another older brother and protector alongside Dane.When they first meet he’s 17 the same age as her brother and she’s just 5 and she becomes the little sister he’s never had.When an event removes her from her family Novas family adopts both Lila and Dane into their own giving the young girl a sense of family and stability she’s never had before and Nova eventually becomes everything to her especially when tragedy hits her life again shattering her whole world, he then becomes her rock and her best friend rolled into one.When Nova short for Casanova on account of his love for the ladies patches into the Fallen MC Lila’s family expands even further but over the years and as she matures her feelings towards him start to change and instead of the brother she has always seen her love morphs into something more.That is until Nova shuts it down doing damage to Lila’s heart in the process trying to move on she tries to separate and avoid the man who has her heart but the lives are so intertwined with each other’s it’s hard to separate herself fully.It’s only when Lila’s finally moving on that Nova Sick of the distance between them and also the fact she’s placed herself in danger finally tries to get back what’s he lost under the guise of protection he directly involves himself back into her life much to Lila’s annoyance.He wants her friendship back and misses her but the chemistry between these two is explosive and it’s not long before there crossing boundaries that Nova had initially set in stone.I really felt for Lila unrequited love is a bloody bitch and she’s got a bad dose.She’s also terrified of letting him too close to her heart knowing he has the power to destroy her completely.But she’s like a moth to the flame and just can’t help herself she loved him for years despite his disinterest.Nova definitely has self-esteem issues if that can be believed he wrongly thinks he’s got nothing to offer other than his pretty face.He struggles with allowing Lila out of the box he has placed her in and also believe he’s incapable of deep love.His inability to not place labels on their relationship is also hurtful as Lila wants nothing more than for him to finally claim her publicly, I also think he believes that they can just go back to how they were before and breaking Lila’s heart would be for her own good he believes he’s just not worth it and doesn’t deserve her.I was annoyed with him but at the same time sad that he couldn’t see his own worth and himself through Lila’s eyes to her he is everything.This had danger and secrets and shocks it brought me to tears and was so emotionally poignant at times.I could feel the love and connection between these two times infinity they really do make a beautiful couple.Nova just needs to get his head out of his arse and claim his flower-girl and I was hopefully optimistic that he would get there in the end and also manage to convince her he felt the same.I enjoyed this one a whole lot in fact it surpassed my expectations and I definitely recommend this series.Reviewed By Beckie 5 “Property of Nova”⭐️So addictive this book was! I literally had times when I was holding my breath and didn’t realize it. This is unrequited love at its finest! Childhood friends to kind of foster siblings to eventually the ultimate of lovers. This book had BITE! So angsty and so intense!!“Bad boys, good men” MOST DEFINITELY!I love how these two have known each other forever and the fierce love that Lila kept stuffed down Nova was pretty much oblivious to. He couldn’t afford to even think about those kind of thoughts about Li. Then that all changes and when I tell you it gets hot asf—trust me. Giana has such a gift of making these relationships feel so real. Literal chill bumps on my skin and flutters in my tummy. It’s rare for book highs like this, but that’s the power of Giana and her word magic.These Fallen Men and their women own me. Such strong alphas that get IT done, but it’s their women that hold the power. The dynamic is incredible! The darkness. The grit of these storylines I’m addicted to. I love that Giana shies away from nothing and I do mean nothing. I can almost feel the blood spray on my skin as I’m listening because I get so immersed in the story. Just so damn good!“It’s like clean laundry and sleeping in our own bed after vacation. It’s like loving someone because you were born loving them, like your parents or siblings, but more. You’re more than my family, you’re more than my best friend. You’re the choice I want to make every single day, to live to the fullest and love the hardest.”
Truly was a beautiful story 🥹

I loved this book so much! It’s been 2 years since I’ve read the Fallen Men series and I have a few books to catch up on before the newest book, book 7 drops in a couple weeks. So thanks to the Fallen Men readalong going on currently, I was given that push to jump back in! And omg am I so glad I did!Nova and Lila were the sweetest, hottest, best thing ever and I could not get enough of them! Being back with the entire Fallen Men family was just everything I was missing and I loved seeing them all in Entrance again so much! Getting to see Zeus and his family again, ugh I just love them all so much. Lila is just 5 when she meets Nova, her new neighbor’s son. He becomes fast friends with her older brother, being 12 years older than her, but becomes a sort of protector to young Lila. His family ends up taking in both Lila and her older brother when their lives get out of control, becoming their foster family. Since Nova is so much older than Lila they barely ever live in the same house and don’t get together as more until she’s 23 and he’s 35. And the angst and caretaking and love between these 2 is just everything! I loved how he tattooed her for years, calls her “Flower Child”, and just all the shared history between them. I love Lila’s friendship with Harleigh Rose over the years and Zeus caring and protecting her too. This whole family is just so special.“Because damn him for loving me like that, so much, but platonically. Always platonically. How could a girl ever be expected to move on from a man who would do anything to keep her safe? He planted flowers all over my body, inside the cage of my ribs, in the fertile soil of my heart, yet he never noticed how they bloomed just for him.”

book.title: Sinner
book.author: Sierra Simone
book.description: I'm not a good man, and I've never pretended to be. I don't believe in goodness or God or any happy ending that isn't paid for in advance. In fact, I've got my own personal holy trinity: in the name of money, sex, and Macallan 18, amen.So when the gorgeous, brilliant Zenny Iverson asks me to teach her about sex, I want to say yes, I really do. Unfortunately, there are several reasons to say no—reasons that even a very bad man like myself can't ignore.1. She's my best friend's little sister.2. She's too young for me. Like way too young.3. She's a nun. Or about to be anyway.But I want her. I want her even with my best friend and God in the way, I want to teach her and touch her and love her, and I know that makes me something much worse than a very bad man.It makes me a sinner.Sinner is a standalone companion to Priest about Father Bell's brother Sean. You do not have to read Priest or Midnight Mass to read Sinner.

reviews.text: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️1/2! Sinner (stand-alone). A sinner meets a saint & while they seduce & fuck each to oblivion they must confront their relationships with family, each other & God!“God, the fucking irony of a sinner loving a nun.”Books in series are stand-alones though most enjoyed if read in order:Book 1:PriestBook 1.5:Midnight MassBook 2:SinnerSinner(stand-alone) is manwhore and venture CapitalistSean Bell’sstory of how he for the third time in his life was confronted with the complexity of his relationship and lack thereof with God.“I shouldn’t be swelling and leaking as I think about her showing me her cunt that she’s promised to keep pure and untouched for her church.”Upon meeting a sexy too young girl at a fundraiser his life is turned upside down when he discovers not only is she fifteen years his junior, she is also the baby-sister of his best friend. But worst of all she is a postulant about to embark onbecoming a nun.“I love to fuck. I love to make women come. I love to see their snug, little cunts, I love to taste them, and push my big cock into them until they stretch.”College senior and postulantZenobia Iverson, Zenny aka Zenny -bugused to crush on Sean, but he barely noticed her. Wanting to make sure she makes the right decision shemakes Sean a proposition:Seduce her, in every aspect of the word; sexually, worldly and carnally.“Does this little nun need to be fucked?” I growl into her ear as I pin her against the wall.”The story follows them as Seanseduces and corruptsZenny with tons of filthy sex (in sacred places). It’s ajourneyof sexual exploration, self-reflection and growth as they are compelled to tackle family, shame, fear, mortality, revenge, guilt, self-worth, lust, purpose, truth, legacy, divinity and love.Seven words to describeSean Bell:Dirty-talker, reflective, nurturing, easy-going, possessive, conflicted and lost.Seven words to describeZenobia Iverson, Zenny aka Zenny -bug:Stubborn, independent, lonely, conflicted, selfish, immature and private.Sinner,told from mostly from Sean’s POV, is a candid glimpse into an enigma of a man who has struggled, since a tragedy, in his relationship with god. It’s a mixture ofMay-Decemberandstar-crossedlovers featuring two lonely souls confused by life and purpose. It ends a year later with compromise, forgiveness, peace and love.Erotic! Thought-provoking! Heart-warming!“I’m acutely painfully aware that she’ll never be mine. She will always be God’s.”***Hero: ★★★★★Heroine: ★★★★Plot: ★★★★1/2Storytelling: ★★★★★Sexual tension: ★★★★★Sex scenes: ★★★★★Story ending: ★★★★1/2******************************************OVERALL RATING:⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️1/2Angst:| HIGH FOCUS |Darkness:| MEDIUM FOCUS |Humor:| LOW FOCUS |Kink:| HIGH FOCUS |Romance:| HIGH FOCUS |Sex frequency:| HIGH FOCUS |Suspense:| MEDIUM FOCUS |ARC provided to me by author Sierra Simone in exchange for an honest review.
