My review will be in the minority and I have waited to post my review out of respect. So here it goes......First, I must say wholeheartedly, Sierra Simone writes beautifully. The intricacies of sexuality and religion are woven quite expertly throughout this story. The struggles of belief and faith battle with the present of belief and faith. It’s a painfully visually struggle between the two sides because of the background of the past. And I liked how the author infuses the story with battling religious views.I will say that I love the hero, Sean Bell. He’s 36, he’s very successful, he loves his family and friends, but has had tragedy in his life and has turned his back on religion. Having lost someone in his life and now fixing to lose another, he has a war raging within himself. And so what I really enjoyed most in the story was Sean’s struggle with his religion, his demons, his responsibilities to his family and the health of his mom.Next, the heroine is Zenobia “Zenny” Iverson, age 21, the young virgin, soon to be nun, and sister of his best friend. I could never fully connect with her. The more I read the more disconnect I felt. And so the connection between Sean and Zenny felt misaligned. There was lots of sex but it didn’t feel right. Maybe the age difference, maybe the youngness of the heroine, maybe the insta-lust, maybe the premise of being together for a month and then going off to be a nun and Sean returning to his daily routine.......it just all didn’t work for me.There are many glowing reviews of Sinners. I am a big fan of Sierra Simone but sadly for me, I struggled with this book.*advanced copy provided in exchange for an honest review*

This book gave what it was selling but also was surprisingly progressive in addressing matters like death, faith, sex and the church, guilt, belief/non belief and race. Priest touched on some of these things but I feel like this book leaned more in to these subjects.Don't get me wrong the smut is still the starring character of the book, and it is done VERY well!😂 But I really liked these pondering questions that were presented throughout the story. You don't have to agree with them but it's interesting to question things that have been engrained in our culture through the church, gender identities/politics and stereotypes.That being said,.... none of these people in this series have any business being priests, nuns or otherwise😂 It's absurd, but we are going to look the other way in order to fulfill the "narrative" Also these Bell men are made of a different stuff! Holy Crap! (see what I did there?) 😜Sierra Simone is incredibly gifted as a writer, her prose are some of the most beautiful I have read the emotion grips you right in the heart and it's very human. 💙💙

NOW LIVE!Amazon US*Amazon UKSinneris the sequel toPriest, one of the first taboo books I read. So of course I jumped at the opportunity to read this book and meet a real Sinner aka Sean Bell. And I can say will full certainty that it did not disappoint.Comparing it to it's predecessor, it wasn't as hot or taboo. I liked that it kept me on edge the whole time as I tried to figure the ending out all while delivering some steamy scenes between these two. It can be read as a standalone with no problem but of course since I'm still in love with Father Bell, I'll recommend readingPriestif you haven't already.ARC kindly provided in exchange for an honest review.

I am not exaggerating when I say that SINNER might be the best romance I've ever read.It is a powerful erotic inspirational romance that twists and turns and evolves on the page from one simple thing to something beautifully complex. While SINNER is in the PRIEST series -- the hero is the brother of the hero of PRIEST, and the heroine is his best friend's little sister, who is one month from taking her vows to become a nun -- it can easily be read as a standalone, but if you're interested in the way an author evolves her craft, I encourage you to read PRIEST first, which is a fabulous book. SINNER, however, is the natural progression of PRIEST -- equally erotic, but beautifully elevated to a meditation on faith, on love, on sexual desire, on family, on responsibility, on sacrifice. It's stunning, and a perfect example of what romance writers can make this genre do.Highly, highly recommended.

I swear! It’s the books I never intend to read that end up being my favorite. Not only is this my favorite so far (I’m still holding out for Saint and what’s to come for the youngest Bell brother) in the series. It’s my favorite by this author.Sean is super cocky, normally that would get on my nerves but he’s the best dirty talker ever so I can overlook it.Sierra’s stories don’t always take place in Kansas City but it’s awesome when they do. It’s fun hearing about all these places I’m aware of or have been to.Even though this is sinfully salacious, some of the best steam even by this writer’s standards. There’s a giant moral to this story; if you read Priest, you know what happened to the boy’s sister. Because of that Sean has always equated sex with wrong, he never had a relationship, he didn’t date and he only slept with women who for lack of a better term were morally corrupt already.Through this story, through being with a girl who is on the cusp of becoming a nun and through the advice of his brother Father Bell he has an awakening. Sex in itself isn’t sinful or wrong, it’s beautiful if it’s with the right person. And Zenny? She’s that person for him. The irony is she wants to marry God.Goddam it! I’ve been hit left and right with the hottest dirty talk, the steamiest scenes that I don’t think I’ve ever been this hot and bothered. So on top of that you need to break my heart by having him fall head over heels in love with the girl that’s already taken?! By Jesus?! 🥺Seriously?! Everything about this book was perfect, I almost loved Zinny as much as I loved Sean but then she had to break my boy’s heart a couple times because apparently he’s selfish for loving her and she’s not a coward. In my mind she’s the one who’s selfish and petty, you heard me. Her reasons for continuing on this path despite the love they share was petty.So, I will grudgingly admit that certain people that shall not be named have been forgiven. Only because it’s the Christian thing to do. 😝
